label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. And I always, whenever my little head aches, and I always, whenever my head hurts? My little head aches, my darling forgets, and I always, whenever my head hurts?  
2. But I surely will not fall ill for you, but I surely will not suffer for you. I will not fall ill if I don’t get you, girl, but I surely will not suffer for you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oh little path, worn path, oh little path to my beloved, when I used to come to you, I desired your daughter, others came to you.  
2. A pity of the journey I walked, a pity of a single step, my golden Nanynka, dear little dove, a pity of youth.  
3. If there were in the beautiful sky as many beautiful stars as my dear, charming girl, gave me kisses.  
4. If there were in the whole world as many beautiful monasteries as my dear, charming girl, gave me lodgings.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oh, what a nice thing it is that I was a hunter, I shot a little dove, it fell on the juniper, I shot a little dove, it fell on the juniper.  
2. My dear saw it, immediately ran there for it, come on, my dear, escort me all the way to our yard.  
3. And I will not go with you, I know I will not be yours, you are the son of rich parents and I have only poverty.  
4. Don't worry about the fact that I am the son of a rich man, I will marry you, just don't be mad at me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oh my God, I feel worse, I am tormented, no one knows what hurts me, and I won't tell. My faint heart hurts, and that, my darling, is for you. We loved each other faithfully, but we will not anymore.  
2. Our landlord told me that I am poor, that your father will not give you to me as a husband. You were courting once, I didn't know about it until my friend hinted it to me.  
3. She came to me and said: Dear Mařenka, I will tell you a piece of news I saw. Your beloved is unfaithful to you, he was courting another. If I were you, I would return his golden ring.  
4. That ring you gave me, I will not wear it, I will throw it into the water and swim after it. As cold as the water is, so is our love disappointed; another girl can love you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oh my dear God, listen to what I dreamed today, that six fellows were standing under our windows, that six fellows were standing under our windows.  
2. Six fellows were standing, among them my Pepíček, who took away my green wreath.  
3. It wasn’t, my darling, it wasn’t because of you, when you used to come to our room at night.  
4. At night to the room, that was at one o’clock, wait until I tell you this one news.  
5. Little lacer, my skirts are getting shorter, what will my dear mother probably say about that?  
6. My dear mother, my friends, that my black dresses are getting shorter.  
7. She went to the little grove, sat on the grass, called out in a sad voice: Come, my darling!  
8. To this call, the darling came to her: Why do you cry and lament, my delight?  
9. How could I not cry and lament to myself, when I can no longer tie my skirts?  
10. He took his hat off his head, tore off the ribbon from it, here, my delight, set your skirt!  
11. I will set my skirt, but I will still be sad, I will never go to the grove again until death.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Andulka, my child, I like you very much, Andulka, my child, I love you. Those people don’t wish me well because I go after you, those people don’t wish me well because I love you.  
2. Defiance against defiance to someone at your house, defiance against defiance to someone at your gate. Two roses are growing there, no one can, no one can love them.  
3. I just sneaked in once, I was beaten like a dog, I just sneaked in once, I received a lot. Those people don’t wish me well because I go after you, those people don’t wish me well because I love you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. My little parakeet, don’t go away from me, my little parakeet, don’t go away from me, wait for me there, in the pink orchard, there we will meet again, my little parakeet.  
2. My little parakeet, you must be mine, I will buy you shoes, and two dresses. The shoes are small, the skirt is getting short, we will go together to ask your mother.  
3. Dear Pepíček, don’t blame me for saying that we were together in the garden at night. We will go together to the parish to ask the reverend to tie our hands together, I will be your wife.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: My little parrot, come to the room, I'll give you a kiss, you will be mine.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Neither you, my parents, think that I would take for myself what I do not like, but what you want. 2. But what I want, my parents, wish for me, after all, you will not be with me until death, my parents.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When the dry linden tree at Limburk turns green, come to me, my dear Pepíček, come to see me.  
2. My darling is angry with me, he doesn’t even speak to me; if it lasts long, let him kiss my kapec.  
3. My darling is angry with me, he’s looking at me from the dormer window; if it lasts long, let him lick my kapec.  

It was said to be sung previously during dancing; now it is played by brass bands at funerals. Interludes recorded according to the lead part.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. It was the first of May, and I wanted to go to my lover. She went to the grove to pick flowers so she could weave wreaths, [refrain] there in that meadow, on a green blanket.  
2. Oh daughter, daughter, dear daughter, where were you walking for so long yesterday? And I was at confession, the maltsters know about it.  
3. When I had picked enough flowers, I saw a handsome young man there, he was sitting under a little tree, and I glanced at him out of the corner of my eye.  
4. That older gentleman was the one who questioned me, and the young man gave absolution, and the worker who dried malt was lifting his shirt up.  
5. And the older gentleman is the best, that’s why he is the biggest of all, he talks little and works a lot, he doesn’t make much fuss with words.  

hvozda = worker who dries malt; tůra (colloquial) = effort, exertion

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. It was once on a Sunday, father and mother went away, it was raining, there was a lot of lightning, and I was lonely by myself.  
2. A little bagpiper came to our window, he was so soaked, poor thing, shaking all over with cold, I covered him with a quilt.  
3. It lasted a little moment, he wanted a sweet little kiss from me, when he got it, he immediately wanted another one, as the time happily passed for us.  
4. If mother knew, she would invite father to me, he would no longer be allowed to come to us, poor thing, that charming little bagpiper.  
5. Father and mother came back, and they immediately noticed on the daughter that she had a lover, Pepík or Frantík.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: What used to be, will no longer be, times are changing, it's lost, dear boys, my pleasure. The water took it from me, it's no loss, boys, we still have plenty of time for marriage.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. How much I have walked, stepped through mud, all for you, dear Mařenko. I stepped through mud, but did not get you, all else I endured for you.  
2. Your parents are tyrants, they did not want me to love you, they did not wish me to have you, they gave you to another and murdered my young body.  
3. When my young body lies in the coffin, you will pass by, stop by me, you will cry, lament to yourself and blame me for the sorrow.  
4. When my young body is carried to the grave, sad relics will sing, on trombones will sound mournful notes so that crying and wailing cannot be heard.  
5. Now I am abandoned like a pear tree in the field, the pears have been picked, it stands there sadly. Oh dear God, give me strength so that another young man may love me.  
6. Now I am abandoned like a dove that has lost its mate. Oh dear God, give me strength so that another young man may love me.  
7. Now I am abandoned like an orphan who wanders the world, looking for bread. Oh dear God, give me strength so that another young man may love me.  
8. Now I am abandoned like a maple tree in the forest, no one will believe what love carries. Oh dear God, give me strength so that another young man may love me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. What were you doing, firefighters, what were you doing that you let the brewery burn down?  
2. Not only the brewery, but also the little cottage where I deceived a poor girl.  
3. Poor girl, miserable creature, wait, you rascal, what God will impose on you.  
4. He will impose on you great suffering, stones will fall on you from the clouds.  
5. From clouds of stones and peacock feathers, every girl is a fool who trusts a boy.  
6. I believed him, he did not love me, I swore to him, and he deceived me.  
7. You can trust a boy as much as a scorpion, a snake, he has ten poisons under his tongue.  
8. He has honey on his tongue, poison under his tongue, be careful, girl, not to be deceived by him.  
9. If he deceives you, he will not marry you, he will ride away on his horse and run away from you.  
10. In a valley, in seclusion, in that little cottage, Pepíček used to go there, he used to go to Tonička.  
11. She sat in the little boat, calling: Pepíček, come and lay your little head on my lap!  
12. She laid her head on the lap, sighed deeply, bitterly cried for every single hair.  
13. She sat on the little bed, calling: Pepíček, come and see what a little girl I have!  
The song originates from Klomín [Chlumín] and is said to have been composed by Josef Šmucr.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Which seems to me, my dear, beautiful, will you be as beautiful as you seem? Even more beautiful, my dearest, take me, take me, you will take care of me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I was waiting, I did not sleep until half past twelve, I did not expect that the newspapers would come to me. The hours strike nicely, one after another, I did not expect that they would take the boy away from me.  
2. You clock of Roletán, why did you stop striking, that you quickly separated me from my beloved? You will not strike anymore, you will not deceive us anymore, and you, my blue eyes, will cry.  
3. Stay with me, my Pepíček, stay with me until morning! And I do not want to, I will not stay, my mother forbids me, that I must not love you, that I should look for a wealthy one. I will not choose a rich one, I would rather go to the army.  
The song is very drawn out, rhythmically indefinite. The marked triplets were sometimes stretched by the singer into simple quarter notes, so a 5/4 measure was created here.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Black wool on a white lamb, black wool on a white lamb, oh, where are you wandering, oh, where are you wandering, blue-eyed Janku?  
2. And I am going on a long journey, we will look for a rich bride.  
3. Luďa says they won’t give her to me, let them give her or not, they will add anyway, to my dear, truly.  
4. Let the crow make friends with the dove, I wander, I don’t wander, I would like to go, I don’t know where, I would like to go to the woodcutter.  
5. Luďa says work is bad, but I will find it, I will not go for poverty, I will overcome this hardship.  

The song has become familiar through the influence of school.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Black eyes, go to sleep, you must, under the maple tree, under the maple tree, wake up in the morning.  
2. Early in the morning, early dawn, before the sun rises.  
3. The sun is already rising, my beloved is walking.  
4. She is walking through the market square, bringing us news.  
5. Such news that they are recruiting for the army.  
6. When they recruit, they will take, I will go too.  
7. I will jump on my horse, wave a white scarf.  
8. Goodbye, my beloved, tomorrow will be different again.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A red rosebud blooms with a white flower, ah, how my blue eyes will cry.  
2. My blue eyes, why do you cry? What you have grown used to, you must unlearn.  
3. And we have grown used to loving Toníček, but now we will have to unlearn with difficulty.  
4. And he used to come to our garden, so I would let him pick violets.  
5. I let him pick violets, and in a little while he gave me a kiss.  
6. Do not come to us, Toníček, to our house, until I knock down the window with roses.  
7. I will knock down the window with roses, I will tear it with thorns, for I love you, Toníček, I love you.  
8. Coachmen, coachmen, get on your coaches, look for my green wreath in Zlosejně.  
9. If we harnessed three hundred pairs of horses, no one would catch your wreath, no one.  
10. We have seen your wreath, we have seen it, how three white ribbons flowed on it.  
11. Those were not ribbons, that was my wreath, which my dear Toníček gave me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Little red rose, why don’t you bloom? Why don’t you come to us, Jeníček, why don’t you come to us? If I went to you, you would cry, wiping your eyes with a red scarf. Little red rose, why don’t you bloom?  
2. Why would I cry when nothing hurts me? We loved each other like two pigeons. Like two pigeons, like two doves, I gave you sincere kisses.  
3. Sincere kisses, your love is false, I won’t believe you when there is a wedding announcement. When there is an announcement at our rectory, then I will tell your brother the blacksmith.  
4. To your brother the blacksmith, to the mother of the bride, I will kiss her hands every morning. Hands every morning, feet every evening, and all this because you are mine after all.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Whose fields are these, unplowed fields? Pepíček is grazing there, Pepíček is grazing four black horses there.  
2. Four black horses, the fifth is a foal, my sweetheart is carrying a lad to me.  
3. Where are you carrying that to me, isn’t it mine? Two other young men used to come after you.  
4. Others used to come, you used to come too, you used to be at our place until half past four.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Far and wide is the milkwort field, my pleasure walks through it, my pleasure walks through it. 2. It walks, it walks, it cries and laments, you were the only one destined to me by God. 3. Destined by God, given by my parents, and only God Himself knows what is between us. 4. Between the two of us, the most steadfast love, it will last until the longest death.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. My beloved child, full of beauty, my heart longs for you, where are you? I suffer for you day and night, while your thoughts are elsewhere for now.  
2. I wish you always happiness and health, for I love you above all else. This love of mine shall last forever, no one but you can extinguish it.  
3. Why do you pride yourself on wealth? You will find many examples. There were many who had thousands, but when they lacked love, everything was gone.  
4. Oh, strong love, whoever tries you must endure much suffering. A short time of joy and then sorrow again—I have experienced more than enough of that.  
5. Love is a just and beautiful virtue; it turns many hardships into joy. Even if a person sat in gold, it counts for nothing if they do not have that love.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A girl at the age of sixteen is already ruining the young world; her sweetheart calls for her to come to him a little.  
2. When the mother heard this, she called to her daughter: Oh dear daughter, what are you doing, calling for your beloved already?  
3. You are only sixteen years old, and you already intend to ruin the world? Oh dearest mother, do not oppose our love.  
4. For you were like me, you loved a young man; our father used to say when he used to visit you.  
5. In that green grove, you gave him a kiss. Shortly after, it came out: that kiss, that was me.  
Melody unknown.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. There is a path to the farm, well-trodden, nice, my Andulka walked there, she is a pretty girl, my Andulka walked there, she is a pretty girl.  
2. The King's horses, they would be vigorous, but their son does not know how to feed them.  
3. He gives them a handful of oats, half a pail of water, but who is to blame for the fact that he goes to the Vencl family?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. It’s no use, parents, no use insisting that I won’t become a priest, you’ll see.  
2. If I had to go to a sick person at night, and I went, I would wander off to a pretty girl.  
3. If I had to celebrate Mass, the music would play nicely, I would go dancing.  
4. If I had to preach a sermon, there would be pretty girls there, I would go making love.  
The melody is not provided.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The boys from Dolany, they are dandy, they wear peacock feathers.  
2. Peacock feathers, black hats, so that all the girls would want them.  
3. The boys from Dolany, they are dandies, no girl should trust them.  
4. One believed them only once, she will regret it a hundred thousand times.  
5. Boys from Dolany, stop showing off, rather save money for the military service.  
6. If you left off those fancy things, you would wear a white shirt.  
7. Every Sunday a white shirt, so everyone would see that he is going to his sweetheart.  
8. A white shirt and tobacco in the pocket, then every Dolany guy would be recognized.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The little cornelian cherry grove has turned green, and in it my pleasure is walking. He walks in the tavern, holds beer in a glass, to toast his beloved, and sits down.  
2. And when he finished his beer, he poured another, to toast his beloved, toasted again. I will do spite for spite, let her drink once a night so that everyone knows I do not love her.  
3. If I, the dandy, had known this, I would not have drunk from that glass of beer. You have few rich ones here, you ignore us poor ones, think about it, dandy, that you yourself have nothing.  
4. I still have something to ask you, my Pepíček, before you come home from the army, surely I will get married. Why wouldn’t you get married, who would you wait for, choose a dandy like yourself.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: The roosters of Dřínov were crowing loudly enough to be heard all the way to Prague, to Prague and from Prague back home, right to my sweetheart’s house.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. If this year were like last year, I would let myself count the apples on the apple tree.  
2. Apples on the apple tree, dear little kisses, that he used to come to us under the window to pick little roses.  
3. Who is the girl in the red skirt who had two measures of wheat delivered to the mill?  
4. She begged the miller not to take the measure from her, to be so kind to her as to add some more.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. If only I knew where you would sleep, my dear Andulka, I would come to you when the moon rises and knock on the window.  
2. I knock on the window; if you are sleeping, my Andulka, get up and come open for me. If you do not open for me, then sleep well, I need not see you anymore.  
3. My Andulka got up and opened the door, there at her little room: If you want to stay here and sleep with me, lie down in the bed.  
4. The bed is made of oak, the quilt is dusty, my dear is like a flower, what a joy it is, while we are still young, we can go to her as a maiden.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When the fair was on Friday, I bought a scarf there for a ducat, for a ducat, I ask for a little kiss from the shoemaker's daughter Tonička, she doesn't want to give it to me.  
2. I went after her, brought her gifts, went into the room, it cost me oxen, rye from the barn, she keeps resisting.  
3. If you don’t want me, let it be, don’t trouble my little heart, if your people don’t want to give you, I don’t want you now, I’ll find myself a better one who knows how to love.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: When I remember my Anička, that she is already lying in a dark grave, tears pour over me, they drip from my eyes, when I remember our love.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When you come to us, my dear, make a bow, knock with a button, tip your hat, trata dytydum trata dytydum, tip your hat!  
2. When you visit the maiden, greet the parents, then sit quietly, don’t talk much.  
3. How cruel is love, when two want one, both make promises, both run away.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: When that Baldov castle was being demolished, my dear was wandering back then. When you wandered, keep wandering, I don't want you, false boy.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I was little, my mother cared for me, when I was little, my mother cared for me, my mother cared for me.  
2. When I was two years old, my mother’s care was great.  
3. When I was about six years old, the first step to school came.  
4. When I was eight years old, my mother began to fall ill.  
5. When I was ten years old, my mother left this world.  
6. They gave me another mother, they sent me out into the world.  
7. When I was fifteen years old, I began to know the world.  
8. When I was seventeen, I began to love.  
9. When I was twenty years old, one young man deceived me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: When I was walking through the cornelian cherry gardens, two maidens accompanied me. They accompanied me and cried for me all the way through the gardens.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I wasn't home, I had a misfortune; I had a girl promised to me, but the water took her away.  
2. Oh water, little water, return my beloved to me; if you do not return her, I'll find another.  
3. My Heavenly Father, have mercy on my heart; let whoever wants go to the deceiver in the evening.  
4. If you don't want me, so be it, do not torment my little heart; you have already troubled it enough, you could stop now.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I was leaving, the music was playing, my dearest, my dearest was crying for me, crying for me.  
2. Don't look after me, come rather with me, I will buy you a skirt, a green skirt.  
3. A green skirt, not too long, so that everyone knows you are my beloved.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I went from the feast today week ago, I met a maiden, I did not know her. She was pretty, nice, white, she gave me apples from her lap.  
2. I was a foolish boy, I took the apples, they were good. After I ate them, I turned completely pale, return to me, my dear, my beauty at once.  
3. I would return your beauty to you if with it the little dove had not flown away. It flew away to the grove, may God protect you, my boy.  
4. I told you these words, that you do not want one, that you want both. You cannot want more than one, accept me, maiden, or him.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I went from my dear one over that hill, I tripped, I fell over the juniper. That juniper smells lovely, that’s why another one goes after my maiden.  
2. If he goes, let him go, I will leave, Andulka will never see me again. And I will cross the border, you my false deceiver.  
3. Just don’t leave, my dear, and that’s how people slandered me. They said I am poor, that your mother will not give you to me.  
4. My precious Nanynka, now you ask, when I leave, but you will marry. That sabre of mine, my wife, she will strike me down if necessary.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I left you, the time was beautiful, the moon was just rising, when I was seeing Milka off, today, today, today for the last time.  
2. When you go away, I will follow you, we will go together into the world, to where happiness awaits us, away, away, we will not come back.  
3. Dear Andulka, it is not possible, when you will get up in the morning, I will already be marching away, away, we will not meet again.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I went to my lover yesterday, the stars were shining, the moon was lovely. My lover, she was not sleeping, waiting for me in the grove.  
2. When we met in the green grove, we talked about our love, the cuckoo called from the beech, calling the cuckoo of love.  
3. Why are you, cuckoo, calling above us, are you mocking our love? I kiss my lover once a week, and you call every day.  
4. When we wanted to say goodbye to my lover, my lover began to cry. Don't go home in the evening, my dear, wait until dawn.  
5. I will escort you through the green grove, no one will see us, only the pale moon, it does not know how to talk, and I will not tell that I came to see my dear.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I was leaving the church, the church of God, I met the mother of my beloved.  
2. Welcome, bride, from the church of God, I heard that you are thinking of my son.  
3. I am not thinking of him, I do not even remember, because I know well that I will not be his.  
4. He always used to say to me that he would marry me when the spring rye on the hill was harvested.  
5. The spring rye has been harvested, he has still not taken me, may God Himself give him no luck.  
6. He was given no luck on his estate for wanting to deceive a poor girl.  
7. The poor girl begged for God’s sake: My Pepíček, marry me so I won’t have to serve.  
8. I will not marry you, my parents forbid me, because your family is proud.  
9. Proud and lofty, high they carry themselves; my mother does not want to allow it.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When you wanted to stay a virgin, (wanted to stay a virgin) you shouldn’t have been herding geese, you shouldn’t have been chasing them into the little grove (chasing them into the little grove) among the boys. Tramtara.  
2. I didn’t chase them there, they flew to me there, as if those little geese somehow knew.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When we came under the grove, I wanted two sweet kisses from her, give me, girl, give me.  
2. You mustn't deny that you didn't give me a sweet little kiss among the roses.  
3. I don't deny it, if you know about that, only if you, boy, give me some semolina.  
4. I'll give you semolina, don't worry, if that boy is like me.  
5. If it's a boy, he'll be a shoemaker, he'll sew shoes for girls to dance in.  
6. If it's a girl, she'll be a seamstress, she'll sew dresses for girls for the dance.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I'll still take a look at those dogwood gardens; if my dear is there, I'll call her.  
2. I don't see her there anywhere, nor do I hear her; when I meet her tomorrow, I will delight my heart.  
3. Oh, what a nice thing it is that I am a handsome huntsman; I shot that doe that gave me a kiss.  
4. If I got another one, believe me, people, I would be happy; I would go to the Golden Deer and have a fine time.  
5. Come, my Nanynka, to delight my little heart; if you give me a nice little kiss, we will go dance at the Jelínek.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Just a little moment until the day ends, come on, Naninka, escort me there to that pond on the dam, come on, Naninka, escort me, it’s already time, come on, Naninka, escort me, it’s already time.  
2. I would like from the bottom of my heart, if my lady – what my little heart loves, our lady forbids it.  
3. Master and lady, I beg you every morning, for your faithful little daughter, I carry her in my heart day and night.  
4. If you do not give her to me, then you’ll see, I will go and join the army, there I will get the red epaulette.  
5. That red epaulette is my pleasure, a saber on the side, a pistol in hand, now I have what I wanted, my dear lady.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Fan, fan, what were you doing that you lost such a young wreath?  
2. Cattle herders, cattle herders, you were grazing horses, didn’t you find my green wreath?  
3. We didn’t find it, we didn’t find it, but we saw two red roses floating on the water.  
4. Fishermen, fishermen, get on the ships, search for my green wreath on the water.  
5. We would search if they paid us, three gold fifty, we will go on strike.  
6. Miners, miners, go underground, search for my green wreath underground.  
7. We would mine if they paid us three gold fifty, we will go on strike.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. The wind is blowing from the mountains, be careful, girl. [chorus] Where would you go if it blew on you, where would you look if it blew on you? 2. It blows on you once, your cheeks will turn pale. 3. It blows on you twice, you will cry, girl.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The little groves are nicely greening, the willows embrace my maiden. As he embraces her, let him have her, just don’t try to woo her in front of me.  
2. If they tried to woo her in front of me, they would make my heart heavy. If I don’t get you, Nanynka, I will tilt my hat to the side.  
3. Spin around, little sun, above those forests, nothing at that Vepřek pleases me anymore. It used to please me, but it won’t anymore, I will find pleasure somewhere else.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Grove, grove, little grove, little green grove, wake me up, my dear Mařenka, wake me from sleep. When I fall asleep sweetly in the morning, my Mařenka comes to me, she wakes me from my dream into my suffering.  
2. When I come to the pub, I have a march played, if I didn’t lead you, Mařenka, you would remain standing somewhere in the corner by the bench, waiting for a young man, like every maiden who has no boyfriend.  
3. When I come to the pub, I have a march played, they are playing it again for me, my dear Mařenka, they are playing it for me for the last time, that I am to be conscripted, separated from you, my dear Mařenka, separated forever.  
4. How much I have walked to you and stepped in mud, and only received those sweet little kisses from you, how much more we had together, we talked about it, in our little garden, when we loved each other.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Deep little grove, I cannot embrace you, I cannot forget your black eyes. I have already forgotten the rose, the little rose, but not you yet, my dear Pepíček.  
2. Deep little grove, I cannot embrace you, I cannot forget you, my dear. I have already forgotten whom I wanted, but I cannot forget you, unless I had to.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Mountain, mountain, you are tall, my maiden, you are distant, you are distant beyond the mountains, love between us is withering.  
2. It withers, it withers until it fades away, there is no one in the world for me, no more joy for me to be found.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The linden tree was burning, burning, beneath it my dear was sitting, sparks were falling on her, the young men cried for her.  
2. Why are you crying for me? I'm not alone in the world, I'm not the only one, the village is full of girls.  
3. The one who cried the most was the one who kissed me on the cheek, who kissed me on the forehead, how well it suited us.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Hostíň, it is on the plain, come, my dear, see me off, come, my dear, see me off across the green grove. I will not go, I am alone, the lady would find out, the lady would find out, go, my dear, alone.  
2. When I went to Dřínov, she called to me again: My Pepíček, even if my mother scolds me, I will still be yours, wait a little moment.  
3. And I don’t want to, I can’t stop, my dear, I’m very afraid that you won’t be mine. When you harvest the green rye, then you will take me, boy, I will be your wife.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: The Hradčany clock sadly strikes, bidding farewell to me and my little doll, my dear little plump one.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: I walked for many years, wandered the whole world, and nothing pleased my heart. When I returned back, the sorrow immediately passed, and the bright sun shined for me again.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I would have been glad to be cheerful, but since it cannot be, I found my beloved here with another, I had to stay awake all night.  
2. How could I sleep this night when love tormented me, I promised you sincerity, and you deceived me.  
3. When I go over the mountains, forests, I will sigh heavily, oh sincere love, where are you, you will pay for your deceit.  
4. Where are our walks, when we used to stroll, where are those our foolish games, when we used to play?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. I have my girl in Roudnice, hunters go to see her there, they go to see her there, they go to see her there, hunters. 2. I will not leave my girl there, I will go to her there tomorrow. 3. I will go to her there on Sunday, so that everyone knows about it.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: I have a girl in Prague in one house, she loves me. I love her too, I do not deny it, she has blue eyes. Oh, you blue little eyes, ah, you little rose-like cheeks! I have a girl in Prague in one house, she loves me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: I don’t want anyone, just my Honza, I don’t want anyone, just Honza. Honza will drive me in a covered carriage, Honza will drive me in a covered carriage, in a covered carriage, with four horses, my golden, golden Honza.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I am a free son, no one knows anything about me, only my Andulinka, only my Andulinka, she won’t tell on me.  
2. If she wanted to tell, she couldn’t, because she loved me and always used to say to me: Come to us for the evening for the roses!  
3. When we were among the roses, we both had fun, we did what we wanted, we forgot about God, what we shouldn’t have done.  
4. The eleventh hour, what will your mother say to you when you are rocking your daughter or son?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I don't want you anymore, I don't like you anymore, I made out with your friend.  
2. He was the guy I was standing with yesterday, and he begged me to love him.  
3. And I told him that he is way too young, to go home and take care of his health.  
4. You forgot, boy, you forgot my love, you forgot, boy, the troubled girl.  
5. Remember, boy, remember the time when we walked home together from the music.  
6. I was crying, you were just laughing at me, when you took the green wreath from my head.  
7. That's why I don't want you, that's why I don't like you, I made out with your friend.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. As I said, I will stop making love, because nothing comes of it, only gossip. If I were to stop by the maiden and say a word or two, they would immediately make it seem like I am going to see her.  
2. I don't go and I won't, my friend goes, I don't go and I won't, my friend goes; if we both went there, we wouldn't be friends, we would look at each other badly.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. How cruel love is when two love the same one. Friends, brothers, it’s even worse when three love the same one.  
2. One stands at the door, the second wants dinner, the third on the bed says to his Andulka: Get up and go open for him!  
3. Why should I open for him when I’m not his lover? I can sleep, make my bed, I’m a maiden like any other.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Just spin happily, little scarf, my dear is angry, I don’t know why. He’s angry, angry, oh he’s angry, but he can’t watch me more than once, trala, but he can’t watch me more than once.  
2. Andulka, Andulka, my Andulka, tie my little scarf in two knots, tie it, tie it, golden child, so I can’t untie it.  
3. I would gladly tie it for you, if my mother-in-law didn’t forbid me, but my mother-in-law forbids me strongly, saying you have no thousands, no fields.  
4. I danced everywhere, not at the Vinč’s, I courted the cooks, not my dear. [...]  
5. And I didn’t dance with my dear, she looks as if she were painted. Red cheeks, white body, as if she had flown out of a chimney.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Our little clover, you have a nice scent, everything in our field is blooming, you are turning green.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Clover in the forest, God, remember what love is like when no one wants me.  
2. If you don't want me, then leave it, don't torment my little heart, you've already tormented it enough, you could stop now.  
3. If you stop once, your little face will turn pale, you will dress in sadness because you don't walk with me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The road to Mělník is long, lined with trees, because my dear one walks at night to other girls.  
2. If he goes, let him go, I don’t care about him anymore, such a young man doesn’t walk the path to us.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The entire path to Mlčechvosts is sprinkled, I sprinkled it with tears when I said goodbye to you, I wet it with tears as I walked here.  
2. Have a good time here, all you girls; when you go dancing, remember me, that I used to be with you, enjoying good times.  
3. Have a good time here, boys from Mlčechvost; when you meet again, remember me, that I used to be with you, enjoying good times.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: A beaten path to Zlosejn, oh dear, beaten, Andulka walked here, crying, oh dear, crying. Pepíček came along, pining, caught up with Andulka, bawling, oh dear, bawling.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The little moon shines beautifully over Zlosejn, I must visit my dear. Shine for me, little moon, shine on the path, until I come to my Andulka, but she says she does not want me.  
2. I come and knock on the window: Open up, open up, my Nanynka. Open up, my dear, for I am cold, so I can warm up a little by you, for you are my feather bed.  
3. My dear neither sleeps nor dozes, but she says she must not open for me. If you don’t open, goodnight and goodbye, I will find another girl, remember that my words will not fail.  
4. Through the forest flies a little bird, his name is a beautiful nightingale. He carries a letter, in it greetings, placing it for my dear on the little table where she was making her wreath.  
5. My dear awakened from a dream, folded her white little hands, read and lamented and sighed sadly: I wish I had rather not known you, I gave my love to you.  
6. The little swallow flies abroad, my dear is looking for another girl. When you find him, always be joyful with him, I wish you much happiness for that, find yourself a girl for your pleasure.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
Villain, without further identification  
1. Where shall we go for a stroll, my dear?  
To a beautiful garden, you and I.  
Don’t talk to me, my dear.  

Where shall we go for a stroll, girl?  
To a beautiful garden, you and I.  
Be silent to me, my girl, about that.  

2. What shall we do there, my dear?  
Pick beautiful roses, you and I.  
Don’t talk to me, my dear!  

What shall we do there, girl?  
Pick beautiful roses, you and I.  
Be silent to me, my girl, about that.  

3. What shall we eat there, my dear?  
Black meat with peas, you and I.  
Don’t talk to me, my dear.  

[…]  
From the handwritten songbook of František Němec from 1859, where it is titled Czech and German Song.  
Melody unknown.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: The millstone, it grinds, it grinds, our love will not end, will not end. The time will come and we will be home again, we will make love again like before.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Everyone has a sweetheart, I have none, everyone has a sweetheart, I have nothing. Véna has Márinka, Matěj has Terinka, everyone has a sweetheart, I have nothing.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Everyone wants to know which girl is mine, everyone wants to know which one is mine. She has a pretty face and a slim body, legs like strings, a nice white forehead. /Chorus/ She is, she is mine, my pleasure, she is, she is mine, she will be mine! 

2. Everyone wants to know if she is tall, everyone wants to know if she is short. She is a pretty girl, neither very tall, nor small either, of medium build.

3. Everyone wants to know what kind of eyes she has, everyone wants to know what her eyes are like. She has heavenly eyes, sometimes she plays with them, she lifts them towards the sky, she smiles charmingly.

4. Everyone asks me how she dances, everyone asks me how she spins. She dances in the Prague style like in a picture, gently she walks, lightly she turns. They say the dance from Zákolany was played for dancing.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Who is coming to us, I don’t know him. He has blue eyes, red cheeks, let him come to us, I will recognize him.  
2. Don’t stop me, mother, he is my Pepíček, a faithful lover, my little heart, my little heart.  
3. But he is my dearest, in the evening he loved me, in the morning he didn’t recognize me, now he has a different girl.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The road to Zlosejn is long, I will look at it, if my Pepíček is there, I will call him. My dear Pepíček, escort me for a mile, escort me for two miles, and I will escort you for four.

2. What did I do to you, my Pepíček, that you are so angry with me, slandering me among people? It can easily happen that we part ways, since I don’t have to, my Nanynka, I don’t have to visit you.

3. Nanynka went for a walk, Pepíček stood by the window, called to his lady-mother to come to him for a moment. Dear lady-mother, you are the cause of how my heart aches when I see her standing with another.

4. Send me for a painter, I will have her painted, when my heart aches, I will look at her. The painter does not want to come to me, I must leave this world, because of one woman I have to waste my life.

5. Half a year after the funeral, Nanynka got married, and when the wedding was over, she went to the cemetery, cried. Go away from my grave, go to your husband, and I will sleep in the grave until the Judgement Day.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A white little shirt, my dear gave it to me, and when she gave it to me, she cried sadly.  
2. Don't cry, girl, don't regret that I lay with you, count carefully how many Sundays that is.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
Villain, without further identification

1. I loved a beautiful mistress, I will love no other more, I give to the world a lovely girl, and I commend myself to God, for my fate did not allow that I should take the one I loved as my companion and give her my sincere hand.

2. Alas for you, dear girl, I wish I had never known you, the sky testifies to me by its light, I should have given my hand to another girl, for your beauty caught my eye, my gaze grew angelically youthful, you have deeply taken me into your heart, ah, where your glance has led me.

3. Oh, how bitterly tears flow from my eyes when I remember the maiden, my heart and head spin around, I do not know what to do in my sadness. When we used to meet together, when I pressed you to my heart, when we sat in the tree-lined avenue, what I said to you, you know well.

4. And when once in the hour of death on my deathbed comes that most sorrowful hour, I will continually call for God's mercy, may He grant me to go with you, dear one. Behold, know my last request, that for remembrance I have my last kiss of love, with you, my dear, I would go to the grave.

“New Song” from the manuscript songbook of František Němec from 1856. Tune unknown.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Love, God, love, where do people get it from, it doesn't grow on trees, it is not sown in the fields, it is not sown in the fields.  
2. If they planted this love in the fields, surely the girls would diligently water it.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. A white goose was flying, flying over the waters, come and see what the boys are doing. 2. One is grooming horses, another is carrying water, the third is writing a beautiful little note, a little note. 3. To whom are you writing the little note, my dear? I am writing it to you, that you will not be mine. 4. You will be the son of mine, the second son, and so you will join our family.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Little oak leaf, don’t fall into the water, the water would take you, it would be a pity to lose you, don’t fall into the water.  
2. Little oak leaf, don’t fall into the water, we won’t get married, we won’t go after girls, we’ll stay single.  
3. Nothing pleases me here but the lord’s garden, where I used to walk and lead my Mařenka, and she was crying.  
4. When we come back from Bosnia, we will get married, a blue-eyed girl, a blue-eyed girl we will love.  
5. Why do you cry, why do you lament, you still have me here, then you can cry and lament for yourself when you don’t take care of me.  
When I asked for [this song from the informant], he said: Ah, that’s the waltz.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Fly, little bird, fly, you have green feathers, every maiden is a fool, every maiden is a fool who trusts boys.  
2. What is there to trust in boys like a scorpion, a snake, they have ninefold treachery under their tongue.  
3. They have honey on their tongue, poison under their tongue: Be careful, maiden, not to be deceived by him.  
4. If he deceives you, he will not take you, you will only lament yourself.  
5. Only yourself, for your lack of sense, for listening to his smooth talk.  
6. The mother weeps and the father curses, and the younger sister laughs on the side.  
7. They laugh at her, her friends laugh, the corset tightens, the skirt shortens.  
8. I will adjust my skirt, I will wear a corset bone, I will fear boys until my dying day.  
9. When she made the bed, she began to cry: I wish I had never known you, boy.  
planžet, from French = bone in a corset, in the bodice

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Parting, parting, what a hard thing it is when one must say goodbye, when a young man must say goodbye to a maiden.  
2. When we parted, we both cried, both of us wiping our eyes with a white handkerchief.  
3. When we parted under the green oak, I always told you that love is not constant, that we would not be each other's forever.  
4. When that oak blossoms with a white branch, then, my maiden, I will carry sorrow for you.  
5. I will carry it for a week, cry every day, but I will never forget that I was glad to have loved you.  
Musicians play this song at the end of various celebrations and even at the grave of a young man or maiden.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. My dear Mařenka, watch this when I stand in front of the gate, that’s me. How I can drum and love you, Mařenka.  
[chorus]  
Just wait, pay attention, when I call out to you: My dear Mařenka, Mářo, Mářo, lovely Mařenka!  

2. Who wouldn’t love you, Mařenka, you who look as if painted. Red cheeks like roses, who could not love you?  

fortna = gate (here to the town or to the barracks)

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A small cottage, with a little window, are you sleeping, or not sleeping, or not hearing, my darling?  
2. But I am not sleeping, I hear you well, but I am not allowed to open to you, my pleasure.  
3. If you do not open, sleep well, I will not come to you, my pleasure, neither tomorrow nor today.  
4. If you do not come to us, you will not leave us, you will not, my darling, step on the grass in our yard.  
5. Step on the grass, gather dew, you will not, my darling, walk around our yard anymore.  
6. And those bad people are to blame for that, they did not wish us well, they did not let two black eyes love.  
7. My black eyes were faithful, but your blue ones, blossoming with love, were false.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Little one, girl, you have a little love for me, you promised that you would give it to me at night or during the day.  
2. You promised that you would give it to me, even standing, two kisses or three, which will be mine.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I have a little log garden, and in it, rosemary is planted; I planted it, I know that well, for pleasure.  
2. When I was planting the rosemary, I gave it to all the lads; come, boys, to us, I will pick for you, I will give you rosemary.  
3. All around, around, stands a pine tree, a pine, under it a little horse, on it Vašíček, he is angry with me.  
4. My dear got angry with me, he didn’t even invite me to dance, didn’t call me to dance, didn’t offer beer; that’s what he did to me.  
5. Sulk, sulk, sulk, picture, only since I recognized your love, which we had, we loved each other, dear picture.  
6. If I forgot my father, I will also forget the boy, you, my dear, sweet lad, even you.  
7. If I forgot my mother, I will also forget the love that we had, we loved each other, dear picture.  
8. If I forgot my brother, I will also forget the gate where we stood, we loved each other, perhaps until morning.  
9. If I forgot my sister, I will also forget the path, on which I walked, carrying a little flower, for you, Vašíček.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Marie, Marie, Marie, Marinka, yo-, yo-, yo-, you have beautiful eyes. Marinka, the eyes are yours, looking into them is mine, I would look, look all night, night, night, night, there is no help.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. When I first saw you, I immediately liked you, the delight of my heart, if you loved me. If you loved me, how I would love you, if you wanted me as your husband, I would surrender to you.  
2. Look into my blue eyes, look at my charm, you don't want to love me, yet there are plenty of maidens in the world. There are maidens like flowers, like roses in the garden, I could choose another, but I want no one else but you.  
3. If I don't get you, I won't take another, when you go to the altar with another, I will mourn for you. I will mourn and lament that I did not get you, and regret until death that I ever met you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I had a little girl like a spring flower, she loved me faithfully like her own mother.  
2. Under her little garden near the forest stands a cross, and on it Jesus looks up to heaven.  
3. And there Mařenka and I used to sit so that our parents wouldn't know we were in love.  
4. Once Mařenka and I were sitting there, and I saw that Mařenka’s eyes were full of tears.  
5. Why are you crying, Mařenka, why were you crying? Surely your mother was arguing with you again.  
6. She argued, argued and wanted to know why my cheeks had begun to pale from their redness.  
7. And I told her that my head hurts, and when the headache stops, my cheeks will turn red again.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I had a girl and I loved her, I spent three years standing under her window. And then suddenly she lost her faithful love, and so I found out the girl was unfaithful.  
2. You don’t like it, little Pepík, that another young man sat next to me? I sat next to him, didn’t say a word, I kept thinking of you, little Pepík.  
3. Marry, Andulka, I will also get married, I will exchange your wreath. I could have taken it from you, you didn’t have to say that I am honorable, I don’t want to do that.  
4. You did well, little Pepík, you did well not to crush my green wreath. If he had left, forgotten me in sadness, who would, little Pepík, who would care about me?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: I loved you, girl, now your friend has you, I loved you, girl, now I don't want you. A guy like that, the cuckoo calls out, a guy like you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I had a sweetheart, and he was from Vojkovice, he swore loyalty to me, made a thousand promises. He used to come to our little sitting room all the time, and there he always gave me false kisses.  
2. False kisses, they were like poison, a hundred times a pity that he deceived me like that. He stopped coming to our house, wait, you deceiver, I'll get you.  
3. Don’t come to us, Pepíček, unless I call you, I will tie our little window with ribbons. Tie them, tie them, Andulka, with blue ones, and I will untie them immediately with kind words.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The moon is shining brightly, I must visit my beloved. The path through the grove is dry, my beloved is waiting for me today, she wants to talk to me today.  
2. She wants to talk to me, she just wants to tell me that my dear friends are waiting for me in the forest, that they want to kill me there.  
3. I walked a different way, oh, from my dearest, I saw a young man running, I stayed lying under a tree, that does not comfort my heart.  
4. I went further along the path to see for myself, I see my beloved, she is walking with someone else, that I did not expect.  
5. I spoke to Mařenka, and very harshly, if they were recruiting today, if they were recruiting today, I would join the dragoons.  
6. I would join the dragoons, the red dragoons, that for my pleasure, that for my pleasure, it was not destined for me.  
7. If I die, I die, oh who will bury me, before they learn about me, worms will eat me in the earth, oh who will bury me?  
8. I will die in the black forest, and there the wild beasts will scatter me, the little birds, the little birds will be at my funeral.  
9. I could have already married, not waited so long for you, I could have gotten someone else, oh my dearest God, what a waste of time!  
10. If you marry only once, your little face will turn pale, your beauty will change, my dear joy, I will no longer follow you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Little moon, little moon, listen to what I think when I look at your face in the evening. 2. Sometimes you are round, other times you have horns, the third time you are bright, and at other times dark again.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. It seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that my Andulka, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that she has blue eyes. Oh, those blue little eyes and rosy little cheeks, oh, those white little hands and gentle little feet, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that Andulka will be mine.

2. It is a pity that you will not be mine, my Andulka, that you will comfort another, that you will not be mine. And my thoughts turn into sorrow, because my expectation, because it is not to be fulfilled, it is a pity that you will not be mine, you sadden my heart.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. My pleasure, you have blue eyes, and I am afraid you will deceive me. 2. Do not be afraid, Nanynka, of my deception, God would not give me any blessing. 3. No blessing on our little estate if I deceived a poor girl.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. You had to enchant me, my dear maiden, you had to enchant me, I started coming to your evening gatherings to be able to love you, to love you.  
2. I didn't enchant you, but because I know how to work, that's why you liked me, you had to come to us.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. The hunter walks along the dam, the hunting knife hangs on him, he meets a pretty girl there, immediately he courts her, he meets a pretty girl there, immediately he courts her. 2. Little hunter, hunter, be careful with the girl, if you don't watch out for her, she will run away from you. Inšvígr, from German = hunting dagger

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. On our little garden, a nice bird is hopping, the tolling bell sounded, people were praying, go home now, boy.  
2. I don’t want to, I won’t go, I’d rather stay here, sit next to you, lie down on the little bed, leave only at dawn.  
3. Don’t regret, my dear, don’t regret that I lay with you, I lay on the hay, you were next to me, I didn’t even know.  
4. If you wanted to remain an honest maiden, you shouldn’t have, my dear, sweet maiden, gone for a walk with me in the morning.  
5. The sun was shining when I went through the forest, and you, my dear, sweet maiden, accompanied me through the village.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In our little garden, snow is fluttering there, a crazy girl who loves only boys. Lalalala lala lalalala lala lalalala lala lala.  
2. My pale Nanynka, she is angry with me, she turned her little mouth towards the hay.  
3. And I, with a birch whisk again: Turn around, Nanynka, your little mouth upwards.  
4. What is on the hill, juniper grows there, what is for Nanynka, when she no longer has a wreath.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. She stood on the threshold, looked pretty, I gave her a kiss, she laughed. Hey zhupy zhupy hey zhupy zhupy hey zhupy zhupy zhup.  
2. Don’t laugh at me, I’m not handsome, after all, I’m the son of a soldier.  
3. She jumped down, told him, "They’re taking, Pepíček, they’re taking to the army."  
4. If they take you, they take you, you will fight, what you win, they will give to you.  
5. They won a black horse, sit down, my dear, sit on it.  
6. As soon as she sat on it, she immediately turned pale, where, my dear, did you lose your beauty?  
7. I lost it in the forest on the grass, while I was mowing grass for the cow.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. On Radlice Road stands a small house, my dear, my beloved, serves red beer there.  
2. And when we arrived there, she stood between the doors, crying sorrowfully, crying as if music were playing.  
3. Why are you crying, lamenting, when you still have me here, perhaps you have no stable for my black horse?  
4. She took the horse by the bridle, led it to the stable, and led Pepíček by the hand, by the hand into the room.  
5. When she came into the room, she sat down at the little table, called to her mother, mother: Bring us some food.  
6. My mother took care of who would wake us in the morning, our red rooster, red, flying from the attic.  
7. The rooster flies from the attic and the hen after him, they were embracing each other right before dawn.  
8. Mother saw it and started laughing: And now you have gotten what you wished for.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. On our dam, water is flowing, little fish are jumping, I would like Pepíček, a handsome boy, for his good manners.  
2. They give me Hátu, she has ducats, but she is a bit hunchbacked. It's no defect, she will love me, and she has a purse of gold.  
3. [...] He knows how to dance, to love girls, he is healthy as a mountain ash.  
4. [...] Boys come back from the army, who are equal to me, I will pick one.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: In our yard, everything crows, even the rooster. I cannot forget you, my joy. If I forget, which is very difficult, my heart still aches for you, my joy.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. On our field stands a beautiful rose, still far away, still far away, but one already smells to me.  
2. One already smells to me, another has bloomed for me, that you must, my dear, go far into the world.  
3. Far into the world, across those barren mountains, ah, my dear, does my heart ache for you.  
4. If I were a little bird or a swallow, I would fly above our little house.  
5. Not just above the little house, but above the stables, just to know what my dear is doing.  
6. First he carries water, then he brushes the horses, and then he makes love, promises another, blue-eyed girl.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In our field, the well stands there, and when its water dries up, that will be the time we are ours.  
2. The water does not dry up, it always increases, my dear Pepíček, that will never happen.  
3. Blow, little wind, dry the water to the bottom, so that I can change my freedom.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. On the green meadow, the grass is green, tell me, my dear, tell me, my dear, with whom were you sitting yesterday? 2. I was sitting with a boy, I wouldn’t deny it, the moon wasn’t shining, I didn’t recognize him. 3. The moon wasn’t shining, the stars were shining, he had blue eyes and pale cheeks.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Above Dřínov is a small grove, in which my Pepíček walks and dreams. 2. He has black hair on his head, remember, girl, my golden girl, those times. 3. Under the hair, a white forehead, as if my golden child had forgotten you. 4. Under that forehead, blue eyes, and they keep turning towards me in the tavern hall.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Above Dřínov on a nice little hill, there is a nice avenue of trees, I had a girlfriend arranged, but another guy is visiting her. 2. As I said, so I will do, I will stop making love, I don’t want a girl if another guy slept at her place, my friends said.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Do not be proud of your beauty, for it will pass, your youth and your beauty will flow away like water.  
2. Come escort me once more, so I may be comforted, so I can take your hand and lay it on my heart.  
3. If you want to be my wife, come, give me your little hand, and then you can also give me a nice little kiss.  
4. Look at my blue eyes, how they grieve for you, who by day and by night only seek you.  
5. Do you see tears falling from my eyes? They fall for you; if you have a white handkerchief in your hand, wipe my blue eyes.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: It wasn't, Toníček, it wasn't because of you, when you used to come to me in the stable. Tralala, the grass is green, tralala, the grass is green.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Don't go, Nanynko, into the forest, cold dew is falling, your slippers are old, completely torn, you would have to go barefoot, you would have to go barefoot. 2. The Dřínov pond is already breaking up, the pond is already breaking up, because my darling, my dear Pepíček, he wanders at night.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Carry me, my little horse, carry me through that deep forest, bring me to my dear Andulka, so charming, and do it today.  
2. Know this, my Andulka, know this, I was at the fair today, I bought you a fairthing, the fair will upset you that you won't get me.  
3. Look forward, my dear, look forward, I was at the fair today, you will get a hussar with long mustaches, with him you will be happy.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The unfortunate gamekeeper’s little house, the unfortunate gamekeeper’s house, when I passed by, my heart ached, my black horse cried out.  
2. Don’t neigh, my black little horse, we’ll ride around three times, if my dear Andulka is sincere with me, she will open and come out.  
3. We rode around the little house three times, the horse reared up at the gate, what is new from my girl, that she does not open the gate?  
4. When someone else passes by Anička, she immediately runs out quickly, but when she sees Toníček, she immediately returns home, he calls: Come out quickly!  
5. As soon as I rode out through the gate, she sent a messenger after me to return to the courtyard because she had opened the gate.  
6. Tell her, my dear messenger, I will send her greetings, that she should marry and not wait for me to march on.  
7. The poor maiden sent the message, two rich ones sent theirs, better is that one poor maiden than both rich ones.  
8. The rich maiden is pretty as long as her mother dresses her, but when she gets away from her mother across the field, she hardly walks because of the dirt.  
9. The poor maiden is pretty, red as a rose, and when she gets away from her mother across the field, she loves her man.  
10. Tell me, my dear Andulka, why does your mother forbid you? She forbids me for your sake, my joy, because you have no field.  
11. Even if I have no field or meadows, I am still happy in this world, God can give me that I will love a girl like a carnation.  
12. How many times I have walked to see you, how many paths I have trodden, still you were not destined for me by God, still I did not get you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Don’t suffer any longer, dear Pepíček, I don’t carry you in my heart. [chorus] I will never be yours, I won’t take you as my husband. 2. I will turn into a little bird in the oak tree, I will sing to people about you. 3. I will turn into a little fish, I will flow to you with quiet water. 4. And I will bring my fishing rod, which will catch every little fish for me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oh, garden carnation, you fragrant little rose, to forget you is not possible for me, to forget you is not possible for me.  
2. I will forget, but with difficulty, then people will remember that you used to be more sincere when I came to you.  
3. You made me very angry, you will not anger me anymore once I wander away from here and you remain here.  
4. I have already crossed three mountains, you called out to me: Oh my dearest of all, how have I angered you?  
5. Brothers, sisters, and friends hate me, they keep telling me to go after you.  
6. Wander, wander, little wanderer, I will wander with you, my parents are in the cemetery, they will not stop us.  
carnation = gillyflower, carnation

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oh, you fair little rose, whoever planted you has brought great joy to my heart. For every time I leave the house, I come to the window—are you sleeping, my Nanynko, are you sleeping, or what are you doing, dear little pearl?

2. I was sleeping, now I am awake, you woke me up, I had a very joyful dream, you were present. It seemed to me, it seemed, just this morning, that you gave me a kiss, my dear Pepíček, that you pressed your little hand to my heart.

3. Oh my most beloved of all, you had a dream about me, and I am again unhappy day and night. You torment my heart, which withers in my body, ease my suffering before it fades away, oh my most beloved of all, do not torment my heart.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: At Christmas, long nights, it is pleasant to sleep with the maiden, no swallows are heard, only the wind blowing. Blow, little wind, from the Danube, where my beloved is, let him come here, I have another, more sincere one than I have you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Around our gate, a swallow is flying, I always said, I always said that Frantík would leave me.  
2. If he leaves, let him leave, I'll still tell him that I tie such a false boy with garters.  
3. I tie with garters, I carry with a nail, that I don't beg anything from such a false boy. Melody unknown.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A little stream flows around Vyšehrad, around it the grass turns green, around it the little grass turns green.  
2. I went there only once, I saw my Andulka with another man.  
3. They were talking about me there, while they were sulking back at me.  
4. Just don’t sulk back at me, only love each other tenderly.  
5. Under Vyšehrad there is a spreading linden tree, under that linden there is a little table, and there my beloved is embroidering a little scarf.  
6. She was embroidering two roses on it, saying that she cannot love me more.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The plowman, the plowman's wife, and below her is the village, said the mother, I would not let him go.  
2. How can I not let him go when he asks so nicely: Open to me, my dear, we will talk about something.  
3. Speak, speak, since you want to tell me so I don't have to stand until midnight.  
4. She stood until midnight, slept in the morning, asked her mother if she had not overslept.  
5. Thank you, mother, for your breakfast. And I thank you, daughter, for getting up.  
6. Breakfast with lunch, I have that every day, and my Jeníček only once a week. From a handwritten songbook. Melody unknown.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Father, Father, Father, our Father, my lady, how are you, how are you? Are you well, are you well, are you well, are you well, when you see me, are you glad, are you glad? I believe in the Lord God, that we both are glad, the Ten Commandments of God, when the kisses burn. Angel of God, my guardian, you are mine, mine, I am yours, angel of God, my guardian, you are mine, I am yours.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. The poor girl came to the mill, bringing two sacks of barley to be ground. 2. She asked the miller to grind it for her, saying she would give him a kiss by the bright moonlight. 3. She gave him a kiss, then began to cry, saying that she would not have recognized him, miller.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Uncle, uncle, what is my darling doing, is he perhaps angry with me because he doesn't visit us?  
2. He is not angry, not angry, he is sowing wheat, when it grows again, he will call for me.  
3. She was sitting in the grove, calling: Darling, come here and lay your head on my lap.  
4. She laid her head on her lap, running her fingers through his hair, a tear fell after every strand.  
5. Why do you cry, why do you lament, when you still have me here, you may cry later when you can no longer keep me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Wait, girl, don’t run away, give me some lettuce, -ku, -ku, give me some lettuce. Where would I, sad, get it from? I don’t have a garden, -ku, -ku, I don’t have a garden. When I become a gardener, I will give you lettuce, give, give, a full basket of the finest, I will cut it for you, I will cut it for you.  
2. Prepare for me, gardener, lettuce for dinner, maybe from orache, just as long as it’s prepared by your hands for me. She washed diapers at the spring, broke her hands, if only I were a gardener instead and didn’t know.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Wait, I'll say that you begged me, wait, I'll say that you wanted me. 2. In the garden a little flower, a little kiss on the side, wait, I'll say that you wanted me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Under Martinovská stands a house, under Martinovská stands a house, I have a girl there, my God, I have a girl there, my God.  
2. The parents do not want it because they do not know the girl.  
3. But I know her well because I visit her.  
4. There on the green meadow, roe deer and deer graze.  
5. And on the other, wide one, wild roe deer graze.  
6. On the third one, there is nothing, only a garden of stones.  
7. In that garden is an apple tree, it bears nice apples.  
8. Blow, little wind, from the Danube, break me a red apple.  
9. The little wind blew from the Danube, it broke a red apple.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Under the Týnec tower, Pepíček lies there, lies and is seriously ill in the green rosemary.  
2. And when he fell ill, he remembered God, wondering if the Lord would accept him into the heavenly paradise.  
3. On Sunday afternoon, that used to be mine, I went out to the garden, I called for Andulka.  
4. When she heard it, she immediately ran to me: Welcome, Pepíček, to us, for I have known you from afar.  
5. How could you not know me, since you slept with me in our little room on the made-up bed.  
6. There we slept together, promised love, you to me and I to you, my delight.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Below the mill, above the mill the geese are grazing, Pepíček, take your little rifle, shoot one.  
2. I won't shoot those geese, I know them well, they are the nanny geese I visit.  
3. If I killed even one, she would be angry when I came there in the evening, she would be upset.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Tell me, my dear, my beloved, why does your pajama prevent you? It prevents me for you, my pleasure, that you have no field.  
2. Even though I come from a poor family, I am still glad to be in the world, God can grant me that I will love a girl like a carnation.  
3. I have walked after you in vain, I trudged the path in vain, if I had known that I would be deceived, I would have been glad to stay home.  
4. Yesterday the poor girl sent me a message, the day before yesterday the rich one did, the poor maiden is better than both the rich ones.  
5. A rich maiden is beautiful as long as she is with her mother, and when she gets away from her mother across the field, she has clothes like diapers.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Pause, watch, little one, wish for love, for I have a sad conversation with my dear Marenka. The whole sky, stop, do not give out the sun’s brightness, look upon my miserable state.  
2. Have pity on me, all the little animals who live in the field, and also you, little birds who fly in the forest, cooing turtledoves, with veins playing throughout your whole body when I see you, little pearls!

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. She was washing diapers by the spring, crying, crying because she didn't know how to swaddle the little rascal, the little rascal.  
2. Swaddle him, my dear, in diapers so that daddy doesn't scold you.  
3. She was washing diapers by the spring, laughing: Do you think I don't recognize you, fool?  
4. By the spring stand four willows, ask her, friend, what is wrong with her.  
5. She won't tell you, friend, you took away her little green wreath.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Why do you keep calling me, my dear? You won’t get me alive. I am completely distressed, ready to die, tormented by false love.  
2. False love will be struck by thunder, and from my honesty a tree will grow. Thunder strikes the oak, I will not come to you, I used to be cheerful, but I won’t be anymore.  
3. If I don’t get you, my dear, I will climb a rock, strike my head on a high rock, wide at the base, I will strike my curly little head.  
4. Young men and maidens will carry me and will have white ribbons, young men with candles, ribbons with bows, they will have tearful eyes.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Why are you so pale, girl, why are you so troubled, why aren’t your cheeks as they used to be, red?  
2. You used to be like a rose, you walked on tiptoes, now you barely drag your feet as if you have been ill for five years.  
3. Yesterday my mother asked me: My little daughter, what is wrong with you? You are turning pale, losing your beauty, you must enjoy yourself.  
4. What I meant to say sadly, I didn’t want to admit, I broke down in the meadow when I was picking grass.  
5. That was no ordinary grass, Pepíček, you know well, it was early in the morning, but you won’t say it.  
6. On the meadow, on the green, when we sat together, sometimes one, sometimes the other, we chatted about something.  
7. You wanted to see, my Pepíček, what kind of garters I have, I showed them to you because I like you.  
8. You wanted to see even more, you wouldn’t leave me in peace, I showed it to you, you tickled me on it.  
9. Don’t cry, girl, don’t complain, don’t damage your health, if you have been wronged, it will be made right.  
10. I will be your faithful husband and you my lady, and so we will love each other until our end.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. She sold fields, meadows all, meadows all, she had a Gypsy woman tell her fortune, Gypsy woman, she had a Gypsy woman tell her fortune. 2. Gypsy woman, my dear, conjure me a husband. 3. When I am going to do magic for you, you must give me 4. a mug of ducats or two, but your dear one will give you another.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. It rained, it was dark, the little dove got wet, she couldn't fly to my darling's window.  
2. She couldn't fly, to deliver the message, it's over, Nanynka, with that love.  
3. When you send me a message, I also send you a message, that I, my dear, will not open the door for you.  
4. I am sending you a message that I am still angry, that I no longer have a deceitful little son.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A road leads through Vožice, there grow three roses there, one is red, the second is green, the third blooms blue, carara, one is red, the second is green, the third blooms blue.  
2. Oh God, God, let me pick one flower from that rose, and I won’t pick it, and I am afraid that I would ruin the world.  
3. Around the village two boys are coming, they will surely come to us, our little room is not swept, what will they say to us?  
4. Mother, dear mother, my little heart, come out onto the doorstep, I will sweep in the meantime, our little room before they come here.  
5. She stood on the doorstep, she seemed beautiful, she was like a rose blossom, as she jumped off the doorstep, she lost her wreath, I have ruined her world. [Informant learned the song from the military.]

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. What a shame a hundred times over for our little garden, I had a bed of marigolds sown there. Tradydata tratata tradydata tratata, a bed of marigolds, tradydata dá.  
2. I am drawn to green, blue angers me because my darling walks with others.  
3. Yesterday he promised me that today he would come to me, so I should wait for him until the moon rises.  
4. If I were not afraid, Nanynka, of your betrayal, I would come to you in your garden.  
5. But since I am afraid that you would betray me, that my little pouch would not last a year.  
6. Don’t be afraid, Honzík, that I would betray you, I will cover the little window with a red scarf.  
7. I will cover the little window, I will wait for you, I will pour a little beer, I will love you.  
8. I kept waiting and my dear does not come, perhaps he loves a maiden somewhere else.  
9. Gentle breeze, carry him my greetings, I am weaving him a wreath made of rosemary.  
10. In our garden I was weaving a wreath, my dear Honzík came to the little window.  
11. The lark sings, the sun is already shining, it is time, my dear, I must go home.  
12. If you love me, come with me to the little grove, come and see me off through the lord's little woods.  
13. She only saw me off a little way, gave me a nice few kisses for the road. It was a dance song. According to the print by S. Pospíšil in Chrudim from 1861. A new song for young men and maidens.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Gladly, gladly, my dear Honzíček, gladly, gladly, gladly I love you. If it is true then that you love me so much? Gladly, gladly, gladly I love you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Morning, morning, morning, early morning, before the beautiful sun rises, I will take a pint, a ribbon, and a rifle, and go to the green grove, my hunting ground. 2. When I came to the grove, I saw a beautiful turtledove there, calling "sugar, sugar" on the beech tree and constantly smiling at me, looking sweetly.  
pint, from German = ribbon, tie; inšvégr, from German = hunting dagger

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Ruined old castles, which once pleased me when I was still with my girl, when we were still going out.  
2. Ruined old castles, you will no longer please me, and no more happiness will blossom for me in this world.  
3. Ruined old castles can be rebuilt again, but my heart can never heal anymore.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A Gypsy woman was sitting under a bush, Nanynka was walking here with her Pepíček, God bless you, my Nanynka, reach out your little hand, let me tell your fortune.  
2. If I, Gypsy woman, had known that you would tell me a good fortune, I would have given you one or two groš, if you told me a good fortune.  
3. Reach out your little hand, delicate body, there are three boys fighting over you here. One has wealth, the second handsomeness, the third has love and sincerity.  
4. But love, Nanynka, and sincerity are a little honored guest with you. But love, Nanynka, and sincerity will last with Pepíček until eternity.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. She was sitting under the pine tree, crying because she had a little one – a sprig of rosemary in the garden. 2. She prayed for God’s rain to help her grow still – a sprig of rosemary in the garden. 3. She met the gamekeeper and asked him if he also wanted – a sprig of rosemary in the garden. 4. She met the priest and said she would show him – a sprig of rosemary in the garden. 5. She met the chaplain and said she would give him herself – a sprig of rosemary in the garden.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A maiden sits in a small chamber, holding her son in her arms, mourning her green wreath.  
2. Be silent, my dear, don’t cry anymore, don’t worry about it, you have a little son, you are his mother.  
3. You call me mother, but you don’t act as a father yourself. [...]  
4. I don’t know how to rock a cradle, I am ashamed of the cradle, rock it yourself, with whom did you seek advice.  
5. I took it in the sitting room between two hunters, they gave me advice when they loved me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The farmer is mowing in the meadow, his scythe does not want to cut, and he looks back, raising his eyes to the sky to see how high the sun is.  
2. The sun is at noon, my dear, escort me, escort me across the village, and I will escort you through the forest, then we will part ways.  
3. When she went out on the path, she sang that little song, she sang hahaha, I have already won, my golden huntsman.  
4. The huntsman killed a jay, put it in his hat, it scratched itself until it scratched its way into the green grove.  
5. There it perched on a little branch, sang that little song, sang hahaha, I have already won, my golden huntsman.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Gray little dove, where have you been, gray little dove, where have you been, that you lost your ash-gray feathers, that you lost your ash-gray feathers, haha, lost them?
