2. I lost them in the field, I was defying my Pepíček.  
3. Just don’t defy me, just watch out, gray little dove!  
4. I will set a trap for you with corn cockle, I will catch you, gray little dove, in the barn.  
5. I will set a trap for you with wheat, I will catch you, gray little dove, in the sitting room.  
6. I will set a trap for you with rye ear, I will catch you, gray little dove, again tomorrow.  
7. When we caught the little dove, then seven musicians played for us.  
8. Just you, musicians, be merry, as long as I have my green wreath on my head.  
9. When I no longer have it, then I will thank you, gentlemen musicians.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The sun is rising, giving a bright glow, climbing upwards, adorning the forests, giving the flowers their scent.  
2. The grove is greening, the birds are singing, a gentle breeze is sweeping through the grass, the forests are sparkling.  
3. In that little grove, she was standing, cutting the grass, when she cut the grass, she tied it into a knot, and was crying sorrowfully.  
4. I recognized her by her voice, that it was my beloved, I went to the little meadow, I went under the little tree, she saw me.  
5. As soon as she saw me, she ran to me, she asked me perhaps: Where were you walking at night? I heard it.  
6. I answered her that I was looking after the rye, that the deer trampled it at night, that I was lying by it.  
7. She quieted down, cut some grass, believed me, hurried home, and gave me a kiss.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The sun is setting, it looks like it will rain, hide me, my dear, under your little jacket. I would hide you, that I love you, that I no longer have enough of the little jacket.  
2. Nothing troubles me, nothing worries me, that my Nanynka’s little jacket is getting shorter. The little jacket is getting shorter, the skirt too, what will her mother say to that?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Sadness, melancholy on all sides, wherever I turn, deprived of pleasure, I trouble my sincere heart. Where has amusement gone, where has my joy gone? Deprived of pleasure, only sorrow follows.  
2. I wish to be a little bird, just to look at you, so I could fly to you, to bring joy to my heart. No transformation is possible, I must suffer like this, you, my only joy, I must be far from.  
3. I hear sad lamenting, across mountains and forests, I hear wailing, until my heart is terrified. Are you, my maiden, are you complaining about me, your sincere lover, lamenting like this?  
4. Out of sight, out of mind, I do not fear that, but then I think that in your love, I do not stand alone in favor. I do not care about that, because I was sincere, your false heart troubles and torments you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. A leaf fell from the maple, it fell on both of us, come, my dear, come and escort me all the way to our yard. 2. And I will not go with you, because I will not be yours, you are the son of wealthy parents and I am a poor girl.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. The masons were building, they were building scaffolding, where have you gone, my delight? 2. My delight with blue eyes, I would rather jump into the water for him. 3. She jumped into the water, as the wheel turns, ah, why did I have to recognize his blue eyes?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Light up, little moon, into the room where they have green shutters.  
2. There at the Vinč family’s, they have them, that’s where the dandy boys go, all groomed.  
3. Groomed on both sides, handsome girls follow them.  
4. There at the Vinč family’s, I was sleeping soundly, someone took my little hat off my head.  
5. No one else but my dear one, she took my little hat off my head.  
6. Took it off my head, hung it up, to make up with her sweetheart.  
7. I knock, I knock, open up, I left my little hat at the girl’s place.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Shine, little moon, brightly, so I can see those lands, perhaps sweet sleep is playing with my beloved.  
2. Blow, gentle breeze, over that deserted quarry, do not wake my darling, perhaps she is dreaming of me.  
3. The little moon was watching when I stood with my beloved, when I gave her a goodnight kiss.  
4. I gave it to her, I gave it, but she did not want to take it; when I begged her, she did not want to give it.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Shine, little moon, shine across the whole world, jump out of the window up to my dearest, make a circle.  
2. Make a circle, call my dear, so she won't be proud and will come out a little bit on her own.  
3. She did not come out, she sent a messenger: Go, my dear messenger, tell him this well, just as I would myself.  
4. The messenger stammered, he convinced himself, oh, I am unfortunate that she did not come out on her own!  
5. Rather than having a girl and being beaten for her, I'd rather dig a pit three hundred and twenty fathoms deep and throw her into it!  
From the handwritten songbook of A. Pochová.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. She sewed a little shirt, ribbon sleeves, my dear Andulka sewed and embroidered it for a tolar of herbs.  
2. For a tolar of herbs, for two gold threads, my dear Andulka sewed and embroidered a flower onto the little shirt.  
3. A flower onto the little shirt, a white daisy, for they buried, laid to the grave our young love.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. It's a pity, girl, that you won't be mine, that you love another, that you want another, it's a pity, girl, that you won't be mine, that you love another, that you want another.  
2. I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming that you will be mine, my maiden, that you will be mine one day.  
3. I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming that my maiden has blue eyes.  
4. I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming that she walks only on tiptoes, hands in pockets.  
5. I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming, I keep dreaming that my beloved Ferina has black eyes.  
6. It's a pity, girl, that you won't be mine, that you love another, that you want another.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The young girl went to confession, she had golden lacing, the priest immediately asked her: Who laced you up, girl?  
2. Father, why do you ask about virgin lacing? I lace myself and I love Pepíček.  
3. Give up, girl, on loving, better take up repentance. If you don't stop loving – you will go to hell.  
4. I am not afraid of hell, only when I have, where I stand, I have my Pepíček, a faithful lover.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Four horses in the yard, four horses, with linden, maple, elm, and poplar, in the yard.  
2. None of them neigh, none of them neigh.  
3. Pepíček neighs with them, he has a little hat tilted to the side.  
4. When he finished plowing the field, he called to his beloved.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Four black horses, the field unplowed, in vain, Jeníček, you tread the path to us, someone else is already coming to us.  
2. Whoever knows the path to my Andulence, I will not tell them, for I go to her, and she does not want me.  
3. A four-leaf clover means luck, I will put it under my hat so that you know that I like you.  
4. If only I knew why my head hurts, whether I’m in the field or in the little church, who will tell me that?  
5. Not everyone who sings and cries is joyful; often in a corner, quietly, they weep over the grave of their love.  
6. In our garden, the wind plays with the leaves, singing a song to the nightingales that love is blossoming in your little heart for me.  
7. And the linden flower will not heal the heart, only from the lips of the bud, the laughter of the dear girl, when it rings to me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: That Serbian pub is nicely paved, my dear paved it with red bricks.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Secret love, it troubles me and keeps me from sleeping, because I must not say that I am your beloved.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. There behind the mountain, behind the tall one, there I have a girl, there I have a girl, there I have a blue-eyed girl.  
2. I would go to her, the road is bad, it rains during the day, it is dark at night.  
3. I would go to her through the alleys, two little stars were shining for me.  
4. One dark, the other bright, oh my God, which one is mine?  
5. Oh, you dark one, brighten up, my darling, make up with me.  
6. How should I make up, if I am not allowed to take you?  
7. I am knocking, knocking on the window, open to me, my Little Enda.  
8. I am carrying a golden ring for you with diamonds all over.  
9. I am not allowed to take you and I cannot, the army binds our freedom.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Over there is a white house, and I like that one, there are two girls there, they would make me happy, there are two girls there, they would make me happy.  
2. But they won’t be, because their parents trouble my heart, because they oppose it.  
3. You took for yourselves what you liked, and you don’t want to give me what would make me happy.  
4. And the only thing that would make me happy is the white rose, because every boy chooses my darling.  
5. Nanynka was sitting as if she were one hundred and one, she sat down from a high chair to a low one.  
6. Nanynko, Nanynko, are you coming to us from afar, you will sit from a high stool to a low one.  
7. Nanynko, Nanynko, are you coming to us from afar, you will mount from a high horse to a low one.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Water flows to the Velvary mills, water flows to the Velvary mills, I do not love what is not to my liking, I do not love what is not to my liking.  
2. What I love, my parents forbid me, they do not allow our love.  
3. Water flows down the valley, come, my dear, let's wander together.  
4. What kind of wandering would it be if we are not married to each other?  
5. In Chržín, the priest preaches, he will marry us, he will bind our hands.  
6. If he does not bind them, we will bind them ourselves, we will put an end to our love.  
7. Under the valleys, the water sadly roars, blue eyes have drowned there.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Water flows from the maple around the very yard, it swept away all the footbridges, only one was left, it swept away all the footbridges, only one was left.  
2. Over which Pepíček walked, leading Mařenka by the hand, he picked an apple, picked two, let one float on the water.  
3. Go, little apple, wherever you please, just don’t get wet, go, little apple, go down, to my Mařenka’s house.  
4. When the little apple floated over, it called to Mařenka: Get up, Mařenka, come outside, your beloved is standing in front of the house.  
5. Why would I go outside, when I am not his beloved, and he is not a rose blossom, to spoil my world.  
6. Why would I go outside, I told him not to come to us, not to wander along that path.  
7. There was love, but there is none, it fell among the stones, among the tiny stones, into the false heart.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Water flows against water, the wind blows into it, my dearest of all looks out from the little window, my dearest of all looks out from the little window. 2. Don’t just look out from the little window, come out in front of the house instead, if you give me one little kiss, I will give you seven. 3. Before I give you just one, I’d rather give you a hundred, so that people don’t think I don’t love you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Water flows, flows, it rushes over the stone, whoever doesn’t know how to cry, love will teach him.  
2. Yesterday, his lady mother told me not to think about her son.  
3. And I replied to her question: Just tie him at home with a string.  
4. At home with a string, by the leg of the table, so the flirt doesn’t make me ashamed.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Water flows from Tábor, it spins around, my dear is angry. 2. When she is angry, let her be angry, I will not go to her, I will not ask her. 3. I will get myself a girl from the village, she knows how to waltz and love boys.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. That little hill in Chržín, on it a nice little grove, a little grove, in that little grove a nightingale, knows many songs.  
2. Don’t sing, nightingale, you’ll wake my Ančička, she hasn’t slept for four nights, waiting for Pepíček.  
3. Waiting is a hard thing, it’s worse than pain. For pain there is a plaster, for Ančička, Pepíček.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. That little hill in Chržín, that little hill in Chržín, on it stands Pepíček, on it stands Pepíček. 2. He went out to the field to hoe out the corn cockle. 3. When he hoed out the corn cockle, he called to his beloved. 4. Come, my dear, come to me, I will tell you what is wrong with me. 5. My little head hurts, my mother has beaten it. 6. Father also beat it, I wouldn’t visit you. 7. They don’t beat me at all, you can see someone else. 8. I won’t go to someone else, I would rather join the army.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The one who falls in love tries very much, and the one who does not love does not promise love, that one does not teach about it.  
2. Love, love, it is cruel, it does not let a young girl sleep, it tears her from sleep and awakens me, my heart settles.  
3. A young troubled heart always remembers, where is my darling, that golden Jeníček, where he might be spending the night.  
4. Your beloved is distant, living in a foreign land, living abroad, he does not remember that you loved each other.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. This local priest here, who preaches so well, will not bind the hands of a maiden, not bind the hands of a maiden.  
2. When we kneel at the altar, he will also unite them for us, until the longest death, my dearest, nothing will separate us.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. That cornelian cherry wasp, it is nicely green, Pepíček lies in it, a handsome young lad, in the green rosemary.  
2. Go there, my dear, go to him, he will tell you what is wrong, his head hurts, handsome Pepíček needs a doctor.  
3. The doctors came to him, immediately found the problem: If you had a little girl and a kiss from her, that would heal you.  
4. When she heard that, she immediately ran to him, gave him a kiss, her Pepíček, and that healed him.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Our house is made entirely of stone, a dear one passed by, but did not greet.  
2. He did not greet because of a small mistake, that I did not say to him: Good night, Pepíček!  
3. When you pass by our house, your heart will ache, even if you were made of stone.  
4. She stood by the spring, drawing water, Pepíček’s mother called to her.  
5. Draw me some water, Mařenka, draw me some water, when you get married, I will invite you to the feast.  
6. She drew water, poured wine, cheers, take it, for your son.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The grass doesn’t grow, only by the water, where the ferrying lads are going, the lads are going.  
2. The lads are not from roses, peonies, they wear white hats.  
3. White hats, peacock feathers, the ferrying lads believe in that.  
4. Andulka, get up, serve breakfast, the ferrymen have the ferry loaded.  
5. Andulka got up, served breakfast, and even led Pepíček to the ferry.  
6. She led him to the ferry, didn’t want to sit down, afraid the water might flood them both.  
7. And when they reached the first stream, Andulka wanted to get off the ferry.  
8. And when they got closer to Prague, Andulka wanted red wine.  
9. Red wine with white jelly: I could have been a maiden till now!  
10. A maiden till now and you a lad, we could have both walked under the wreath.  
11. Both under the wreath, a nice green one, we could have walked both under the same one.  
12. My Andulka, you are to blame, you lured me into your hall.  
13. Into your hall, then into a small room, where you gave me sweet kisses.  
14. Sweet kisses, they were like honey, it’s a pity, girl, that I deceived you.  
15. I only deceived you once, I will regret it a hundred thousand times.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Green grass, cold dew, covered with flowers, dear Andulka, if you are sincere, let me into your little chamber.  
2. I won’t let you in, I’d rather leave, my dear Pepíček. If you don’t let me in, I will ride away, I will saddle my black horse.  
3. What a shame, dear Andulka, that I walked the path to you, if I had known you wouldn’t love me, I would have rather stayed home.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Here it hurts me, here it pricks me, here everything tenses me up, send for the doctor to tell me what is wrong with me.  
2. The doctor is no longer at home, nor his assistant, send for grandmother to brew me some juniper.  
3. Juniper won’t help at all, nor any spices, send for Pepíček for my pleasure.  

It is also sung as the well-known march "Kmochův Nade mlejnem, pode mlejnem."

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Those lads from Dřínov, they know how to dance, they escort pretty girls, they know how to flirt.  
2. They walk along the road, leading the girls, their bangs are combed up, hanging down to their eyes.  
3. One of them is small, he has blue eyes, he promised me love, now he promises another.  
4. One of them is big, but he knows how to dance, he escorts all the girls, but he loves none of them.  
5. One of them is red-haired, quite handsome, he has cheeks like two roses, like a bud in spring.  
6. One of them among them knows how to perform couplets, he loves a pretty girl, her name is Mařenka.  
7. One of them among them is dressed up, he escorts all the girls and talks with everyone.  
8. We forgot one, but that doesn’t matter, he has legs like two hoops, just a little bit lame.  
9. And the boys from Lhota, they come here too, one day they will get such a beating here that they will remember it.  
10. The Dřínov road is nicely well-trodden, that’s where my delight used to walk.  

Servants from estates: Růžena Kalcová, Plicková, and Svobodová composed this.  
At the end of each stanza, the singer told me: "This is X."  
Melody unknown.  

šmaťchavý = lame, limping;  
kupletovat = apparently composing, singing, and performing couplets

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. You false hypocrite, you have false eyes, whoever you meet on the street, you look back at everyone. 2. You false hypocrite, you have false eyes, yesterday you spoke with me, and today you no longer recognize me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. You, little church in Chržín, you stand on a nice little hill, on two, on two, you stand on a nice little hill.  
2. Lords come out of you like angels from heaven.  
3. The first came out all white, and that was my darling.  
4. The second came out like butter, my heart just trembled at her.  
5. The third came out like an angel, thunder strike you, girl, thunder strike you down.  
6. Thunder strike you down into two parts for false love.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Those royal oxen drink muddy water, be careful, boy, so they don't kill you.  
2. If they did kill you, it would be a pity, the cornel inn would cry for you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. You wanted to sulk at me, you didn’t want to love me, you wanted to give me the finger, my picture. I will not cry for you, I thank you nicely, I thank you nicely for your love.  
2. You carried falsehood in your heart when you promised me love, when you didn’t want me, girl, I don’t have to have you. I’d rather go out into the world where roses bloom for me, since you were false, I will leave you be.  
Echo of Mozart’s minuet from Don Giovanni. Reported by Josef Švec, bandleader. It is said to have been sung by old Jan Řehák, a musician from Všestudy, a violist who used to play in a quartet with the young Antonín Dvořák.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. At Dřín's place, the little bridge is sagging, beneath it is a little stream, spreading out. There are little fish in it, but no one catches them, they are very small.  
2. Madam, you have a beautiful daughter, but you lock her up before me. You should have locked her up before I started coming to you.  
3. Madam, your daughter is looking out the window, she threw the keys into my hat. Pepíček, here is the key, you can open my little chamber.  
4. We both sat down on the little bed, it lasted only a short moment, and then we fell asleep, until above us the swallows were singing.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Near Hosín, there is a green meadow, a forester was grazing, he was grazing a deer. Hey hupy hey župy hey dupy dup.  
2. It was not a deer, it was a doe, wait, young lady, in the evening for me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. There is a stone by the monastery, a doll sits on it, calling: Boys, come here, I am no longer in the monastery! 2. Nothing pleases me in the monastery because there are no boys there, and I like to dance, I love handsome boys. František Douda from Dražičky near Tábor also knew this.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Near Veltrusy, there is a chapel, which Ančička whitewashed; when she finished whitewashing it, she called her Pepíček. 2. Cute Pepíček, you have a dirty little jacket, who will wash it for you, neatly clean it, since you no longer have a mother? 3. Since I no longer have a mother, at least I have Ančička, she will wash it for me, neatly clean it, the scarf and the little jacket.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. By the little pond on the hill, in that green grove, sit three girls playing cards.  
2. One is Karolínka, the second is Lenka, and I won't tell you the third—she is my beloved.  
3. I took her by the hand, she took me by both, come, my dear, onto the little boat, let’s sit close together.  
4. I won’t go, I’m afraid, you are a great traitor, you trampled my marigold in our garden.  
5. My marigold blooms nicely white in the garden, you took my wreath away, what will mom say?  
6. I don't have it, you don't have it, neither of us has it, who will sell it to us, my darling?  
7. In Mělník, there are three merchants who sell it, but, my Andulka, they want a hard dollar.  
8. Whether they want it or not, we’ll give it to them anyway, press it, my darling, to your false heart.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. He tied the little horse to dry wood, to dry wood, Ančička will not be, will not be your wife, will not be your wife. [chorus] Well then for the little son, well then for him! Why didn't he leave the girl, if she wasn't for him? 2. He tied the little horse to a juniper, an oak, that night I had a suitor for kisses.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. That rascal, look, do you see, is turning green, my sweetheart is leaving me, look, do you see. If he doesn't want me, let him go, I won't beg him, look, do you see, for a lazy one.  
2. I have said goodbye to my sweetheart. I entrusted him to God. To God and kind people, that they may feed him white bread. White bread, rolls, I nourished him with kisses.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. On Sunday at four o'clock, I heard sad news that you were seeing off another girl as the moon was rising. 2. When you were seeing her off, "take care, do not come back to my heart, someone else might still have a more sincere heart, to love faithfully." 3. I will go to the Kladno road, sit under a poplar tree, and there I will cry and lament to myself day and night. 4. And there I will cry for a whole year, until a stream flows from my eyes, until from my eyes, through false words, an eternal stream flows.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In the field stands a pine tree, a pine tree, my Anička was sitting under it, la la, my Anička, there.  
2. A nightingale flew in, carrying a letter, to his Andulka from the right side.  
3. My dear Andulka, don’t be afraid, I am bringing you a letter, you will be pleased.  
4. Before the moon rises from the dark mountain, you will have your beloved by the chamber.  
5. He will be waiting for you, slowly knocking on the door.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In those dogwood fields, there is a spring that stands until the water dries up, then we will be ours.  
2. The water does not dry up, it keeps increasing, it won’t be, boy, it will never be.  
3. No one, no one knows what is between us, no one, no one knows what the heart desires.  
4. It desires, it desires that, no one knows what, for a handsome sweetheart who is beyond the brook.  
5. He is not beyond the brook, he is beyond the mountains, no one, no one knows what is between us.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In the valley below the hillside, in that little cottage, Pepíček promised, promised Nanynka. [chorus] Tralala tralala, it was no fun.  
2. Pepíček used to come, he promised her love, and when he made love, he wandered away.  
3. When you wandered, I won't be here, I'll tie my little bundle and wander too.  
4. The girl wandered from Prague to Moravia, she had blue eyes all tearful.  
5. When you wandered, I will come to you, like a little sheep to the green grass.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Farewell, deceitful love, adieu, farewell, all sincerity ceases, for you are full of falsehood.  
2. Your heart is full of bitterness, for you are without any care, I am glad that I am already free from your malice.  
3. What sad news do I hear at this hour, surely it is my only, my dearest mouth.  
4. No, no, I hope that he still loves me, but if he is angry with me, I must beg him for mercy.  
5. Behold, you deceiver, begone from me, do not throw yourself before my eyes anymore, now seek your happiness elsewhere.  
Melody unknown.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Yesterday the sun was shining on me, and today it is not, I loved a maiden, she was like a flower.  
2. We went for a walk together into the green grove, and there we made love until the white day.  
3. When we woke up, the dew stood on the flowers, my dearest was crying, lamenting.  
4. Don’t cry, don’t grieve, I can take you home, if I deceived you, what does it matter to anyone?  
5. I always liked your name, Pepíček, times have changed, now they call you daddy.  
6. I also liked your name, Nanynka, times have changed, now they call you mommy.  
7. I am not allowed to go among the maidens, I am rejected by them, the young men laugh at me because I am a woman without a husband.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The priest from Vepřek preaches nicely, handing out pictures at the altar. I will also go to that sermon, since he gave me a picture, I will thank him.  
2. The priest from Vepřek is very kind, he gave me a painted picture. I will sit him at the table, sit next to him, he gives me a kiss and I give him one.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Stars, stars, how small they are, they accompanied me, they accompanied me from my Anička. 2. When we said goodbye, tears flowed over me, in the Libšice church the bells tolled for kneeling.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A fairy wreath for lovemaking, made of green rosemary, a fairy wreath, oh fairy, what, my dear, did you think?  
2. I thought, and I will think, that I will not get you, my dear pleasure, you are not meant for me.  
3. When we wanted to arrange a wedding, you did not want to allow it, you would not allow anything when they were cutting my hair.  
4. They will take off my worldly clothes, they will dress me in a habit, in a black habit, that is for my beloved.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I know one maiden, about her I will not speak, nor will I appear with her before a friend. I keep her name secret, I will tell no one, my intention and my truth I will tell to everyone.  
2. What a joyful, merry moment it is when a young man strolls with a maiden. On a walk, while dancing, they enjoy themselves, only when they are content in their hearts.  
3. The sun set, the moon rose, I went to my beloved, I came to her before midnight, the stars were shining. I knocked on the window: Open to me, maiden, if you are not sleeping or what are you doing, my darling?  
4. She was sleeping, immediately woke up, answered: Who is looking here at this late hour? If it is you, my František, wait a little moment, my father and mother are sleeping in the little room.  
5. The moon set, the sun rose, I left my beloved, sweet little birds sang to me, the nightingale was peeking, the quail was counting, as my dearest saw me off.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Turn around, my maiden, around me, like a little falcon bird, around me. Make a circle, my little dove, you will be mine, you will be mine, you will be mine.  
2. If you were, my dear, merciful, you would saddle a little horse and ride after me all the way to Brno.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Get up, my dear, the day is already white, come on, let's go to the grove to pick strawberries.  
2. I would get up, but my head hurts, take, Pepíček, the white scarf and tie it on me.  
3. Pepíček tied it, his hands dropped, what did my eyes see, what were they afraid of?  
4. I was not afraid, I was just worried that my dearest is dying in my arms.  
5. I am not dying, I am just fainting, because my Pepíček goes elsewhere.  
6. If you go, go on, may God grant you happiness, and I will look for a boy to my liking.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. You, Loretánské clock, why didn’t you chime longer, that you separated me and my sweetheart in such a short time?  
2. The clock is not to blame, Samáš is the cause that I didn’t come to you yesterday, you were seeing another.  
3. I used to see others, I used to hang out with others, but I constantly thought of you, my Pepíček.  
4. Pepíček was walking down the street, wearing his hat askew, people told him: There you will meet your maiden.  
5. Don’t talk to me about her, I can’t look at her, if I glance with one eye, I’ll quickly go inside.  
6. If she couldn’t look at me, why did she visit us? I didn’t ask you to love me.  
7. A tinsmith was walking down the street, a peasant woman called to him to repair her jug with two handles.  
8. He answered her that he had very strong wire, and she should wait until his friend comes.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. You little stars, you are so small when I look at you and contemplate your clear beauty. You were created by God to shine at night, so that you might sometimes be a little help to me, to light my way when I go to my beloved at night.  
2. That bright star, the brightest of all, always shone for me when my dearest escorted me through the forest. A gentle breeze softly blew, and behind the woods the pale moon was setting. The bright sun smiling beautifully into her little windows.  
3. If there were as many beautiful stars in the sky as the sweet kisses my dearest gave me! But there are not, and there never will be; it is of no use. I used to go, but now I must not, I will go no more. Your lady forbids me to visit you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. A little dove flew out from the cliff, from the cliff, it awakened the blue eyes from sleep, it awakened the blue eyes from sleep. 2. If the blue eyes had not been asleep, they would have caught the little dove. 3. It was not a little dove, it was a bird; if you do not want me, my dear, let it be so. 4. If you do not want to love me, do not want to; someone more worthy will court me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Behind our little cottage is a small garden, there I used to sit alone most gladly.  
2. I grow flowers of all kinds so I can make a wreath for my beloved.  
3. Come to me, darling, help me water, in our garden we will make love.  
4. When my dear came, we sat together, instead of watering, we preferred to love each other.  
5. I will weave you a wreath of pure little roses, and I will also give you a few kisses.  
6. Then we always sit in our garden and constantly think about our love.  
7. I do not grow a rose, only one bud, the cause of that is a little boy.  
8. Little boy, I call him Vašíček, he left him to me, you are his father.  
9. Little boy, cheeks like a little rose, he left him to me, I am his mother.  
10. Believe me, girls, this is our song, I cannot sing any further because I am very thirsty.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Behind our garden there is a quiet little stream, tramtají dájídá, there is a quiet little stream. I used to sit there, weaving a wreath, waiting for my darling there, waiting for my darling there, tramtají dájídá, I was waiting.  
2. My dear ran up, sat down beside me, took the wreath from me, hugged me all over.  
3. The little stream took the wreath and quickly flowed away with it, oh, where have the times gone, where have they disappeared, what haven’t my eyes already seen, my love?  
4. It’s been so long since he came to us, maybe now another girl is coaxing him away.  
5. Let her coax, let her coax, I won’t coax, I won’t sit by the stream anymore.  
6. Come, my dear, come and see how I will be hugging others again.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Beyond the water, beyond the water, beyond the little stream, my darling played with a little dove, my darling played with a black gull: Marry me, Pepíček, farewell!  
2. I will marry you, you won't even know it, you won't forbid my announcements, nor will you let me arrange the wedding ceremony, that you, my darling, will not do.  
3. If I can't get you as my wife, I will choose you as my bridesmaid, so that you will serve me one last time, placing a little wreath on my head.  
4. That cannot happen to you, Pepíček, out of pity I couldn't give it to you, because of crying I couldn’t even enter the church, due to false love I must die.  
5. For that I only beg you, my Pepíček, to light a candle at my passing, to stand alone at my passing, pressing my cloudy little eyes.  
6. Press my little eyes, light the candle, pray the Lord’s Prayer for my little soul, so that our love, ignited by a single little flame, will burn out.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. My doll started sulking at me, saying she doesn't love me anymore and that I wasn't allowed to come to her. 2. Sulk, sulk, my doll, you will stop sulking when I wander away from here, you will remember me. 3. When you wander away from here, I will wander to you, my parents are in the cemetery, they will not stop me. Melody unknown.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Little garden, little garden, you smell of flowers, the sun shines brightly into our little window.  
2. The sun shines brightly, a bright star has risen, my dearest of all came after me, came after me.  
3. She came to the stable among the black horses: Get up, get up, my Pepíček, I have come to you.  
4. How can I get up when my head hurts, if you have a faithful heart to me, come and bandage it for me.  
5. She took a white scarf, tied it around her head, then turned away from me and began to cry.  
6. Why do you cry and lament when you still have me here? You can cry then, when you no longer watch over me.  
7. I cry and lament because of one word, you said good night to everyone, but to me you said nothing.  
8. I say good night to all the ill-tempered girls, only to the one I am angry with I will not say good night.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I will go to your little window, to see my Anežka: Anežka, oh my child, come and comfort my heart!  
2. Why should I comfort you, when I am not your sweetheart, you have nothing to do with girls, you hold a breviary in your hand.  
3. Why should I have nothing to do with girls, when I am not yet a priest, I have been a top student, and always an excellent one.  
4. I studied for seven years in Prague, meanwhile I did not love any girls, now I will go to the monastery, I will become a father (priest).  
5. Wait, girl, you will cry when I preach a sermon in that hostýn church, the first will be about you.  
6. Wait, girl, you will long for it when I celebrate the holy mass in that hostýn church, the first will be for you.  
7. The altar servers carry the holy book, in my darling all the limbs tremble, they tremble because of falsehood, which will last for eternity.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The cuckoo cuckooed in the forest, in the game preserve, my lady cried at home in the chamber.  
2. Why are you crying and lamenting, you will be mine as soon as the cuckoo cuckoos three times at Christmas.  
3. How can I not lament, if I won’t be yours, since the cuckoo never cuckoos at Christmas.  
4. My little rosemary has grown, nice and green, I had a lover, but now he belongs to another.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: It seemed to me, it seemed, that early in the morning you gave me a little kiss, my dear Pepíček, and then you laid your little hand on my heart.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A green meadow full of flowers, dear, the world already grieves me here. Wander with me, if you are faithful to me, we will go together into the wide world.  
2. I will not go with you, my dear, people would talk about us, slander us, complain about us, because we walked together.  
3. Let them talk, we won’t heed it, we won’t pay attention to people’s words, we love each other, we won’t be deterred, let’s wander together into the world.  
4. You will go, I will follow you, like a lamb follows the grass, we will make a bed for ourselves on the green grass, which will be our blanket.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. No one knows what love is, what I must try, what I have taken into my heart, I must leave behind. You yourselves took what you liked, no one forbade you, you didn’t care.  
2. I wish you much happiness, my dear Pepíček, when you will go to another's party by moonlight, then remember me, that you used to come to me, and I again so far away, right outside your door.  
3. When the little tree blooms with a white branch, when my beloved has left me, God will not abandon me, then remember me, that you used to come to me, and I again so far away, right outside your door.  
4. When I will be sleeping in the dark grave at the cemetery, and you, my Pepíček, will be mourning for me, then you will remember me, that you used to come to me, while I will be sleeping peacefully in that green grass.  
břitov (dialect) = cemetery

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: No one knows like me how bad the road is at night, what a pleasure it is when my dear gives a little kiss, what a pleasure it is when my dear gives a little kiss.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: And that old klutz, even if he doesn’t come after me, he buys for two pennies and fixes his little shop. klutz (colloquial) = oaf, klutz

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: And you dear boy, kuku, kuku, tell us the truth, how many Prajzes did the girls here have last year? Plenty. Musicians sang this around the year 1867 in Kozomín.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: And you, my charming coffee grinder, I would spin with you every moment. Right, left, left, right, I would spin with you every moment.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: And you, smudged shoemaker, don’t touch my knees, and you, smudged shoemaker, don’t touch me there!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. And in Kostelec, the tower tolls seven o’clock, come on, boys, come here, I’ll tell you something! Hejeje, how nicely we’re marching, hejeje, how nicely we’re walking.  
2. Seven o’clock struck, the bell rings, they ran out of the factory, there’s no end to them.  
3. Each with a ribbon in their hair, dirty little hands, so you could recognize the Kostelec factory girls.  
4. On Sunday they go to the music like some ladies, on Monday to the factory like silly girls.  
5. [...] that she will go to the fair for Saint Mark’s Day.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Oh, we poor orphans, oh, we poor orphans, we drink, we drink, we drink, we drink, we drink day and night.  
2. If the beer would end, what would become of us, what would become of us, what would become of us, what would become of us, what would become of us orphans.  
3. Beer has its custom, that it makes the wise foolish, the foolish wise, and fools out of the wise.  
4. Two people lead him by the shoulders so that he does not, so that he does not, so that he does not, so that he does not, so that he does not kick a stone.  
5. A third one straightens his legs, the right over the left, the left over the right, crossing one leg over the other.  
6. Puddles like paving stones, he gives greetings to the columns, to the columns, to the columns, to the columns, to the columns.  
7. People look out the windows, thinking, thinking, thinking, thinking, thinking that they are going with a bear, but they are with godfather Matěj.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Oh, that cursed marriage, it drives a man crazy, oh, that cursed marriage, it drives a man crazy.  
2. A man can help himself out of prison, but not from a bad woman.  
3. I got four thousand for a wife, what I have left is a piece of old monkey.  
4. I gave four thousand for debts, what I have left is a piece of old disgrace.  
5. And she has hands like rollers, God, I got a slacker!  
6. Hair disheveled like a dragon, eyes red like a crab!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Let the crinoline spin and the skirt be nice and white, let the petticoat spin, let the waistband spin. aj ce (dialect) = let it; rajf, reifenmantel, from German = coat

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Oh, Franta went among the drunks, sold his coat, bought cards. Better cards than a coat, when you're feeling lonely, we can play. Franta (archaic) = drinker, wanderer, vagabond

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Ajci, ajci, ajci on, our dad has a hangover. Ajci, ajci, ajci he has it, he will make money from it. 2. Ajci, ajci, ajcisi, don't touch my boob, and I have a sore one, it hurts me a lot. ajci (dialect) = let it be

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Alou, gentlemen musicians, cheerfully, because my dearest has left me. I don’t mind, I’ll put a flower on my hat, because I’m a handsome lad, I’ll sing to myself.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The budgerigar is a good girl, she jumps like a deer, when a boy gives her a little kiss, she cries with joy. She was sitting on the dome, calling after us: Blue-eyed boys, I will come after you.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Andulka, show me your little frog, Andulka, show me your little frog, plump, fat, plump, fat, Andulka, show me your little frog. 2. Andulka showed the little frog, she had a red bow on it, plump, fat, plump, fat, she had a red bow on it.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I am overseas with a wheelbarrow, I will carry a keg of monajs. Glory to you, Mr. Vajs, for brewing such good monajs! monajs = hard liquor This song is a parody of the well-known song of American Czechs "When I Will Live Across the Sea."

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When we build that monastery, oracionibus, we will put nuns in it, oracionibus. With that big bim bam bim bam gloria, oracionibus.  
2. Whoever wants to be the abbot in that monastery must have one two meters long.  
3. Whoever wants to be a nun in the monastery must have one as big as a spindle.  
4. A nun died in Cokrhel, they dragged her to hell by her ass.  
5. A nun died wearing a black cap, they dragged her to hell, all shoemakers.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I leave the pub, who will see me off? No one, no one, no one, I'm not known here, no one, no one, no one, I'm not known here.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Villain 1908 Curse the one who has nothing, does not care where to hide it. Boldly lies down, boldly gets up, no one steals anything from him.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. There will be thirst, there will be, when the innkeeper goes to sleep, we will stop drinking beer, we must go home already, then we will feel sad.  
2. The innkeeper is already going to sleep, he must not pour any more, so let's pay nicely, slip out of the pub, and go home to sleep.  
3. Good night, little brother, give me one more kiss, and go home to sleep too and dream something about good beer.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. There was a rich gentleman who bought cloth for clothes and bought a scarf for his wife; the Jews took his estate. Alright.  
2. The daughter asked her mother what kind of life they would lead: they will buy a velocipede and go to the "paďous." Alright.  
3. The teacher from Mělník bought a velocipede, rode it downhill, and tore his entire face. Alright.  

štof = quality (woolen) fabric; melnickej (colloquial) = from Mělník  
The saying "di do paďous" means "go to hell."

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: What are you doing behind that mountain? A maple cradle. Hurry, hurry, once you make it, you will also rock it.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The devil told me to marry an old woman, leave the virgin, marry the old woman, leave the virgin, marry the old woman, and she chases me with a hazel stick.  
2. I will drive the old woman away, send her off: I will leave the old woman, take the virgin, better to sleep with a young one than love an old one.  
3. The young woman loves, always just caresses. She chatters like a swallow, sweetening love, sweetening love.  
4. The old one is cold, like a quilt. As soon as he lies down, he already snores, sometimes he caresses, but still scratches, love cold, unloved.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: God give us health in that Prague region, God give us health, give! In that Prague region give us God’s health, in that Prague region give us God’s health, give us God’s health, give!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Have another one so you don’t freeze in January, I’ll have one too, have another one so you don’t freeze in January, I’ll get drunk too.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
Dřínov 1909, sung by Antonín Havíř, Antonín Votýpka, and Václav Černý

1. The young men of Dřínov, there are about twelve of them, those Dřínov boys, those Dřínov boys are ashamed of us.  
2. They are ashamed of us, they themselves have nothing, only the one petticoat they wear.  
3. On Monday they wash it, on Thursday they starch it, on Sunday they dress it up for our parade.  
4. At the music, the boys know how to look around, after the music, on the contrary, they know how to poke around.  
5. I have a girl in Dřínov, she sent me a letter, asking me to buy her clothes, because her breasts are bare.  
6. I wrote back to her that I was all broke, that I drank everything away, that I myself go without pants.  
7. If that wasn’t enough for you, she quickly replied, to buy her at least a skirt and some fancy ribbons.  
8. I took pity on her, immediately bought clothes, and ran to the seamstress to have them made.  
9. What surprised me, my good people, when I came to the Vinčovs, whom should I see?  
10. There was my Mařenka, already wearing the clothes, and she was very proud that it was Lila’s.  
11. But this beast Kerak found out about it, and before I could think, she already had them?  
12. She immediately approached me and gratefully said: May God bless you for this! And she looked proudly.  
13. A gentleman came for her to go dancing, she nimbly turned, and began to jump around.  
14. What’s it to anyone, she got herself a boyfriend, and immediately went out with him, and told me nothing.  
15. Wait, you old witch, we’ll talk about this, I immediately went with her to dance, I slapped her in the circle.  
16. Oh my dear Pepíček, what are you doing to me? Be quiet, don’t even talk to me, and give me the clothes right now!  
17. She didn’t want to give me the clothes, I pulled them off her, and she ran home in her petticoat.  
18. I took a friend, we went to Prague, paid the seamstress unnecessary expenses.  
19. And then loud singing arose from several throats: We don’t give a damn about you, lick our ass!

Composed by Antonín Havíř, Antonín Votýpka, and Václav Černý in January 1909.  
In the folk songbook, it is titled The Song of the Dřínov maids.

Note: At night during music, if a couple goes out, they are “walking the dog.”

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: If it were Vodolka, which is Dolany, I would give you a kiss on both sides. But since it is beyond the water, beyond the cold little water, I won't give you even one, my darling. Vodolka = the village Odolena Voda

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. If the gate didn't creak, I would come to you for kadlátka, for kadlátka, for hay, my dear Mařena.  
2. If the gate didn't creak, I would come to you for dried apples, for dried apples, for hay, my dear Kateřina.  
3. The girls greased the gate so it wouldn’t creak at night. Come to us, golden Pepíček, I will give you a sweet little kiss.  
4. If I had what my father has, I wouldn’t go after girls, I would lie nicely on the stove, and it would be without any argument.  

kadlátka, karlátka = a variety of plums

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. If daddy hadn’t been annoyed, tarara, not annoyed, tarara, not annoyed, I would say who was with me yesterday, tarara, was with me yesterday, tarara, was with me yesterday.  
2. Argue, daddy, or not, Pepíček was with me in the garden.  
3. If our daddy hadn’t hummed, I would say what Pepíček gave me.  
4. Hum however you want, daddy, he coaxed a little kiss from me.  
5. Then Pepíček promised me when he kissed me goodbye.  
6. Say what you want, daddy, after all, you will gladly marry me off.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When a farmer wants to succeed in Bohemia, a farmer, in Bohemia a farmer, a farmer, in Bohemia a farmer, nothing ennobles him as much as a wooden shoe, a wooden shoe, like a wooden shoe, a wooden shoe. Here in Bohemia: [chorus] Come, come, old one, stretch out your leg, let’s step a little bit in the old Czech style in a circle!  
2. Because they laugh proudly about wooden shoes, laugh, because they do not lose their shape.  
3. When I want to give Andulka a sign and give it, nothing knocks for me like a wooden shoe, a wooden shoe. It was said that the wooden shoe was sung to while dancing. How it was danced, they did not know how to tell.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. When I was going to Handy, I fell into a pit there, it was my luck that I was barefoot. 2. If I had been wearing shoes, I could have been killed, I would have broken my nose because of Handy.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. When I was a young hare, about four weeks old, that boy, the steward's son, was chasing me around the manor garden. 2. There was an old tree stump there, I crawled under it, and so I sat there quietly, nobody knew about me.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When I was dragging around with the horses, someone else was dancing with my beloved, I let the horses stand, I went to look at them, ha ha, I let the horses stand, I went to look at them.  
2. When I went through the Mělník gate, dew was falling behind me, it was falling like semolina, it was a dust storm!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. When I came back from my dear one, the black horses neighing: Where have you been wandering, scoundrel? We haven't eaten yet! 2. Just don't neigh, horses, I'll give you a piece of hay, before you eat the hay, I'll cut you some noodles.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When I went to the forest with my little rifle, twang, twang, I met Toníček and Ančička, when I went to the forest with my little rifle, I met Toníček and Ančička.  
2. What are you doing here that you keep moving those leaves?  
3. We are collecting acorns and beechnuts, we will feed the chickens with them.  
4. That’s not true, that’s a lie, I saw you climbing on something.  
5. When I was climbing, I was climbing on hazel bushes, I was picking nuts for Anička.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I went to Prague for sausages, I met a "pumprdlík" on the way. It wasn't a "pumprdlík," it was a "profous"; if I hadn't run away, he would have bitten me. "pumprdlík," from German = juggler; "profous," from German + Latin = military prison guard.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I went across the Prague bridge, I bought a pear there. Stick your head into the sack, drink some buttermilk, the goat has four horns, what will the brother-in-law say to that?

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When I was rolling the field, hoj caj caj, rolling, hoj caj caj, rolling, a stone fell under my roller, hoj caj caj, the stone, trádada.  
2. When I was rolling with my beloved, a stone fell under my back.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I fell asleep in the forest, someone cut it off, and all of it quietly, right by the belly. I don't know the tune.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. If you didn’t want me, take Rasa, you’ll have a piece of meat every day. 2. On Monday, on Tuesday, on Wednesday again, so you know that Rasa loves you.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When our Děreček married grandma, married, grandma married, married, grandma cried, Děrek laughed, laughed, Děrek laughed.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When we gathered like this, friends from the wet district, when we gathered together like this, let's pour ourselves a drink, friends from the wet district, let's pour ourselves a drink again.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The factory girl is a pretty girl, no one wants to love her, her skirts are short, her heels are worn out by her shoes.  
2. I begged father three times, I carried my little hat under my arm, for him to forgive me for what I have done to the factory girl.  
3. The factory girl is a pretty girl, I would find it hard to leave her: Forgive me, father, you have also loved the factory girl.  
4. On Monday we will go to the fair, on Tuesday we will still be there, on Wednesday from the fair, and on Thursday with a pretty girl to the park.  
5. On Sunday, oh yes, oh yes, the factory girl must be mine, it's no use, my father, you forbidding me the factory girl for nothing!  
According to information, the song originates from the Kladno region.  
sešmaťchaný = worn out (footwear damaged by bad walking)

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Dřínov, without further specification 1. Two grandmothers were arguing over a small piece of butter, they grabbed each other's skirts, one was shaking the other. 2. A mother and daughter were arguing about what kind of life they would lead, they will buy a velocipede, they will go to hell. 3. A frog has no hair, but a crayfish has claws; if it sewed a woman’s mouth shut, she would still babble with her nose. 4. The old woman tied the old man to a big stump, "If you don’t keep quiet, old man, I will cut you!" 5. Our late father, may God grant him heaven, used to take old maids for rides in a wheelbarrow into the forest. 6. The young groom got married, took a young wife, sat on a spinning wheel, and let her suck a pear.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Háječek, turn green, my lady, don’t be angry! It doesn’t work, it doesn’t work. If it doesn’t work, let’s leave it be.  
2. The lady cooks coffee, the gentleman sniffs rapé.  
3. The lady tears feathers, the gentleman smokes tobacco.  
4. In Klomín on the square, they beat a peacock with fists. According to the singer, the people of Klomín are called "pávoškubci" (peacock pluckers), because once they plucked a peacock.  

rapé, from French = a type of snuff tobacco; Klomín = Chlumín (in the Mělník region)

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The firefighters are on their way, they are carrying the fire engine, the chief is on the fire engine, let the poor work hard! fire engine = fire pump

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Move, my bones, I still have two at home, move, my bones, I still have others. 2. Kořalička, my aunt, often nudges me, kořalička, my aunt, often pushes me.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Pretty girl, pocem, mrtaráda dyjadá, I am learning to be a shoemaker, lala dyjadá. 2. I am learning to flirt, to love pretty girls. 3. When shoemakers got married, the horses’ hooves were startled. 4. Every mother is happy when her daughter gets married. 5. The daughter is even happier when a groom presses close to her.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Honza is carting manure for me, a black mare flew in, grabbed my Janek, my God! [The singer's song] was known from Slovaks who were working in the Lhotecký yard.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Honzíček from Horoušánek is kind, put hole after hole and lick his ass. I don't know the melody. The world-traveling fool Honzíček from Horoušánek used to sing this when he was teased. He played the violin.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I like Honzíček, he plays with me, I like Honzíček, he is kind. He likes me too, he tells me that a thousand times, I like Honzíček, he is kind.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The bagpipes played near the rogue, I heard them, they offered me a gardener, but I didn’t want him. And I preferred a tailor, that’s something better, he will sew me a corset made of some trimming. trimming, border, edging, braid

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. He used to come to us, he used to come, a clerk from the office, wearing a little hat tilted to the side like some kind of emperor. 2. In one hand an inkwell, in the other hand a pen, he wanted me to write him into my little heart. 3. If you were a young man, as I am a maiden, I would give you the wreath that I wear myself. 4. My little green wreath is jumping on my head, and your honesty is crying in the cradle. Variants from Zlosyně and Dřínov (1912, performer not specified):

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. I have a girl in Vienna, she sent me a letter saying she no longer has money for a skirt, and that I should send her some for it. 2. And I replied to her that I am completely broke, that I am looking for her all over Vienna, and I don’t have a single piece of pants.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I have a spinning wheel, the distaff won’t take for me, neither the distaff nor the flax, I’d rather lie down with you. I don’t know the tune.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I have a little bird in the forest, tradda, it brings me eggs, tradda, red, white, however I like, it never spoils, tradda.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. I have a thorn in my left leg, leg, leg, leg, I have a thorn in my left leg. Whoever wants to beat my thorn must dodge, I have a thorn in my left leg. 2. I have a thorn in my left leg. When I jump up, I will knock the thorn out of my leg, I have a thorn in my left leg.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Chrzín, without further identification 1. I have a German wife, she does not speak Czech. [...] 2. I have a German wife, she pees in the kitchen. I tell her every day: Wife, go pee outside! The melody was created based on one of Pleyel's duets.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I'm still wondering what kind of working hard this is, Pajmáma is dancing with Pantáta. Pajmáma with Pantáta, they are dancing nicely, Pajmáma with Pantáta, they are doing nicely. Trala lala lalala la lala lala lalala la louloulou louloulou, they are dancing nicely, louloulou louloulou, they are doing nicely.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. I am a fine lady, I love Franek, I don't want Pepík, he owes the shoemaker. 2. I prefer a tailor, that's something better, he will sew me cuffs, then I will be a stylish girl.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. I am a master seminarian, don’t worry about the fact that I am all black, beautiful girl. 2. At my parish, you will be lady, we’ll say that you are an aunt, and that will be the way to make love.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I had a boyfriend, I loved him, he came to see me in the pigsty, and a cow kicked him there.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I am an orphan, my dad ran away, my mom hanged herself in the hallway by her belly button.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. I am that carpenter journeyman, a cheerful fellow, I can say that. Achich ach alas yemin ach jeru ach jeru yemin.  
2. I fell deeply in love, Pepi left me and went to Vienna.  
3. As soon as I start cutting the boards, I hear Pepička crying.  
4. The other day, when I was boiling glue, someone knocked on my door.  
5. I heard a voice like Pepi’s, so I hurried outside to go out.  
6. It was our domestic poodle that bumped into the door.  
7. I went into the room sorrowful, the glue had all already run out.  
8. Either I will take my own life, or I will go after Pepička.  
Sung by Josef Řízek around the year 1870.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. How I blew Nanynka, blew, that I blew her ring, blew, I only blew it in jest, blew, I didn’t even look into her eyes, didn’t look.  
2. How I blew Kačenka, that I broke her window, she jumped at me like a rooster, I ran away and took my hat.  
3. How I blew the innkeeper, that I blew him out of the line, that’s why I blew him out of the line, so he blew me back into the line.  
4. How I blew the cook, that I blew her roast, I blew her roast, that’s why she acts like a spider on me.  
5. How I blew Pepička, that I blew her out of the hall, how I blew Pepička, that I blew her out of the hall.  
6. Then I blew my wife, that I blew her money, I blew her money and wasted it on beer.  

Sung according to the print *Cheerful Song Composed for the New Viennese Dance 1834.*  
In Prague at M. Gintl in Plateise.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. What kind of spice is fern, they will take you, my maiden, to Prague. They will take you, my dear, where you have never been, they will take you, my maiden, to Prague.  
2. What kind of spice is calamus, they will take you, my maiden, to Němec.  
3. What kind of spice is parsley, they will take you, my maiden, to Hungary.  

calamus = a marsh plant whose rhizomes are used in pharmacy and confectionery

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. I have never seen such a chaplain who would always wander around pubs until morning. 2. I have never seen such a priest who would hear girls' confessions in a room at the rectory.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Once you, once me, once our Bára, we had a water sprite painted in Prague.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The peasant went to plow, forgot to harness, the peasant went to plow, forgot to harness, forgot to harness. The accompanying voices sing nasally, occasionally pinching the nose with fingers. The solo singing is also muffled.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Just pour me beer, taverner, pour beer, pour beer, don’t mind that I’m young, that I’m young, that I’m young.  
2. Pour me a beer while it flows, while your profit doesn’t run away.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Káča was climbing the birch tree, the birch tree, the birch tree, I will climb there after her, climb.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Káča, Káča Kulichová, do you have the geese at home? The geese are in the damage, Káča is in the pub. Hajha hajha, black goose!  
2. Káča, Káča Kulichová, do you have the geese at home? The geese are in the barley, Káča, drive them out! Hajha hajha, black goose!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Káčo, stick out the rod, guests are coming, guests are coming, guests are coming to us!  
2. I won’t stick it out until they come, until they come to us.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Where are you rushing to like that, you’ll get out of breath, wait until spring, when the violet blooms. I’m rushing to Úpor for our Veruna, we will dance together.  
2. I prefer Bětuška, I want her for my wife, when she falls asleep at home, she thinks of me. When I want a kiss, she says: "In a little while! When our folks are asleep, I can give it to her."  
3. The host’s girls are real darlings, they know how to wash clothes, they know how to love. On Friday they do the laundry, on Saturday they iron, on Sunday they go to church.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Where is my home, Honza is carrying manure. Water is rushing in the glass, a sausage is rolling under the bench. Dad is beating Pepík, Pepík is sadly wailing, and he is shouting that it hurts, he is beating him with a thick stick, he is beating him on the butt.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Where there is a brewer, there is youth, there is also beer, a brewery. Where beer is brewed, things go well, where beer is brewed, things go well. [chorus] Let’s go there and drink it until the white morning, let’s go there and drink it until the white morning.

2. We drink beer to know if we understand each other, if we like each other, if we are friends.

3. To drink beer, to be merry, to have a nice girl in love, this, dear brothers, delights us every moment.

4. To drink beer, to live for the homeland, to properly respect language and king, that is our right, may Czechia be healthy!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. If I were a butcher, I would put a ladder to the window and knock on the little window: Give me tomorrow, Anynka!  
2. If you were Catholic, I would take you to my heart, but since you are pagan, I will not marry you.  
3. You don’t want to go to church, you don’t want to serve the Lord, you gladly go to the pub because you dance there.  
4. Play for me, you scoundrels, drive the old women out, drive them out of the pub, they are old creatures.  
5. They sit down on the benches, watch the young men, who is dancing with whom, who loves whom.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
When the feast is approaching, my Marjána and I are dressed up. Marjána has red stockings and I have leather pants. We come to the pub, no one is dancing yet, we dance in the middle of the circle, I turn around and shout: Oh! Who is spinning here? Our lord's servant. When I get up in the morning, the lead horse calls "huhu," I bring him food. I clean up, scoop out the manure, and think to myself: the whole sty is mine! The melody is unknown.  
podsední = the left horse or ox in a pair

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Kozomín 1911, informant not specified Boys, damn peasants, don’t you want to enlist, gentlemen, the best thing is to go to the army with you! Sung by Jan Louček around 1860.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The chimney sweep has a spot, spot, spot, the chimney sweep's wife does not, does not, does not. The chimney sweep needs it when he sweeps chimneys, the chimney sweep's wife does not, does not, does not.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: To whom, auntie, is better than how you are, than how you are, than how you are, you lie down in bed, uncle lies down with you, uncle lies down with you, uncle lies down with you.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. I bought a goat from a shoemaker, a damn goat, she doesn't want to be milked. 2. I grazed her in the moss, in a forest clearing, I watched her as she carried her tail. 3. As she carried her tail, she kept flicking it, a damn goat, she seemed to be sneezing. 4. That creature sneezed seven times, the damn goat had no kids.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: How would you get out, Janko, if your master caught you? I would make a door out of the window; I wouldn't wait for him to open it.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. The little goose was flying, flying from high up, she couldn't make it, she fell into the brook, fell into the brook. 2. She fell into the brook, drank the water so that the tavern keepers wouldn’t pour it into the beer. 3. Tavern keepers into beer, Jews into spirits, so that the girls from Chržín wouldn’t drink them.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Dřínov, without further identification 1. Left, right, tailor, shoemaker, these are two knights, one repairs rags, the other glues shoes. 2. Left, right, tailor, shoemaker, these are two knights, the shoemaker soiled his pants, the tailor cleans it up. left, right, German = left, right (commands)

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. My dear mother, sell the geese, buy me a tunic that suits me.  
2. My dear daughter, I won’t do that, I will not sell the geese just for your adornment!  
3. My old father, sell the oxen, buy me a hat full of ribbons.  
4. My dear daughter, I won’t do that, I will not sell the oxen just for your adornment!  
5. My dear mother, sell the fox skins, buy me shoes full of bows.  
6. My dear daughter, I won’t do that, I will not sell the fox skins just for your adornment!  
7. My dear father, sell the horses, buy me dresses that are for me.  
8. My dear daughter, I won’t do that, I will not sell the horses just for your adornment!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: My dear Líbr, Líbr Augustýn, I will not let you go home, home, my dear Líbr, Líbr Augustýn, I will not let you go. You are an old drunkard, you smell of booze, my dear Líbr, Líbr Augustýn, I will not let you go.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Mařenka and Pepíček love each other, they lie down together in one bed.  
2. Mařenka and Pepíček love each other, whatever anyone needs, they give each other everything.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Farewell, old rag, farewell, old basket. You used to be worth four kreuzers, but now you are worth two groschen.  
2. No one knows what iron is, no one knows what a ring is. A ring is iron, my dear Tereza, no one knows what a ring is.  
3. No one knows what Domažlice are, no one knows what Taus is. Taus is German, Domažlice is Czech, no one knows what Taus is.  
4. No one knows what a shooting range is, no one knows what šísštot is. Šísštot is a shooting range for our sharpshooters, no one knows what šísštot is. Sung around 1894 at Chržín.  
šísštot, from German = military shooting range; šorfšic, from German = sharpshooter.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. The grandmother had four apples and the little boy only two, give me one apple, grandma, we will have the same. 2. The little boy had a little coat and the grandmother a skirt: Come on, little boy, to the mazurka, just for a little while! 3. One old woman from Babylov, the other again from Třešť, they threw them into the pond, the old women are still there.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I had a husband named Martin, I sent him to Rome. May God preserve him for me, so that an accident doesn't catch him!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The girl swept the little room, the girl swept the hall and found a white scarf there, she showed it off. The householder looks out the window: Hey, girl, hey, what do you find in my little house, it’s not proper for a girl, just give it back here, hey, just give it back here!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Myslík's mare walked as long as she could. When she couldn't, she lay down, he pulled her by the hair. 2. Come on, little mare, come home, I'll give you a quarter of an oat cake, the little mare agreed, then he didn't give her anything.  
štvrc = a hollow measure, a quarter of a vetel (from German viertel)

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Hunters, fellows, you caught a doe last year yesterday at dawn in the wheat.  
2. That's a lie, don't believe it! It wasn't a doe, it was my doll yesterday, today.  
3. Peasants, to work! Go to the corvée, plow the lord's field, peasants.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. On Mount Tábor at the farmer's, the girls were fighting over a soldier. 2. My dear maidens, don’t fight, here are some pants, share them. 3. One fought, ran away, the other cries that she has nothing.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: We’ll go to the hops, we’ll pick them, sixpence for the spindle, a girl to bed, we’ll sleep with her.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. She stood on the threshold, thorn thorn thorn thorn thorn thorn, she seemed pretty, thorn thorn thorn thorn thorn thorn, I gave her a little kiss, thorn thorn thorn thorn thorn thorn, she laughed, thorn thorn thorn. 2. As soon as she stepped off the threshold, she was very frightened, that they were taking her to the army, she told me with tears. 3. If they take her, they take her, they will take me too, I have to, my dear, part with you.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: On that bridge grows a carnation, on that bridge grows oat. When the oat grows up, Ančička will grow up, on that bridge grows oat.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. On that green meadow, deer are grazing, there is a hunter in a green vest herding them. 2. Wait for me, my dear, I will shoot a deer for you so that you will like the green vest. 3. Wait for me on the meadow, I will shoot a hare for you so that you will like the hunter's cap.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Over our little house, a little dove is flying, I didn’t know that my dear would leave me. 2. Whether he leaves or not, I won’t send a message for him, I tie up such boys with garters. 3. I don’t tie, I don’t tie, but I will tie, that I, my Pepíček, will do out of spite. Dance song.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. I talked myself into having a girl who works at the rectory; I couldn't go to see her there because of the pastor. But I still went there, carrying my shoes in my hand, gently, quietly knocking on the window.  
2. When I was coming back from my dear one, it was raining heavily; I fell into a ditch there, which upset me. I dirtied my shoes, vest, coat, and pants, and so I went from lovemaking as if from work.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Drink, little brother, drink, drink deeply, maybe we won’t meet from today for a year. 2. Drink, little brother, drink, drink even more, maybe we won’t meet from today for a month. 3. Drink, little brother, drink, drink every day, maybe we won’t meet from today for a week. The young men sang in the pub.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Our old man with the old woman are dancing the shuffling dance, hey stomp stomp, shuffling dance, hey stomp stomp, the ball!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Our dad keeps pushing me: Franta, get to work! But I have no desire, I don’t feel like it.  
2. It was easy for dad, he took mom, and I’m supposed to take another, strange maiden.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Our dad praises mom for burning little firewood, our dad praises mom for burning the fence!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Our dad went mushroom picking, he will definitely get lost there, get lost. He won't get lost, he is in the forest, he will bring us mushrooms!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Our Káča has something, she hid it in the hay, and when it settles, she will explain it to us.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Our Káča is crying, what is wrong with her, what is wrong with her, what is wrong with her? Thieves stole her skirt, thieves, thieves, thieves. 2. They also stole her shoes so she wouldn’t go to the music. 3. They also stole her clogs so she wouldn’t go with the soldiers.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Our mother is a mother, and I am her son. She had a bag of shajny, and I carried them for her. When I confessed this, the priest told me that if she still had twenty-kreuzer coins, I should take those as well. Shajny, from German (Einlösungsscheine) = paper money, so-called Vienna currency, used from 1811 to 1816.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Our Mary has a little one, a little one, a little one, our Mary has a little one, a little sheep, bébébé. 2. When she went to school, the sheep ran after her, a little sheep. 3. When Mary is to get married, the sheep mourn for her, an old sheep.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Don't choose, Pepíček, one who has a dirty skirt, choose, Pepíček, one who has a lace skirt.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Something for the onion, something for the horseradish, we will pay for some, we will barter for some.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. God forbid that I should die before I pay what I owe here.  
2. To the innkeeper for a drink, and to Mařenka for a kiss.  
3. To the innkeeper for a beer, and to Mařenka for a little heart.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I don't want to, I don't want to, I don't like you at all, I don't want to, I don't want such a rascal. I don't want to, I don't want to, I don't like you at all, I don't want to, I don't want you to come to me.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Don’t come, boy, don’t come to us, I don’t like to see you in a torn coat, in a torn coat, I am ashamed of you.  
2. Come to us rather on Sunday, when you have a dress, I will gladly watch over you, the lady of the house will too.  
3. When I have a dress, I won’t come to you either, I’ll talk myself into one who gives money.  
4. When you have a dress, my dear Pepíček, we will also often go to the little grove.  
5. A torn-down cottage made of modern bricks, if you don’t want to love me, then kiss my ass!  
6. A torn-down cottage, the wind blows into it, pigs are dancing there, the swine are watching them.  
7. I have a little wife, her name is Barka, I will take her to the fair on Saint Mark’s Day.  
8. If I don’t sell her there, I will take her back, I will take her to Prague on Jewish Fridays.  
9. Buy, Jews, buy her, I’ll make a deal with you, you give me a bit more, I give you a bit less!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: She didn’t want salad or a meat patty with oil, with oil, she tied her dress, she wanted to wander with Matěj, with Matěj. meat patty (colloquial) = meatball

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The most beautiful human life is the maltster’s life, it is the maltster’s life. Only there does the work succeed, where good beer is brewed, where good beer is brewed. The mug always full of divine yeast, a joy to watch the merry crew, noisy in the taproom, cheerful jest, smoke and mist pierce through.  
2. The axe goes for Pepík: Get up, Pepé, into youth, loosen up a little more, here’s the key from the foreman. The young man jumps up, rubs his eyes, runs to the cellar, fills the mug, what was behind, then quickly forward, he would spend the world.  
3. A girl went for yeast, they send her to the brewhouse, the brewmaster is said to give it to her, he likes pretty girls. The girl stands shyly behind the barrel, “Sir brewmaster, could you give the better yeast?” He took her by the chin, pressed a kiss on her, had better gain with it.  
4. And that malt-drier like a fierce dragon, uncombed, unwashed, often thirsty in the throat, despite working hard. He likes best to enjoy caraway schnapps in the small room, he has his flask for that devil, don’t be surprised at him, he would drink like a wolf, his throat is pitch-coated.  
5. Observe the friend with the green from Gotvald, the Stachovka is from Czech Pilsen, what a handsome young man he is. Wherever he wanders, he turns the malt mill, pays excise and gets more beer, cheerfully raising a full tray: Hail Gambrinus!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Someone is coming to us, your venerable lordship. Just, sir lord, only to my no, I will not allow that.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Don't look, Pepíček, at the little woman, she has skirts down to the ground. And when there's dew, she goes barefoot, she gets her skirts all wet.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: I do not grind, I do not grind, I do not grind, I do not grind, the water took our mill. It took all our wheels, even the saw with the water troughs, I do not grind, I do not grind, I do not grind, I do not grind, the water took our mill. Water troughs, from German = wooden water chute, trough, tubs.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Don't worry, my wife, about how we will make a living, we will kill mosquitoes, we will cook meat.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Don’t stand, young men, under the windows, better go into the room, if your little feet hurt, sit on the benches. [chorus] Brandejs, Brandejs, he’s the one I like the most, Brandejs, Brandejs, he’s the one I like. 2. I took an old grandmother, I thought she had something, but she has a rooster in the pantry and it crows at her.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
