translation: Don’t knock, don’t knock, our people are not lying down yet, don’t knock, don’t knock, our people are not lying down yet. If you keep knocking for a long time, the lady will ask: Who is it, who is it, that won’t let us have peace?

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. She didn’t know where she had it, until her mother told her, she didn’t know where she had it, until her mother told her where she had it, a skirt with lace, lalala, where she has it, where she has it, a skirt with lace.  
2. Girl, you are a fool, you even have it embroidered on your body with a red thistle! Sometimes in the first verse it is sung only up to the word “told,” and in the second verse “you have it right on your ass” is sung instead. Everything else is sung as “lala.” There is supposedly a third verse as well, but it didn’t quite fit the melody: One bear, two bears, / they were of the same fur, / she didn’t know where she had it, / she held it in her fist.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. It doesn't matter how well the raftman dances, look in the stable how he grooms the horses, look in the stable how he grooms the horses. 2. The little horses are dirty, the bedding is torn, let's take a look at what kind of lad he is.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Oh Velvary, oh Velvary, where are my tolars? I ate, I drank, I feasted, I loved pretty girls, oh Velvary, oh Velvary, where are my tolars?  
2. Oh Roudnice, oh Roudnice, where are my thousands? I ate, I drank, I feasted, I loved pretty girls, oh Roudnice, oh Roudnice, there are my thousands!  
3. Oh Melnik, oh Melnik, you have them in your bag. Where they drink good wine, tolars roll around, oh Melnik, oh Melnik, you have them in your bag.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Eight skittles, the ninth king, I only bet once, I already won. 2. I won Mařenka the grumbler, she has a curly little head. bublnatý = one who grumbles, a grumbler

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Master shoemaker bought a cage, put a sparrow in it, it died in a drop.  
2. Master shoemaker bought a cage, put a canary in it, it died by morning.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

6. Pepi- and Pepino, marry a cooper! The cooper keeps banging, banging, usually on credit, I don’t want to and I won’t marry a cooper.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

If she left the boys, she would rinse chestnuts, and give the water from the chestnuts to the cows in the trough. 3. A soldier comes into the room, stands like a candle, and the farmer, a scoundrel, stands in the corner like a beggar. 6. jug = a two-handled earthenware container for milk; chestnuts (figuratively) = a piece of dried mud or dung on cattle, but also on a person’s backside, etc.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

translation:

translation:

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The little dove from the oak flew to the dove, dear one by the ponds, there the little horses graze, I will go there to him.  
2. The gentle little dove pecks with the little dove, why shouldn’t we peck like that too, when we are young, oh.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Mountains, valleys, deserted forest, mountains, valleys, deserted forest, I have not seen my maiden yet today, not yet today.  
2. Whoever has seen her, tell me, please comfort my saddened heart.  
3. I have seen her, but I will not tell, she was picking rosemary in the garden.  
4. She picked two roses there, she placed them into Pepíček’s bed.  
5. He will sleep well for you, he will come to thank you tomorrow morning.  
6. Thank you, thank you, my little hat edged, I doff it.  
7. Do not doff it in front of me, for they will take you away tomorrow morning.  
8. They will take you to Prague, there they will give you a black coat and a saber.  
9. They will take you to Brno, there they will give you a blue coat and a whip.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Cemetery, cemetery, green garden, the most precious seeds are falling into you. They fall, they fall, but do not grow, maybe the gravediggers plant them deep.  
2. I planted rue with broad leaves, maybe my dear mother will rest at the cemetery. The rue has already withered, the wind has carried away the leaves, my dear mother still sleeps there.  
3. If I knew I could raise my mother, I would carry soil from the cemetery in my lap. I would carry, carry until I brought her out, then I would know that I had a mother.  
4. Rise, mother, I am going to marry, rise, mother, and give your blessing. Rise and go up to the high rock, there you will see how they are weaving wreaths for us.  
5. They weave, weave, weave in four knots, so that the priest may tie our little hands. If they do not tie them for us, we will tie them ourselves, then it will be the end of love, of loving.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Husinec little church, carara -íčku, carara -íčku, you stand on a nice little hill, carara -íčku, carara -íčku.  
2. Maidens come out of you, they are like maidens from heaven.  
3. The first came out all white, and that was my beloved.  
4. The second came out all black, and that was the unfaithful one.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. She used to kneel by the chapel, at the glass doors of the monastery, she looked too deeply into the eyes of the young priest.  
2. Then her heart was gripped by unease and a secret sorrow gnawed at her soul, she went to confess her guilt, the priest confessed beautifully.  
3. The priest spoke about faith, made the sign of the cross, you, girl, great sinner, come more often to prayer.  
4. And the girl, very devout, whom the priest watched over closely, until she was quite saintly, then he confessed her again.  
5. They knelt together for a long time, soaking the ground with their tears, now they speak of love together, and not of any hope.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I do not know what troubles me, I perish in distress, love without hope will wear me out alive.  
2. It seemed to me, it seemed very often, that my beloved has another and does not love me.  
3. When I walk along the path he used to take and told me, an unhappy girl, about love.  
4. When I walk at night, I will dig a grave for myself, there I will lay my body, worn out by love.  
5. The leaves turn yellow, turn yellow, dry and fall down, and I, an unhappy girl, must go to the grave.  
6. Perhaps the grave is not as terrible as your love, love without hope will bury me alive.  
7. When I lie sleeping in the grave, soak in the green grass, remember me often, the innocent victim.  
8. When your heart moves, you will understand as I do that it is better to sleep in the grave than to suffer unfaithful love.  
9. Farewell in the world, enjoy joy, I will pray for you in eternity.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: As I said, I will do, I will stop making love, because it leads to nothing but gossip. If I were to stop by with Andulka and say a word or two, people would immediately gossip that I go to see her.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I will never again go to the spring for water, to the spring for a little water in the evening by the moonlight.  
2. They stood there behind the bushes, who they were, I will not say, they spoke in a dark voice, I heard kissing.  
3. My heart ached, sorrow was settling, my sorrow grips my heart, strength has left me.  
4. But I will forsake you, my dear, but I will regret that love for you even in the grave.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The path to Libeň is well-trodden, my beloved walked it, crying. Pepíček walked it too, longing, caught up with Andulka, howling.  
2. Why do you howl, my Pepíček, you do not yet know my love? Come to us, Pepíček, on Sunday, I will weave you a green wreath.  
3. Pepíček did not come on Sunday, the green wreath withered. It did not wither, it did not wither, it fell off, because my Pepíček deceived me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Where have the times gone, the times I lived in my youth, when I wove a wreath of little roses into your black hair?  
2. I wove a wreath of little roses, you plucked the flowers from me, it's a pity for your beauty that you allowed yourself to be deceived.  
3. We went out for a walk into the green grove and there we made love until the white morning.  
4. And when we woke up, the dew hung above us, my dearest of all was crying, lamenting.  
5. Do not cry, do not lament yourself, we will go home together, if I have wronged you, what does it matter to anyone.  
6. I always liked your name, Mařenka, now the times have changed for me, I am already a mother.  
7. Among maidens, I must not go, I am rejected by them, young men take it as a joke that I am a woman without a husband.  
8. Remember me, my dear, when you leave the music, and I will remember you when I leave the military training.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A red apple was rolling, rolling, who will get you, my golden Ančička?  
2. Two were rolling, rolling facing each other, who would I get to, if not, Jeníček, to you?  
3. Who would I get to, if not, my dear, to you, you are handsome, I am too, we will fit together.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Lidulinka was bathing in the cold water, Jeníček was riding that way, Jeníček was riding that way on a beautiful black horse. 2. What, Lidunka, what are you doing here in the cold water? I am weaving wreaths of green rosemary for Jeníček.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The road to Prague is long, lined with trees, planted by my Andulka when she used to walk here.  
2. She planted it with rosemary, fine white clover, as a sign of our love, that we would not belong to others.  
3. Andulka went for a walk, Jeníček stood under the windows, calling to his lady to come and look.  
4. Dear lady, you are the cause of this, how can my heart not ache when I see her with another.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Lasale, Lasale, I love you gladly, Lasale, Lasale, I like you gladly. I look after you, that I like you gladly, that I like you gladly.  
2. Don’t look after me, your little eyes will fall out, you should have looked when you were a virgin.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A little goose was flying, flying from high up, it couldn't make it, it fell into the brook, it fell into the brook.  
2. A little goose was flying, flying to the field, where my joy used to be.  
3. The sun is setting, setting behind the mountain, how could I ever forget all of you?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The little goose was flying, flying from above, the little goose was flying, flying from above, tell me, my dear, sweet Andulka, if you would want me.  
2. I would want you, but your people do not want me, because your mother is highly proud.  
3. She is highly proud, leading herself upwards, and I am a girl of a poor mother.  
4. Take a richer one and I a poor one, and so we will forget each other.  
5. Do not worry, my dear, my old mother, she will not say a single bad word to you.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The pigeons flew, they flew over our orchard, the one who loved me faithfully, he no longer loves me, the one who loved me faithfully, he no longer loves me.  
2. When the moon shone, footsteps were heard under the windows, there my blue-eyed sweetheart always waited for me.  
3. The moon no longer shines, I do not hear his voice, the one who loved me faithfully, he no longer loves me.  
4. The moon has set, the stars floated behind it, my cheeks have paled from sorrow, my sweetheart is far away.  
5. When we said goodbye in our garden, we both talked about never meeting again.  
6. When we said goodbye, we shook hands, be happy with your lover, you deceitful boy.  
7. Never love me, never have moments of longing, may a tear never glisten again in your eye.  
8. Because you will never know how betrayal and deceit hurt, be happy with your lover, the one whose heart she chooses.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oak leaf, don’t fall into the water, the water would take you, it would be a pity for you, don’t fall into the water.  
2. Oak leaf, don’t fall into the water, we won’t get married, we’ll go after girls only when there are draftees.  
3. The draft will come soon, they’re recruiting for the army, whoever has blue eyes, whoever has blue eyes, they will be drafted immediately.  
4. I have blue eyes, I won’t go there, I have a girl at home, a pretty sweetheart, I am going to get married.  
5. We will have a cradle made for the children, we will have it made, we will have a stick made for the wife.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Parting, parting, what a hard thing it is when you have to say goodbye, when a young lad has to say goodbye to a maiden.  
2. When we parted, we both cried, both of us wiping our eyes into one, into a handkerchief, into a white one.  
3. When I go through the forest, I will cry aloud, I will tilt my hat to the side, forget my beloved, and be cheerful again.  
4. When we parted under the green pear tree, I always told you that love is not constant, that I would become a nun.  
5. I will become a nun, you will become a priest, I will confess to you, profess my love in church before God.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In vain, girl, you grow pale, you suffer for love, he will not return to you anymore, you long for him in vain.  
2. Don’t think about him, he doesn’t deserve it, imagine another boy, forget about him.  
3. Forget about him, that’s easy to say, but when my little heart doesn’t want to forget.  
4. The Libeň stream, the one that flows down, it is the witness of our love, now of my sorrow.  
5. Tall maples stand sadly in the field, when I look at them sadly, my little heart aches.  
6. My little heart aches, great sorrow torments me, take me, mother, take me in your arms.  
7. Don’t cry, daughter, you’re still young, your fortune has not yet rung its bell.  
8. My head aches, there is a sharp pain near my heart. Oh God, my dear God, my daughter is not breathing.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Farewell in that house, where a maple tree grows, farewell to you all there, my path to you has grown over.  
2. Farewell, my beloved, keep your love and give it to whoever you want, I say goodbye to you, and thank you for the love.  
3. You have ended our love, you have chosen another, your love is fickle, inclined to everyone.  
4. A bluish flower blooms, it introduced me to my beloved, it blooms blue and lovely, my farewell is painful.  
5. When we say goodbye to each other, two hearts will grieve, two hearts, four eyes will cry day and night.  
6. When you leave us in the evening, where will you spend the night? Somewhere in the forest on the grass, your little head is cut.  
7. Bring me to the doctor, so he can heal my head, to such a doctor who heals in an hour.  
8. When I die in my youth, bury me quietly, with this inscription on my grave: Here lies one wounded by love.  
9. The heart is wounded by love, laid in the grave, it is better to sleep quietly in the grave than to love falsely.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: I liked you, blue-eyed girl, I bought a spotted ox. We will go to the field, we will carry almonds, we will forget about ourselves.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Blue eyes, why are you crying, you will not be mine. You will not and must not, it’s useless to think of me.  
2. My poor mother was struggling, she raised me with difficulty. She rocked me, cared for me, and would have given her life for me.  
3. My father even poorer, he loved blue eyes. He loved blue eyes so much, it’s a pity for them a thousand times.  
4. My mother gave me blue eyes, left me an orphan. A poor little orphan, and unhappier than all others.  
5. At the market square, there under the arcade, my beloved is betrothed to another. I would go to him, but the path is bad, it rains by day, and it’s dark by night.  
6. I went to him through the alleys, two little stars were shining for me. One bright, the other dark, my beloved is angry with me.  
7. And you, dark one, brighten up, my darling, make peace with me. How can I make peace with you if you do not want to love me?  
8. I will now go away into the world, but where will I stay overnight? Somewhere in the forest on a little patch of grass, there I will lay my head.  
9. Who will heal my little head? It hurts so much for you. It hurts so much, what have you done? You never loved me.  
10. If you loved a poor girl, you would say, boy, that I would be yours. Then at the altar you would promise, that forever you want to love me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: My dear old Pepíček, no one wants me for you, no one loves me for you, they point their finger at me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. In the mountains, in the valleys, at that deserted castle, little Pepíček is grazing horses in the little meadow. 2. He grazes them, grazes, until he shivers with cold, his most beloved brings him breakfast. 3. She brings it to him, brings it in a red scarf, take it, little Pepíček, for your love.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. At the ball, music was playing, everyone was having fun, a maiden stood by the window, her heart was aching.  
2. Suddenly she looked back, saw the stars, and two hot tears flowed from her eyes.  
3. Then her mother approached her and quietly said: He will not fall from the sky for you, why do you trouble your heart?  
4. Then the maiden wept with a sorrowful voice: I did not know we would part so soon.  
5. Oh, they took him from me, took him, from a young girl, and placed him into another's cold embrace.  
6. So we loved each other in our little garden, and the nightingale above us sweetly sang about love.  
7. Oh, it is all over now, over our love, the path is overgrown with grass leading to our meeting place.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. A small farmer from Svatá Hora has a mare, he has a pretty daughter, God, give her to me. 2. If I got her, I would rejoice, I would rejoice to have a pretty wife.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. On that lord's meadow, the grass is green, my dear is grazing there, my dear is grazing there, my dear is grazing a deer.  
2. And that was not a deer, it was a hind, wait for me, my dear, for three years.  
3. To wait for three years, I could get married and properly raise a little son.  
4. Properly raise him for the president lord, so he could serve him even to his grave.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In that field near Jičín, there is nice greenery, there lies Pepíček, a handsome young lad, in the green rosemary.  
2. Go to him, my dear, ask him what he is doing. His little head hurts, handsome Pepíček, he needs a doctor.  
3. Two doctors arrived, they knew the problem immediately, just a young girl, just for a moment – and he will be healed.  
4. My dear, when she heard that, she ran to him at once, took his hand, gave him a little kiss, and so she healed him.  
5. Stay here, my dear, stay here until morning, we will sleep together and make love until the white morning.  
6. Before we sleep together, we have to get married in the Jičín rectory by the priest, who will tie our hands with a stole.  
7. When our hands were tied, people did not wish us well, that we would be sincere spouses until our end.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Over Prague, the little moon rises gracefully, on the way the graceful girl is walking. 2. She walks, she walks, constantly turning around to see if her sweetheart is returning from work. 3. Yesterday he promised me that he would come to me today, so I would wait for him until the little moon rises. 4. The little moon has already risen, the sweetheart did not come, God, grant me awareness of where he might have gone. 5. Perhaps he liked some other girl, God, grant me awareness of who that might have been?

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Drink, my dear, drink the wine, you must not wear the wreath, you must not wear the wreath, you will have a son.  
2. You must not wear the wreath, you are not worthy of it, you used to stand with the soldiers until the white dawn.  
3. I will go to church, I will stand on a stone, my green wreath burns like a flame.  
4. It burns like a flame, painted lad, we are far apart, so we could talk.  
5. The dragoon is shoeing the horse and packing the saddle, my dear at home wrings her hands.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Do not wait, my dear, for your cavalier, do not wait, my dear, for your cavalier, he lies under the tree, he lies under the tree, he lies under the tree stabbed by a sword.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. There are not and will not be as many little stars as my lady has given me kisses. There are not and will not be, as long as the world stands, as many little stars.  
2. There are not and will not be as many black crows as the blows I received for my dear.  
3. There are not and will not be as many monasteries as the nights my lady has given me lodging.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Libeň 1909, without melody, informant not specified Unfortunate magazine, that I forced into Kolín. And from Kolín to Hradec, oh my dear, fall in, fall in, turn around, my darling.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Oh, how nice it is, two blacksmiths in the town, two blacksmiths in the market square. One can forge, the other can love Šafář's Nanynka, one can forge, the other can love Šafář's Nanynka.  
2. Yesterday, Šafář's daughter from the courtyard sent me a message that she will embroider a black silk scarf in a circle, in a wheel pattern.  
3. I will go there too and ask her to embroider it for me because my little heart is completely saddened, so she can comfort it.  
4. What pained me most was the peacock feather that I tore apart, even more painful was my disappointment that I angered it.  
5. Water flows in leaps by our windows, I cannot stop it, I angered my own joy, I cannot calm it down.  
6. The Podskalí gang, oh God, what a beauty, they wished us freedom so that we could go, that we could go to the riverbank for water.  
7. The Podskalí water is very good, it is cold as ice, we gave each other false kisses, they were as sweet as honey.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. On Christmas, long nights, it’s good to sleep with a girl, you can’t hear the cuckoos, only the wind blows. Blow, little wind, from the Danube, where is my beloved? Let him come here, let him come here, we will love each other until the break of day.  
2. Oh my dear mother, my head hurts, go get me a doctor, let him heal it. Go get me a doctor, let him bind my little head. You, my dearest of all, I want to die.  
3. Don’t die, my dear, you are still young, your lady promised you to me. Whether she promised or not, what do I care, scoundrel, about you, I have another, more sincere one than just you.  
4. When they carry you on the bier, I will not cry. When they put you in the grave, I will laugh at you. But God in heaven will punish you, for you have taken from me my wreath, my little wreath, my green one, I still don’t have it.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The hour struck, the second will strike, Pepíček is still wandering the street.  
2. Stop, Pepíček, stop wandering, and I will open the door for you.  
3. Open the painted door, Mařenko, and I will see who lies with you.  
4. Pepíček does not lie here, no one lies here anymore, where you used to lie, the place is empty.  
5. Carpenters built scaffolding for the masons, where did my pleasure go?  
6. My pleasure, you have blue eyes, and I am afraid you will deceive me.  
7. Do not be afraid, Mařenko, of my deception, God would not give us any blessing.  
8. No blessing on our little farm, that I deceived a poor girl.  
9. A poor girl has to endure much, take her, Pepíček, so she won’t have to serve.  
10. I will not take her, my parents will not allow it, as my family is of high standing.  
11. Of high standing, carried high, many a maiden just trembles at me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. A road leads around Vožice, and on it green, nice wheat is growing. The wheat is green, my darling is angry, yesterday she was very angry with me.  
2. I walked back and forth here at night many times, once I saddened my heart. I saw my darling, the dove of my heart, standing with her friend Pepíček.  
3. They were talking together there, as if doing this out of spite towards me. She took him by the hand, led him to the little room, that’s how they were doing it out of spite towards me.  
4. Just don’t do spiteful things to me, just make love to me before sleep. I’ll jump on my horse, twirl my little scarf, and I won’t see you, false one, anymore.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Water flows around Vysočany, have a drink, Pepíček, it is good. I don't want any more, I've had enough, thank you, Mařenka, for your kindness.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Write a letter, mother, write to my beloved that I still send my regards, that I must go to the grave.  
2. She wrote a very sorrowful letter to her darling, informing him that she must no longer speak to him.  
3. But tell me honestly, what troubles you so much that your little heart will not let you sleep day or night?  
4. Secret love, that troubles me, that will not let me sleep, because I must not say that I am your beloved.  
5. Unfaithful one, false one, miserable liar of the world, that I trusted you in the moonlight!  
6. My mother often scolded me for you, that I, so very young, let myself be deceived.  
7. Do not remember the times when we stood together, our love is over, the end of our loving.  
8. Stop peeping, you cuckoo, in the green thicket, I say farewell for the last time to the unfaithful darling.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: Wait, I’ll tell you, you were begging me, wait, I’ll tell you, you wanted from me: a little rose in the garden, a little kiss under the windows, wait, I’ll tell you, you wanted from me.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation: 1. Under the Šumava hillside, nicely painted, violets grow there, blue as clouds, I will tell you about them. 2. They grow there behind the bushes, I will tell you about them, if you love me, or a friend, that I do not know yet.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The crib is made, the patient lies in it, three doctors stand by her, treating her illness.  
2. Then one of the three doctors shrugged his shoulders, you're a lovesick virgin, there is no help for you.  
3. Send a black coffin here, lay me into it, place my slender white body inside it.  
4. Fetch my lover for me, my darling, so he may come to visit his patient.  
5. Oh, my dear patient, your lover spoils you, to make you give him peace, that he does not love you.  
6. Send me a painter, I will have him paint me, so when my heart aches, I can look at him.  
7. Oh, my dear patient, your painter sends word that he says today is a great holiday, that he will not paint today.  
8. Give me large candles here, place them by my head, my black shiny hair, brush it for me.  
9. And in that one moment, the doors flew open, step back, you three doctors, nothing hurts me anymore.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I shed tears for you, that you do not love me, I carry sorrow in my heart that you are distancing yourself from me.  
2. The sun has stopped shining, your love has ceased, I must not ask for your hand, it is already promised to another.  
3. The days have passed, those I longed for, gone are the beautiful dreams in which I found bliss.  
4. Oh, away and away with everything, away is my hope, now I weep like a child, what good will it do me?  
5. You are not moved, your heart is stone, it drives me to despair, it torments my heart.  
6. If only you knew a little of the pain in my heart, surely you would forever banish your cruelty.  
7. But you constantly seek a reason to part, and you won’t even see me to my grief.  
8. No one loved you as faithfully as I did, you were my heaven, this I saw myself.  
9. I thought of you by day, also by night, I sought help from you in my suffering.  
10. So farewell now, remember me too, I say for the last time that my promise was not a lie.  
11. Be happy in this world, enjoy your joys, if happiness blooms for you, I am without sorrow.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Why does the Jizera so sadly murmur, a road winds around it, along which a young man walks thoughtful, his heart is very pained.  
2. Gradually he stops, takes paper and pencil out of his pocket, begins to write a sad letter, it must be given to my beloved.  
3. He wrote a short but sad letter, in which he says farewell to his beloved, why does cruel fate forbid our love, that you may never be mine.  
4. And I, sad, unfortunate, do not know where the cold waves will carry me, whether they will throw me onto the shore or carry me further and further away.  
5. When I shall rest in the cold grave, let a beautiful forget-me-not be planted, in memory of our faithful love, that I had a faithful beloved.  
6. When I shall rot in the cold grave, offer the Our Father for me there, in memory of our faithful love, that I was your faithful lover.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. In front of the neighbors by the brook, geese are fighting, take it, Jeník, take the gun, shoot one, take it, Jeník, take the gun, shoot one.  
2. I won't kill those geese, I know them well, they are the lady's geese, where I usually go.  
3. If I killed one of them, she would be angry, and if I came there afterwards, she would be upset.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. Dear daddy, he takes care of us, he works hard from spring to spring, from spring to spring.  
2. Dear mommy washes our shirts, she rarely has a moment of peace from work.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. I bid you farewell, my dear lover, I press you to my chest for the last time, I press you to my chest for the last time.  
2. The days of my life are shortening, I will mourn for you in a foreign land.  
3. Ah, I do not know, I do not know if I will return, if I will see the lost land again.  
4. Before the flocks of stars rise, I will mourn for you in the trenches.  
5. Oh, believe me, believe me, my dear lover, that I have no other girl to love.  
6. I bid you farewell, I say goodbye to you, I bid you farewell, until we meet again.

label: MILOSTNÉ
translation:
1. The maiden sits by the lake in the shade, her little head resting in her lap, her little head bowed down to her breasts, where a withered flower once was long ago.  
2. She takes the withered flower in her hand, a painful sigh tears from her breast, oh God, where is he who loved me, who always promised me faithful love?  
3. He went to a distant land, leaving behind a wounded heart, he said, before the first frost seizes the flower, he would return home from the army.  
4. The harsh frosts have long passed, from the bud the rose has bloomed, the forget-me-not's flower has long withered, my beloved does not return home from the army.  
5. Black clouds, float across the sky, greet my darling out there in the distance, may the angel hurry to him on wings, give him a sweet kiss from me.  
6. I know I sin when I wail so sadly, what am I to do when I know no counsel? Oh, who will comfort this troubled mind when I am so lonely in the world?  
7. The rose in the garden is still protected by thorns, so that no one can easily reach it, but no one in the world can save me anymore, no rose blooms in the garden for me.

label: MILOSTNÉ

3. The clock on the tower struck eleven when my rascal was coming home from his sweetheart.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I am sleeping peacefully, you must let me sleep, and when I wake up, you must give me coffee.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I come home, I knock on the window, my wife asks me how it got wet there. It got quite wet there, I am quite wet because I was waxing the pine cone all night.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When the first shoemaker was born, boom and boomadra, the whole world got angry, boom and boomadra. And the devils in hell were shouting how they betrayed the shoemaker, boom and boomadra, boom and boomadra.  
2. I went there to have shoes made, he said he couldn’t make them, that he couldn’t make them because he had ugly leather.  
3. And I started at him abruptly, saying I must have them by tomorrow. The shoemaker was immediately frightened, and sat straight on a chair.  
4. And then he sees a pig from the window, remembered that he has no bristles, and rushes out the window, sits on the pig.  
5. With one hand he holds the pig, with his teeth he tears out its bristles. [...]  
6. I am the constable’s pig! Even if it was the steward’s pig, I must have the bristles so I can make shoes.  
7. Then the pigs got angry about how they would ruin the shoemaker; when he stands under the window, they will eat him with the chair.  
8. When shoemakers meet a boar, they tip their hats to him: Hail to you, only boar, you give us bristles.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When the shoemakers got married, none of us were at the wedding—not the woman, nor I, nor our godmother.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When everyone got married, I will still wait, I will still wait until my hair turns gray, then I will throw it to the cats, then I will throw it to the cats.  
2. When everyone got married, I will get married too, I will find myself a nice boy, I will give him a kiss.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: When I was working at the brewery, I had a sweetheart from the office. Aunt troubled me, tormented me day and night to give him up to her. Why did I do it?

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When I was walking a narrow path, a rye harvester, I met there a pretty maiden, a rye harvester. I immediately began to ask her, rye harvester, where is the way to Perštát, ha ha.  
2. Stay on my right hand and go over there to that alley. Be careful, just ask about the green house.  
3. When we came to the pub, we had the beer poured, the young barmaid was there, she poured us the beer.  
4. My mother used to say that I shouldn't want a soldier, and I don't want a farmer, I prefer a soldier.  
5. A soldier comes into the inn, stands like a candle, and the farmer scoundrel stands in the corner like a beggar.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

[Chorus]  
When I went to Prague for peas, for peas,  
a mule fell on the road, a mule.  
If you had fed it oats, oats,  
it would have brought you to Prague, brought you.  

1. The gardener went to the garden with a hoe, with a hoe,  
dug up a big rosemary there, big, big.  

2. The rosemary smelled so lovely,  
to make you, my maiden, happy.  

3. It wasn’t rosemary, it was horseradish,  
get rid of it, gardener, out the window!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: If you didn't want me, take Rasa, you'll have a piece of meat every day. On Monday, on Tuesday, and again on Wednesday, so you know that Rasa loves you.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. When Jirka was coming back from the feast, he got a stomach ache, yippee, whoopee!  
2. There was no doctor immediately available, so they sent for the baker.  
3. According to an old custom, the baker rolled Jirka on the lawn.  
4. Jirka took a big dump, he felt incomparable in the world.  
5. People from the magistrate came, saying the poop belongs to the state.  
6. Mr. Barometer came, saying the poop measures a meter.  
7. The poop weighs fifteen pounds, damn, that was something.  
8. They put the poop in a box so that flies wouldn't soil it.  
9. For our singing, lick our asses, gentlemen.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

2. They pulled each other along the ground, grabbed each other's hair, and jabbed sharpened knives under each other's ribs.  
3. All the shoemakers from the region gathered here for the funeral, their bells ringing solemnly, the stone playing sadly.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Talkative, talkative, she's the one I like the most, talkative, talkative, she's the one I like. I make love to her, she makes love to me, me, me, because she is talkative, she troubles my heart.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

Needles, needles, needles, Ferda lays bricks, earns six gold coins, walks in a shirt.

earns six little beetles, begs in the glade.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

A young man passed by and asked her for a wreath. 4. She did not give it to him, she even laughed at him.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

3. Come here, little chimney sweep, come into my little room, I will tell you something, you will sweep my chimney.  
4. People, what I tell you, that was a strange chimney, I went up and then down again, until I found that hole.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

Just don’t scold me anymore and give her to me as my wife, I will live with her always, true until death.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

translation: At the Krumlov Castle, I have a pretty girl there, I have a pretty girl. I like her, I like her, as long as I have her in my hands, once I let her go, I immediately think of another, only God knows, only God knows.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

2. Even if I had no coat or shoes, as long as I have a lot of tobacco, even if I had an empty pouch, as long as I have some tobacco.  
3. And when the wife is most stormy, that's when the man smokes best; the wife storms loudly all the time, the man stuffs his pipe for that fun.  
4. When I go to the other world, where I will no longer see the tobacco flower, I will call the priest and sing one last time.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

translation:

Kucmoch, kucmoch, I like him, after potatoes he has heartburn, I prefer coffee, it doesn't upset my stomach.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

translation:

2. Gentlemen, also kings, generals, they have servants; we have them too, though they are not salaried, they are worn out over time, but we have enough of them to serve.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. The old woman carried the devil in a basket, the old woman carried the devil in a basket, he cared about her soul, he cared about her soul. 2. label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

Libeň 1911, sung by Jan Zíka and Josef Čmuchal  
Our oven has fallen, our oven has fallen, who will build it for us?  
The old bricklayer is not home and the carpenter doesn't know how.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Under our windows, a little stream is flowing, water me, my dear, pretty girl, carára rárá, my little horse.  
2. I won't water him, I'm afraid of him, he would kick or bite me, who would heal me?

5. To have a pretty girl and be beaten through her, I'd rather dig a grave, slap her face, throw her into it.  
6. What was I supposed to do, since I loved her? I had to sit down, take her around the neck, give her a kiss.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. People keep telling me, telling me, telling me that I am supposedly a night wanderer, that I am supposedly a night wanderer. 2. But I'm not wandering anywhere, only following my pleasure. 3. People keep telling me that I am supposedly a drunkard. 4. But I don't drink much, only a little red beer.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Listen, folks, tramta tradydá, the verses of this song, tramta tradydá, how the girls dress up, tramta tradydá, to please the boys, tramta tradydá.  
2. From the Smíchov factory they wear new tunics, when they come from the factory in the evening, they say they come from sewing.  
3. In the little forest of Smíchov, there you can see fun, in the evening there are little dances, these are girls from the factory.  
4. Those girls from Podolí have bottoms like barns, they play at being ladies, and weed the grass with the boys.  
5. They have dirty skirts, and also stockings underneath, they are factory girls, with little nails by their shoes.  
6. And when Sunday comes, their heads full of flax, they wear hats to the side, and strut like that.  
7. The Smíchov girls walk along the embankment, the dandy boys nod at them, and they immediately run to them.  
8. On Sunday they go to the music, dressed in tunics, starched skirts, that's how you recognize factory girls.  
9. The boys want to fight over them because they know how to dance, just don't fight over them, watch their petticoats.  
10. On Monday they go to the factory, in torn jackets, those are factory girls, clever little girls.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. God bless you, coffee in the grinder, I would cuddle you every moment.  
2. Soup doesn’t taste good in the morning, the cup licks out the dregs.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Why wouldn't you want, sweet girl, to love a butcher? Why wouldn't you want, sweet girl, to have a butcher? The butcher just buys, scoops up money, why wouldn't you want, sweet girl, to have a butcher?

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. It’s raining, it’s raining, it’s just pouring, where, little horses, shall we go? We will go to mommy, she is baking hares.  
2. It’s raining, it’s raining, it’s just pouring, where, little horses, shall we go? We will go to Prague, we will buy sweets there.  
3. It’s raining, it’s raining, it’s just pouring, where, little horses, shall we go? We will go to Šárka, we will buy a stroller there.  
4. It’s raining, it’s raining, it’s just pouring, where, little horses, shall we go? We will go to grandma, she is baking pancakes.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. In front of our windows sits a hare, -howling, -howling, -howling, it has long ears for listening, -listening, -listening. If I had those ears, I would also listen just like that hare, -howling, -howling.  
2. In front of our windows sits a hare, it has big eyes for peeking. If I had those eyes, I would also peek just like that hare.  
3. In front of our windows sits a hare, it has long legs for running. If I had those legs, I would also run just like that hare.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The unfortunate maternity ward, as beautifully built as it is, come, my dear, look how I lie here troubled.  
2. Two windows face the garden, two windows face the courtyard, they are securely barred so I could not help myself.  
3. They led me to the room, laid me in bed, and when I woke up, angels were standing by me.  
4. Those are not angels at all, they were all doctors, their sleeves rolled up like some mercenaries.  
5. In the room hangs a picture, a picture of the Holy Trinity, to which I raise my hands when the pain is at its worst.  
6. The rounds happen six times a week and the seventh on Sunday, remember me, darling, when we have not seen each other.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The gingerbread man has arrived, the gingerbread man has arrived, come on, girls, buy marzipan for the boys.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: A crow flew in, sat on a fir tree, a second one flew in, sat next to it. And the third one on the tip, sang a song: Don't drink it all, little boy!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The coachman came to us, he had blue eyes, he was asking me where I sleep at night. Holala, lala, how I love him, he was asking me where I sleep at night.  
2. I sleep in the attic, under the quilt, come and see at one o'clock.  
3. Four came up to me there, I threw three of them down the stairs.  
4. The one left, the one stayed with me, and said, "Jeníček, how did you sleep?"  
5. I slept, but only a little, my Mařenka, I dreamed about you.  
6. What I dreamed, I will not tell you, you would regret it for life.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Radnice, vodnice, that is our food, we have it every day, that is our food.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Prague 1914, "it was sung in Prague breweries." Ric, pic, krindy, pindy, it's not like it used to be, ric, pic, krindy, pindy, it's not like that anymore. How it used to be sung is no longer common, ric, pic, krindy, pindy, it's not like that anymore.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: A dilapidated cottage, the sun shines into it, my beloved laments that I must leave her.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: She was sitting on the sand, calling: Matýsek! Matýsek, come here, I'll shake the bag. She was sitting on the plain, looking at her, seeing how sunburned she was from all that sex.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: An old woman is sitting by the barracks, selling pears, but no one is buying them, so she is complaining. She sent an apprentice to get three beers, and he came back to tell her: They are closed.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: A sparrow is sitting on the church like a young man, ha ha ha, a crow flew to him, gave him a wreath. And I don’t want you, crow, because you have black legs. And I don’t want you, my little sparrow, because you play cards!

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. A boy is sitting on the border, looking over the field, happily singing to his flock of sheep.  
2. Rye, rye, little rye, in our field, you always blush, tell me, what hurts you?  
3. When Cain killed Abel with that cruel stick, his blood was spilled over the entire field.  
4. Over the entire grain, over the entire rye, because brother was cruelly murdered by brother.  
5. Since then, year after year, no matter how the grain ripens, I must blush with Abel's blood.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The farmer is a big beast, he defeated the chimney sweep, didn't know how to handle him, threw him into the pond.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: An old woman was jumping along the road, carrying an accordion in a basket; she was met by wanderers who played on it. No, we will not get married, we'd rather go after girls; no, we will not get married, we'd rather go after girls.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Libeň 1909, sung by František Procházka and František Pilger 1. An old woman was jumping, recumferde imr fojer, over the pond, he jumped after her, cinka břinka, cinka břinka, chimney sweep. 2. She took a shovel, hit him on the back.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Glory, hail to Matice, Matice, Matice, Czechs are lads, lads, lads.  
2. Glory, hail to Matice, Franta is carrying the water sprite.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. Fair young lady, what is sir doing? He is walking around the suburbs, selling brooms.  
2. Fair young lady, what is the landlady doing? She has a shop in the arcade, selling brooms.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. These are nuns in the monastery, the monks come to them, one of them prays devoutly that God will give her all good things.  
2. I come, knocking on the door: My dear nun, open to me, for God himself sent me to you to give the little angel some straw.  
3. Straw is not available, nor can it be bought with money; if you are the little angel from heaven, come, lie down in bed with me.  
4. And when it was midnight, the little angel turned to the nun, started gently tickling her there, she said he could stop now.  
5. And when the day was already white, the little angel jumped out the window, the little angel jumps under the window, and the nun cries on the bed.  
6. And when it was dawn, it was written on the gate that the nun slept with the little angel, and she gave him her skirt for a pillow.  
7. Wait, wait, little angel, I will accuse you in heaven that you begged for straw from the nun and made the nun a mother.  
8. And when a year had passed, the nun carried in her arms: Hush, hush, little seraph, after all, your father was no angel.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Uncle Nimra bought a chestnut horse for half a fifth of a grejcar, came home, grabbed his wife, and danced the madhura with her.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: A hunter shot a jay, shot it in the collar. It flew away from him, perched on an oak, there in that green grove.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The tailor was walking from the fair, leading a doll, and the doll is like a flower.  
2. I will sew you a dress from pure silk, the trim on it just shines.  
3. He wanted to love her so much, the little wind blew, and carried the tailor away behind the woods.  
4. Where are you, my dear, my sweet tailor, I would catch you by the paws.  
5. Listen for a moment, listen for a moment, where did these tailors come from here:  
6. One evening at dusk, I went out for a walk, a goat jumped out at me from the thorns.  
7. The goat kicked, the goat threw the tailor out, with a needle, a brick, and scissors.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The farmer went to the market with the farmer's wife, they bought Marjánka a spinning wheel. That little spinning wheel doesn't want to take the yarn, you shouldn't have bought it for me.  
2. What kind of idea was that, that you bought me a spinning wheel? I would have preferred new shoes so I could go to the music.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. A wanderer went from Prague straight to Příbram. He stopped at a pub, danced as if on water. He danced like a madman, the bottle kept tipping over, whether it tipped or not, the devil wouldn’t take it from him.  
2. During the last polka, the dude whispers to the girl to come outside with him and bring the bottle. She replies: Go into the garden, I’ll bring you the bottle out to the field near the forest.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Chickpea, chickpea, beloved chickpea, who will play you, chickpea, when we are sleeping in the grave, chickpea, chickpea, beloved chickpea.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Grandmother went to Bechlín, carrying chickens in a basket, four gentlemen met her and drove her into the rye. 2. Someone felt sorry for the old woman, someone said: Let the devil take her! She did not have to roam at night, she can wait until daytime.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. My dear went to the market, looking for a tailor to sew her a dress from a poppy flower, tralala, tralala, from a poppy flower.  
2. I will sew you that dress from a poppy flower, you must spin thread for me from the May rain.  
3. I will spin that thread for you from the May rain, you must make shoes for me from mosquito leather.  
4. I will make those shoes for you from mosquito leather, you must make the bed for me in the middle of the Moldau.  
5. I will make the bed for you in the middle of the Moldau, you must come there behind me on the millstone.  
6. I will come there behind you on the millstone, there we will love each other until the break of day.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. A Jewish woman went to Slaný, carrying a tied-up Jew, the Jew laughed until he spat. 2. The Jewish woman went through thorns, tearing her lining. The Jew also – his trousers. 3. The Jewish woman also went, also, bruising both her heels. The Jew also – his shoe heels.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The tinker men were crossing the street, each took a half of the roll, each a half.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:

2. Where are you going, dolls, which one of you will be mine?  
3. The little one, she is mine, she has eyes like mine.  
4. She has garnets on her neck, with ducats between them.  
5. How will I pull this off? I will come for her.  
6. With four black horses like a farmer to the mill.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The shoemaker is hanging from a pea stalk on Lysý, it quickly spread throughout the whole village. [chorus] And you dirty little shoemaker, not even the devil wants you in hell, and you dirty little shoemaker, as if you had bad luck.  
2. The shoemaker's wife ran to the priest to have him bury what the people lie about.  
3. The priest says: That cannot be, he must only be buried beyond the wall.  
4. The shoemaker's wife ran immediately like crazy, that the shoemaker's soul will go to hell.  
5. The eldest journeyman grabbed the bellrope, rang the master's sad hour.  
6. The shoemaker comes to hell, the devil told him he does not want to have bad luck, only that he should go on.  
7. The shoemaker comes to heaven, Saint Peter says: You cannot enter heaven, you would scare God.  
8. Oh, what a poor soul I am, where should I go now, I must go back to earth to frighten people.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: There to Vyšehrad, everyone likes to go, whether it rains or not, there's always mud there.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: There in Žamberk in Albertýn, there is a great bustle, about one hundred and thirty patients are breathing fresh air. They have no work to do, only to write many postcards until there is nothing left in their pockets from it.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: That Vamberk castle in the mountains, a wax maker used to go there with pastries.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Auntie, where are you going? What are you carrying? I carry little boxes for pins around the world.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. You keep drinking and doing nothing, what kind of farmer will you be?  
2. You keep working and drinking nothing, what enjoyment will you get out of life?

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Those cobbler's pliers jumped onto the wheel during heavy rain and bit the cobbler. 2. And one awl jumped out of the window, the cobbler’s wife wanted to catch it, but it pricked her.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. At the Jungmans’ place, there was a great commotion, at the Jungmans’ place, there was a commotion.  
2. The lady was ill, she craved wine at night, the lady was ill, she wanted to drink wine.  
3. She sent her man there, she herself had an officer, she sent her man there, she had an officer there.  
4. When the man returned with the wine, the officer quietly disappeared, when the man returned with the wine, the officer was already gone.  
5. The officer walks around the market square, singing a new song, the officer walks around the market square, singing a new song.  
6. Be silent, my dear, and don’t sing, since you have slept, don’t speak, be silent, my dear, and don’t sing, since you have slept, don’t speak.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. By the monastery stands a stone, my dear is sitting on it. She calls: Guys, come here, I am no longer in the monastery. 2. Nothing pleases me in the monastery because there are no guys there. And I like to dance, I love handsome guys.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: The old fiddler died, the village inherits everything, a violin on the peg and a child in the cradle...

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Run, Káča, run, run, a wild tomcat is chasing you, a wild tomcat is chasing you. 2. Káča runs as fast as she can, the tomcat cannot catch her. 3. Káča runs even more, the tomcat is stepping on her shoe. 4. Káča runs to the henhouse, the tomcat is stepping on the bellflower.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: It's done already, it's done already, a few dumplings in the little pan, a bit of lard in it.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. I will not marry a widower, I would be afraid of a widower, that his deceased wife would stand by the bed. And little wire-haired guy, curly boy, little wire-haired guy, hairy one.  
2. She stood by the bed and called to me not to hurt those orphans.  
3. A widower lies down on a straw bed, he doesn’t even ask if we love each other.  
4. A widower lies down on a hay bed, he doesn’t even ask if I am his wife.  
5. A widower lies wherever he pleases and constantly shifts his old bones.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. In Strasbourg on the street, they sell there butchers, they sell for two, they sell a two smelly tripe stew. 2. The smelly tripe stew is baked in lard, then Ptáček’s laundry fights over it.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Around the moon, the guys from Libeň pretend that Libeň girls only have two skirts. 2. On Friday they wash them, on Saturday they starch them, and on Sunday they go out in them to the pub.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Get up, teamsters, we are going to Prague, so have the horses ready. 2. The teamsters got up, fed the horses, and harnessed them at four o’clock.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: But it won’t be like that for us when we get married, it won’t be like that for us when we wed. We will have a cradle made for the children, we will have a stick made for Káča.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: All the girls are dancing, one nicely after another, what I, a girl, will try, try, try, try, since I have to watch it, have to watch it.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. The hunter went out with his little shotgun, snip, snap, old pots, Pepíček and Ančička showed up there, snip, snap, old pots, the hunter went out with his little shotgun, Pepíček and Ančička showed up there, one, two.  
2. What are you doing over there in that thicket, why do you keep bending over?  
3. We are looking for acorns, beechnuts, we will feed the chickens with them.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Behind our little cottage, a pig is jumping there, don’t go there, Růženka, it will bite you again.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Behind our little barn, there is a lot of barley; the woman tied the man with a strap. Oh dear, woman, let me go, I won’t speak any harsh words to you.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. Play for me, you scoundrel, drive the old women out. Drive them out in front of the pub so they don’t make a fool of themselves. 2. They sit on the benches and gossip about the young men, who dances with whom, and that she also loves him.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: Damn monkey, the one shaking its head, it turns the eyes against all the drunks.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation:
1. I know a little mill, I know a little mill, I know one, lodoviko, čer, čer, čelovik, valašaj, rupu, cupu, one, two, three.  
2. In that little mill, there is a miller’s wife.  
3. The miller’s wife had three daughters.  
4. The first is named Marica.  
5. The second is named Fánica.  
6. The youngest is named Nánica.  
7. The first married a milkman.  
8. The second married a blacksmith.  
9. The third married a baker.

label: ŽERTOVNÉ A TANEČNÍ
translation: 1. I lost my leash, leash, leash, where can I find it, find it, find it, find it? 2. The dog found it for me, and caught a cough from it.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: And you, dear boy, tell me the truth, how much beer did the throats of those Prussians hold? F. Homolka: The Prussians before Prague in 1866. Our Prague III, 1925–1926, no. 1, pp. 9–11.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: Oh God, I swear, what were you doing that you took my darling to the army? You took my joy, it torments my heart incessantly, you took my joy, it torments my heart incessantly.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. Oh, you greenish mountains, oh, you bluish forests. What a waste of my youth, when I lived without worries, my delight.  
2. The nightingale jumps on the tree, joy at home weeps, the nightingale is a small bird, for me joy weeps that I have been taken away.  
3. Why did you, parents, why did you raise me? You cannot work, and I must march, there is no help.  
4. I will go out into the wide world and there I will fight. At Gorica on the little hill, there I will lay my head, there I will sleep.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. When we march out through that Prague gate, send my dear greetings there.  
2. So that she marries, she doesn't wait for me, that I have taken another girl.  
3. A girl like that, with a steel saber, she will party everywhere with me.  
4. I was born in Prague, where I will die, I do not know; where will I lay down my young bones?  
5. I will put them in the garden in a small pile, take them, Nanynka, as a keepsake.  
6. She gathered the little bones, tied them in a handkerchief, and cried over her Pepíček.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. When you go away from us, little soldier, where will you spend the night?  
2. On the grass, on the green, sprinkled with cool dew.  
3. When you go to sleep, little soldier, what will you dream about?  
4. About mother and father, about sister and little brother.  
5. When you get up in the morning, little soldier, what will you have for breakfast?  
6. I will go to pick strawberries, I will drink clean water.  
7. Mother and father are mourning that the little soldier is no longer here.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. Boleslav, Boleslav, beautiful city, Boleslav, Boleslav, beautiful city, there are roses, beautiful rosemary by the road, there are roses, beautiful rosemary by the road.  
2. How could I not weep sadly, cry out, when I see you dressed in military clothes.  
3. Those military clothes trouble my heart. Don’t cry for me, don’t lament, we will meet again.  
4. How will we meet if you are just leaving? What will you send me, my lad, what will you send me?  
5. I will send you more than one letter during the year, so that you always believe in my faithful love.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. Brothers and friends, gladly go to the army, brothers and friends, gladly go to the army. For it will not be like this for you as it is in the army, they will give you new clothes, money, and also bread.  
2. There you have it, little soldier, a rifle, just take it with joy. The pistol is on the table, saddle your horse, a black horse, I will sit on it.  
3. The general rides fiercely, holding a saber in his right hand. He commands us to calm down, God knows where I will perish, I fight for the Czech homeland, at home I have a pretty girl.  
4. If you love me, girl, make sure to redeem me. Pay a hundred gold for me, then redeem me, I am a prisoner for the Czech homeland, at home I have a pretty girl.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. Farewell from afar, my wife, your husband is dying there in Serbia. Farewell from afar, my wife, your husband is dying there in Serbia.  
2. Farewell, brother, my sisters, farewell, my joy.  
3. How hard it is to die when a wife remembers from afar.  
4. Therefore only death, I no longer want to live, when I cannot stay in my homeland.  
5. Farewell from afar, my wife, your husband is dying there in Serbia.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. It was a beautiful summer day and all around the roses bloomed, I often remember the youth of my age.  
2. Above me, the clouds, like white swans, majestically spread their white wings.  
3. Fly away, clouds, fly to those mountains, greet my sweetheart and our homeland.  
4. I like girls, I like boys even more, and my sweetheart, I like him the most.  
5. What is this, my darling, what have you done, that you let yourself be taken to the army so young?  
6. I did it for my Boženka, because she didn’t come to me when they were playing volleyball.  
7. I did it for my parents, because they had a hard heart towards me.  
8. The corporal says to them: Get in line, aim your rifles, the enemy is here!  
9. The first bullet flies, wounds my sweetheart, knocks him down from his black horse.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. What have you done, Pepíček, why did you join the artillery in the army?

5. When we said goodbye under the green oak, we always told each other that we would not be each other’s anymore. I have to go far away, across the wide sea, and I will no longer see your blue eyes.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. Prison, oh prison, chains on cold hands, prison, oh prison, chains on cold hands.  
2. And I wait day by day for the doors to move with a key.  
3. Then the jailer comes, calls my name, surname.  
4. You have to go upstairs, you have some visitors there.  
5. Then he leads me, I don’t know where, up those small stairs, I don’t know where.  
6. When I came upstairs, my sister was sitting there at the table.  
7. She has a white scarf in her hand, wiping her blue eyes.  
8. And you dear little brother, I bring you something in a basket.  
8. Go, sister, go home, I have an execution tomorrow morning.  
9. When the sister heard this, she fell to the ground, fainted—fainted.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: Soldiers, soldiers, you are rogues, you only know how to make love for fun.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. A private is often abused like an animal; he sleeps on a stone or somewhere in a hole.  
2. When he is taken as a recruit, he is full of pride; when they give him a rifle, he becomes a fool.  
3. When he is once slapped, pushed here and there, he must not even move—that makes him a big fool.  
4. When the soldier marches where there is some celebration, wherever a soldier steps once, grass does not grow there.  
5. A soldier, when walking down the street, must stroll, must constantly salute, and toss his head from side to side.  
6. On his head he has a helmet, a collar on his neck; as soon as he is issued, he drinks away his six crowns.  
7. A soldier, when he is with the band, hates to pay; when he has to pay three crowns, he often disappears.  
8. After three years when he goes home, he is a drunkard, cannot work at all, and straw sticks out of his shoes.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. The soldier is returning from military service, his heart rejoices with happiness, and his first journey is to where his bride lives.  
2. He cannot find his beloved, then he begins to weep bitterly and asks the tall fir tree where the blue-eyed girl is.  
3. The slender fir bows: Wait, I will tell you about her. A week ago yesterday she had a funeral, she mourned herself to death for you.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. Bad, mother, bad, the Brandenburgers are here. They have big hats, they steal geese, hens, bad, mother, bad, the Brandenburgers are here.  
2. Bad, mother, bad, the Brandenburgers are here, they have big coats, they steal silver, ducats, bad, mother, bad, the Brandenburgers are here.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation:
1. I know a beautiful castle where my beloved lives; she sits at a nice little table, embroidering a white scarf, she sits at a nice little table, embroidering a white scarf.  
2. It is good for you, my Mařenka, to embroider a white scarf, but what about us poor soldiers, we have to stand still.  
3. I stand still like a rock, I stand still like a wall, then a heated cannonball flies over and knocks me out of my calm.  
4. It knocked me out of my calm and they put me in the hospital, write a letter to my dearest that I am wounded.  
5. My right leg is torn off, my left arm is cut off, come, my beloved, come and see how cruel war is.  
5. I will not look at you, I am angry with you, you deprived me of my green wreath, I am looking for it with you.  
6. I must find it with you, only with you. I will give you a nice little cushion made of black velvet.  
7. A little cushion under your head and a loving embrace, so that you, girl, do not think I was a scoundrel.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: And you, you fool, where have you been for so long? It’s already nine o’clock, you’re supposed to be at the barracks.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: The old woman has a thorn in her ass, thorn. The old woman has a thorn in her ass, thorn. Potatoes, potatoes, little potatoes, potatoes, potatoes, little potatoes.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: Go, soldiers, go lie down, for you have nothing to eat for dinner. Go, soldiers, go to sleep, you will have work to do. Tomorrow morning at half past seven we will go to Bosnia.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: Franta beat Kašpar until his wound burst. Franta beat even more until his trousers started to bleed.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: The firefighters are on their way, they are taking the fire truck, then the commander rides the fire truck so the younger ones have to work hard.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: Fire, fire! The firefighters are coming, they're bringing a fire truck!

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: I am Honza from Skalice, ears like a rabbit, I look like a shaggy scarecrow. I will break the plowshare in our village into bits and pieces.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: He rides, he rides, the postman doll, he rides, he rides the postman. In front he has a little horn, at the back he has a little chest, he rides, he rides the postman.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: We don't want to drill, but we have to.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: A little soldier went to the drill, he needed to poop. He didn’t have a piece of paper with him, so he scratched it with his finger.

label: VOJENSKÉ
translation: Get up, Franta, the coffee is already brewed!

label: LEGENDY
translation: The hunchback shat behind the door.

label: LEGENDY
translation: 1. The path had been trodden since the time of Christ the Lord. 2. Two souls were walking on it, and with them a great sinner. 3. They were met by the Mother of God: Where are you going, sinful soul? 4. I am walking the broad way, where sinners go to hell.

label: LEGENDY
translation:
1. Twelve virgins fell asleep, waiting for their beloved, only two did not sleep, they waited for their beloved.  
2. They did not sleep, they suffered and awaited their beloved, a chaste bride, brighter than the sun.  
3. First was Saint Catherine, she was the bride of Christ, the bride of Christ endured everything.  
4. The second bride of Christ was Saint Dorothy, illuminated by beauty above all the virgins.  
5. Her splendid beauty pleased everyone, until in the end the king asked to love her.  
6. She did not want anyone, only Jesus Himself; then the king grew angry and ordered her to be punished.  
[Singer did not know the song further.] It was sung to him by his grandmother.

label: LEGENDY
translation:
1. In the town of Ratiboř, that is in Prussian land, a great famine prevails, followed by a terrible disease everywhere.  
2. A little girl was walking through the forest, sadly lamenting for her mother who lay sick, searching for roots.  
3. There was a lot of snow, she could not find anything, and perhaps her dear mother must perish without human help.  
4. In that forest, there is a chapel of the Five Wounds of Christ the Lord; the child knelt sadly, clasped her little hands, and wept mournfully.  
5. Oh dear Jesus, heal our mother, we suffer great hunger, three poor children, I am the eldest of them.  
6. As the eight-year-old girl lamented so, she saw an old man coming from afar with two crutches.  
7. “Praised be Jesus!” The child replied: “Forever and ever amen, may the wounds of Christ the Lord be blessed.”  
8. “I hear you, my child, you cry bitterly; here is a piece of bread, for the Lord God will not let you perish from hunger.”  
9. “Give this bread always, always pray, you will be blessed by Jesus the Lord, just remember Him.”  
10. “That is why, old man, I did not ask for bread, I am searching for roots for my dear mother, so she may walk healthy.”  
11. The little girl took the bread, and as she turned around, the old man disappeared before her eyes; she never saw him again.  
12. Oh, it was not an old man, it was Christ the Lord Himself, who gave me this prayer and that piece of bread with His own hand.  
13. The little girl was going home, she heard a calling, she saw her mother on her deathbed, dying sadly.  
14. “Jesus and Mary, dear mother, do not abandon us, I have already received a piece of bread for you!”  
15. She handed it to her faint mother, and as she tasted it, she was immediately freed from that heavy illness.  
16. Now the mother rose, kissed her little daughter, and with her children recited that beautiful prayer three times.  
17. So, dear Christians, let us honor the wounds of Christ; He will give us blessing in heaven and on earth by His own divine hand.  
18. God’s providence always dwells with us; accept us, Your children, us miserable sinners, mother of Jesus Christ.

label: BALADY
translation:
1. Mary walked through the wide world, carrying her little son in her holy life.  
2. She came one night to a blacksmith: Blacksmith, blacksmith, let me lie down.  
3. I won't let you lie down, I won't let you lie down, I have many journeymen, I have many journeymen.  
4. They forge at night, making nails, which they use to nail the Lord Jesus to the cross.  
5. When the Virgin Mary heard these words, she ran to Bethlehem and gave birth to her son.  
Sung by a caroler at Christmas 1911.

label: BALADY
translation:
1. There was a prince who had a beautiful daughter, until the Turkish sultan heard about her, until the Turkish sultan heard about her.  
2. The prince and princess are crying. Why do you cry so, why do you wail so, mourning yourselves?  
3. How can we not cry and mourn ourselves when we have to give you to the Turk, to the Turk?  
4. He wrote a short letter and gave a ring with it, so that it would be delivered to her house.  
5. Have a chest made, I will lie in it until the Turk comes, a corpse I will be.  
6. They had a chest made entirely of ducats, and the lid was set with garnets.  
7. They had a pit dug with a silver hoe and the soil thrown out with a golden shovel.  
8. The Turk rides to the gates, his beloved is cold, people tell him that she has just died.  
9. Never have I seen such a dead girl who would have red cheeks, rosy little cheeks.  
10. He unclasped the chain from his neck, put it around hers: You were meant to be the Turkish queen.  
11. He took a handkerchief from his sleeve, wiped her forehead: To whom did you pledge yourself, my delight?  
12. The Turk rides away from the gates, waved the handkerchief, my almighty God, he struck his own head.

label: BALADY
translation:
1. The lady was sick, cingi lingi boom, she would not see the morning, cingi lingi boom, the lady was sick, trará židlajdá, she would not see the morning, ha ha!  
2. She wanted to drink wine, but there was no one to get it for her.  
3. She sent her master, she slept alone with the hussar.  
4. The master returns with the wine, the hussar jumps behind the stove.  
5. Tell me, my lady, why is the bed overturned?  
6. A little mouse was here, the cat chased it.  
7. Tell me, my lady, whose is that iron helmet?  
8. The hussars rode this way, they left the helmet here.  
9. Tell me, my dear, whose are those shoes with spurs?  
10. Shoemakers went to the fair, they forgot the shoes here.  
11. Tell me, my dear, who is that behind the stove?  
12. It is a poor hussar, his legs are broken.  
13. If you were a kind master, you would give him something.  
14. The master reaches into his pocket, pulls out three ducats.  
15. Tomas, poor hussar, get your legs fixed.  
16. The hussar walks around the market and sings a new song:  
17. For sleeping with the lady, I got three ducats!  

hussar, from Polish + Turkish = member of light cavalry; spurs from German = spurs

label: BALADY
translation:
1. The military draft was announced, announced by the emperor, the lord, the military draft was announced by the emperor, the lord.  
2. A small farmer had three daughters, they were his support.  
3. He called the eldest, the one he loved the most.  
4. Come, daughter, come home, you will go to the army for me.  
5. No, father, I will not go, I will not fight.  
6. He called the middle one, promising to reward her well.  
7. Come, daughter, come home, you will go to the army for me.  
8. No, father, I will not go, I will not fight.  
9. He called the youngest, she was the dearest of all.  
10. Come, daughter, come home, you will go to the army for me.  
11. Yes, father, I will go, I know how to fight.  
12. When she joined the army, she killed three hundred Prussians. Prussians = Prusové

label: BALADY
translation: 1. When the dragoon enlisted, he ordered his Andulka, chacha, not to marry for him; she waited for him for three years. 2. She did not wait even a week; she married the very next day. In the third year, she went to the grass, to the lord's brewery. 3. When she had cut the grass, she looked behind herself. There she sees a black horse and the dragoon with another woman. 4. Come, dragoon, from the right side, help me with that bundle of grass. [...] 5. I will not help you with the bundle; give it to my horse to eat. Before I let it be eaten, I would rather trample it. 6. The dragoon jumped sharply off the horse and slapped his Andulka. Here you go, you unfaithful one, you false deceiver.

label: BALADY
translation:
1. When I went through the dense forest, two virgins were reaping oats there, when I went through the dense forest, two virgins were reaping oats there.  
2. I ask you, my maidens, my heart’s doves,  
3. is my beloved healthy, as she always used to be?  
4. Your beloved, she died, her funeral was last week yesterday.  
5. Show me the path from where they carried my Anička.  
6. Along this path, downwards, straight to the new cemetery.  
7. Hardly had I gone around, I did not find my beloved’s grave.

label: BALADY
translation:
Libeň 1912, sung alternately by František Černý and Marie Halamová  
1. When I came from the tavern, the clock struck three, open up for me, my dear, everyone is hurrying, open up for me, my dear, everyone is hurrying.  
2. I won’t open for you, I’m angry with you, you tore a rose blossom from me, I do not find it.  
3. You will find it, you will find it, after a year, you carry a handsome huntsman on your arm.  
4. When the year was over, she carried a little dragoon on her arm in a striped blanket.  
5. My son, my son, what am I to do with you, should I raise you or kill you?  
6. My mother, my mother, don’t do this to me, please raise me and send me to the army.  
7. You will receive praise from His Majesty the Emperor for raising a handsome uhlan.  
8. You will receive praise from the prince himself for raising a handsome huntsman.  
7./2 You will be praised by the prince himself for raising a handsome huntsman.  
8./2 You will be praised by the princess herself for raising a boy for loving.  
9./2 You will be praised by His Majesty the Emperor for raising a handsome uhlan.  
10./2 Uhlans, uhlans, you have beautiful horses, I will join you too, which one will you give me?  

dragoon = member of mounted infantry;  
striped (from German);  
umordovat (from German) = to kill;  
uhlan (from Polish + Turkish) = member of light cavalry;  
huntsman = member of a special kind of infantry

label: BALADY
translation:
1. A farmer is going to the mill, hup-hup, with four black horses, hup-hup. A farmer is going to the mill with four black horses.  
[chorus] Hup-hup, every girl likes to shake, hup-hup, every girl gives, gives (doesn't give), gives (doesn't give), every girl gives, and tra-ra-ra ram-ta-ra-da ram-ta-ra-ta-ta tra-ra-ra tram-ta-ra-ta-ta.  

2. When they arrived at the mill, they asked about the miller.  
3. And you, dear miller, where will the ox be put?  
4. Put it in the mill close to Mařenka's bed.  
5. And when it was midnight, Honza jumped out of the sack.  
6. And when it was morning, Honza eloped with Marjánka.  
7. And when a week had passed, Manča cried every day.  
8. And when a month had passed, Manča cried even more.  
9. And when a year had passed, she carried the boy in her arms.  
10. And when six years had passed, the ox was taken to school.  
11. And when twenty years had passed, the ox carried a bayonet.

label: BALADY
translation:
1. Who is walking in the cemetery, treading the grass on my grave, stepping on the green grass, crushing the cold dew?  
2. It is I, the spiritual father, who serves the divine services. Since you are the spiritual father, tell my young lady  
3. to sew me a new shroud, a new shroud, just and fair, which is not spun on Saturday, nor rolled on Sunday before Mass,  
4. for in it I cannot decay nor find peace in the earth. The lady immediately took the keys and opened all the chests.  
5. Who is walking in the cemetery, treading the grass on my grave, stepping on the green grass, crushing the cold dew?  
6. It is I, your young lady, carrying you the new shroud, which is not spun on Saturday, nor rolled on Sunday before Mass.  
7. Since you are my young lady, come here into my pit. As she entered the pit, it closed behind her.
