translation: I chose Mariančka with the pink skirt as my protector: no matter how she is, she wants me; she sent me a message about the fair. "I chose Mr. France in the green jacket as my protector: no matter how he is, since he wants me, he persuaded me about music."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little cloud is rising, it will rain: hide me, my dear, under the pillows; I will not leave you, my delight, until the sun rises again over the hill.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I went to my dear one, the dog growled at me, I gave him a crust just to make him quiet: Courage! Courage! here’s a crust for you: just don’t bark at me when I am with you! I came to the window, quietly I knocked, unhappy Courage immediately barked at me: Courage! Courage! here’s a crust for you: just be silent when I am with you! My dear Lidunka! oh my darling! and Courage: woof, woof, woof! paid no attention to it. Courage! Courage! you have no conscience; you wouldn’t bark if I were with you! Suddenly the master came out of the door: I jumped out of the yard the same way I came in. Courage! Courage! you don’t know love; you wouldn’t have barked if I had been with you. The following stanza is probably a later addition and only detrimental to the previous ones: After all, I didn’t bark, I only growled; if I had known that, I would rather have stayed quiet. Just whisper: Courage! here’s a crust for you; I won’t even make a sound when you are with us. Compare Jič. Zpěv. 330; Suš. 208.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was walking away from my beloved, from the maiden, little horseshoes jingled under my shoes: jingle, jangle, little horseshoes! I loved two dark-eyed maidens. When I was walking away from my beloved at dawn, the neighbor’s dog started barking at me; and I said to him: "Quiet, little dog!" and he almost bit my little foot.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not go, young men, to Lady Mařena's place; although she will drive you out like dogs, as she did me: when I came there, the dog came at me, I had to turn back, barely escaped.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: They always tell me that I am unmarried, that I don't have embroidered aprons: if I don't have them, I don't have them, I can have one; I will have it sewn by a seamstress from Volyně. The Volyně seamstress is well known, she will embroider it with tulips; with tulips, and also beautiful flowers: then you will want me, young men!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Briefly or at length, nothing pleases me when my dearest one does not graze here! If he were here grazing, it would be easier for me, my heart would be happier again.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Take care of me, mother, take care of me, mother, until the green apple ripens: when you raise me, you will marry me to a dear one, then, mother, you will look at me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Take care of me, my mother, like a Meissen apple, take care of me, my mother, like a rose blossom: only after you raise me, then you will see how nicely a white coat will suit me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Petrovický Castle among the mountains: don’t forget what you gave me! “I gave, I gave, I gave an embroidered white scarf with tulips, nicely trimmed.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not say, my lady! Do not say anything that we talked together, do not say anything; that we stood together, gave each other kisses, do not say, my lady, do not say anything!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Above Pušperek, that little hill is green, and on it sits a swallow, calling out: I will go there to listen to what the swallow will tell me. The swallow spoke about my beloved, that I must leave her, as clear as day; as clear as day, the sun shines: that she is my golden maiden like a flower.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Behind that little barn of ours, behind the barn, I found a painted whistle there; that whistle played nicely: I did not expect any false notes, my boy!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My head aches, my dear boy! Take the scarf out of your pocket, tie my head; my dear boy! Tie my head: once you have tied my head, I will give you a kiss.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Green grove, green forest: what you promised me yesterday, I am going for it today; what you promised me yesterday, I am going for it, my dear, you promised, you did not give, you deceived me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A pear tree stands in the wide field, greening, rosemary grows beneath it, it is not picked: I must pick it, even if I were to perish there, and you must, my maiden, forget. If our lords found out that a twig was plucked from the rosemary: they would summon all the district officials here, they would immediately capture us for the army.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A beautiful rose, a carnation, bloomed for me: my lady did not want to give me a kiss; she did not want to, she did not give it because she was angry: how my lady will be appeased again.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The girl went to Vamberk, carrying chicks in a basket; her beloved met her and said he would trample her basket. "Tit for tat, my dear! Don’t be so angry: you can take the chicks, just don’t trample my basket."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: No one knows what love is who has not experienced it: I would not go to my beloved if I did not have to: but I think I must go there to visit my lady, even if all people would consider it wrong of me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don't knock, don't knock, madam is not asleep yet; don't knock, don't knock, ours are not lying down yet: if you keep knocking here for long, madam will ask: who is it? who is it? she won't rest at night.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Sing, nightingale, cheerfully here in our valley: what is destined for me by God, it will not pass me by; let people say what they want, that they are taking my beloved away from me: if my beloved wants me, no one will take her away. When I used to visit my darling, the moon was shining for me; when I was returning home, it was often daylight. She ran out to meet me in the orchard: "Will you come to us soon, boy? When I don’t see you, my golden one, I walk around completely troubled."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don’t go there, come rather to us, I am a better girl; come rather to us, don’t go to her, I am a better girl: if she is richer, I am more sincere; don’t go to her, come rather to us, I will gladly take care of you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If my dear were ribbons themselves, and everyone liked her, and yet I would not marry her: I will marry an honest girl, even if she had only one bouquet, blue eyes, like mine, oh! like mine.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If it were covered in gold, my dearest is still just mud: I don’t care about that, I scrape off the mud, my dearest is worth it!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Red and white, I like that, red and white I like: red cheeks and white hands, a pretty girl I like.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Why wouldn't I be, the bailiff's daughter, why wouldn't I be fair white: I drink the cream, I wash with milk; why wouldn't I be fair white? Why wouldn't I be, the innkeeper's daughter, why wouldn't I be like a rose: I drink wine, I wash with beer; why wouldn't I be like a rose?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I don’t care about anything, only when I have a little garden under my windows, I don’t care about anything, only when I have a graft under my windows: there are little apples on it, Ančička picks them, they are good, they are sweet, they are like honey.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On that bridge, the grass is green, on our bridge the grass grows: my girl mowed it, she is still very small; and I will wait for her until she grows up.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Around Kestřany, the grass is green, around Kestřany the grass is growing: when the grass grows, Ančička will grow; when the grass grows, then she will be mine. There are few of you left, Kestřany girls, there are few of you left, very few: there were eleven of you, now only two remain, the soldiers came, took you away.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She stood in the brook wearing blue shoelaces, and had an ashen skirt, a pink kerchief, a sage green apron: she was a girl to be loved!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She was standing on the balcony in a linen shirt, wearing lace-up shoes with bows; and I kept begging, carrying my hat in my hand, to make me a bed with blankets. She told me she was still young, that she hadn't plucked geese yet; when she does pluck them, she wants to make me a bed with nice white blankets.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wait, I’ll say you were pestering me, wait, I’ll say you wanted me: a little rose in the garden, a little kiss on the way; wait, I’ll say you wanted me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I won't give it, I don't have it, I am still little, I don't have it, I won't give it, I am still young: when I am bigger, a bit wiser, then I will give you the scarf.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, I don't know, I don't know why you like me, oh, I don't know, I don't know why you love me: I am not rich, nor am I very beautiful; oh, I don't know, I don't know why you love me. "Your blue eyes are my favorite, your blue eyes I love: those blue little eyes and white little hands: because they can do everything."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would like to have you, if only I knew, if only I knew that you would be mine; but it is so difficult, my joy, because I do not know if you will be mine. "Just love me, do not be angry with me, just love me, I will be yours; or it is so easy, my joy, because I only want to be yours."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I do not see you, I walk sorrowful; when I do not see you, I am mournful: but when I behold you, I have a joyful heart, freed from all worries.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The road to Lužany is all dusty, who doesn’t mind walking it? Who doesn’t mind when the girl is pretty, who doesn’t mind seeing her off?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Above Pušperk is a green little hill, above Pušperk is a green hill: it has become cloudy, it will rain there; it has become cloudy, it will rain there. It will rain there, it will just pour; it will rain there, it will just pour: my beloved is there, I have to go there; my beloved is there, I have to go there.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The little moon is shining, I must go to my dearest, to my beloved; the little moon has set, I have found my beloved: she was waiting for me under the birch grove.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Around Kolín is a nice plain, around Kolín the water flows: my dear Nanynka, my lovely one, serves there, my golden delight serves there.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Take me, take me, my black horse, take me, take me, to my beloved: if you take me, I will give you whatever you want; take me, take me, I will give you oats.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I walked away from my dear, they honked at me nicely, a cuckoo called from the oak: the cuckoo called, my dear cried because she had angered Jeníček. My dear Jeníček! So do not be angry that I did not come out to you; so do not be angry, do not look for reasons: if you like me, just take me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Everyone asks me where my doll is, everyone asks me where my darling is? She is sitting under the little tree, playing with a wreath: that is my darling, a lovely girl.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Over there, across the water, two girls are coming through the grass; they would need a crossing: get up, ferryman, ferry my girl so she doesn't come to drown.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh heavenly God! Am I not handsome, that no girl wants me in Klatovy? I will go out into the world, where happiness blossoms, and there I will find myself a nice girl. Heavenly mother! Am I not pretty, that no boy wants me in Pušperk? I will go out into the world, where happiness blossoms, and there I will find myself a boy too.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I will not be here, I will leave this place; I will not be here, I will go away: I will go to a world where the rose blooms, there I will choose whatever I want, there I will choose what I want.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Defiance against defiance to someone into our house, defiance against defiance to someone at our gate: two roses grow there, no one can reach them, no one can love them. "I am not a dog to climb to you through the window; I am not a dog, nor a beast: when I want to have your daughter, I will not go through the window, I will go through the door."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Come to us during the day, sit next to me, come to us during the day, sit next to me: have some beer brought to us so that I can drink; I will put bread and cheese and salt on the table.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I don't get you, I know what I will do; if I don't get you, my darling! I will go to a monastery, become a friar, and there I will pray for you. If the Lord God grants us, and you will still be mine, then we will mock our enemies: if the Lord God brings us together, no one will separate us, until someday the shovel at the grave.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Whose little horse is that? It clatters along the road: oh, my dearest is going to the army! I will tie up my dress, I’ll go with him too, no one will separate us until death.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear godmother, if you are so clever as to know what I have in the lantern? I have Ančička there, the candle of my heart – as soon as I get her, I will shine a light to you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Spin, spin, spin away from me, I was at the mill; spin, spin, spin away from me, I want to sleep: I was grinding wheat, my golden Jenik; spin, spin, spin away from me, I want to sleep!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That's why I didn't want you, because you were very sleepy, that's why I didn't want you, because you were sleeping so deeply: I came to you, then I left again, I gave you a little kiss, you know nothing.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The wind blows from Podlážky, the girl looks like a picture; the wind blows from Podlážky, it is cold, cold, cold. They are heating, smoke rises from the little village, take me, girl, to the little room, they are heating, smoke rises from the little village, it is cold, cold, cold.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Be angry with me, or don’t be angry, just greet me when you meet me; be angry with me, or don’t be angry, just give me a greeting: and when you meet me and give a greeting, people will think you are not angry; be angry with me, or don’t be angry, just give me a greeting.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The meadow is green, the forest is green, only you, my dear, do not tell on me; the meadow is green, the forest is green, only you do not tell on me: and if you do tell, you will tell on yourself, that I did not leave you without a kiss; the meadow is green, the forest is green, only you do not tell on me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Budgie, my child, I like you; Budgie, my child, I love you! People do not even wish me well for coming after you; people do not even wish me well for coming to you. Stubbornly against stubbornness to someone's house of yours, stubbornly against stubbornness to your gates. People do not even wish me well for coming after you; people do not even wish me well for coming to you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I don’t want anyone, just Honza himself, I don’t want anyone, only Honza: Honza will carry me in a covered carriage, in a covered carriage, drawn by four horses, oh my golden Honza, Honza!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Blue-eyed girl, don’t sit by the stream, blue-eyed girl, don’t sit there: the water in the stream is deep, if it takes you, it will be a pity; blue-eyed girl, don’t sit there! Blue-eyed girl, don’t sit by the stream, blue-eyed girl, don’t sit there: the water in the stream is swirling, 1) it will sweep away your eyes; blue-eyed girl, don’t sit there!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Father, Father, Father, our Father – my lady, how are you? Healthy, healthy, healthy, healthy you are – when you see me, you are glad. I believe in the Lord God – that we are both glad: The Ten Commandments – when little kisses burn. Angel of God, my guardian – you are mine and I am yours.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Love. Love me, I love you, we will be friends together: whoever loves me, I love more; and whoever loves me little, I love nothing. Love is a beautiful virtue, it will last forever.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little pigeon flew out onto a green oak tree. It flew out, cooed, until the oak swayed. Behind it, a little dove was looking for a mate. The mate flew off to the noble clover. Beyond the clover to the forest: "My little pigeon! Where are you?"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She stood in the garden in a golden corset. She wore a golden cap, her hair curly. A thin shirt, sewn by a little doll. She sewed it from hemp in that green grove. She sewed, embroidered, wept sorrowfully. "In that castle tower lies my beloved. Keep watch over him so he does not leave me." They put guards on him, four cuirassiers. And in the large house, they saddled horses for him. "Saddle the horses for me, give me a long saber. Along with a carbine for my young wife."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Black eyes, go to sleep, you must get up in the morning. Morning, morning, early morning, before the sun rises. The sun is already rising, my dear is walking around. She is walking around the square, bringing us news. Such news that they are recruiting for the army. When they recruit, they will take, 1) what a pity for the lads a hundred times over! They will take our handsome ones, 2) the lame ones will stay with us.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The girl was mowing, mowing grass near the dry land. When she finished mowing, she looked around to see where Janeček was grazing the horses. He was grazing them in the valley, on the young clover. Eat, horses, it's good – my beloved is unfaithful. I bought her a blue ribbon: here you go, girl, for parting! Whenever she braided that ribbon, she always cried over it. "Where is the one who bought it for me? He has saddened my little heart!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little dove flew out of the reeve’s little window. She flew out, cooed, and called to her beloved. Come, my dear, from the other side, help me with a load of grass. "The grass is heavier than the aftermath, why, my dear, did you take so much?" I took as much as I wanted so that I would have enough for the cows. He turned around and laughed, slapped his maiden. It made her cheeks flush red, and she wiped them with her handkerchief. With a handkerchief, a white one, that she had received from her beloved. She made a wreath from little flowers and wove clover into it. The flower in the wreath withers: God be with you, my little girl! Prachenska. – Cf. Suš. 537; Kol. I. 295. 1) "May father and mother help, they did not want to give you to me. They were still rocking you in the cradle, but they were already promising you to me."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was leaving my girl, two little stars were shining for me. One bright, the other dark: oh my God! where is mine? Oh, the bright one, that is yours, and the dark one, that is mine. Dark star! shine brighter: my maiden! reconsider. How then shall I reconsider, if I do not intend to take you? The millstone is heavy: the state of marriage is even heavier. The millstone grinds down, the state of marriage does not wear out. The millstone is crushed: the state of marriage lasts until death!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
Why, guelder rose, do you stand in the ditch? Are you afraid of drought? If I were afraid of drought, I would stand even deeper.  
Why, guelder rose, do you have no berries and do not blush with them? I had plenty of berries and I blushed with them.  
Birds pecked off the top, maidens broke off from the stems.  
Every maiden has her boy – and I, sadly, have no one.  
Oh, if only I had someone, I would have taken comfort!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I quickly bet enough, a nice rose to my liking; oh, the little rose is falling, my beloved is leaving me. He leaves and does not want me, because I do not have thousands, thousands, not even hundreds – the path to us has grown over with grass. I had a dear boy, what of him when he does not want me? When he comes to us, he sits quietly, not saying a word. Marry, girl, marry, take someone who is your equal: I am equal to you in honesty, in wealth it is not possible!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I walked through forest and non-forest, I got lost, I don’t know where I am; I walked on a road and off-road, I met a pretty girl there. She met me, didn’t know me, came up to me, called out to me: “Welcome, my fellow countryman! Don’t go to another maiden.” Why would I go to you, if you cheated on me? You repaid my love and sincerity with unfaithfulness. Don’t think you’re the only one destined for my heart: there are plenty of girls everywhere, like juniper berries. I came to you for three years, I brought gifts to you; I brought, you took, but you loved another. I came to you for three years, and was cheated by you; in the third year the juniper ripened: it was the end of our love. The world turns like a wheel: 1) love now, if you have someone; the world turns like a stone, our love is already amen!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh my ancient maiden! No one wants me for you; they don’t love me for you, they point at me because of you. If I knew you would be mine, I would pave the way you walk; I would pave it with little tiles, the path where your little feet walk. The sun is standing above the mill, where will we meet again? We will meet on the little path, and give each other a little kiss.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I don’t know, I don’t know which way to go – all of it has grown over with grass; it has grown over with clover, so I wouldn’t go for free gifts. I bought her lacing with silver, lacing trimmed with gold, lacing, golden buckles as a token of our love. “Who was it then, son, who begged you to bring me gifts? You brought them, I accepted them, but I loved another.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: To Pažežnice the path is narrow, 1) entirely overgrown with thornbushes; the thornbushes bloom white: my maiden, open up! My golden Spaněvská path, it is not so much you I long for as for the path, my girl! "Oh golden Spaněvský grove, I am always thinking of you: not so much the little grove as you, my lad! Oh golden Spaněvský trails, how you were green: turn green again, please my heart’s delight!" There on those Spaněvský meadows I heard a cuckoo cuckooing; the little cuckoo cuckooed, my maiden began to cry. The cuckoo cuckooed cuckoo: "Give me, my lad! your hand, give me your hand, give me your right hand, the path to us has grown over with grass. Give me your hand, give it to me, so that we may not be separated: not for a year, not for two years, not until the end of the world!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The wheat was turning green when I was leaving the little kiss; it was turning green happily when I was leaving you, girl. The wheat was turning green, the blue eyes started to cry; now I must go away from you – may the Lord God from heaven comfort you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Let me go, my dear, let me go: you are false – God forgive you! Release me from my promise, I will wait for another. You think I don't know? And I will find out everything from you, what, you son, what are you doing: that you are fooling yourself with another!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was harrowing those fields, at that time I trained the horses; I harrowed them with a black mare when I went after my maiden.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Boy, boy, my little lad! You torment my little head; you trouble me and afflict me, you sadden my little heart.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When a beloved says goodbye to a beloved, they cut their hearts in two, their hearts with a saw – a paper, silver, gold-inlaid saw. When a beloved says goodbye to a beloved, they light a white farewell for each other: and we did not light for each other – yet we said goodbye.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Mountain, mountain, you are high! My maiden, you are distant, you are distant beyond the mountains – love between us is fading. It is fading, fading, until it withers! There is no one in the world for me, no more pleasure for me to be found!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Upward the road, downward the other – let us part, my dear! Our farewell is hard, when we are used to being together! Ah, when we part, 1) two little hearts will be saddened! Two little hearts, four eyes will cry day and night. Blue eyes, why do you cry if you will not be mine? You won’t be and you must not, it’s useless to think otherwise! 1) An older version of the song goes like this: Ah! When we part, two little hearts will be saddened: one mine, the other yours, both will cry. I will be beyond the mountain, the tall one, you beyond the water, the deep one: two little hearts, four eyes will cry day and night. Eyes, eyes, why do you cry? Are you lamenting to yourselves? Ah, don’t cry for him, you will get another one.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh dear, oh dear, what am I doing! I had a girl, now I don't have her; I had a girl promised to me, someone else came and took her away from me. When I ride through that valley, my horses will neigh; especially the near one, the one that carried me to my sweetheart.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a boy, I have nothing; I gave a scarf, I will give no more: oh my pleasure! Yesterday it was, today it is no more. Well my heart knew, it just could not tell me: that love is not constant, that which hides from people. My dear went away, went to Silesia, took the little key from my heart: took it from both himself and me, left me with none.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It's a pity for you, son, it's a pity for you: your father doesn't want to have you as a son-in-law, nor does the daughter want you as a lover; give up this fruitless going. "I will not give up going until I break your little door, the new ash door, so that this love affair ends."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The path to Lužany is covered entirely with snow: anyone who wants to make love to a girl must clear the way.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Red, blue violet – where were you, my dear, picking it? I was picking it in the garden, my heart ached. 1) Green, white hazelnut – ah, who will comfort my heart! Many have saddened it, but no one comforts it.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If you are an honest young man, also avoid girls. When a girl turns towards you, pull your hat down over your eyes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She ran out of the house, whispered to him: "You must go, boy, you must go to the army!" I will go to the army, I will fight: whatever I win, I will give to you. I won a black horse: sit down, my dear, sit on it. She sat on the horse, immediately went pale: where are you going, my dear, where are you taking your beauty? "I have taken it to the wide fields: oh, how my heart hurts now!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The willows are the grass above the ditch, helping one another. Jeníček was standing there behind the oak, he threw an acorn at Andulka. Don’t throw, don’t throw the acorn: for we will not be yours.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The path was trodden, Anička walked this way, crying. Jeníček came by, whining; he caught up with his beloved, longing for her. "Why are you longing, my dear? Do you not know my love? Come to us, my dear, on Sunday, I will give you a green wreath." Jeníček did not come on Sunday, the green wreath withered. It withered, withered, fell off, for Jeníček had deceived her.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You always used to shine on me, little moon, when I used to visit my dear one. Shine for me, little moon, even today: but you will no longer shine for me. But you will no longer shine for me: I will not go to my beloved anymore today.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Behind our homesteads are long fields, where my lad plowed; he plowed in all his love there: what have you done, my picture! "Why do you girls deny me, where do you go to that grass? To the grass, to the grass, to the little grass and to that green clover?" I harvested it yesterday at dawn, and it grew again by morning: as the grass grows, golden boy! so my little heart leaps in my body. When I went for grass behind the lord’s manor, my dear was plowing there with oxen; I greeted him nicely, but my pleasure was not thanked. Be angry, my dear, be angry, be angry, even if you don’t speak to me for a whole week: I will wear the jacket inside out, yet you will come to beg me, boy!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Across the water, across the water, by the little water my dear played with a little dove, my dear played there with a black eagle: "Marry, Jeníček, with God's blessing!" I will marry you, you don't even know it, you cannot forbid me my banns, nor will you let me arrange a wedding ceremony, that you, my dear, will not do. Since I did not get you as my wife, I will choose you as my bridesmaid, so that you serve me one last time, placing a little wreath on my head. "That cannot happen to you, Jeníček, out of pity I could not give it to you, with crying I could not even enter the church: for false love I have to die! For that, I only ask you, my Jeníček! that you light a candle at my passing, that you stand alone at my passing, pressing my cloudy little eyes shut! Press my little eyes shut, light the candle, pray the Lord's Prayer for my little soul: so that our love, ignited by a single little flame, burns out!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What were you thinking, my poor girl, that you let me go for love because of poverty? I suffer for you day and night: and meanwhile your thoughts are elsewhere! Your love is false, the world is false: if you did not want to marry me, you could have said so right away! I would not have thought of you: I would have chosen another for you. So what do you value in wealth? You see there are plenty of examples everywhere. There are people who had thousands: they lost it all, they have nothing. Money flows away like water: and all that will remain to you is a wicked woman!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On the little hill stands a pine tree, and my Ančička sat beneath it. A nightingale flew in, carrying a letter, and handed it to Andulka from the right side. Here you go, my dear girl, don’t be afraid, I bring you a little letter, you will be pleased. Before the sun rises from the mountain, you will have your beloved by the chamber. He will be sadly waiting for you, tapping on the window. When he knocks, don’t open; someone else is also knocking. And if you open, you will deceive yourself, and your love will change. It will change once and never again: your beloved will leave you and go elsewhere.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Blow, little wind, from the east: I loved a girl, I will love no more. You won’t, Andulka, you won’t be mine, for your mother is very poor. Sad times have come upon you, lads, you must not take to yourselves base beauties. Let her be lame or hunchbacked, if only she has money, she is still golden!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would [like to be with] you, my darling, I would like to, if my friend didn’t stop me. "I would [like to be with] you, my dear, even more, if none of our people stopped me."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little girl was swimming on the Danube: the ducklings are bobbing behind her. How could you not swim there, little girl: how could you not catch me, Honzíček!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I do not get you, my dear, I will climb a rock, and strike my head; on a high rock, on a pointed one, I will strike my curly little head.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Give, God! To Barbora Martin with the scythe, to cut the grass with dew. Before he cut the grass, the scythe fell; before he put the scythe back on, the grass withered.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I once walked through the grove, oh, through the grove! a young hunter met me on the path. The sun was shining, it was warm, oh, it was warm! at that time my little heart bloomed with love. We sat together until evening, oh, until evening! the hunter shot at a deer. It was not a young stag, it was a doe, oh, it was a doe! "Remember me soon, maiden." Later, when she was mowing grass, oh, mowing grass! I wish I had not known the hunter! When she was washing clothes by the pond, oh, by the pond! I wish I had not known the hunter! Before I recognized the hunter, oh, the hunter! I was as red as a little rose. But now my beauty is gone, oh, beauty is gone! the hunter cannot be loved anymore. The hunter is on a walk with another, oh, on a walk! the girl bitterly cries in her little room. Do not go, maidens, through the grove, oh, through the grove! lest you be met there by the hunter.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little goose was flying, flying over the field, where my joy used to be. The little goose was flying, it will fly no more: it's a pity, girl, that I am not allowed to have you! When I used to come to your village, I had a little hat full of rosemary. Now I don't come to you, another one goes there: just don't think of me anymore, my dear! "The sun is setting, setting behind the hill: how then, boy, could I forget you!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little goose flew in, flew into the yard: tell me, my dear, would you like me? I would like you, but my parents won’t allow it: because your mother is proud. She is proud, she carries herself high: and I am a girl from a poor mother. Take yourself a rich one, and I a poor one: then we will forget about each other. What a poor girl earns, she has: a rich daughter’s mother provides for her. She provides them with delicate little towels, that poor girls first spun for them!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I got married, you did not: who, my dear, were you waiting for? I was waiting, my dear! I was waiting for you: for you to get married, to see how it would go. For you, my dear with black eyes: I would have jumped into the water for you. Into the water, the little water, where it swirls around: ah, what a shame a hundred times for those your black eyes!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a little dove locked in a chest, but it flew away to the wide field. To the wide field, to the green oak; there my golden dove cooed to itself. Don’t howl, don’t croon, my golden dove; don’t make your dear one’s sorrow greater! "I don’t cause it, she makes it herself; when I’m not home, she sits with others. I bought her a ribbon of changing colors, to weave through her curly hair. I bought her another, a pretty white one: so she would remember that she was my dear."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In the mountains and valleys, Jeníček is strong like a pea; and his beloved was picking pods there. When she had picked them, she sat on the little meadow; she waited, called out: "My dear Jeníček!" As many pods as she took out of her apron, so many times she sighed to herself. As many green grains as were in those pods, so many tears she shed from her eyes. "You always promised me that you would take me only after you reaped the spring rye under the little grove. You reaped the spring rye, yet you did not take me; may the Lord God give you no happiness. No happiness on your little estate for deceiving me, a poor maiden. A poor maiden—oh, how much she must suffer! She must silently endure every injustice from you."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I have great sorrow for you, girl, that you have left me, miserable little maid. When you used to come to us and loved me, I did not think you would leave me! When you left me, 1) I thank you with love: we will no longer go for walks together. Thank you, my son, for your love: may the Lord God give you abundant blessings!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Faithful friend, like one hand: I had a boyfriend, you took him away from me. You took him away from me and you are going to get married: I shouldn’t have taken you to that grass! To the grass, the little grass of our wheat field; people used to say we were like two sisters! Not so much sisters as friends: we used to weave beautiful scarves for the boys together. We were inseparable: I was sincere – you betrayed me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Merrily, merrily! Even if evil happens: the heart almost bursts and the cheek smiles. My heart, my heart! heavy as a rock: I force myself to laugh, I would rather cry!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In our little orchard, there are many bluebells: there my dear had green grass. When she had cut the grass, she sat on the path: "Come, I will caress you, my dear Jeníček!" As many hairs as she stroked on his head, just that many tears she shed from her eyes. He took a handkerchief from his pocket and wiped his forehead: Why are you crying and lamenting, my dear girl? "How can I not cry and lament myself when my belt cannot hold me? Neither my belt, nor my little skirt: I received those from my Jeníček!" He took her by the hand and led her to the shop: I will buy you a ribbon, set your skirt straight. "I would set it straight if it held; if only I hadn’t seen you!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wide, deep Vltava pools: I had a maiden, now my heart aches. I had a maiden, I had her for two years: now they have taken her away – love is forbidden. How much I walked, how much mud I stepped through: yet I did not get you, my golden maiden! How much I walked, stepped on stones: yet I did not get you, my delight!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The distant wide Stupenské field: my pleasure walks over it. It walks, it walks, it cries and laments, folding its white hands behind its head. Why do you cry, lament, dear maiden: when you were not destined for me by God. Destined by God, given by mother: do not cry, do not lament, there is no help!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: He stood outside the door, whistling to Lupínek, and the doll stood on the other side of the door. Don’t whistle, don’t whistle, you won’t be mine: you’ll spend a few more years in the army. A few years in the army, and I’ll be a maiden at home: so that everyone knows that you used to go with me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hussars, hussars! You have beautiful horses: I will ride with you, which one will you give me? Either the white one, or the black one: I will ride with you into the wide field. Into the wide field with the cold dew: I will still look at the Skuteč crew. Those Skuteč boys are fine lads: such a pity, a real shame, that they are soldiers!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Nothing saddens me here more than the lord's garden, along which my beloved walked and wept. She wept, lamented, and wrung her hands when she saw me off there for the last time. If I had known that you would die outside Prague, I would have made you an applewood chest.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Sázela had a lovely blue violet; when she planted it, she asked for a gentle breeze. The breeze did not come, only the dew arrived so that the blue violet would not wither. The blue violet is like peacock feathers: every girl is a fool who trusts a boy. I also trusted and was deceived: I lost a ring with three stones.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She walked through the woods, picking hazelnuts: "What good are hazelnuts when nothing pleases me!" She walked through the grove, picking violets: "What good are violets when no one wants me!" She walked across the field, the plowed field: "What good is the field when my head hurts!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On Kobelí Hill our little son grazes; horses walk on a silk rope for him. That Kobelí Hill is all trampled; my dear goes there all in tears. Why do you cry and lament when you still have me here: you can cry later, when you no longer watch over me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If only I felt as I once did, when crows used to ride on bald horses for me. If only I felt as I used to, when the sun would shine on me day and night. One from the east, another from the west, a third shining over our garden. The sun shone on me in a blue vest: when it no longer wants to shine, let it shine for whoever it wants!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I were there, where my thoughts are, where my pleasure will dine! He will dine at the oak table: if I were there, we would eat together.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On a green oak tree, two little doves are playing; people envy them for loving each other. People, good people! Do not envy that: it is a beautiful thing when someone loves someone. We loved each other like little doves; we said goodbye like little swallows. We loved each other, it was not long ago: and already one old woman has separated us.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I knew who was parting me from my beloved, I would sprinkle salt between his eyes. Salt between the eyes, sand between the teeth: so he would not let false words escape from his mouth. The cause was an old woman, she separated our hearts from each other. Allow it, God! Allow thunder upon the old woman, let stones rain on her for nine days. Nine days of stones, on the tenth day thorns: so she would no longer disturb faithful love.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When you didn’t want me, you should have told me, I could have not come to you for seven years. Not come for seven years, not spend money, not drag my black gelding for nothing.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On the Slíchov meadows, I lost a ducat, and there by the little willow, red ribbons. Slíchov lads! Where did you graze the horses? I lost the ribbons, did you find them? "We did not find them, we only saw them: red ribbons floating on the water." Fishermen, fishermen! Board the boats, search for the red ribbons on the water. "If we harnessed three hundred black horses, we still couldn’t catch more red ribbons. If we harnessed a thousand horses, we still couldn’t catch any more red ribbons." She sat in the little boat, wept greatly, because she could no longer catch up to her ribbons.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It was raining, it was dark, the little dove got wet, she couldn't fly to her beloved under the window. She couldn't fly to deliver the message: Your beloved asked me to greet you, Andulka. "And I will tell him that I am not angry, that he just comes to us, that I like him."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Tell me, my dear, which way leads to you? "Under our windows a tulip grows There’s a tulip under the window, by the path a little stream." – Be careful, Andulka! so you won't be deceived. If he deceives you, he won't marry you, he'll mount his horse and leave for the army. "Don't worry, my dear, I won't do that: I won't go to the army for just one girl!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh field, little field, unplowed field! Jeníček is grazing four black horses there. He is grazing them there, grazing, wading his feet in the dew: Andulka is coming to him, carrying a little boy. I am carrying your little son to you: if you loved me, love him too! Keep your little son, I will pay you: I will set tolars on an oak little table." I don't want tolars, what do I need money for? I only want you, Jeníček! I want to have you as my husband. Even if you planted as many as the algae in the pond, you won't pay me for my maiden beauty. Even if you planted as much as the hay in the meadow, you won't pay me for an honest name. Even if you planted as much as the fine sand, you won't pay me for the green wreath!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That Zbirovský, the castle 1) vaulted all around, my dear is sitting there chained. Tell me, my dear, why did they chain him? "For you, my dear, because they did not wish me to have you." What brought him there? The lords of Zbirov: so that he would abandon deceit in love. What brought him there but his young age: what a pity, Jeníček! that you have ruined my world!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That Český Krumlov castle on the green meadows: my dear is sitting there, his little feet in chains. I am his sweetheart, we know each other: let me go to him so we can reconcile. What wrong have you done, my dear, what have you committed? You haven’t harmed anyone! "I have done no wrong, only that I slept at your place: that is why I received iron on my feet."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
Young man, young man! You have a beautiful daughter: I will come for her if you give her to me.  
"Karlíček, Karlíček! I do not believe your words; you are deceitful, it shows in your eyes. You are entirely false and have a proud heart: no maiden wants to trust you anymore. You deceived me too, promised me love: I believed you, I was unhappy."  
She sat, sighing, tearing small feathers: "May no maiden trust young men!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You troubled me, my dear! You troubled my heart: until yesterday evening I gave the tolar to the music. A tolar to the musicians and I drank away the gold: I would have bought you, my dear, a scarf for that. A nice scarf for you, a new hat for myself: you must already, my dear, forget me. Forget me and leave me behind; for my father and mother forbid me. I thank you for your love, my dear maiden: why did you believe Karlíček, who spoke!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A girl traveled from Prague to Moravia, her blue eyes all tearful. She traveled from the brook to the river: "Ferry me, ferryman! I am a handsome little girl." "I won't, I won't ferry you unless you promise you'll go with me." "I don't want to and I won't go, I'm not your sweetheart, I'd rather return back again." – She sat on the field margin, cried bitterly because she could not fasten the silver belt. She picked ears of grain, tied them to her waist: "If only I had not recognized that miller's crowd! When I saw him too drunk, I should have avoided the wide field. When I saw him walking the narrow path, I should have avoided where the sun does not shine. When I saw him drinking bitter beer, I should not have gone alone on the grass!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Fly, little bird, fly, you have green feathers: every girl is a fool who trusts boys. What is there to trust in boys, like a scorpion or a snake: they have ninefold betrayal under their tongue. They have honey on their tongue, poison under their tongue: be careful, maiden, so you won’t be deceived! If he deceives you, he will not marry you, you will have no one to blame but yourself. No one but yourself, for your short-sightedness, for listening to his smooth talk. – The mother cries and the father curses, and the younger sister laughs on the side. Her friends laugh at her too: tight corset, skirt getting shorter. “I will let out my skirt, I will wear a petticoat: I will be afraid of boys till my longest death!” When she made her bed, she started to cry: “If only I had never met you, boy!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I will not allow it, I will mow down the Novodvorský grove so that the brewer does not come after my beloved.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I get ready, I will cut down the green grove so that a breeze will blow for my beloved. When I get ready, I will cut down the green alder trees so that a breeze will blow for my dearest. Below the lake, above the lake, the water is muddy: my beloved bought silk for a thaler. For a thaler of silk, for two gold coins of thread, she will sew a little shirt for her beloved. She sewed the shirt, not knowing for whom: she gave it to her beloved – they took him to the army.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I leave here, the wind will blow; my dearest will sadly watch after me. Look or don’t look, I no longer care for her: you didn't want to see me off to the Chejnice fields. To the Chejnice fields, on the Bělocka road: you didn’t, my maiden, you didn’t love me. Whether that’s so or not, I am a free man: I can love a girl, or let her be. – “I have become an orphan, like a maple in the forest: this is what I did not expect, what love brings. I have become an orphan, like a linden tree in the field: this is what I did not expect, where my beloved walks. I have become an orphan, like a rose in the thorns: this is what I did not expect, what love can do!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The water flows, it flows from the stream to the river: my beloved told me that she does not want me. The water flows, it flows to those lordly mills: it is hard to love what is not pleasing! What was pleasing has left me; and what cannot bring me joy would follow me. I am abandoned now like a little forest bird, which flies back and forth all the time, and where it would like to go, it may not. I am abandoned now like that little lark, which flies back and forth all the time, looking for a little friend. Oh my dear girl! What did you think, that your sacred promises, that you had annulled them? I annulled them, for human words: now I see and realize that you are not sincere. I annulled them, for the sake of human talk: now I see and realize that I will not be yours.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Water flows, it flows down the creek to the river: a young soldier spoke to a blue-eyed girl. Oh mother, dear mother! give me advice: a young soldier has started to follow me. "Oh daughter, my daughter! leave the soldier alone: soldiers will ride away, you will be left alone." Oh mother, dear mother! your advice is bad: I have already tied my dress, I will go with the soldiers. Soldiers, soldiers! you have beautiful horses: I would also like to ride with you, which one will you give me? "No other than the black one: he will be nicely saddled, you will sit on him." After a year, she returned to her mother, carrying a little soldier wrapped in a spotted blanket. Oh mother, dear mother! help me now: my baby will call you: Grandma! "Oh daughter, my daughter! my advice was bad: may whoever gave you that advice now help you!" Oh God, oh great God! what was I thinking that I did not want to obey my dear mother! Now I am alone, like a little dove flying into the world, searching for a mate. Now I am alone, like a pear tree in the field: when the pears are picked, it stands sad. Now I am alone, like a post by the road: only now do I realize the misfortune I have!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She didn't sleep even a quarter of an hour for three nights; she kept watching what newspapers were coming. Newspapers keep coming, one after another: that you are going, my darling, that you are going to another woman! Under the Přeštice tower my heart lies, lies, lies like a stone – love is done for!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The red apple was rolling: just don’t be angry with me, my golden girl! I am not angry with you, but I’m sorry that you go to another through the green rye. Rye, rye, little rye! who will reap you? my dear is far away, I must go for him. Rye, rye, little rye! who will bind you? my dear is far away, I must send a message for him. Grass, grass, little grass! who will mow you? my dear is far away, I must carry sorrow.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That night, as I lay in my little bed, I dreamed that my dear one was leaving for the Czech borders. Leaving, ah leaving! He will not return anymore: who will shorten those winter evenings for me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: By our spring grows a white beech fruit: that white beech fruit was my beloved. – What, my beloved, what is the matter with you, my dear heart? Did your mother scold you, or did you not sleep well? "Ah, my mother did not scold me, and I did sleep: but I long for my green wine. All the maidens are going through the grass, nicely one after another: and I, the poor little one, am rocking the baby! Rock the baby, Jeník, rock for a moment, rock a little until I wash the diaper!" I do not know how to rock, I am ashamed of the cradle: rock it yourself, with whom did you take advice? "I took it in the grafting bed, I took it with two young men: they gave me advice when they loved me."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh acorn, acorn! How heavy you are on the oak: the entire oak bends to the ground beneath you! You always promised me, when you used to come to me, as long as you still loved me, my dear. You promised me properly, swearing on your soul that our faithful love would never be broken. The love is already broken, everything is ruined: it has been cast into the deep water.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: By our lake stands a small green linden tree, and on that linden tree, on that green one, three little birds are singing. They were not little birds, they are suitors: oh, they are vying for one young girl, each trying to win her. One says: "She will be mine;" the second says: "God willing;" and the third says: "My dear sweetheart! why are you so sad?" How can I not be sad? They order me to take the old man: my heart is troubled, I cannot be cheered. And in our chamber there is a green bed: oh bed, bed so lovely and green! Who will sleep on you? If the old one sleeps there, may he never see the morning; and if the young one sleeps there, may God grant him a good harvest and health.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Shine, little moon, slowly on that lord’s courtyard: don’t think, my dear, that I would be yours. Others took the grain from beneath, I would take the chaff: don’t think, my dear, that I would marry you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Shine, little moon, gently on that lord’s grove: don’t think, my dear maiden, that I would take you. I walked through the forest, I was cheerful, singing to myself; I heard the sad lament of my maiden. She stood under a little tree, I did not see her; she threw an apple at me, she spoke to me. – Under the little grove above the small orchard grows the mayflower: where did you put, my maiden, the red roses? “The red roses have withered, the wreaths cannot be worn: do not think, my delight, that I must want you. What good is rosemary that does not smell: what good are you, my delight, when you have another! What good is lavender that does not bloom: what good are you, my delight, when you do not want me!” Under the little hill in the village was a garden; my maiden used to walk there, picking flowers. When she picked a little bouquet, then she wept: “If only I had never known you, my delight!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Shine, little moon, softly on the black forest: don’t think, my maiden, that I will come to you today. Shine pale, shine little moon, on the manor yard: don’t think, my maiden, that I will be yours. When I used to come to you, you used to cry; the lady of the manor welcomed me, you did not want me. Now you would like to, but you have no one: back then I was saddling my black horse. There stands my black horse over there, on it a white cloak: what do you say to that, my maiden? "What could I say sadly, I am to blame myself; for I am the one who caused you that white cloak!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The little cuckoo sang in the forest, in the game preserve, oh, in the forest, in the game preserve! My darling cried at home in the pantry, oh, at home in the pantry! Why are you crying and lamenting? You will be mine: when the cuckoo sings three times at Christmas. "How can I not lament if I will not be yours: after all, the cuckoo never sings at Christmas! A lovely little whim has grown in me: I had a lover – now another has him!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A pear tree stands in the wide field, its top is green; beneath it grazes a black horse, held by my beloved. Why, my beloved, do you let it graze from evening until morning? "Wherever you travel, my dear, I will go with you." Oh, I will travel far, over those blue mountains: if only I had never known what pains my heart! Oh, I will travel far, over deep waters: if only I had never known dark-eyed maidens! Oh, I will travel far, into the wide world: if only I had never known a false heart!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was walking by the forest, there was heavy dew; I sat down under a little tree, listening to the blackbird. Blackbird, blackbird, black bird! You sing so sweetly, all the girls come from the grass, and you look at them. All the girls come from the grass, only mine does not: she was cut there, she is bleeding. I will harness four horses, I will go for her; I will take her to the doctor who heals wounds. "Doctor, I beg you, heal her for me: I want to pay you for her as if she were my beloved."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Above the Danube, below the Danube, on the green meadow, there were three girls playing, they were playing for wreaths. One was Kateřinka, the second Madlenka, the third I will not name – she was my girl. I took her by the hand, took her by both hands: Come on, girl, come with me, let's float on the water. "I don't want to, I won't go, you are a treacherous beast: you trampled my marjoram in our garden." That marjoram, rosemary, blooms with a blue flower: it took away my wreath, what will my mother say! I don't have it, you don't have it, neither of us have it: if we go to buy it for ourselves, who will then sell it to us?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Above the little hill, below the little hill, there stand two maidens: I greeted them, but they did not thank me. I took one by the hand, and the other by both hands: Look, my maiden, look at me. "I will not look at you, I am angry with you: you took away my wreath, which I still do not have. The wreath, green, woven from rosemary, which I gladly gave you because you were my joy." What will you wish me, my maiden, what will you wish me, when I walk with another girl on walks? "I wish you so much bad luck that you won't live long, and when you ride on your little horse, may you break your head!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A white rose has grown for me, I will not pluck it: I loved Vojtíšek, I will love no more. I loved him faithfully, I carried him in my heart; I did not expect that love would deceive me. My beloved was walking down the street, wearing a hat tilted to the side; people told him: There you see your maiden. "Do not speak to me, people, about her, I cannot look at her; as soon as I see her with one eye, I immediately lower my head." If you could not look at me, why did you come to me? I did not ask you to love me. You had the door open to a sincere heart: but now it is closed, you will not enter it anymore! What did I do to you that you now hate me? I am the same as I was, so why are you ashamed of me? Is this now what I get for the sincerity I had for you; for you, dearest, I would have bled! Remember, dearest! your promises: that the Lord God would punish you, that He would not bless you. Remember, dearest! my young years: years I lost with you – there is a sentence after joy! I would have taken a livelihood, yet I did not give it to myself: eternity greater than the whole world, that is what I feared.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
When I walked through the oak forest, drowsiness overcame me; and by morning, rosemary had blossomed behind my head. I cut all the green rosemary twigs, set them afloat on the water, on the cold water. The maiden who will catch the green rosemary will be my dearest by the cold water. The maidens went to the river in the morning, filling buckets: then those rosemary twigs were floating right to the footbridge. Anička, the miller’s daughter, leaned over the twigs and, unfortunate little dove, fell into the water. 

1) Bells ring, bells ring three times, what could that mean? Tell me, you little birds! Surely it’s not my beloved? "Your beloved, your joy, they are laying in a coffin; four men in black robes are putting her in the grave." 

Ah, my dearest God! You have taken my bride from me! Tell me, you little birds, the way to her grave. "Behind the hill, in the little church, priests are singing in the choir; five steps behind the little church, in the grave, lies your beloved." 

There I will go, I will weep, I will sit on that dark grave, and for you, my maiden, I will bear a heavy sorrow. I will bear the heavy sorrow until death frees me, and a wreath of rosemary will be laid on my shroud!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear grazed ponies, my dear grazed horses, always just along the garden bed, always just along the garden. And when she finished grazing at the end, she turned them back, saying she wouldn't graze them, let whoever wants graze them. I'll cry out once more beyond that green forest, so my dear knows, so she knows where I am.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On one field, wheat; in the other field, rye: tell me, tell me, my dear! Which one do you pity? “I do not pity the one who has those black eyes; for he was ashamed of me, he used to come to me at night. Oh! I pity most the one who has those raven horses; for he was not ashamed of me, he used to come to me during the day.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I will go up the little hill, and I will still look to see if my boy is rafting there, I will call out to him. Come on, boy, come home, stop rafting! for my head hurts from all the waiting. I waited, I did not sleep, I waited for you: I cannot be, my Jenik, I cannot be without you! "Fly, little bird! over those forests, greet my dear there, so she doesn't wait for me, for I already have another."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Come on, boy, come home, stop plowing; my head is already aching from all the calling. "I don’t want to, I don’t want to, I won’t go, I still have a bed to plow: you didn’t even have to call me for so long."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Early in the morning at dawn, she went to the meadow to pick grass; she looked back and saw her beloved walking behind her. Come, hurry, my dearest! Let me catch up with you quickly; I have to tell you something that will surprise you. Lady Mother woke up in the morning and said: Daughter, what is wrong with you? You are losing your beauty, you are pale, are you perhaps unwell? What sad thing could I have said to answer her? I broke my back when I was carrying the grass.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I leave here, there will be great crying for me: the girls I used to love will cry for me. And I will also cry when I leave here: I will no longer love the maiden I loved. She looked out the little window, she was like a rose blossom; she wiped her black eyes, as if her world had fallen apart. Don’t cry, don’t wail, don’t lament, I will come to you soon: I will come tomorrow morning, before the sun rises. Oh, you clocks, oh clocks! What have you done to me, that you have separated me and my maiden so briefly? However you have separated us, you won’t bring us back together again: and you dear black eyes, when will you have cried enough?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The road to Prague is long, lined with trees: my beloved planted them when she was seeing me off. She planted them with rosemary, with beautiful white clover: as a sign of our love, that we will not leave each other.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Water flows from Skuhrov, rising from the banks: my dear washed her clothes there, the water took them away. When it took them, let it take them, I will not go searching for them: and that is because, my maiden! I will no longer be yours. I came to you once, you were standing at the window: you knew that my little heart was about to burst with sorrow. I came to you again a second time, you were standing in front of the house: people said well that we would not be together! If we are not, then we are not, as long as it is not my fault: I was sincere with you, and you were false.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Tell me, my maiden, tell me honestly, who comes to you, my maiden, who comes to you besides me? "Oh, no one comes to us, there is no trodden path: only the one we used to walk – and that one has overgrown. It has overgrown with rosemary, with small clover: that is surely a sign that we will not be each other's." If we won't be, then let us not be, yet we are both free: we will not fear punishment for being unfaithful. The little ring you gave me, I will not wear it: I will throw it into the stream, losing all worries. And the scarf you gave me, I will keep as a memento: so that I always remember your false love.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My beautiful black horse, with green ribbons on it: tell me, my maiden, are you sincere to me? "I am more sincere to you than you are to me, my dear: on weekdays you come to me, and on Sundays you go to another!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: This suffering, this torment, it is all for you: that I had to break seven fathoms of stone. Seven fathoms of small stones, eight fathoms of wood: my golden little mouth prepared this for me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When the dry linden tree near Nymburk turns green, come to see me, my maiden, come to see me. I will no longer walk around Mělník: I loved one girl, I will love no more.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Madam! Where is your daughter? I have come to visit her; three years have passed since I last saw her, I have come to find comfort. "Our daughter lies in the cemetery, there is her bed: do not think any longer, dear Jeníček, that she will be your wife!" As my mother spoke these words, my spirit faltered, that creature no longer exists in the world, the one my heart longs for. Madam! Show me the place where I am to look for my beloved; I will go to the cemetery, I will dig diligently, so that I may still guard her. As soon as I took a step into the cemetery, I saw a new grave there; two red roses gave me a sign that here lies my heart. I ask you, red roses! Is this the grave of my beloved? The roses bent down, gave me a sign that here lies my heart. Arise, my dear, my golden heart! Speak a word to me! "I would gladly rise, speak to you — my little heart is faint." Oh, I, a sad, unfortunate creature in this miserable world! My rose has withered, dear to my heart very much, it will bloom no more for me. Rest in this dark grave, I am going away from you now; I will never again see your rosy cheeks like the morning light! Unhappy are you, meadows of Jičín, where I used to walk, when I led my maiden, beautiful little dove, by the hand! Unhappy is the Jičín gate, unhappy the road! Because it did not allow me to love, did not permit what delighted my heart. Unhappy are such parents who forbid their children! They forbid them, do not let them marry, but cast them into the grave.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
When I used to come to you, standing beneath your window, I did not think that we would not be ours. The fairy wove wreaths for loving from green rosemary; the fairy wove, but did not shine—what did you think, my dear? 

“I thought to myself that we would not be able to be together; I thought and I think that I must leave you. They wanted to arrange a wedding for me, but I did not want to agree: that I should take for myself what my heart did not want to have. They wanted to arrange a wedding for me, but I did not want to agree: now they are already preparing it, they are already cutting my hair. 

In the monastery down in the valley, I will voluntarily lock myself away, defying this world in the flower of my youth. Come, my dear, to see when I will be singing in the choir, when they will be dressing me, pinning on the black habit. They will strip me of worldly clothes, clothe me in the habit, give me the holy cross, prepare the rosary for me. 

I will walk through the monastery, pray the rosary, love the crucifix: I will think no more of the world. Step back now, worldly bridegroom! Step forward, heavenly bridegroom! I am already pledged to you, be my protection!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What, lads, what are you doing that you don’t get up for so long? They wanted to recruit you here, I didn’t let them call you. "You did well, girl, not to let them call us: if you had called, you would have regretted it." They drummed on two drums when they were recruiting Vojtíšek; they blew on two trumpets when they were coaxing him to join them. He didn’t know what to do, whether to keep getting recruited by them: they drummed, blew – until they finally coaxed him away.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My little sun has darkened, oh who will comfort my dearest, my sorrowful little heart! It has darkened for me on all sides, that I have many false and few sincere people. I cannot go anywhere without those deceitful people judging me. Oh judge, judge as is fitting: may God reward you, you deceitful people, may God reward you on your soul. On your soul, as well as on your body, because you all, even my friends, because you are all against me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Swans flew here: oh how are you, my most beloved, oh how are you in the world? "It is better for me to be in the world than to long for you, my pleasure, than to desire you. Desire is a great power: even greater is, my pleasure, even greater than illness. In illness, I send for the doctor: but desire, my pleasure, brings me to despair. In illness, I send for the doctor: but desire, my pleasure, brings me to the grave."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The secret cause. My maiden, what were you doing that you got so scratched? – "I was mowing grass, my darling, I was mowing grass." But I mowed it too, I didn’t fall apart? – "It was dew, my darling, it was dew." My maiden, what did you eat that made you turn so pale? – "Small fish, my darling, small fish." But I ate them too, I didn’t go pale like that? – "They were bitter, my darling, they were bitter." My maiden, what did you drink that made you lose your beauty? – "Water from a puddle, my darling, water from a puddle." But I drank it too, I didn’t lose my beauty? – "It was muddy, my darling, it was muddy." My maiden, what hurts? We will send doctors for you. – "Everything, all over, my darling, everything, all over." When the doctors arrived, they immediately knew the cause: she was healthy, had a son, and was rocking him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Tell me, my dear darling! Why does your mother forbid you? "She forbids me for your sake, my delight, because you have no fields." Although I have no little fields nor meadow, still I am glad to be in the world; may the Lord grant me that I will love a girl like a carnation. A beautiful red carnation changes its beauty: then you think to yourself, my delight, that there is none above you. I have walked enough after you, I have endured enough for you: if you were not destined for me by God, still I did not get you. If you did not want me for love, I do not care for your wealth: if you did not want me, my dear darling, I will trample the path elsewhere!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In Klášter, the ponies are harnessed; in Srnín, they are rafting: spoil my dear darling there, spoil her greetings!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: One path leads to Tábor, the other is from Tábor: I will not go there by that path, I would get lost there. I will not go there by that path, I would get lost there: my dear little horse grazes there, I would upset him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Far, wide fields! Why did you turn green? When you were supposed to please me, you saddened me! My narrow golden path, I feel sorry for you! When I remember you, my heart aches in my body. Is this the narrow path I used to walk? Is this the beautiful maiden I loved? When I walked with my dear to the Hradec fields, I talked with her about love, now my heart aches! My little heart hurts, it aches, it just started to ache: because my dear maiden loves another. How much I walked for you, how many nights I didn’t sleep: when you were not meant for me, yet I did not have you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, a bird flew out of the mountain: oh, why then are you, my dearest, so sad? "How could I not be sad: when my dearest has fallen asleep to death for me!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wasps, wasps, wasps, hornets flew out at me from the rock, from the rock, from the rock, from the little rock, when I was walking the other day from the kiss.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I know a little house, 1) and in it a little window, under which I used to stand, calling out to you: Are you asleep, my maiden? "I am not asleep, oh I am not asleep! 2) I hear you well; only I may not open to you, my delight." Why shouldn't you? You are not alone, nor does your lady forbid you, my delight. If you do not open, then go to sleep with God: I will not come to you, my delight, within a year from now. Before the year was up, he came again: only that he found no love with his beloved, the charming girl. Oh dear girl, what did you think, that you had ended our love, my golden image? "I did not end it of my own fault: that false people deceived us, separated us from each other. I ended it because of human talk: that they did not wish me well, would not let me love your black eyes."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was weeding flax, I did not know what was hurting my little heart: I did not know that my beloved would break up with me. When I was weeding poppies – a hundred times a pity, a pity for that love, that it does not last until the end, a hundred times a pity!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I am a beautiful daughter from a poor family: yet I will not want, nor will I marry just any young man. Any young man who walks proudly: he would not notice, he would not notice my poor mother. My poor mother raised me with difficulty, for one groschen, for two, for one groschen, for two she bought bread. When she raised me, people torment us, both me and my mother, they trouble the hearts of me and my mother.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Ah, if only I were overseas, overseas by the cold water! I would pour myself a drink, I would pour myself a drink, a silver one. I would blow the horn, I would cry for my beloved, for the insincere one, I would call out. "Ah, my dear daughter! What were you thinking, that you would grieve so much for one false boy?" Ah! Not even that little rain, not even that little rain driving through the forest can extinguish my love. Driving through the forest, across the green field, where it plowed, when it used to come to me, my joy. I was sincere, you were false: God will not grant you happiness, God will not grant you happiness in that other marriage. God will not grant you happiness on your little farm: that you deceived me, that you deceived me, a poor girl! – You dark little star! If you knew tears and had a heart, my golden little star, you would weep sparks! You would weep with me, cry all night, because for the dowry of a wealthy bride they will separate me from my beloved.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Parting, parting! What a difficult thing it is when one has to say goodbye, when one has to say goodbye to a maiden, a young man. When we parted, oh how we cried, oh how we wiped our eyes with a white handkerchief. When we parted under the green oak: I always told you that love is not constant, that we will not remain together. "When that oak blooms with a white branch, then, my maiden, then I will bear sadness for you.
