I will carry it for a week, I will cry every day: but I will never forget, I will never forget that I loved you gladly."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Which is the cruel love when two people love each other: when they must part, they lament with sorrow. When we said goodbye in the green grove: what I endured for you, my dear boy! When we said goodbye under the green fir: unhappy tongues, that they separated us! When we said goodbye under the green oak: I always told you that we would not be together. She gave him her hand and began to cry: If only I had never known you, my boy!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Yesterday was Sunday, I was not at home: I was cutting grass in the green grove – I did not celebrate the holiday. Before I finished cutting the grass, I was stung all over: because, Jenicka! I got a false little kiss from you. Living in the grove among the grass – do not want to do this, maidens and young men with green wreaths! you may harm yourselves. Yesterday, an unhappy day, where did my beloved go? I got nothing, he went to another girl, perhaps he already forgot about me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Look forward to it, my maiden, look forward! I was at the fair today: I bought you a gift from the fair that will upset you: that you will no longer be mine. Carry me, my little horse, carry me across that green forest: tell my dear there, my sweet maiden, that I will no longer go to her from today. Carry me, my little horse, further across that green grove: tell my dear there, my sweet maiden, that I have taken another. "Reap, my lad, reap! Remember me too: that we used to desire each other, to love each other, remember that too!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Look forward to it, my dear, look forward! Tell me, do you want me? I am happy to wait for you, only if I will be able to wait for you, if you will then marry me. "And I will not marry you, I will go to the army; I will saddle a horse for myself, have a quarrel with you, and then I will forget." Ah! I should have married, and not waited for you: I could have gotten someone more faithful than you, my dearest, what a waste of years a hundred times over! "You only marry once, your cheeks will immediately pale, your beauty will change – adieu, my pleasure! I will not come to you anymore."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Cold water flows around the little garden: it seems to me that my golden kiss will leave me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, it's raining, it's raining, little dew! My little eyes would sleep, mine would sleep, yours would sleep, both of them would sleep. Oh, it's raining, raining forever! Spoil the greeting a hundred times, spoil the greeting to my delight, that I already have to leave it. I am mounting my little horse, looking back once more, to see if my delight is here somewhere, so I can bid it farewell once more.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh little path, trampled path, oh little path to my beloved! When I used to come to you, I desired your daughter, but another came there. I used to come to you, you promised, I came to you every day: when I left, immediately another came, I was deceived. A pity for the path, a pity for the many steps, a pity for the black horse, that many times, countless times, it carried me to you, sorrowful!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A bright little star shone for me when Ančička loved me: it shone – there will be no one to comfort me, oh, my Ančička! On Saturday at four o'clock I received sorrowful news: that when the moon was rising, another young man escorted her. If he escorted you, may he have you! No more voice will come to my little heart: I will have another, more sincere to me than you were. When I walk along the Beroun road, I will sit under a vine: there I will cry and lament for myself for a day and two nights. I will sit there and cry right away, so that a stream flows from my eyes, so that a stream flows from my eyes for my dearest's sake with tears. Let the tears flow down the little brook to the miller's little room, where we stood together and loved each other – no more! My little star has gone behind the woods, nothing pleases me anymore in Beroun: there was joy – there won't be anyone to love my dearest! "My bright little moon used to shine for me when my Jeníček loved me: it used to shine – there will be no one to comfort me, oh my Jeníček!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a dear handsome one, there was none equal to him in the world; my girlfriends envied him and lured him away. He always loved me faithfully, promised that we would be ours; but in a short time the love spoiled, he left me. I used to confide in you not to have anyone else besides me; you didn’t listen, you sought others for yourself, you betrayed me. After all, I do not have a heart of stone, when I think of you, boy, I remember how people spoke when they saw you standing with her on the doorstep.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In our yard, a large puddle up to the waist: that my dear one has lost her beauty because of it. She begged the lady of the manor for freedom: that she would go to that spring for water. And in that spring, the water is cold as ice: that some girls are worthy to look at!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She stood in the window, looked outside, she was like a rose blossom: she saw her beloved riding here on his horse. "Mother, mother, golden heart! My dear is coming to us: the hall and the little room are not swept, what will he say to us? 1) Mother, mother, golden heart! Go out to him: once I sweep the hall and the little room, we will call him here. Mother, mother, golden heart! My dear is riding away – along the white road to another beloved – he will no longer come to us!" 2)

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The cuckoo cuckoos, the dove coos on the green alder: do you think, my dearest, that I am the worst? Oh no, I am not, give me the handkerchief, I will wash it for you: so that you will have a white one when you go to another on Saturday evening.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: At the lord's court our Vítoušek plows, he has mottled oxen: when he finishes plowing the fields, he will call for me, he already recognizes me from afar. It is in vain, my dear, Vítoušek my beloved, it is in vain that you call me: call day and night, there is no help for you, you will not summon me anymore. I already have another sincere boy, he is dearer to me than you: do not call me any more, deeply lock away our love forever! The first two stanzas of this song originally come from Kamenický. With the changes that you see here, and with the documented third stanza, I found it as a national song in Horažďovice in January 1843.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Across those barren forests – pleasure, where are you? Distant, oh distant! Oh, only the Lord God knows when I will see you again, my golden pleasure! Rock walls, part open, level the valleys, roll down the hills: so that I may see her, or at least hear where my pleasure is!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Under the mountain a grove, beyond the mountain scrub pine, on the mountain a black tower: tell me, my dear, dear Jeníček, do you love me? "I don’t want you anymore, I don’t love you anymore, I don’t care for you anymore: I have another girl, she is dearer to me than you ever were." If you were good, you would always come to one, you would always come to us: but since you are worthless, you hold honesty for nothing and go cheat elsewhere again.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Swallow’s song. Above your barn the swallows are singing, singing, oh singing! so that I won’t come to you anymore, that they won’t give you to me, won’t give, oh won’t give!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Here you go, my lady, here you have a groš: you can keep it for now, while I am marching; here you go, my lady, here you have a groš.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I do not regret that money, unlike you, my dear, when I come to the pub and see another man embracing you: if he loves you, let him have you, so he doesn't make me miserable!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Along the meadows and along the fields, come, my dear, see me off to that pond at the dam; come, my dear, see me off, I have time now. "I would gladly from my heart, if my lady allowed; what my little heart loves, my lady forbids me."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If my dear daddy had given me a rosary and a breviary while I was a little boy, I wouldn’t have become a liar with pretty girls. "If my dear mother had sent me to a convent while I was a little girl, I wouldn’t have known my Jiříček!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The little wren peeks at the beech tree in the grove – answer me, my dear, answer me, where are you? You are the only one who wounded my heart, answer me, where are you? The nightingale sang in the green grove that we, my dear, will not be together anymore; we will no longer walk together, we will no longer lead each other, oh, we will not anymore! "I had a little dove, it flew away from me; that I would cry, it did not care: it flew away into the ferns, no one will replace it for me, oh, it won't be replaced!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
Under our windows flows a little stream – give my little horse a drink, my dear! "I won’t, I won’t give a drink, I’m afraid of horses because I am small."  
Under our windows grows an olive tree – tell me, my dear, who comes to you? "No one comes to me because they don’t care for me, for I am poor."  
Under our windows grows a rose flower – tell me, my dear, why does the world sadden you? "The world does not sadden me, but my heart aches, I would cry immediately!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She lowered her eyes as if she were sleeping, and her mother was looking out the window. My daughter! Don’t stand there, you know people gossip, that it’s a shame. “My dear mother! It is not a shame: I was making peace with my beloved; the promises are already broken, our hearts separated – I am to carry a saber.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear lady, why are you closing the gate in front of me? Come out of the house, show me your daughter's storeroom. My dear lady is looking out the window, she threw the keys into my hat: "Here is the key, you can go, you can open the storeroom yourself." As I began to open the door, I heard my beloved crying. Why are you crying and lamenting? After all, you still have me here, my maiden! We sat together on the bed; it seemed like just a little while; then the morning star appeared: May dear Lord God watch over you, my heart! The morning star rises, I must go: come with me, my maiden, to see me off; see me off, it’s time: who knows if I will return to you from the army again!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Behind the little forest, a rose sadly finishes blooming; my lady is ill, sighing mournfully: when that rose bloomed, my lady traveled with me into the world. She traveled with me through the fir forest, where she picked a beautiful flower in a clearing; she gave it to me for my hat when we sat down on a meadow by the cold river. She traveled with me across three streams, one of which was very deep; she dipped her skirt, drying it in the sun, the poor girl. She traveled with me all the way to Moravia, where she gave me a wreath from her head; there we danced together until we both tore the precious wreath. Then we danced, that was on Ash Wednesday, the musicians still played for us at the end; Nemodlenka was also played, saying that my lady will never find her wreath again.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That love of mine, which pleased me the most, has already been canceled: even if I lived for a hundred years, I would trust no boy.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: How far I walked to find you, wandered through that green grove! What do I have now for having trudged through the mud: someone else came and stole my maiden.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The meadow is green, whitened by snow: where has our love gone, where did it disappear? In a short time it changed, flew beyond the mountains, beyond the forests. "Who asked you for those gifts of yours that you used to bring me to the chamber?" I brought them, you took them: yet you loved me falsely!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You do not please me, my dear: you walk sorrowful, say nothing; say nothing, speak no word: I am afraid you will leave me. My dear gave me a farewell, so that I would seek pleasure elsewhere: where shall I seek it? When I remember you, I will weep.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Which makes my little heart in my body afraid, that a great war awaits us! Awaits, awaits my black horse, on it is an embroidered scarf. Where then shall I go, in which direction, to still see my maiden? I will go out, look back, see no one – I will be saddened. I wrote and sealed two letters, received no reply, I wrote to her, wrote – I will write no more; I will go off to war – I will be there. Over the creek the little bridge bends, grass grows there uncut; under that bridge are little fish, no one catches them – they are tiny. A gray dove flew out, brought a little letter from my beloved: my dear writes, writes, writes, that he will come to her as soon as possible. She laid her head on clover, listened carefully, who was coming; she heard the black horse’s neigh: these are the horses of my delight! “Where then, my little heart! will you go, that you do not take me with you anymore?” I will leave – will not return, I leave you here entrusted to the Lord God.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The sun rises over those forests, nothing in Klatovy pleases me anymore; it used to please me – it won’t anymore: something else will please me somewhere else. Turn, sun, over that house – we loved each other, we won’t anymore; we loved – we won’t anymore: farewell, my lady, with God!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The watches here strike from the darkness so loudly that the young men leave from here: don’t go early in the morning, I will tie my scarves, I will go with you. The young men here walk haughtily, they do not notice poor girls: only the wealthy heart they trouble, a poor maiden counts for nothing. The green juniper does not ripen, that my beloved is already leaving me; he leaves and does not take me – yet he will not find anyone more sincere!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I used to come to you through the woods, oh woe! through the woods, you used to be, my dear, more cheerful, oh woe! more cheerful: but now you are, girl, pale, but now you are, girl, pale; perhaps your little heart cannot sleep, oh woe! cannot sleep. When I used to come to you through puddles, oh woe! through puddles, you used to be, my dear, like a rose, oh woe! like a rose: but now you are, girl, pale, etc. When I used to come to you under the window, oh woe! under the window, you used to be, my dear, blood and milk, 1) oh woe! blood and milk: but now you are, girl, pale, etc. When I used to come to you to the little yard, oh woe! to the little yard, a little huntsman used to follow you, oh woe! little huntsman: the little huntsman went beyond the woods, the little huntsman went beyond the woods; now my maiden carries sorrow, oh woe! carries sorrow.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I knew that you would be mine, I would give you my ring: but since your thoughts are elsewhere, I won't even let you wear the ring. If I knew that you would be mine, I would give you my handkerchief: but since your thoughts are elsewhere, I won't even let you dirty the handkerchief!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I wish you as many chills as there are vineyards around Prague: may they shake and break you for your false loving! I wish you even more: may your lungs come out through the back of your head, the back of your lungs, beside your heart: so that you no longer deceive any maidens!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On our village green, a tall tree: so that thunder would strike that love! It struck fiercely, shattered the heart: so that it would no longer disturb love. If I do not succeed in this world, I will only lament you: that you, girl, poor maiden, left me, and then took another.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a sweetheart, now I have nothing, he gave me thousands of kisses; others begged him, until they took him away, because they envied me. I would complain, but I have no one to, I know I won’t get you, Jeníček: I will go into the woods, I will call: where are you, where are you, my dearest Jeníček! Blow, little wind,1) to the right side: that I greet my Jeníček; that I greet him, thank him for his love, for his false loving!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear, sweet little darling! You coaxed me to the palate; to the palate, I was drunk: now you must wear a white cap. If I had known this earlier, I wouldn't have welcomed you: go around, go with God: you are not, boy, worthy of my love!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: How much I have walked, stepped through mud: before I got you, my golden darling! Stepped through mud, didn’t get you, what else have I endured for you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a lover, one, oh one, there was none equal to him in the wide world, oh equal: he loved me for a long time, promising that we would be together, and now he has left me for the last time, oh left me! My little heart has been through so much, oh so much, for someone wanted to harm me, oh someone; my sincere little heart will almost die of sorrow: whoever causes suffering will not escape revenge, oh will not escape!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When the prickle, the prickle in the field was wiped out, back then it still loved me, loved me, promised: but now it has already, already, already left me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When that rascal was courting me, my dear loved me; loved me, promised that he would marry me in a year, in two, in three years. Now that rascal is losing interest: now my dear is abandoning me; abandoning me, he doesn’t want me anymore! And that’s why my heart hurts, hurts, hurts.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In Jičín they blew their horns until everything echoed, my dear cried until his heart sank; they played the march, we said goodbye: "But we will meet again soon, soon, very soon!" I wished him much luck on his way, I gave him a nice few pictures as a gift; a few pictures as a farewell: so that people would know he was my joy.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I used to walk to you through that little grove, a bright little moon would shine on the path for me; the little moon would shine for me, and I would sing to myself, I had gone a short distance when someone called. He called in a sad voice: "Stop and halt, pleasure is coming after you, it is bringing you something: it brings you a sad letter, sealed in black; it is written little in ink, more in tears."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: No one knows what pains me, and I know well; no one knows what troubles me, and I will not say: call a doctor here to bandage my little head; or I will die, and you will not know what grief I have! "Even if you were dying, I would not come for you; if the death bell tolled for you, I would not cry: because you have deceived my heart, God's revenge will not miss you; the Heavenly God will send his heavy punishment upon you."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had strength like millet on a hilltop, I will not live it; I loved one girl, I will not have her. I had strength, I did not grieve, I loved, I let go: to take with disgust, to torment myself with her until death!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I waited, I did not sleep until half past twelve: I did not expect the newspapers to arrive! The hours go nicely one after another: I did not expect the boys to be taken away from me! Oh, lovely hours! Why were you so short that you parted me so soon from my dearest, parted us in the world – you will not bring us together anymore: for you, my black eyes, will cry for a long time!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a girl in Neveklov, among dense forests; I used to go there every single day, but now I no longer go there: two others go there, but not so sincere: be careful, girl, some will deceive you! She sat there in the little window like a white rose bloom, lamenting, complaining that the world had not turned out well for her. Pity you, my dear! That you have changed so much: if you did not want to love me, the girl is already different again.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The sun is setting behind the cloud, my poor little heart aches: that you are forgetting me, my dearest little pearl! But how you will forget when you marry another: he will quarrel with you, and you will be innocent of fault. Sad times will come for you, you must sit in the corner; like a hen laying eggs, you must immediately sell them; you must go get him tobacco, whether it is cold or frosty, even if you have to wade through water up to your waist, my maiden.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Yesterday morning I received the mournful news: that my dear is gravely ill, which I did not know. Oh, what am I to do? Whom shall I ask to write me a few lines, so I know to whom I should send them? I see, I see that it is useless, that I must go there myself to be able to speak a few words with my dearest; to ask him if he wants to have a doctor? Oh, how I would dread losing him! “Don’t send me a doctor, my lady, don’t send me a doctor: rather, you, my lady, order me a fine funeral; what you would give to the doctor, rather give to the cantor: the cantor will sing for me when they carry me to the grave.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The hussars are riding, they are riding from Vienna, is my dear one riding too? I would see him, I would hear him: under him a beautiful little white horse.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear stood on a high rock, my dear stood on a little hill; she stood there so long until she began to cry: "God be with you, my lad!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wait, my dear, Christmas is coming, wait, my dear, Christmas is on its way: the path is far, the wind will blow it over: I will not go, my dear, after you any further. Which path then, my dearest, which path will I take away from you? Whether the upper one, or the lower one: how could I forget you?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It is not here, there is nothing here to delight me, it is not here, there is nothing here to please me: what used to please me has washed away with the water; it is not here, there is nothing here to please me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Everyone wonders when they see us together, where our love has gone? It has gone away, it will not return, it flew beyond the mountains, beyond the forests. The farmers from Rájov are good, honest men, they parted me with my doll: they took her to Kaplice; that is why my heart is in sorrow.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The violence of the darnel on our field, enemies have risen against me; the world spins with me, everyone is watching me, not even wishing me black eyes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What kind of farewell is this, my delight, what kind of farewell will it be with us? When we part, we will both cry, ah, how will we forget each other? "What kind of heart do you have, that you remind me, that you remind me of the parting?" Easy to forget when they prevent us, so that I cannot come to you, they will not allow it!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That was our last farewell when we shook hands: we shook hands, both of us cried, we wished each other much happiness. I thank you for your sincerity, I thank you for the love you had for me when you used to come to us; I thank you, my dear!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What kind of heart would I have if I did not remember our past lovemaking: our lovemaking came in turmoil; it is over for us now, my dear maiden! When I walked past the green grove, black eyes were looking after me there: eyes, do not look, better hide yourselves, so my little heart does not break!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I leave Vítkov, I will cry, I will look back over my shoulder: my looking back does me no good, it only makes my heart heavier. When I leave Nepodřice, I will not look back, because I have no pleasure here: pleasure, where are you? Behind the dark forests; where have you gone, that you are not here?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: How many times have I crossed the Lázecké mountains, my head never hurt: now my head and legs hurt when my beloved has forgotten me. It had to be an unhappy hour, it had to be an unhappy hour when we met, saddened that we cannot be together.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wicked path, not good! Where have you led me, wicked path, not good, where are you taking me? I am leading you to a world where the rose blooms, I am leading you to a world, to a foreign land. When they were parting, they were both afraid that they could not hold each other's hands; they held hands and began to weep: If only we had never met!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A red little rose dipped in blood, hanging on a green oak: whoever goes there will pick it: I would tear it apart, it is not possible.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Why then did you cry, my dear little doll! when you walked through the grove a week ago today? – That's why I cried, I couldn't help it: because my young lad has left me. He had been coming to us for three years, he spoiled my happiness, and I didn't trust anyone, no one else but you alone: and you behaved like a beast!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Under our window, two roses grow, under our window a tree grows; on the tree fruit, very nice – only that it is unripe, that is the problem.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If you love me, sell whatever you have to pay my ransom from the army; if you pay my ransom, you will not lose on me: then you will be my dear. "My golden little dove! If you had a little cottage, I would still think of you: but to become a servant, that saddens me, my mother will not allow it."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The little sun is setting, my delight, the little sun is already setting behind the hill: I know this well, and I am not mistaken in this, that I will not win you, my maiden. A little path trodden through the grove upwards, a little path trodden, my dear, to you: if I had known that you would not be mine, I would have trodden the path elsewhere. It's a pity, my dear, that you are not sincere, it's a pity, my dear, that you are so proud; you think of yourself that there is no one above you: but your hope will deceive you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Why were you crying then, little Hanička, little mouth, when you walked through the grove today a week ago? – How could I not cry and lament for myself when my dear boy rejected me! When you used to visit us during short nights: why don't you come to us during the longer ones? If I was sincere during the short nights, I would be even more sincere during the longer ones!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, my dearest! You are always worse, every day you look for someone else: you will go for one, you won't get that one, just wait, my boy, how you will remember!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Why am I so wicked, chased away by people? They would drown me in water! Those false people did not wish well for the boy, they made up all sorts of stories. Don’t pay attention, my dear, to human slanders, don’t pay attention, my dear, to bad advice: if you, boy, listen to what they lie, we will never meet together!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear Honzíček! Give me back the little bouquet that I gave you yesterday morning. "I was picking walnuts, and it fell into the river; the swift water took it away from me."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Everyone has a sweetheart, I have none; everyone has a sweetheart, I have nothing: I had one too, but that wicked Dorka the shepherdess stole him from me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was not at home, the girl married me; when I was not at home, I was in Bavaria: then I come home, she is standing in the house, wearing a white cap, crying at the door.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: How nice it would be to serve in the village, how nice it would be not to care: to never look at his dear one, his sweet lady? I will look at her, hug her a little, then ask her: how are you? "I’m doing quite well, like peas by the roadside: whoever comes, picks a pod, and runs away again!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Nor do you, my parents, think that I would take for myself what I do not like, what you want: but what I want, that wish for me; after all, you will not be with me until death, my parents.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Two years, three winters, my delight, that I have known you; yet I cannot examine you myself, my golden maiden: you taught me to love, yet you yourself, my golden image, did not think of love. Remember the evening, my delight, in the second May, how beautifully the nightingale sang in the grove, my golden maiden; the moon shone brightly, as in the day, when we looked, my delight, where it would settle. That nightingale, my golden maiden, settled on a fir tree: there I gave you, my delight, a kiss for the first time. From that moment I enjoyed sweetnesses which I cannot get enough of, my golden maiden. If you had been, my golden maiden, more sincere, there would have been no one in the world, my delight, dearer to me: but your heart has two wings, and it flutters here and there after all kinds of flowers that sparkle.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In our yard, everything crows, even the rooster – I cannot forget you, my delight. If I forget, it is indeed very hard: still, my heart aches for you, my delight!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Who would not love you, Mařenka, when you are just as the painter would depict you, with red cheeks like roses, no one can resist you: ah, it is a pity, a pity only that we will not be yours, my dear Mařenka, Máro! Lovely Mařenka.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that my beloved has blue eyes; it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that she has little blue eyes; little blue eyes, rosy cheeks, white little hands, nice little shoes: it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that Andulka will be mine. It seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that my beloved is not sincere with me; it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that she is false to me: she has blue eyes – she ignores me; she has white hands – she embraces another: it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me, it seems to me that she has another in her heart. It is a pity for you, girl, that you will not be mine, that you will make another happy; it is a pity for you, girl, that you will not be mine, that you love another! my longing, good intentions: all now turn to sorrow for me! It is a pity for you, girl, that you will not be mine, you sadden my heart.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Take care in that house, a maple tree grows there; the path to you has already overgrown for me, take care, all of you there! I loved you faithfully, I gave you my heart: but you had deceit in your heart, take care, my dear, there! "Only you, my dear, take care, give your heart to whoever you want: there is nothing left of our love, we will no longer be ours!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Cold water flows to the dearest one into the little courtyard, to the dearest one into the yard. Whoever brings out this water will lead my girl, the dearest one, will lead my girl. It rains water, it rains downpour: you, girl, the dearest one, you will deceive me, girl. "I have deceived five of them: I am still thinking, my lad, I am still thinking of nine. If I deceive the sixth: I will have you, my lad, I will surely have you!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was going to Tábor, I caught up with Barbora: Come on, Baruško! Come with me, so my journey will be joyful. “Before I would go with you, I’d rather not arrive today: go, go, go by yourself, I don’t care about a soldier!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I'll ask you once more, should I come to you, girl? "Come to us, boy, once a year, when there is a fair here. At the fair, buy shoes – after the fair, do not come anymore!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I grieve you, my dear, get married, I will not hold you; marry with God's blessing, since I am not your equal.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was passing by the rectory, the wheels were squeaking at me; the priest is looking at me: Boy, why are they squeaking? "The wheels are squeaking because my Ančička doesn't want me; and I pour water on them: I'll find myself another girl."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If you were a pretty girl, I would take you to my heart: but since you are worthless, I do not want you any more.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Water flows from the maple 1) all around the yard; it carried away all the footbridges except for one. Pavloušek walked on that one, leading Mařenka by the hand; he plucked an apple, plucked two, and let one float on the water. "Go, little apple, wherever you please, just don’t get wet; go, little apple, go down to my Mařenka's house." As the little apple floated by, it called out to Mařenka: "Get up, Mařenka, come outside, your beloved is standing in front of the house." "Why should I go outside, when I am not his beloved; and he is no rose blossom to ruin my world. Why should I go outside, when I told him not to come to us anymore, not to wander along that path. There was love, but there isn’t – it fell into the stones, into the small stones, into a false heart."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Peas bloom, vetch too: mother is nice, daughter too; peas bloom, vetch not: daughter is nice, mother not. Few wheats, few ryes: you were a girl beaten for me; few wheats, few ryes: I don't need to have you, girl.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Behind Strakonice, a green hill; a small cloud appeared, it will rain. It will rain a quiet drizzle: that my girl will answer me. Where shall I go, where shall I turn? wherever I turn, I overturn everything! On that upheaval, I will hang a scarf: there you have it, my dear, my sorrow!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My doll is angry with me because I wasn’t there last night. Being there last night was not possible: my stepmother forbade me. "If you wanted to forbid yourself, you shouldn’t have started coming to us at all!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: So why are you angry, my joy? You haven’t spoken to me since the feast day. Don’t be angry, don’t look for reasons: just keep well away from bad people!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Longings come to me, one after another: that I said goodbye yesterday to my beloved. You gave me a handkerchief, I still have it: when I go plowing, I will bury it. I will bury it in the black earth: you won’t get the handkerchief nor me, girl. You gave me a ring, I still have it: when I go plowing, I will bury it. I will bury it from border to border: no one will find out where the ring lies!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Jeniček, you are wandering, coming after me: I already have someone else, you don’t know about it. "She who is good has no other boys; she has her beloved and is faithful to him." Child of my dear! don’t torment me longer: if you love me, say it sincerely. "What use is it to like you: if you do not intend to take me sincerely!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Unknown girl! Come, you will come with us: we have flowers behind our hats. "Unknown boys! Go by yourselves: I will stay at home among the mountains."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You were beautiful, Andulka, before you knew any young men; as soon as you got to know those young men, your beauty immediately changed. What use is such a maiden who has three or four young men; one in the room, two outside: I do not want that maiden! The moon used to shine nicely for me when I used to visit my beloved: shine for me, moon, even today, but you will not shine for me any longer!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
Just treat me well, my dear, and I will buy you a lady’s handkerchief.  
1) “I do treat you well, my dear: you gave me a ring, but I no longer have it. I was washing my hands in the stream, and I dropped it into the water.” – Spin around, little sun, above those forests, for nothing pleases me anymore, my dear. I had a sweetheart, I loved her: but my friend has already taken her from me. Now that he has taken her, let him have her: only let him not embrace her in front of me. And if he embraces her, let it be only at night, so that my eyes do not see.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Just spin, little scarf, cheerfully: my darling is angry, I don’t know why? If he’s angry, let him be angry, I don’t care; I have plenty of such happiness everywhere. Such happiness blooms for me everywhere: under our windows in winter, in summer.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a sweetheart, but I don't have him anymore: he got angry with me, he goes elsewhere; when you get angry, just be angry: but I have another, he is sincere. My dear thinks that I will ruin him: I tie false love with a handkerchief; when I tie it up in a handkerchief, I let that love flow down the stream. Flow, false love, all the way to Prague, to one young man, to one maiden! – "I, false love, am floating in the river; I was set afloat down the brook."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The road to Radvanov is green, there is a pretty girl, she doesn’t marry; marry her, my dear, or not, what does it matter to me one way or the other!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Around the Monastery, a green hill; it clouded over as if it would rain: it rained, it rained tiny hailstones, so I have to leave away from my darling. When I come here, you will see how my sweetheart carries a scarf after me: if she carries it, let her carry it, I do not care about it, I will not be sad, nor am I.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She cried, she lamented while washing the dress, because her golden ring had drowned. "Why do you cry, lamenting over the golden ring? You must leave your parents as well." Dearer parents than both of us: my mother cared for me, I was a servant. She made my bed, carried me: you must, young men, ask for the mother. "Before I would ask my mother for you, I'd rather carry a little sword at my side. A little sword at my side, that's my woman: she will protect me when needed." The trumpeters sounded, the music played; my beloved was leaving, I was still asleep. If you had been sincere, you would have woken me, said a word or two to me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A nightingale flew in, sat on a little branch; another flew in again, sang a little song: that his dearest already has another, that he is prettier, than supposedly I am. If you don't like me, I won't break my neck: I will still marry for you; if I am ugly to her, let her not laugh: I will always get someone prettier than she is.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear served among the mountains, she wrote a letter saying that she was already married. When you got married, I got married too: I will easily marry you, my maiden.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don't sulk at me, girl, don't sulk at me too much; I am a free man, I can help myself: after all, I am not yet married to you, nor subject to those Chuděnský lords.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wait, parents, may God punish you for not wanting to give me your girl. You took for yourselves what you liked: but you won't give me what would please me! If I am the son of poor parents, I will not run away for the sake of one maiden from the town. I will not run away from the town, I will not join the army: I will not do this for one maiden!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I go, I will ride past the miller: he has a beautiful daughter, he will show her to me. If he does not show her, I will not beg: I will stab the little horse, it will carry me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I wander from Prague to Buda, I will drink red wine there. The little red wine jumps from the glass, my golden darling cries sorrowfully. Today she cries sorrowfully, tomorrow she will have another: hey, innkeeper, pour some red wine! Red wine is good to drink: may lightning strike false maidens in the heart!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You will not, my dear, you will not be my wife if you have ribbons hanging over your shoulders. Ribbons over your shoulders, a jingling on your shoes: I will not come to your room anymore!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On our field, two little rosebuds stand; I am still far away, but one already smells to me. One already smells to me, and the other is blooming: that you must, my dear, go far into the world. Far into the world, beyond those blue mountains: how my heart aches for you, oh my dear! If I were a little bird, a winged swallow, I would glide over your little room. Not just over the little room, but over the stables: just so I would know what my dear is doing. First, carrying water, then grooming the horses; the third time, writing a little note at the table. For whom, my dear, are you writing the little note? "For you, my dear, that you will not be mine."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A swallow is flying over our little house: I did not expect that my beloved would leave me. Whether he leaves or not, I will not die for him: I only tie such boys with garters. I do not tie, I do not tie, but I will tie: I will do it to spite you, my beloved.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I am poor, a poor mother: I still will not open the door to just anyone. Before I open it, I will ask him: is he worth opening it for? If he is not worth it, I will answer him: that I have no interest in such a worthless fellow.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Chlumecký little castle 1) made of small stones, my dear passed this way, he did not greet me. If he did not, let him not, is he perhaps angry with me? If you want to be angry, let us both be angry. Let us not speak even half a word to each other: am I perhaps a poor girl to you? If I am a girl of a poor mother, I still would not go out the door for you. Do you perhaps think that I would say for you: and I would tie your love to a rag!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I am sorrowful, I care for nothing, I will buy cheerfulness: for you, my dear, I will not sadden my heart.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You would be beautiful, but she is not here through you: only a pity, a great pity, that there is no money! "You ask about money, whether I will have it: first ask me whether I will want you! If you are looking for rich girls: I do not want you, even if you had thousands!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
I will tie the little horse to a dry oak: don’t kiss me, my dear, when I am not here.  
I will tie the little horse to dry wood: don’t kiss me, my dear, when it’s not necessary.  
I will tie the little horse to a dry little fir: don’t give me, my dear, false kisses.  
My black horse stands by the little fir; my dear beloved is making mischief against me.  
I wore myself out when I was sharpening the scythe: a hundred times a pity that I begged you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Why are you asking if I have much money? It's none of your business, don't pry into my chest. Why are you asking if I have a large dowry? It's none of your business, I won't be your wife.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The stewardess of Pušpers has a handsome son; she told me she would not let me have him. I told her, for her love, to tie him at home with a string. At home with a string, on a silk cord, so he wouldn’t deceive poor girls. Poor girls who go through a lot: they have to listen to all kinds of people.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: So what if you swear you won't marry me? Marry me or don't, you are not the only one in the world! You are not two, nor five, when the world is full of you: marry me or leave it be, I will not cry for you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Varvažov Castle, stone upon stone: my dear girl sent me a message for a single kiss. And I will not send it, nor give any: she would then think that I am angry with her. "A barren tree in the forest: God, remember! what it is to be loved when Honzíček does not want it!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I suspect, I suspect, I suspect that I must leave here: the one whose doll I loved, I must leave her. I will leave her lightly, and if I have none, I will run up the little hill, look toward the mountains.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don't come to us in your coat, wear a vest, for I know well that you have another girl! You spend the whole evening with her, you only stop by at mine; you have all your love for her, to me you just say so. "What is it, my dear lady, what are you guessing?" Oh, my dear Jenik! You have smooth cheeks. – Your hair curls into tiny little rings, and your falsehood shines from your eyes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On the little hill, a tree; beneath the hill, a meadow: oh, when I remember my dear dove! When I think of him, day or night, the whole world spins around me. How hard it is to die when there is no pain: even harder to love what is not pleasing. What was pleasing has left me; what is not pleasing comes to me by itself.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A torn-down cottage, the sun shines into it: my beloved told me that she does not want to have me. If you do not want to love me, 1) do not trouble my little heart: after all that I have walked to you, now I must stop!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little dove flew out from the cliff, waking the black eyes from sleep. If the black eyes had not been sleeping, they would have caught the little dove. It was not a little dove; it was a bird: if you do not want me, my dear, then let it be. If you do not want to love me, do not want to: someone worthier will court me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The gardener went into the garden with a shovel, with a shovel, he dug there a bushy marjoram, bushy. It was not marjoram, it was horseradish, it was horseradish: but my dear lady, we will not, we will not! "Whether it was marjoram, or horseradish, or horseradish: if you don't want me, my darling, don't come here, don't come here."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The little wheat in the field is not yet swaying: wait for me, my maiden, another three years. "Why would I wait for you, when you have another: you wear a red ribbon behind your hat from her."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A long way to Ounětice across that little lawn: come with me, girl, to see me off, the moon is shining. "Why would I, my dear girl, see you off? When my dear mother forbade me!" – The magpie jumps in the garden, looking for a grain: don't think, my dear, about my little heart. The magpie jumps in the garden, looking for a barley ear: don't think, my dear, that I will come to you again.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What are you, little crane bird, flying above me for? What news do you bring me that you are chirping so? "I bring, I bring you news, I bring you bad news: that your dearest of all is seeing another." If he sees another, let him see, I do not care about him; I will not bear sorrow for him, nor do I have any. Why then would I be sad and troubled: when I have lost such a boy gladly!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I came to you only once, you didn’t want to let me go: your eyes were like a cat’s, God forgive me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You didn’t want to lend me the scarf, nor do you want to give it to me: you must, my dear, be very afraid of your mother. “Not so much of my mother as of my father, because he used to care for me when I was little.” What’s the matter, my dear, why are you so proud? You’re not married yet: take good care of yourself so you don’t remain that way! “If I remain, then let me remain, as heaven decides for me: I will never cry, my lad, because of you!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I will no longer drag my hobbies for you: you will return my gifts to me, my maiden. “How I still have all your gifts to return: if you are angry about them, I will give them all back to you. Since I do not wear them and do not care about them: if you are angry about them, I will throw them out the window. You bought me a ribbon and gave me a little bouquet: I gave you a scarf and a kiss. That kiss is worth more than that ribbon by a grosh: don’t haggle, my lad! after all, you are the worst!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Bloom, bloom, rose, little rose! That I loved you, rejoiced with you, dear little pearl! Saddle the horses, hand over the sabers: call out my beloved, my charming girl, once more. He mounted the horse, began to cry: return to me, my beloved, charming girl! What have I done to you? "You did, you gave me one little ring: and from it, my golden girl, you plucked out a stone!" I did not pluck it out, it fell out by itself: and that as a sign, my delight! that you will cry. "Why would I cry when I have no sorrow: still for you, my delight! a little tree has not grown. A little tree has not grown, apples do not ripen: still for you, my delight, they will tie up my head."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That wicked miller’s wife has a pretty daughter: kneel down, boy, in front of the house, pray to the Lord, and she will give her to you. “Oh, she won’t give her, won’t give her! 1) she hides her: she thinks that four pairs of black horses with golden bridles will come for her.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a nice sweetheart, and now I have none: I'll ride my horse and go to town, I'll trick another one. I'll trick another, much more sincere: you, girl, don't call to me, you, girl, don't call to me until the longest death!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I walked past the gate, I saw my beloved standing there; as I walked by, my heart ached – oh, woe, what am I to do! I will go there once more, and I will beg the lady: dear lady, please ask father to give me his daughter. "I cannot give her to you because you are a soldier; my daughter neither knows how nor understands how to march with soldiers." If you do not want to give her to me, have her painted: put her behind glass, and when you feel lonely, go look at her.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The green borovička stands, woven around in a circle; beneath it, a little horse, on which Jiříček rides to Brno. Stop, little horse! And eat some grass, I will go visit Anička: it all seems to me that my beloved is unfaithful to me. Thank you kindly for your love, oh my dearest image! I will no longer come, I will no longer be an obstacle to you! "If I have forgotten my brother, I will also forget the gate where we stood, often talking together until morning. If I have forgotten my sister, I will also forget the path I used to walk, carrying flowers in a white apron. If I have forgotten my father, I will also forget my heart, and you, my dear, my beloved boy, even you. If I have forgotten my mother, I will also forget love, you, my dear, my beloved boy, my precious image."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Who is coming to us? I don't know him; I won't let him dirty my little hands: let him buy gloves so he doesn't dirty my little hands.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: There is a small grove over there, there is a grove over there, over there is, my dear, a cheerful land: I will give you nothing, look elsewhere, I do not care about you. "If you leave me, I will leave you too: for another is already stepping on my heels, even kinder and more tender than you are!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Sleepiness. Oh, shine, bright little moon! Will you come to us soon, Jeníček? "I won't come, I won't go, I won't love you, little angel. I came to you yesterday at noon, and you were just getting out of bed: such a doll will never be a housekeeper!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It was raining hard, there was mud; yet I used to come to you, I didn't care about it: the more sincere I was, the more deceitful you were, I took that for granted. The nightingale is a small bird, it cannot be caught; a young man cannot be cheated by his maiden: before I let myself be cheated, I'd rather join the army; the army is free, the girl is deceitful. Do you then think that I am in grief? I still am not asking for your daughters: there are plenty of maidens everywhere, like juniper berries wherever I look.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wait, my dear, I will tell you that you didn’t care what time it was: the clock in the monastery struck four when the young men accompanied you. You gave me a scarf, I still have it: when I go plowing, I will tie it; I will tie it, sow a mixture: I will get another pretty maiden.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: So what do you think, that I won't get married because I don’t have a handsome fiancé? If I don’t have one, I’ll get one: I won’t let you deceive me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What I was looking forward to the most, that love has been canceled for me! It was canceled in a short time: but it will come back again soon.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear girl, I don’t want you: I would have to wear a white cap, a white cap, without a drawing pin: I wouldn’t have as many lads.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Under our windows, there are two ponds; my dear little black horses swam there: he sat on one, knowing well that he would go after another – he did not tell me. Yet my little heart told me well that my pleasure went elsewhere! If it went, let it still go: I do not want you, false boy!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: But I heard it well, Jeníček, how your lady mother scolded you: all of it because you come to me, because I am not rich like others. If I am not rich, I don't pretend to be; therefore, I will not let anyone cheat me: whoever wants to have me will want me because I know how to manage properly. Yet my mother did not let me go here and there to be deceived by anyone; not in any carriage, nor in the thorn bush: she raised her Anička strictly. Whoever wants to come to us, whoever wants to have me, must politely ask my mother, tip their hat, kiss her hand: "Give me your Anička, lady mother." The following composition is a later necessary addition: Before I would ask the lady mother for a daughter, I would rather carry a little saber at my side: the steel saber is my wife, it will comfort me when I am in the army.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That enemy Turk is rising: the emperor says he will go against him; we friends will go too, we will leave our lovers here. "I have already been orphaned like a turtledove who has been deprived of her mate: oh dear God! grant me strength so that another young man could love me!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My beloved is angry, I will not go to her, I will not beg her for a word; if she does not give it to me, another will: open up, little mouth! if you are sincere. "What are you, boy, what kind of fool are you: I made the bed for you, you lay down; I made the bed for you with a white quilt: you got angry, you went to another!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I went to Prague to see a maiden, she made a bed for me out of oat straw: oat straw – such a fair maiden! Since you have made your bed, lie in it yourself. Whether it is this way or that, somehow it will be: the world will not perish for one creature; the sun does not shine for one flower alone: yet I do not have to have you, my dear. Did you think, my beloved, that I would cry when I no longer walk the path to you? One maiden, tip the hat aside! For you, my dear, I will get plenty. It is cold, it is cold, I tremble with cold; open up, my dear, I have something for you: I bring you love tied in a scarf, because I want nothing from you as a keepsake!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Around Polička the water flows – my beloved told me that he no longer wants me: if he doesn’t want me, he doesn’t want me, I don’t care about him; I will not carry sorrow, nor do I have any. Why then should I carry sorrow for you, when I never truly loved you? I loved – but only in jest: you didn’t know, girl, what a beast I was. A nightingale flew out, sat on a fir tree, sang a lovely, beautiful song; beautiful, lovely, oh! charming: that my dear boy is already seeing another. If he is seeing someone, let him! I don’t want you, since you married wearing the same shoe on both feet – the same shoe without a spur: but I, my dear, do not need him!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My beloved thinks I will cry when he walks the path with another: I would be a fool to cry, to trouble my heart for one creature.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Under our window an olive tree grows: tell me, my dear, who comes to you? "The coachman comes to us, he has black eyes: don't come to us, Jeníček, I prefer him!" It is easy for you, my dear, to sulk when you have no work, only to preen; only to preen, to look out the window: it is easy for you, my dear, to blow your horn!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: And I always wonder, why does my little head ache? And I always wonder, why does my head hurt? My little head aches, my darling forgets; and I always wonder, why does my head hurt? But I certainly will not fall ill for you, I certainly will not be sick for you! I will not fall ill if I don’t get you, girl; but I certainly will not be sick for you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If Penčice were beyond Voděrady, I would give you a kiss on both sides: but since they are beyond the water, beyond the cold little water, I won’t give you a single kiss!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If you had, my maiden, a hundred sheep, and I only a juniper behind my hat, you would not be mine, it is not possible, nor will God grant it to you, my maiden. If you had, my maiden, a whole field, and I only a little garden like a palm, you would not be mine, it is not possible, nor will God grant it to you, my maiden. If you had, my maiden, a hundred homesteads, and I only a tattered barn, you would not be mine, it is not possible, nor will God grant it to you, my maiden!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Above Pušperk, that little hill is adorned, people used to walk there, my delight; they walk in the shade, carrying beer in a glass: when he was supposed to come to his girl, he came from the shade. It didn’t take long, he poured again; when he was supposed to toast his girl, he gave another to drink: “I’m doing this out of spite, I won’t even let you drink: so that everyone knows I don’t love you.” If I had known that, you hypocrite, I wouldn’t have drunk that beer from the glass: you have few rich people here, you mock the poor, remember, hypocrite, 1) that you have nothing yourself!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You think, my dear, that I have to have you: that is still up to me, if I want you; there are plenty of girls in the world, I can easily manage without you, I can convince myself of ones different from you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Protivín Castle, beneath it a brewery, they brew beer there like water; a girl told me that she does not want me: if she does not want, let her not want, it's no loss!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Which way, which way is the path to Vílov, which way, which way is the path to you? The path is far, the wind will blow it away: dark-eyed girl, you will not be mine!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It's a pity, my dear, that you are not sincere; it's a pity, my dear, that you are so proud: you think of yourself as flawless: yet your hope will deceive you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You pay little attention to me, my dearest! You pay little attention to me, my beloved: would that you were more careful not to get someone worse than me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a girl like rosemary, I had a girl like a picture, an angel is in heaven and a rose on earth – if you do not love me, may thunder strike you down! For one creature the world still stands, for one creature does not perish: I will go to the land where my happiness grows, there I will persuade myself another girl.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In Tábor at the market, they sow rosemary, in Tábor at the market, they sow oats; the green oat, my beloved is angry: he didn't come to us yesterday – don't come today either! What, my God, is such a great distress if I anger one boy? I will twirl a handkerchief, three, four times; I will not bring death upon myself for you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Why are you coming here, what are you coming here for? I don't want you, why are you coming here, what are you coming here for? I don't want you: if I did want you, 1) I would forbid you, even if there was a moon at midnight.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A grudge for a grudge against someone into our house, a grudge for a grudge against someone into our gates: your steps are bad for my feet, I will no longer chase after them. I thank you for the love, I wish you happiness, I have already let you go from my heart; now my heart is locked like a castle for you, I have thrown the key into the water.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Everyone has a boyfriend, I have none, everyone has a boyfriend, I have nothing: if I wanted one, 1) I would have one too, like that Andulka, the steward's daughter.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would be bad for your son, I would be bad for the little son: he is looking for a rich woman who has ducats; he is looking for a rich one, I am poor.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When you loved me, you always promised me that you had four houses in Netolice: you barely have one there, without thatch, without windows; you want me to marry into a barn!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Here he’s looking, listening, poking his snout, here he’s looking, listening, to what I say to him: what would I tell you, you horrible creature! Go away, clever boy, go to hell! I have endured many such rascals, who always say: "in two years!" when two years pass, he runs after another: you clever boy, go to hell!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Aha, aha! The path to us is long, aha, aha, the path is long; overgrown with reeds: I do not beg you; aha, aha! the path is long. Aha, aha! The path to us is long, aha, aha! the path is long! overgrown with clover: my dear, the wolf take you; aha! aha! the path is long.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The little moon is shining, I must go to my dearest in Rájov: the little moon set, I did not reach there; I had to stay at Třísov. Our love used to be beautiful: when it ended, what came of it? God grant luck, pretty girl! But another little rose also smells sweet.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You are not as you pretended to be, you are not as you make yourself to me: you have a false heart, you deny it in vain; you are not as you make yourself to me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Marry, marry, my dear Jiříček! Marry, marry, I do not care about you: I do not want you, I am not preparing to marry; marry, marry, I do not care about you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You young men are all about recreation, you young men are all about fun: you go to pubs, you cheat on girls; you young men – may the devil take you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Which way, which way, which way is the little path for my Honzík, which way, which way, which way is the little path for my dear? He is looking for a rich one with bows down to their feet; but a good girl is not vulgar, she is not his!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I don't want to, I won't come to you, you didn't want to open for me; I don't want to, I won't come to you, you didn't want to open for me: neither the latch nor the little door, you put them on a chain; I don't want to, I won't come to you, you didn't want to open for me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Leave it. When I came to your house, did you really think I had no one else? If you don't want me, leave it, you don't have to love me, you don't have to love me more, my lady! When I came to your chamber, did you really think I was not your equal? If you don't want me, etc. When I came to you, you were proud; you never once came out to meet me: if you don't want me, etc.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don’t believe, girl, don’t believe anything, I am a boy from Nechanice; don’t believe, girl, don’t believe anything, you won’t be mine: today I love one, tomorrow I promise another; I am like a bird – I love, then I leave it.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Dorota went to the mill, he met her – oh my God! he met her, Vavřina. "Come, Vavřino, come to us, I will gladly – oh my God! I will gladly watch over you." She came home to her mother: "Prepare, mother – oh my God! prepare dinner, mother. For dinner, a jug of beer: he will come to us – oh my God! Vavřina will come to us today." "But I will give you Vavřina, if I take – oh my God! if I take the spring. But I will chase that match from the cart – oh my God! from the cart!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: For a worthy young man, we have benches, and for the useless ones, stools without legs. For a worthy young man, we have glasses, and for the useless ones, pots are broken.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Revenge. What are we to do, Matěj! Matěj! when the girls don’t want us? Don’t want us? “Let’s leave them, Martin! Martin! after all, they’re just riffraff, riffraff!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You see, girl, you see how you are cheating me: I won’t let myself be cheated – I’ll rather cheat you myself!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I have a girl from Rokycany, I haven’t seen her alive; she is an old-fashioned girl, she doesn’t fall in love.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh Jesus, oh Jesus! They have stolen our Zuzka; if they wanted to return her to us, I would be willing to work a week for her. Not just a week, two weeks, I would give a year-old calf for her; from the calf will come a cow – from our Zuzka an old woman.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was seeing the girl off, someone was throwing clods behind me; whether they threw or didn’t throw, I was still seeing her off.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I didn’t know what to do, to ruin three garden beds in one day: one of millet, another of poppies, and the third of parsnips.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I am a poor lad, I have a few little shirts: my sweetheart is ashamed of me because she always sees me in the same ones. Don't be ashamed of me, girl, I will buy some tomorrow: two thin ones, two thick ones, but God won’t abandon us. Even if I am without a cap, as long as I have a pretty girl; even if I am without a hat, as long as I have a lovely girl. I am a man dressed, my coat turned inside out five times: take me or leave me, I have only one coat. If I were the son of a farmer, I would dress nicely, but since I am the son of smallholders, I wear a coat without buttons.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: At White Mountain, a peasant plows, he has a pretty daughter, give her to me, God! Hey, coats, coats, give her to me, God! Hey coats, coats, coat. If I got her, how I would boast: I would boast three hundred thalers with her! Hey coats, coats, etc. A beautiful wife, three hundred thalers: I would have her carried in a covered carriage; hey coats, coats, etc. Footmen in front, footmen behind her; I would have her called: a noble lady! Hey coats, coats, a noble lady! Hey coats, coats, coat!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I will not marry you, my dear, because you look like a bear: a bear has hairy little legs, and you have a pierced little heart.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If you were a girl like any other, you wouldn’t be all filth: you don’t even know how to cook peas, and yet you want to cheat a boy!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I told the reeve: she is scratched by the shrews.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Who wouldn't love you, my dear, after all, you are, as if painted by an artist: red cheeks, white body – as if flown out of a chimney!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear always told me that she would share her heart with me: when she wanted to share, she didn’t have a knife; I handed it to her – but she no longer wanted to!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The Hradčany clock strikes nicely, so that the young men leave from there: if my beloved goes, I will go with him; if he has something in his little bundle, I will take it all with him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I love you, Honzíček, I love you: I promise you everything – I give nothing. I will do everything for you, even undress you, polish your stockings and shoes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If you didn’t want me, take Rasa; you’ll have a piece of meat every day: on Sunday, on Monday, and on Tuesday again, so you know that Rasa loves you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On Mount Tábor, at the farmer's, the girls were fighting over a soldier. My dear girls, don’t fight: here are some pants, share them. One grabbed them and ran away; the other cried because she had nothing.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The merry girls walk very proudly here: they were digging up spices from the black cherry; drying them on the stove, rubbing them in paper, so that the Vesečky lads would want them.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The Vondrojic maidens are very proud: they beat the tomcat under the black cherry tree; they boiled his front, they roasted his back: no wonder these proud maidens got cowed!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The cheerful dolls think to themselves that the count will come for them in a carriage: here he comes – here he comes – it has two little wheels, a little smokestack at the back, and a little horse in front.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Marjánka Součkojic is tying a ribbon; she buys cosmetics, applies them to her face: red around, white in the middle, like a boy's face she watches, she immediately likes it. She didn't want to do anything, just kept sweeping; she received a letter, read it for a week: after reading the letter, she bought a wreath, gave it to her beloved for one dance.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The one who threshes a lot, who reaps a lot, she is a good girl, she suits me. The one who doesn't want to thresh, who doesn't want to live, for her let the little Jew come.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I dreamed, it seemed like on Sunday morning, that the music was playing, there were few lads. There were more maidens: 1) three hundred three thousand; they stood in three rows – all gossips themselves!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Tekla the blacksmith knelt by the window, washed herself with milk to be beautiful. But she is not really beautiful, her beauty changes: her tight corset – the boys laugh at her!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: That Mary the tailor, a big show-off: she had a rabbit embroidered on her back. On the back, a fox on the apron: so that they would recognize her, 1) as the innkeeper's wife.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Andulka Hájkovic, she is a good girl: she does nothing, just looks out the window. On Thursday she sews, on Friday she does the laundry, on Saturday she rolls it out, on Sunday she takes it.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh my dear God! What distress I have: I had a beloved, he came to ruin me. I had a beloved, he was from the neighboring village: he climbed after me into the attic, the chickens ate him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not let me, my dear, do not let me carry a weapon: you have a lovely little mouth, you can beg me. Do not let me, my dear, do not let me perish: you have a lovely little skirt, you can wrap me up.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Daddy, daddy! There is mischief in me: take away the spring, drive it out of me. Dad took the spring, Janeček runs away: are you, Janeček, a great little rascal!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Those Cekov cows have little grass, they have bedraggled whores – they would like to get married. One has a blacksmith, another a gingerbread maker, the third stands on the street waiting for a shingler. When she finally got him, she gave him a little kiss: "Look, boys! On my crossbow, I am already married." One got married, the second remained as she was, the third lamented that she had missed her chance.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: By our pigsty, a bunting sings to itself: that from great love, little often comes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A well-meaning intention. My dear laziness! Do not overcome me: when I marry, I will give myself to you completely.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don’t come, son! Don’t come to us, I don’t like to see you: your coat is torn, I am ashamed of you. Don’t come to us until Sunday, until you have another one: then you can proudly say that you are my dear. Don’t come to us until the holiday, until you have clothes: then I will gladly look after you, the lady mother too.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The clerk used to come to us from the office, his hat was crushed like some kind of emperor. In one hand an inkwell, in the other a pen: he wanted me to write him into my little heart. If you were a young man, as I am a maiden, I would give you the wreath that I wear myself. My little green wreath jumps on my head: and your honesty cries in the cradle.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Honesty, oh honesty! Where do people find you? You are not born in gardens, you are not sown in fields. If honesty were born in gardens, every maiden would be honest!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would sing, I would sing, but I don’t have the sheet music, my skirt is torn, and wicks are hanging from it.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I was standing there on the balcony, laughing at our Kačka for dyeing her hair black just so she could wash it.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Duck! Duck! come home, they are taking your boy to the army! – "If they take him, they have him: may God give me another one!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When was that short night, back then there were many ghosts: through the sky walked, pretty girls haunted.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The moth on the balcony often pushes our Důra; Dad came, chased the moth away, the moth no longer pushes Důra.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
