translation: In Pušperk, there is a small courtyard, with few girls there: one bigger, the second smaller, the third like a little pot.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I was born by the stove, by the stove, my grandmother covered me with a kuthan, with a kuthan.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My little maiden wandered to Hungary, to Hungary; she burned her middle white ribbon on the coal, on the coal. My little maiden wandered to Germany, to Germany; she burned her middle white ribbon on the pot, on the pot.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: No one knows what love is who hasn't experienced it: whether old or young, one must go crazy!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Above Pušperk is a little hill, on it black soil, they distilled spirits there three times a week. None of them succeeded except for one, after which Adamojice Marje got drunk. She went out through the little gate: Boys, come to us: our gate creaks, I am soaking it. I am soaking it with warm milk, sweet cream: so that it never creaks when the boys come to us. Mother got up in the morning: "Daughter, who was here?" When it was you, she did not recognize him, I will not tell you. There was no beast here, nor a boor: but it was the boy I love!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: None is as sprightly as Lidka Hájková: she runs up the hill, calling the boys. "Come on, boys, come to us! I'll gladly watch over you: our little door doesn't creak, I water it diligently. In the morning with warm milk, in the afternoon with cream: so that our folks don't know that handsome boys come to us."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Water flows against water, the wind blows into it – a blue-eyed doll is looking out the little window. Don’t just look out the little window, better come out in front of the house: if you give me a little kiss, I’ll give you seven. Before I give you one, I’d rather give you a hundred; so that people won’t say that I don’t love you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear went to the orchard, caught a sparrow there; she put it in her handkerchief, saying she has a boyfriend.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A pear tree stands in the wide field with green leaves, rejho-rej, ritum-rej, with green leaves. Beneath it stands my maiden with red cheeks, rejho-rej, ritum-rej, with red cheeks. Her beloved passed by: Greetings, God bless you, girl! rejho-rej, ritum-rej, greetings, God bless you, girl! She thanked him: “May God give it to you, shoemaker! rejho-rej, ritum-rej, may God give it to you, shoemaker!” If I am a shoemaker, then you will have a shoemaker as your husband, rejho-rej, ritum-rej, you will have a husband. “First, you must sew me shoes from mosquito skin, rejho-rej, ritum-rej, from mosquito skin.” You must sew me a shirt without thread, without a needle, rejho-rej, ritum-rej, without thread, without a needle. “You must build me a castle on a cabbage stalk, rejho-rej, ritum-rej, on a cabbage stalk.” The stalk will wither, the castle will fall, and you must be mine, rejho-rej, ritum-rej, until the longest death.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In Varvažov, on the little hill, there are few sour cherries: there are little dwarfs here and they are very proud. One is a dwarf with short legs, and he has begun to choose among the local girls. Where the shoulders are, there are knees as well, a belly like a basket: God, please preserve us from such a soul! Any dwarf with short legs would want to choose beautiful girls for himself. After all, he has legs like sticks, hands like axes: may the Lord protect us from such a creature!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: He came to court me, didn’t even say: God bless! He thinks, ragpicker, that I’ll marry him right away! After all, he has nothing else but those two sheep: and who knows if they’re his? We’ll ask father. Dad says they are, that they are, that they are very fat; that they’re well fed – they’ll be for the cabbage!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I left Moravia, I had new shoes, I had a paper coat, straw trousers. I had a hat made of cobwebs, with a mouse feather on it: if you don't want me, take whatever you want, my dear darling!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I went to Moravia to fight with a sabre, my hinges broke, I had to return. I would like you, I would like you, 1) you are still little: I will wait for the one who in two years will be knee-high.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not choose, do not take over, so that you do not take over too much, so that you do not receive what you would then dislike. The count will not come for you, nor the lord of the estate: do not think so highly, here is my advice for you. If you are thinking of the governor or the burgrave: you will not get a pensioner, nor an overseer. When the clerk loves you, he will then give you a kiss, he will write you a note: "Look for someone else!" And when, after all others, the day laborer finally comes; then she will gladly take him, otherwise she will be left with nothing! As they loved him, so he will be too; they will go together to those lordly threshing floors. – Finally, after all and everything, the shepherd came to her: If the maiden wants to marry me, I will be her husband. "My lord is no servant, he is a chancellery official: he carries a pen on his shoulder when driving out of town. He takes the pen in the morning, making the sound: hoo, hu! get up, girls, drive out the cows, oxen to the herd!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I went for a walk through two alleys, I took a new hat and white gloves. I met two young girls there, they were standing together: I greeted them, they laughed at me. I took them by the hand, both hands: Come, Tonička! Come, Pepička! Let's sit together. "But I don’t want to, I won’t go, I am angry with you because you took my ring, now I have none." Be silent and don't speak, after all, you're not alone here. "And you’re not a young man, like I am a maiden!" Let me not be, let me not be, but I was: as long as I did not come to you, my Pepinko.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I was just away from home once, and misfortune happened to me: I had a blue-eyed girl, but the water took her from me. And I will go to the monastery, I will pray to God to return her to me, the backflow water.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, my dearest God! How cheap it is this year: a bushel of men for half an apple, and even then pale ones, and two bushels for a whole one – still quite cheap: one woman for five thousand – not even available yet!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I’ll do as I say, I won’t be without a sweetheart: I’ll run to the barracks, I’ll bring my sweetheart! “The devil, girl, not a sweetheart, the devil, girl, you’ll get: a soldier marches from there, and then you’ll be left alone.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If you were a pretty girl, I would take you to my heart: but since you are worthless, I don't want you anymore.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: By the monastery stands a stone, a doll sits on it; she calls out: Boys, come here, I am no longer in the monastery. Nothing pleases me in the monastery, the world is dearer to me: and I like to dance, I love handsome boys.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a nice sweetheart, I loved him dearly: as soon as he knocked on the window, I opened it for him. "If you have a nice sweetheart, see him off politely: but if he loves you falsely, throw stones after him."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I lost my whistle near our meadows! Johana with long legs found it, tip-tap tip!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In that little room on the white bed, my dear, a cute girl, is weaving a pretty little flower. And in that flower, a dove coos: that she is my dear, a cute girl, a big beast. "You are even bigger, my dearest! You go after others, you hang out with others! You are a bigger beast!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When you come to us, do not go alone: take a friend with you, someone I like – and you stay there.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You boys, you are! You would persuade: you wouldn’t leave an old goat next to the cart.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I do not advise any of my friends to climb the stairs at night to a lovely girl. I only did it once, I won't do it again today: I fell down the stairs headfirst, barely managing to crawl away!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If the Lord God grants me what my heart desires: such a bird that jumps behind the plow, with blue eyes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: There by those manor gates I stood there a hundred times: they have a nice girl there, plump all over – I like her very much. In the back she is chubby, in front she is flat; she has a wattle on her neck, lips three inches wide, and a hairy head.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A little boy walked this way: a brimmed little hat, buttons on his sleeves, a bow under his chin, a tassel under his knees.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My doll is beautiful, she threw me out of the stable, threw me down, called me an ox – that is exactly why she is beautiful.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My goodness, my child! I like you very much, even your appearance, that you are a nice person – only that you walk with a limp.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The girl went to the grass in the lord's garden, the boy followed her; she picked some grass and gave it to the little cow to eat. When she milked it, the cow tossed her into the trough; the stewardess said that the cow was a heifer.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: But I know my Marjánka, she has a shop in the pub: she sells radishes, has a sharp little mouth; but I know my Marjánka, she has a shop in the pub. But I know my Marjánka: she has a scar on her forehead, a drop by her nose, a little mole on her chin; but I know my Marjánka, she has a scar on her forehead.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Káča has, Káča has a lace-up boot with fringes: whoever wants to dance with Káča must not dirty them; Káča has, Káča has a lace-up boot with fringes. Káča has, Káča has a lace-up boot with wheels; whoever wants to love Káča must pay her back 1) a pair of horses, a pair of oxen, and finally a barn.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Káčo, Káčo, little Káča! Káčo, my child! you gave me a little flower, a handkerchief, a little kiss: surely you are not, Káčo, surely you are not mine!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Unfortunate is the Krumlov Castle! Unfortunate is the Krumlov courtyard! Jeníček passed this way, broke a nail: one whole and half of another!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What those of ours have shown me is that they have lovingly tied me; they tied me with a woven thread: it broke, and it came to me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was walking through Prague in the Lesser Town, I met a little girl all dressed up; I spoke to her in Czech, she in German: may the goat take you, naughty little girl!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Clover, clover, green grass: The Pušpersky lad is overwhelmed by a whip; but not all, only some: if I wanted to speak, I know which. And that Adamojic, he's exactly one: he lined his hat with paper; a hat with paper, silver buckles: – but I don’t want you, you fool!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: At Saint John’s, a small little hill: that there are few girls in Litvfnojcích; few, very few: but they are nice, not as clumsy as those Vessky girls.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Under our windows grows an aspen tree, and our Anče is like a hoe: wherever she goes, she sleeps there; whatever is good, she eats it all; in my life, I have never seen a girl so greedy.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The ring is already being cleared out– cleared out– swept out; my lady is already leaving me– leaving me– leaving me: if she doesn't want me, let her leave me; I don't want her, the id– the id– the idler!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In the mountains, they sow peas; in the valley, lentils. A gentleman came to us and took a liking to a cat: the cat was rich, she had four kittens, kitten – kitten – kitten – kitten – kitten – kittens.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My parents keep asking me where I left the shirt. I left it on the landing when I was looking for Madlena: I didn’t find Madlena, I lost the shirt; my parents were angry with me because I hadn’t come back for a long time.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don't go to Pušperk, that little village: there are few pretty girls there, they don't have featherbeds; I came to see one, she was sleeping on hay, her head was wrapped in a torn apron. And I complained that I was very cold; and she told me, "Go, lie down in the hay." But I didn't want to, I wouldn't go, my mother would laugh at me for bringing a handful of hay on my jacket.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh dear, it hurts! and I don't know what is wrong with me: a caterpillar crawled into my hat; I am inflamed with love, my heart like a flame: caterpillar, climb out of the hat!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Which way, which way, which way, which way to the manor garden, which way, which way, which way, which way to the manor gate: the gate is closed, wedged with a straw: which way, which way, which way to make love to the girl?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was walking away from Nandlí, I tripped over the roots, it was my luck that I was barefoot: if I had been wearing shoes, I might have been killed, I would have smashed my nose to pieces.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Once again I will go to my dearest, once again I will go to my beloved: if they drive me away – I will go to another, even if there is mud up to my knees.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Káča has fifty-five on her dress, Káča has a tolar on her dress; the tailor took her measurements on a sheet of paper, the tailor took her measurements on paper.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: And I always cry for my Kačenka, and I always cry for my Káča; Káča has money written in the book; and I always cry for my Káča! And I always cry for my Kačenka, and I always cry for my Káča: Káča has a heap of treasures in the henhouse and I always cry for my Káča!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I want no one but Káča, Kačenka, I want no one but Káča: how much I will weep for you, my dear Kačenka, how much I will weep for you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I loved you, girl, now your boyfriend has you; I loved you, girl, now I don't want you: I will find a girl like that on the street, a girl like you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I am all distressed for my Herman, I am all distressed for my dear. "I am completely distressed, wearing linen trousers, I am completely distressed in linen clothes."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Take me or don’t take me, I have only one coat; take me or don’t take me, I have only that one coat: it has been turned three times already, it still isn’t paid for: take me or don’t take me, I have only that one coat.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Tell me, ragpicker, does the poplar also bloom? Tell me, ragpicker, does the poplar also have flowers? The poplar blooms in winter, in summer, just so you know, ragpicker! So you can see, ragpicker, that the poplar also has flowers!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Daddy! Play. – "Son! What shall I play?" – We have a little hen by the stove in a fluffy little basket. Daddy, play! – "Son, what shall I play?" – We have a grandma by the stove in a little fur coat, curly.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Give me, God, a little son who plucks the harp, so that he plucks a tune for me in the evening by the window. Evening by the window, daytime by the little table, so that everyone knows I have a little harpist.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She stood by the cimbalom, sulking until I dropped a tolar into the cimbalom. A tolar into the cimbalom, I drank away two zlotys, and I even bought myself new shoes. New shoes for myself, slippers for the girl, so she would give me sincere kisses.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Farther and farther, farther and farther, I will buy you a dulcimer; when there is time, I will also buy you a bass; farther and farther, farther and farther, I will buy you a dulcimer. –

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Bass. I dare, I dare to earn something. Violin. We also dare to earn something. Bass. I dare, I dare to earn something.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Royal duetto. The king, the king played the bagpipes, the queen meanwhile creaked on the bass; the king and the queen played the bagpipes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: These are bagpipes, bagpipes, these are bagpipes! As soon as I hear them, I become completely joyful, entirely, entirely, entirely.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Strakonice bagpipes whistle everywhere. Whistle throughout the entire town. Kačer’s bagpipes whistle everywhere: in the ravine and in the forest, wherever Kačer carries them.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The Strakonice bagpipes roar that a girl lends me a scarf. "I lend, I lend – or nothing: I am a girl from Strakonice." In Strakonice, behind the altar, the bagpipes lie along with the piper. Whoever does not believe it, let them run there, for the bagpipes are still lying there.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Is it true or not that the piper is floating on the water? How could it not be true? said the blue-eyed girl. Go, people, go with your hooks, pull him up along with the bagpipes. They killed the little piper, they did, 1) who will play the bagpipes for us now? There is still one like him here: may God preserve him for us! Play, little piper, play! or give me those golden bagpipes. "Before I give you those golden bagpipes, I'd rather play them for you." Play, little piper, as well as you can, you know it well. "How could I play well? I haven't even blown the little pipe yet! When I have blown the little pipe, then I will play well."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Just keep playing the bagpipes for me nicely, bagpiper, and I will pay you like no one else: I will give you a kreuzer, and also a toonie – you will have such money!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: He went this way, had bagpipes, but didn't even play a note: may the imp smash his bagpipes to pieces!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I know a little bird in the forest, in the forest, in the forest, carrying spotted eggs: green, red, white, just as each one likes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Violin and bagpipes. To the bagpipes! to the violin! to the violin! to the bagpipes! That is why I bought bagpipes, to charm girls with them. To the violin! to the bagpipes! to the bagpipes! to the violin! That is why I bought the violin, to charm girls with it. 1)

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Musicians! What are you doing? You have beer and you are not playing. Play the violin for me, cheer up those girls. Play the bass for me, cheer up all the crowd. Play the bagpipes for me, cheer up everyone everywhere. Play all together for me, escort me all the way home.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Musicians, play for us, I will give you a penny; I will pay you if I don’t lose it – otherwise, I won’t give you anything.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hey there, gentlemen musicians! Courage to all, and to me too: whatever I have in mind, that's what I'll sing to you. I had a girl next door from the village, I loved her, but I don't want her anymore; I loved her for two years – love is over now! Two sweethearts follow each other, one goes to her, the other leaves her: she loves them both, and love fades! After all, I can still choose rosemary, carnation; from thorns comes a prickly rose, like a deceitful maiden. Thorns prick the hands, and a false maiden the heart; I don't want a rose from thorns, and I'm done with a false one.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Play for me, musicians, and I will sing for you. Play me a song: that they took Ančička away from me. I don't mind that, I will tilt my little hat to the side. After all, there are plenty of girls everywhere, like berries on the juniper.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Play for me, musicians, merrily, let the little heart in my body dance; play, musicians, trumtrará – so that my darling has no worries!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Play for me once more to the health of my girl, to the health of the girl, to my health, because we sincerely love each other.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: We are boys at liberty: like those little fish that are small, in clear water.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: We are the boys from Veselí, we love music; we make money from lead buttons.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: We are their boys! From the Guard, we have no livelihood: only our good name, that we behave well. Come on, boys, friends! Be very cheerful: twelve girls for half an apple, twenty-four for a whole one!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When you have no money, don’t enter the pub: show-off musicians will cut you up in many places.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: As far as I am alive, I have never seen such a fool who would go to a pub when his pocket is empty!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Who dances a lot, pays the music, and is welcomed into heaven by the holy angels.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: All the saints were dancing, with God among them; Saint Peter was jumping so high he had to hold onto the beam.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On the little hill in the pub, the music is playing: let's go see who is dancing there. Soldiers are dancing there, they have white uniforms, green epaulettes, officers too.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Dance with me, I'll give you a cake, dance with me, I'll give you two. Our mom baked them yesterday, and I hid them for you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The bailiff's daughter from the farmyard told me yesterday, hop! from the farmyard, that she has a skirt with a hem decorated all around, hop! all around.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not be angry, mother, that I wander: I am seeking pleasure that I do not have at home. But you, my mother, also used to wander until you had pleasure at home.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Spin, girl, spin, as long as I have you in my hand: once I let you go, I entrust you to God, let whoever wants have you. If only I were always as I am now: I wouldn’t come to you and beg you, you proud little girls!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The lads from Hrádek each have their own girl: the music hums, each one jumps a little, each one is with his own.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hajho geese from wheat, hajho geese from rye! The little girl is better than the big one. The little one spins by herself, and the big one cannot: she is waiting for the suitor to help her.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: To the circle! Hop, Anko! Mařenko! to the circle, to the little circle: which is spinning, there it thrives, long flax, beautiful flax, and golden hemp.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Heigh-ho! Heigh-ho! Janek used to be there: he lost his cap at the tavern in the village; Anka is looking for it, saying she won’t give it to him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Those young girls are dancing, the old ones are standing by: tell them, lads, to say goodbye so they go home instead. "We won't go, stubbornly we won't go, we'll stay right here: we'll flirt forever, those who are still young."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In our village, there is a new gate, but no one has passed through it yet: four maidens are lamenting that they are not taken to dance. I would take one of them, she cannot pass by; I would take the second one, she hits me with her knee. I would take the third one, she does not want to come to the cimbalom: she has little feet like rings and a chin to the side. I would even take the fourth one, she sees poorly: if there were a crowd of people, she would hit each one!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The merciful dancer. When I come into the pub just in time for a dance: if I hadn’t asked you to dance, you would have had to stand; you would have had to stand in the corner, watching the young men, like another, false girl!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It doesn't matter about the raftsman who dances nicely: look into the stable, how he cleans the horses. The horses are dirty, the groom is dirty too: may you be struck by lightning, what a rascal you are! It doesn't matter about the girl who dances nicely: look into the pigsty, how she cleans the cows. The cows are dirty, the milkmaid is dirty too: may a goose kick you, what a girl you are!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, my God Almighty! I have a headache because my pleasure is angry with me. If it is angry, let it be angry, I have no sorrow: play, musicians! that my dearest is not pleased.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I know, I know, I know which one is the young man: he spins in the circle like a wreath of roses. I know, I know, I know which one is the maiden: she spins in the circle like rosemary. I know, I know, I know which one is the wife: she spins in the circle like a little dove. I know, I know, I know which one is married: he spins in the circle like a wealthy gentleman.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Plant, Honzíček, plant the horses, we will ride them to the Golden Crown: at the Golden Crown they are playing merrily, young men and girls are dancing nicely. Dance nicely with me, spin nicely with me; I will buy you a scarf so you have a reason: a red scarf, my love is in it; if you love me, then take me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She is pretty, kind, dresses neatly, comes into the pub, after all, she is standing there. Some German woman,1) maybe a fool, comes into the pub, she is spinning around!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Which is all the same to me, girl, when you ignore me in the pub, in the pub, in the pub in front of people, as if I were not your sweetheart!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was little, my mother did not let me attend music or enjoy its pleasures: but now that I am grown, I know what music is.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The doll who is rich, she has a sweetheart; but I am of poor parents, no one pays attention to me. Yet I dance in the tavern from evening till morning: the rich girl is angry with me for courting the poor.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Because those Bavarian German women are dancing, because those Bavarian German women are walking: a Bavarian German woman dances on the wooden floor until her skirts fly off.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: How far will you escort me, Bavarian German girl? "Just a little beyond the water, I cannot go further; beyond that, my dear, you know the path."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Who has succeeded as you have, Vávra: the goat enchanted your eye, not long ago. Who has succeeded like that weaver: the goat enchanted him, the kids went to dance.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Why wouldn't the goat butt, she has horns; why wouldn't the girl jump, she has legs; take the little goat by the horns, lead her to the pen; take the little girl by the hand, lead her to the dance.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Our Káča is crying, what is wrong with her? Thieves stole her skirt; they also stole more from her, the lacing and the shoe; that is why Káča is crying.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The cornflower danced with the poppy, the water sprite with the parsnip, and the garlic with the onion, and the hemp with the tow.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Parsley bed, carrot bed: we won’t go home until the harvest is done.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: We will not go home until morning, until the beds are made, we will not go home until morning, until it is day; when dawn breaks, we will go for breakfast, we will not go home until morning, until it is day. We will not go home until morning, until the music is over, we will not go home until morning, until it is day; when the morning star rises, the music ends, we will not go home until morning, until it is day.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I won't leave the pub, I'd be a beast! Not until I get myself a girl. Once I get her, I'll go home and leave the pub to God.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I will not leave the pub until dawn breaks, until my dancer escorts me out. I will not leave the pub until the day is bright, until my delight escorts me out. My delight, my golden little lady, until she escorts me out before our gate. I will not leave the pub until two weeks later, until my beloved comes for me in a carriage. A painted carriage, four black horses: until my delight takes me away. I will not leave the pub until four weeks later, until the wolf takes all the old maidens. The old ones will be taken, the young ones will grow up, like clover growing with the dew. The clover with the dew is nicely mown, like a girl dressing up for a holiday. Dressing up for a holiday, adorning herself on Sunday so that the young men can see her in church.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The monastery pub nicely painted: who painted it? Young men with maidens. Young men with maidens, men with women: with my dearest white hands.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: This local pub made of small stones: if you have no money, don't go in. Even though I have no money, I will go in to court the dark-eyed girl. This local pub is very tall; a wild little goose flew over it. It wasn't a goose, it was goslings: the local girls play from above.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The little goose was flying, flying from above, she couldn't fly over, she fell into a stream. She fell into the stream, drank the water: so that the innkeepers wouldn't pour into her beer. They didn't pour into the beer, rather into the spirits: because the local maidens are jealous of her.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: But it will not be so for us when we get married; it will not be so for us when we become wives: then we will sit at home with the children; but it will not be so for us as we ought!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I go to the pub, I am not afraid of women – when I don’t have one: once I have her, then I won’t be allowed to leave the house anywhere.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Learn, girls, learn to prance: young men will want you, young men will love you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I like Honzíček, tadl-dy tadl-dy dydl-dá, he plays with me, tadl-dy tadl-dy dydl-dá. I prefer Venclíček, tadl-dy tadl-dy dydl-dá, he courts me, tadl-dy tadl-dy dydl-dá.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The swivel is better than the swivel rod: my doll has a mouth like sugar, like poppy seeds.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Play me a rejdička, I don't want a rejdo, because it was danced by dirty cobblers.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Come, Honzíček, onto the razor, come, Honzíček, onto the razor: I am small, you are big, I won't come to you. I bought a razor in Prague, it wouldn’t shave my beard; I convinced a girl, she didn’t want to love me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Lend me some money, girl, I'll send you something home; lend me some money, girl, I'll put something in it for you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Ring. The peasant does not understand what iron is; the peasant does not understand what a ring is: a ring is iron, my dear Tereza! Such iron is a ring.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You Vorlický little church, you stand on the rocks; my joy has died, it already lies on the bier: the bier is red, my girl is pale: she drank the strong liquor, she got drunk. And I always, and I always, hear the ringing here; my joy is already going to the grave: gravediggers, leave her be, for she is my girl: she drank the strong liquor, she got drunk.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Peter, Paul! What do you want from me? "A basket, a basket of salad, a basket of salad." – Where would I sadly get it, I don’t have, I don’t have a garden, I don’t have a garden: once I become a gardener, I will give you salad, give, give, a full basket, on my vow! I’ll cut it for you, cut it for you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Wait, I'll tell on you that you were at the tavern, at the innkeeper's tavern, wait, I'll tell on you. "Brother! Don't lie, say it was someone else."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I was strong yesterday and today: I will no longer, false girl, I will no longer lovingly love you; I will no longer, false girl, I will no longer love you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Even today I will sow oats, oats, even today I will sow oats: I scattered oats, yet it did not sprout for me: what is this? I will sow rye, rye, what is this? I will sow rye.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Little onion. Poppy, poppy, little onion, poppy, poppy, onion: she used to sit on the log, little onion danced, poppy, poppy, onion!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: And I have hope for our Matěj, and I have hope for Matěj: hope ran away with Matěj to hell, hope ran away with Matěj away!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hop, girl, take off your coat, take off your coat, we'll play the drill, play the drill: the girl did not take off her coat, she danced the drill instead.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hop, girl, hurry up with your shoes, hurry up with your shoes, we’re going to work, to work! The girl polished her shoes until the shoe squeaked. Hey, girl, you hiccupped, you hiccupped. "Not true, the shoe squeaked, the shoe squeaked: I haven’t hiccupped today yet, you lie through your throat like a dog."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Andulka went into the cabbage, into the cabbage, into the cabbage, Andulka went into the cabbage, into the little cabbage, she picked leaves, leaves, leaves, she picked leaves, little leaves; Pepíček came to her, broke her little basket: hey hey hey! hey hey hey! you will pay for it.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The maidens were walking down the road, down the road, down the road, they were met by two young men, two young men. Where are you going, maidens, where are you going, where are you going? Which one of you will be mine? The little one, she is mine, she has eyes like me: the little one, she is mine, she has eyes like me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Shoemaker. Two dolls were dancing, the shoemaker was dancing; one told the other that no one wanted him. They danced him, they didn't know how; they came home, they were learning him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Run, Káča, run, the big tomcat is chasing you! Káča runs as fast as she can, the tomcat cannot catch her.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Come here, girl, come here, come there, I'll ask you once more, I'll ask you to the music, do you have a faithful heart for me? How many scarves did you give me, that you kept reproaching me for them? You gave me only one, and I didn't even want it yet!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Around Třeboň, horses are grazing there. Give the mares, I tell you, give the mares oats.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Play for me my Lito– Lito– Lito– Lito – Litoměřice song: for the health of Kačena, who has cows frightened, 1) a green skirt.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Like this in Rokycany, like this in Rokycany they dance lightly: like this in Rokycany, like this in Rokycany they work little by little. My dear mother! If I give him a kiss or two, that he would give them back to me. "My golden child! He would cheat you: he would give you a kiss, leave you behind."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The soldiers were walking along the road, I saw them, and what they were saying to each other, I heard. I danced the Hanácká, play the Hanácká for me; I tore my shoes, give me new ones.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hussar. Gladly – gladly – I would gladly get married: I would marry a hussar with red whiskers. I have three hundred gold coins, the hussar – I will give, give, give, I will give them to him on my crossbow.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I had a dear rogue, a rogue, I loved him: I had a silver ring, a ring, I gave it to him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What kind of fellow is that with the white apron, constantly hopping around there, following your daughter; now he’s holding her, now he’s got her, now he’s hugging her there: madam! be careful, so he’s not behind her.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Kalamajka mik mik mik: a chimney sweep got married: whom did he take as his wife? Pastejř's Mařena.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Polish woman. I am a Polish woman, you are a Polish man: let's dance the polka vigorously.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Uncle Nimra bought a chestnut horse for half a fifth of a thaler; he came home, grabbed his wife, and danced the maděra with her. Our master Matěj went out to the ball, went through the kitchen: and all the girls there doused him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Cook, girl, cook, cook, scribbles will fall on you; cook, girl, cook, cook, they will fall on you: if you do not cook, you will regret it; cook, girl, cook, cook, they will fall on you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Not me, you bought me new shoes, not me, you bought them for me: you bought me new shoes so I would have them for work; not me, you bought them for me. Not me, you bought me shirts, not me, you bought them for me: you bought me shirts so I would wear nice white ones, not me, you bought them for me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I have a thorn in my left leg, I have a thorn in my left leg; in my right one, I have a thistle, the saddler will take it out for me: I have a thorn in my left leg, I have a thorn in my left leg.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I leave Běta's, I'll stop by my aunt's: she has, she will give a piece of lard for the shoes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I am a lad, you are a boy, I am not afraid of the boy: I will take the boy by the shoulders and throw him over my head.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Look, girl, look, the guys at the Klášter pub beat me up: all this is for you!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Dawn is breaking, dawn is breaking, little dawn, I am still in the pub: the horses in the stable are neighing, who will then comb them?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My family keeps asking me where I was yesterday, why I didn’t groom or feed my horses? My black horses in the stable were neighing: and we were still shouting with my girl in the pub!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would sing, I can't, I don't want to be silent; I would go home, I can't: the wolf is sitting in the way.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Who will see me off when I leave the pub? No one, girl, no one, no one, since I'm not known here.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was leaving the Mělnice pub, the bridge broke beneath me; how much I called out before I managed to get through: my dear, come help me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Little Pepíček, where did you put the horseshoes? "I hammered them on when I was seeing my sweetheart off from the pub."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I leave the pub, I will saddle up, I will tie my horse next to the gate. I will tie the horse by the bridle: so that you know I am coming.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Everyone is asking, who are they playing for? – For Janeček, for Holeček! Don’t ask anything, just cheer for him – for Janeček, for Holeček!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: So, young man, why don’t you get married? You don’t even know how to court! If you’re up for a beer, I’ll tell you: I know of a root in the meadow. Alder, elder, oak leaf: whoever wants to get married must eat it. I got married as soon as I ate it: I got a little girl, she is like honey.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You Láz girls, what will you give us? We will advise you to get married. We advised the Rojice girls: they all got married within a year! Pick some wormwood, tuck it behind your head. I also had this remedy: I got nicely married within a year.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: A young man is lucky, he can get married: but a maiden is not the same, she must wait until some scoundrel ruins her.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The girl who is pale should not get married: the one who is like a rose can get married, from her comes a beautiful woman.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: In our little orchard grows a spice: those who do not get along should marry. Marry, Jeníček! Take me for yourself: if you have one trouble, you will have two.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Little wheat, little rye, little rye, we must not get married, get married; the barleys are green, we must be without a wife.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Boy, blue-eyed boy! Do not rush those years: these years will be military service, and a woman will cry for you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hemp grows, grows, grows by the road, and it is nicely green; among it grows a black-eyed girl, when she grows up, she will be mine. My young son, listen to my advice, give up that marrying: you will marry once, your cheeks will pale, your beauty will change. Addendum: Taxes, contributions, and levies will come upon you, you must go to forced labor: if you do not go once, the bailiffs will pick you up, and you will still be beaten with a stick.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: It’s all the same to me since I’m a single man: just wait until I get married one day, it won’t be the same for me then!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What do you care about a little woman, sonny, she has a skirt down to the ground; and when there is dew, she walks barefoot, she tears her whole skirt.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: One poor woman sent me a message, two rich women sent one: I prefer the one poor woman over both of those rich ones. The rich one is only pretty as long as she sits with her mother: and when she gets away from her mother, she barely cares about dirt.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would like to get married, but no woman wants me; I would court, but it is difficult for me. Maidens and widows alike, each one fears that I might beat her with an oak stick. But I am not a Turk, I am a Christian: if she were to ask me, I would certainly stop.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would like to get married, but I am afraid of women: maybe she would nag me, lock me up at home, that is what I am afraid of.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would like to get married, but I am afraid: if I marry an old woman, I will shortchange myself. I would like to marry a young woman, but it is not certain: she desires others, flashing her eyes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
My dear mother, I am getting married.  
"My dear son, you will regret it."  
My dear mother, I won’t regret it.  
"My dear son, I can see that already."  
My dear mother, she is rich.  
"My dear son, she is rude."  
My dear mother, she sews nicely.  
"My dear son, she drinks a lot."  
My dear mother, shirts.  
"My dear son, jellies."  
My dear mother, like a goddess!  
"My dear son, she is German!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear little mother! I am going to get married, I have a nice girl, I will go for her. "My golden little wheel, 1) you intend to get married? You will regret it, you do not know what you are doing! Just wait and see what will come your way: when you have to pay the old woman and the priest. You will give the old woman two gold coins, the priest a ducat: you won’t be stomping in the pub, little Honzík!"

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear Matoušek! You have a dog in your little hat, and courage you carry in full pockets: but when you get married, you will change your beauty, and then, Matoušek, you will lower your eyes!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Káča has money, I will marry Káča: I will drink away the money, I will drive Káča away.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: On our hill, I saw a fox: if only the Lord God would give me a wife Liduška! On our doorstep, I saw a crow: if only the Lord God would give me a wife Márjána!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Beautiful German girl! You have a daughter: I will not leave here, my dear German girl, until you give me a little daughter, a daughter.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: What are our people doing that they won’t let me marry? I need a wife as much as a coachman needs a whip. The coachman can manage on his own, but no one can help me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When everyone got married, I also got married: I send my wife to work, and I lie at home myself.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I have stacked two loads of cut grass, I have stacked two loads of hay: if I had married this year, I would have had a wife who rakes.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If my fields were near Sedlice, I would still get married: I would go to the pub, get myself bagpipes, and persuade a girl.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Say: "Yes, I want to have you, to live faithfully with you." I will earn the money – I will let you spend it.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Young men, what do you want with my girl? Young men, what do you want with my maiden? May God grant on Sunday that you all know your path is forbidden.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: They said I was dead, and I was not yet wailing: they gave me an old woman – I would give the old woman to the devil! An old woman is like a frog in winter, cold in summer: a young girl is like a featherbed in winter, warm in summer. We give the old woman a rake so she can rake with it: we give the young girl a boy so she can love him.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not take a girl who is old, so she does not crawl behind the stove too soon; if she crawls behind the stove, she cannot get out: when you are not at home, who will help her?

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don’t take that old woman for money, my girl: when you leave the altar with her, you will be ashamed; when I go with a young woman, you will go with the old one: you will look back at me sadly, my girl! And when you come to the tavern, you won’t be allowed to dance; your grandmother will be threatening you from the door: “Come, husband, come to bed, leave the beautiful girls: if you wanted to love them, you shouldn’t have married me!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If Liduška would just listen, I would gladly lead her to the altar; but since Liduška only jokes like that, she would consider the old man a complete fool.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Grandpa, grandpa! How did it come to you that you must love old frost? Old frost from old shingles: you must love a little every day.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Give it a rest. Give it a rest, old man! Give it a rest: you’re completely broken, tied up with ropes, give it a rest! Sit on the penny, pray the rosary, give it a rest!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: No one knows how I feel when the old man hugs me: as if I were eating sloes and blackthorn, lying down in sharp thorns, that's how I feel, ah! that's how I feel. No one knows how I feel when the young man hugs me: as if I were eating sugar and almonds, lying down in powdery feather beds, that's how I feel, ah! that's how I feel.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, if only I were a nightingale, that little bird. I would fly over the mountains to my dear one in the chamber. I would listen quietly to why my dear sighs. She sighs for the young, dislikes the old. The old one smells of sweat, like nettles on the fence. The young one smells wonderfully, like violet and rose.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Better is a life than oats; better is a young man than a widower: the young man lies down on a pillow, and the widower remembers the deceased wife. Better is a little beer than water; better is a maiden than a widow: the maiden lies down, says nothing, the widow has the deceased husband in mind.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I have never seen acorns on a beech tree: why would a young man leave his maiden for a widow? After all, the maiden is nice, white, red, red, and the widow is hunched over, hunched over at both ends. I have never seen juniper on the meadow: why would a maiden leave her youth for a widower? The young man is nice, white, red, red, and the widower is stained by a woman, by a Woman.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Grandmother said I should marry a widower: a widower has half a heart, half of it he gave to the deceased; a young man has a whole heart, he will share it with me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I do not want a young man, I want a widower: I will inherit the clothes from the deceased; all the clothes, including the caps, I will not need to buy any.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: No girl wants me because my little pocket is empty. I will go from one girl to a widow, I will fill both pockets. In one, I will put peas to scare the girls. In the other, I will stuff cabbage so that the girls will want me.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Above Chrašťouci on that little hill, they are building a feeding trough because they have old maidens there, and they will feed them. The Kolín woman gave a skirt, the blacksmith a coat: so that they would not put any there, as they will get married.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: True love. I, people, I have only loved for fun; now I truly think I must go to the rectory. Father! please, I carry my hat in my hand: leave off that fun, marry me to my beloved.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: When I was walking from the forest along the path, I met a pretty girl, modest, not tall, graceful, with black eyes. I began to ask her if she would be getting married soon. "I would get married, 1) if my mother allowed me. My dear Jeníček, handsome, when you leave the pub, go home, I would hear you, it would warm my heart."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Under the oak, behind the oak, there were one or two red apples; she gave me one; she didn’t want to give me both, she started making excuses that she didn’t have them, that she wouldn’t give them, that she was worried about them. I planted the wheat; I will not live by it; I had beer poured; I will not drink it: I courted a girl, another man stole her from me, and so he put an end to our love.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: The groom is a lawyer. That gentleman lawyer bought a horse for three hundred, and a carriage for two hundred, to go to the bride: to which one? to which one? to that Lenorka, the merchant’s.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Rosemary. (Tune 442.) Rosemary grows on that Prague bridge; no one waters it there, it will not grow. When I walk by there, I will water it: grow green, rosemary, 1) I will get married.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I am a young girl, I would like to get married: when I don’t have pillowcases, put feathers in them too, what advice do I have? “Don’t worry about that, just love each other: sew with straw, pour in noodles, don’t be shy about anything!”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not fear, my dear, do not fear want, for I have money hidden in the ditch; hidden in the ditch, covered with turf: do not fear, my dear, we will not perish. Do not fear, my dear, do not fear hunger, for I have coins hidden in the orchard: coins in the orchard, I will go for them: just wait, my dear, until I find them there!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Hančičko, round world: wait for me for a hundred years! “I don’t want to, I won’t wait, I won’t wait for you: you are a little servant, I would get married right away.”

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Do not marry, my maiden, until you are one hundred years old: when your teeth fall out, they will no longer hurt. A grain of oats grew on the little hill: just bring it to the barn for me, my dear.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: All the girls are already going home from the grass, but my little lady is still sleeping: if she sleeps like this when she gets married, what kind of happiness will that be for her!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: You don't have me yet, and you're already bubbling at me; once you have me, then you'll beat me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Briefly, I swear I will not marry, that I do not have a Damascus bodice; if I do not have one, I will get one for myself: I am playing that tune to spite you, girl!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: She was standing in the garden in a nice corset, and her skirt was striped, her cheeks like a cloth, a bonnet with ribbons: you will be a beautiful woman, girl!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear mother! Advise me quickly, should I court, should I marry? "My dear child! They would torment you for not even knowing how to make a bed yet."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Reference. My dear mother! What will happen to me, what will become of me when I get married? "Do not worry, my child! Neither be glad: your beloved will tell you, you must not ask me."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Don't worry, just get married while you are very young; just get married, don't worry, while you are young: once your roses fade, you won't get a husband; don't worry, just get married while you are young! Don't worry, just get married while you are pretty; just get married, don't worry, while you are pretty: times will come, your hair will gray; don't worry, just get married while you are pretty!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Oh, mommy! Vašíček the mynah bird keeper is coming to us: I won't marry him, he is like a goldfinch. Oh, mommy! Jiříček the bailiff's son is coming to us: I will marry him right away, he is a handsome young man.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Our cranes’ geese: Mom! I will get married. "Don't get married, my daughter! I'll pluck them first."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: Tell her, tell her, tell her to marry if she can; tell her, tell her, tell her to marry if she may: let her marry if she can, if she is able to support a man; tell her, tell her, tell her to marry if she may.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would like to marry you, even if you were a second husband: but people say that you are full of debts. "I have no debts, nor do I worry about them: my father pays for everything, I am like a master!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I would get married, but my parents won’t let me, so that I end up where there is no bread. Where there is no bread, they don’t bake cakes: with just potatoes they’ll stuff their tripe.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If the Lord God granted me that the bagpiper would take me: I would carry the bagpipes for him, I would beg bread for him; if the Lord God granted me that he would just play the bagpipes for me!

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I said, I said that love wounded me; it wounded me with a sudden shot, it injured my heart; I said, I said that love wounded me. I will go ask the reeve who should pay the doctor? who will heal my wound? who will pay for the wound? I will go ask the reeve who should pay the doctor. Oh, reeve! I am in pain; tell me honestly, how does the law show who dares to shoot? who should pay the doctor when the wound is proven? "Take the doctor yourself, let him bandage your wound; you must pay for the pain, he must be paid for the wound: the one who caused the wound must pay, so the law tells me." You are mistaken, sir, you do not understand me: my wound is not visible, my heart is pierced; how can that help me? the heart cannot be bandaged. "My dear lady! the law has nothing to do with you: our pastor teaches otherwise, instead of the heart he ties the hands; go consult with him, I have nothing to do with you."

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: If I do not marry that year, people will say I have nothing: I have ducats in a chest at my father's: come on, boys! I will give them to you.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: And whoever marries me, I will honestly tell him what I have, what I have in the burial chest: I have three lumps of sugar and pastry homes, and also, also butter for the valleys.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: I have laments on my heart, this for my beloved, because he answered me yesterday, and I have no other: oh dear, oh dear! I do not care about anything, and so I will still marry; and whoever comes after me, I will give them all the furniture. Old tubs, two wooden spoons, and a broken chest; the bed is broken down and covered with a quilt: there are no feathers in the quilt, I sold the feathers from it, and spent the money walking with my beloved.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation: My dear Honza, Honza, little Honzíček! I would like to give you my little hand. I am a young girl from Litoměřice, I look like other people, don’t worry about anything. They call me Andulka, that is my name: don’t be afraid, little Honzíček, my lady. You won’t have to do anything, I will support you; just stroll around like a gentleman. My lady wished to give me something so I wouldn’t worry about what she provides for me. Nine pounds of carrots, a sack of flour, two sacks of peas, four buns. A cracked pot, four small ladles, a bowl of wild apples, broken tubs. One old chest without a bottom, without a lid, which my godmother Hedvika promised me. For our wedding, our father will give us an old crow, ox horns. We will sing, Honza, Honza, little Honzíček! Give a kreuzer for this beautiful song.

label: Písně věku mládeneckého a panenského
translation:
Ančička, shepherdess! Don’t say anything to father: just tell mother that we know each other; don’t say anything to father, Ančička, shepherdess!  
“Honzíček! Just come to us and tell father yourself to give his consent, to bless us together; just tell father yourself when you come, Honzíček, to us.”  
Ančička! I am still afraid when I think about it: what will father say, how will he welcome me? When I think about it, Andulka, I always get scared!  
“Why wouldn’t he welcome you, when he told stepmother that he would like to see you, that you should come to him, he even told stepmother himself — why wouldn’t he welcome you?”  
If you become mine, how happy we will be, my dear: we will love each other, work together; how happy we will be, my dear, when you become mine, Ančička!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Where have you been flying, little dove, that you have worn your cheek, your beautiful feathers? "I have flown over the mountains, to my beloved, my darling, despite everything." If you fly again, I will catch you, my little dove. I will catch you by the barley ear: I will have you, my little dove, again soon. I will catch you by the rye ear, I will have you, my little dove, very soon. I will catch you among the flowers, I will have you, my little dove, in the nets. I will catch you on the beech tree, I will have you, my little dove, by the hand. I will have you by the hand: I will not let you go, my little dove, for a year. I will have you with both hands: I will not let you go, my little dove, until the grave.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Above the mill is a grove: there my boy walks, tossing his handkerchief. On his head is a black hat: he cannot forget you, my golden child! Under the hat are black locks: I cannot let you, my golden child, go out of love. Under the locks is a white forehead: how could I not like my golden child? Under the forehead are blue eyes, with which my golden child always gazes at me. They gaze, gaze, and will gaze, until we kneel before the altar in front of the divine face. And when we both kneel together: the wedding cake will unite us, and we will belong to each other – we will go home. We will come home, sit at the table, and thank the lady mother for her daughter. "Thank you, I kiss your hands for giving me and raising what I love."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Little clover, little clover! My maiden, if you wish, let's go together for a walk, to the office for the announcement; little clover, oh little clover! Little clover, little clover! My maiden, if you wish, let's go together before the priest, he will tie our hands with a stole; little clover, oh little clover!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation:
My dear Ančička sleeps in the little chamber, Honzíček, the lad, toasted her: “Ančka, my child! Here you go, drink to my health!”  
“Why should I drink to your health? I am not your beloved: Honzíček, child, you are deceiving me!” she said to me.  
“We will settle this with the mayor, the one who lives in our rectory: he will take a hoe and tie us together at the altar.  
We will love each other, you will sew and I will cradle: and so we will be happily together until death.”

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: However, I can still manage that I will go for my dear one. On beautiful black horses, which I used to ride to the mill. To the mill, then from the mill back home, straight to my girl’s courtyard.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Reaper. Reap, my golden little sickle, cut the grass for the little horses: the white horses trotted for two miles along the dear paths. Reap, my little sickle, for the last time, but tomorrow they will take me away: the white horses will ride for two miles to a joyful wedding.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Willow water. Willow water flows, willow water, into the Pavliš family’s courtyard, into the courtyard. Whoever stops the water, stops it, will win Mařenka’s affection, win it. I will stop the water, stop it, I will win Mařenka’s affection, win it.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: That Koutek Castle, visible from afar, my sweetheart sews there, she sews a shirt: I will go to her there, I will love her, and I will take her as my housekeeper.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Whose girl is that, whose is she, who is sewing this little shirt for me? Whose little girl is that, who gave me this little shirt?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Don't stand, young men, under the windows, come rather into the room: if your little feet hurt, sit on the chairs. We did not come to sit with you, we came to ask: you have three beautiful girls, can we have one? Jeníček, don't take one who is very proud: she might not even come out the door to meet you. Jeníček, don't take one who is sullen: she would frown at you from morning till night.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I am supposed to get married, but I do not want to; I would have to wear a white cap. I have curly hair, woven with a pink ribbon.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: When I stood under the windows, I was not allowed to enter further: how beautifully they trumpeted, they coaxed my beloved, I was not allowed into the room – oh, how heavy my heart was!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Four horses are going over there for water for my dearest, over there for water four horses are going for my beloved.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My dear serves across the water, and I serve on the other side: how am I to manage to bring my maiden to us? I will buy an ivory oar, silver- and gold-rimmed, and I will sit in a little boat, I will go for my girl.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Although it was raining, just pouring, it would not be difficult for me to go to the church in Čimelice: I would see what I wanted. I would see the devils’ eyes that always turn towards me; they turn, they turn, they will keep turning – God willing, they will be mine. How great is the sorrow when a girl leaves a boy: both have heavy hearts when they remember each other.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: On those Klenov slopes, on those Klenov meadows, Jeníček lost a ducat, Jeníček lost a ducat. Go look for it, my dear, go look for it, my dear: if you find that ducat, we will get married because of it.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My golden Ančička! What now, so little? As if she were not three years young. Three years young, seven years of little rain: that the tavern keeper Honzíček used to come to you.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: None is as small as I am, yet the dry linden tree is green for me; turn green, dry linden, turn green: just don’t be angry with me, my dear! That’s why I bought myself a kerchief, so that you, my dear, would like me: the speckled one, the green one, the white one, so that you wouldn’t go to another.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My dear Honzíček, painted child: if you weren't handsome, I wouldn't marry you. But since you are handsome, that's why I will marry you, I will lead you by the hand to the altar. If you didn't have those black little eyes, I wouldn't give you so many kisses.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: If you want me, girl, if you want to have me, you must not torment me or beat me: if you torment or beat me, mother will look on sadly.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Master's servant, lend me a pistol, I'll take a shot: so my dear girl can hear it all the way to the lordly castle.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Our rooster crows, crows, it will be early morning: my Jeníček is thinking, thinking of me, madam! If only it were possible for you to give my joy a little kiss! Madam, get up, get up, master is calling you; prepare breakfast, prepare it, there will be a wedding at our place: I will be getting married, I must get up early in the morning, early morning. My Jeníček is coming to us, coming to us, from over by the grove; truly, I know him well, know him well, with his black horse: just hurry up a lot, my joy, I’m glad to see you. Glad of that, I am glad, I am glad, my heart is jumping; my intention pleased, pleased, for you, my boy: I will not be afraid that I have to sleep alone all by myself.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come, we come to you: if you give us your dowry, if you give us your dowry, if you give us your dowry, will you give it to us? "We will, we will, we will gladly, we have cared for it from youth, we will, we will, we will gladly, we will give it to you: why wouldn’t we give it to you since we have raised it for you? Then come only for it, come, come, come to us!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Praised be Lord Jesus Christ! We have come to you; if you give us your daughter, so that we may know: will you give her or not? Now tell us, so that we may turn back if we are on the wrong path.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Budgerigar, hide yourself, the enemy is approaching: if you do not hide, they will take everything you have: the ring on your finger. "I hid, tucked myself into the straw: they got in, took everything I had, wherever I was."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Just go, bridesmaid, to the chamber, the bride is standing behind the door, rubbing her little eye with a nice white handkerchief. Just take her by the hand, bring her before her mother, so that she forgives her for whatever harm she has done her.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Inčko, thank you for your little daughter, Inčko, thank you for your daughter: that you gave her to me, that you raised her for me, Inčko, I thank you very much for her.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: God have mercy on me! Where is my daddy? There is already a green little lawn growing on him. A green little lawn, a yellow carnation: pity you, daddy, pity you a hundred times over! God have mercy on me! Where is my mommy? There is already green grass growing on her! Green grass blooming with a white flower: you will no longer marry me, my mommy! Oh, I have no mommy, I no longer have a daddy: I only have a little sister and that little brother. God have mercy on me! Where shall I turn? Whom shall I complain to when I have no parents? I would complain to my little brother: but it won’t be the same as with daddy! I would complain to my dear little sister: but it won’t be the same as with my mommy. Behind the stone fence a nightingale sings: that there my dear daddy is resting. Behind the stone fence a little dove flies: that there my dear mommy is resting.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Blessing to the orphan. Oh father, get up! Oh my father! Rise from the grave, give us your blessing. Oh mother, get up! Oh my mother! Rise from the grave, give us your blessing. "Daughter, daughter, do not call! 1) Oh my daughter! Do not call to them, 2) do not cause me trouble. I am far from you, oh, I am very far from you, lying deep in the grave!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: A black horse, saddled, under a red blanket: the girl is betrothed, nicely dressed, just to kneel at the altar. They kneel at the altar, the priest will bind them: until the longest death, no one will separate them, no one will untie them.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: They are already leading my beloved to the wedding, they are already with her up on the little hill: may the Lord God grant you happiness, my beautiful girl! "May the Lord God grant it, my Jeníček!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: They are already leading my beloved to the church: back then you were mine, my maiden! "I am not yet, my dearest, I am still my mother's." They are already leading my beloved to the altar: back then you were mine, my maiden! "I am not yet, my dearest, I am still my mother's!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: You seem beautiful to me, my dear! Will you be as beautiful when you get married? "Even more beautiful, my dearest; take me, take me, you’ll watch over me!" You seem beautiful to me, my dear! What will your mother give you when you get married? "A cow with a calf, a sheep with a lamb, a speckled hen with chicks." "Take me, my dear, take, take, take! I am a good housekeeper, I am, I am: I get up in the morning, I feed the cows; every day I spin a little skein."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Tell me, my dear, do you love me? Tell me, my dear, do you want me? "I wished for you, that I love you: who else but you!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Why then, my dear, did you rise so early, when the lark had not yet sung? "I rose early to prepare for the little cows, so that you, my dear, would love me."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Wait, dark-eyed girl, you will cry: when the wedding is over in a week, you will cry every day: wait, dark-eyed girl, you will cry! Wait, dark-eyed girl, you will cry: when the wedding is over in a month, you will cry even more; wait, dark-eyed girl, you will cry!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Do not enter someone else’s farm, Bětuška: there they will blame you for having nothing of your own. Do not enter someone else’s farm, Bětuška: there they will blame you for having a poor mother. One will push you, another will kick you: it’s a wonder your little heart does not burst inside you.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Budgerigar! What do you think? I make the sign of the cross over you: I make it over you because they are going to take you away, because you will no longer see me!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: When I did not take you as my wife, I chose you as my bridesmaid, so that you would still serve me, placing a wreath on my head. "That cannot happen to you, my boy, I cannot give it to you out of pity, I cannot go to church because of crying – oh strong love, you must leave!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: How then shall I say sadly, when I kneel before the priest? He will ask me if it is from good will? It is not enough to be from good will: it was done under compulsion, my father and mother are forcing me, I have no desire at all!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The local parish priest here preaches nicely, handing out pictures at the altar; I will also go to the sermon, I will ask for one picture. The local parish priest here: gracious, he gave me a picture, it is lively; where shall I, very sad, where shall I put it when I have no place for it anywhere! If I put it behind glass, it won’t fit; if I put it in the chest, it will get suffocated; I’ll put it behind the table, sit down by it: he will give me a kiss, and I him. From Klatovy. – Compare Suš. 474; Kol I, 241. If I put it behind glass, it will get cut; if I put it in the books, it won’t fit; I will put it in the bed under the blanket, I will enjoy it all winter.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: They are already leading my beloved away from the altar, back then you were, my maiden, back then you were mine! "I am already yours, my dearest! I am no longer my lady mother's."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Thank you, Father! For giving me what I wanted. You gave me a wife, Kateřina: and I will keep her under the blanket.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Tell me, my little star, 1) are you bright or dark? If you are dark, clear up: my dear maiden, reconsider. "I have already reconsidered, I have left my father and mother. Father, mother, and friends, I chose only you. And not for a year, not for two years: but until the end of the world. Until my end, yours, not to look at anyone else. Not to look, not to smile, only to have my heart with you!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What, my dear, did you think you swore at the altar before the divine face? "I didn't want to, I had to; and when I knelt, I said 'I promise,' I was crying!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I am late. How sad I thought before the priest, that I had taken the hand before the great altar: what I wanted, I have now, I can no longer hide from it, very sad girl!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Four cows, little grass, give them a little at a time; four horses, two lads, quickly harness them: we will go for the green fodder, to the green meadow; there we will love a black-eyed girl.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Those famous and wise divine judgments are the greatest treasure of our happiness: they judged, they betrothed you, the bride; you had a rather difficult path to the state of marriage. You relied on your little roses: now tiny bees will embrace you! Even your mother cannot protect you, you must take the wedding cap and be a lady. Still rejoice in your maiden wreath; but soon you will take the cap for it: your destined groom has had it brought, wishing you health and happiness with it.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: You, newlyweds, be well! May the Lord give you happiness: so that no worries ever catch you. You, young newlyweds! Always love each other: if you want to be happy together, avoid quarrels. Avoid quarrels, always love each other: if you ever argue, let no one know!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: That Strážská pub built from maple wood: then why do you go there, lads, when you are not needed there? "If we were not needed here, we would not come here: they say there are such pretty girls here, we came for them."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Play, musicians! Let the moments pass quickly, for my maidenhood will not return again!

label: SVATEBNÍ
