translation: At the altar, she knelt, reflecting to herself as she gave her hand to her bridegroom: it cannot be otherwise, I must take him, until my full death I must be his. Oh, you musicians! play cheerfully for me while I still have a wreath on my forehead: when I leave here, how sad I will be! How will I sadly forget you! My dear mother walks around me, not saying a single word to me: my dear mother! say a word; soon you will lose me, like a tree loses its apple! Why is it, you bridesmaids! sitting here quietly and sadly lowering your little eyes to the ground? Oldest bridesmaid! say a word; soon you will lose me like a grain from the ear of corn! Now they will braid my wreath, unbraid my hair, put a white cobweb on my head: it cannot be otherwise, I must leave, until my full death I must cover my head!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What are you doing behind that mountain? "A little maple cradle." Hurry up, hurry up; once you make it, then you’ll also cradle yourself.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Annička got up on a white day and went to the stable for firewood. She called Jeníček to come and chop some wood for her. He grabbed a wooden axe and carved a green cradle. "Here you go, Annička, rock the cradle, and don't look at other lads!" "I'm not looking at others because I only like you."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Pantáto, pantáto under the wreath! Move, move the small coin, the dollar, the dollar, a bottle of wine: so that I would know where I have lived.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation:
My maiden was bathing in cold water, a young lad came to her on a black horse.  
And what are you doing, my maiden, in the cold water?  
“I am sewing caps, weaving wreaths from red roses.”  
To whom will you give the green wreaths, my maiden?  
“One to you, another to myself, a third to my companion.”  
Why didn’t you give him to me earlier when I used to visit you?  
“When I had no sense, which I have now.”  
Hold back your black horse for me, my maiden, and I will go to harvest ripe oats for myself.  
“As surely as I am alive, I did not hold the black horse: I’d rather go myself to harvest ripe oats!”

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What is that there, my dearest, green in the field? What is that there, my dear little heart, green in the field? "Green, my dearest, is my father's pea, green, my dear little heart, on our dear fields!" We will not waste time in vain, my dearest: you take the sickle and I’ll take the basket, we will go to cut it. What can you do then, my dearest, in the house? "Milk the cows, prepare lunch, wash the clothes. I can also, my dearest, make the white bed: once I have made it, my dear little heart, we will go to sleep on it."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I have an apple tree, I will climb it; I will shake it just once, then I'll climb down again. There are nice red apples on it: I will pick some for pleasure.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Go slowly, little moon, across the lordly courtyard: do not think, my dear maiden, that I will be yours. A magpie jumps in the garden, looking for a grain: do not think, my dear maiden, about my little heart. The magpie jumps in the garden, holding an ear of grain in its beak: do not think, my dear maiden, that I will come to you again. Shine, little moon, slowly across the lordly forest: do not think, my dear maiden, that I will come to you today. What is that little bird with a crest flying above us? What news does it bring us, that it says nothing? It brings, brings you news, brings such: that your dearest is going after another. What is that little bird with a crest flying above us? What news does it bring us, that it chatters so? It brings, brings you news, brings such: that your dearest will soon be married to another. The dear one sits by the little table like a rose, she will not allow her wreath to be undone. Do not grieve, do not grieve, my maiden, for your wreath: yet a pretty white cap will suit you well!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Bridesmaid. Whoever wants to have a live rose at the wedding must not love any other: God rules, whoever falls for her must love her until he drops. Best man. I want to love her, I want to have her; I will love her whenever I want: at breakfast, at lunch, and in the evening I will take her to bed.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: No one has it like you do, mamma: when you make your bed, you don’t feel discomfort. And I, poor thing, alone on the stove: I turn to the right, I turn to the left, everywhere I feel cramped. God willing, I will also get married: I will hug little Honzík, give him a little kiss.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I have a little dove, my golden Jeníček! I have a little dove, I do: come to me, my dear, when the day grows late, I will give you a little dove, my golden Jeníček! I will give you a little dove.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: To the assembly of trumpeters, twelve young men were blowing for me. Trumpet to me, trumpeters, merrily, as long as I have a wreath on my forehead. Once I lose that wreath, I won’t be merry for a year!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: They are already leading her, they already have her, to whom will they deliver her? To whom would they deliver her if not to the groom in the chamber?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The budgerigar sleeps, Jeníček with her: what she doesn’t know, he will tell her – they are both witnesses. The budgerigar sleeps, Jeníček with her: they play music, people ask – they know nothing.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I walked around it three times, tadyadyadum – walked around, but I never found where my beloved lies. I found a small window, tadyadyadum – a window: are you sleeping, my maiden, are you sleeping, my maiden? 1) "Just now I fell asleep, tucked in with a stalk. Climb up, Jeníček, push aside this barrier. If you have a fine shirt, stay here with me." Tell me, my dear, my beloved, who will call us in the morning? "A swallow is sitting on the beam, it will chirp: Wake up, Jeníček! from your dear, the white day will soon come." I haven't even started to sleep a little, and I already have to leave again.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The little swallow is flying, saying that dawn is breaking, dawn is breaking: wake up, my Andulíčko! If I had the keys to that dawn, I wouldn't let it dawn tomorrow until breakfast. If I had the keys to the white day, I wouldn't let it dawn until the full year!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I am not used to waking up early: who will then call me? Who else but my dear, my adorable little Honzík.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: If the Lord God wills, dear Jinín mother, that the Meissen apples turn out well. If the Lord God wills, all the saints: that the children of the Polanskys may thrive.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Twelve maidens fell asleep for the love of their beloved; only one did not sleep, she waited for her beloved. She waited for him with music and with great longing; when she finally saw him, she rejoiced with happiness. As he spoke a word to her: "Where are you, my golden dove?" she immediately recognized him and replied to him. "I am coming to you, my delight, I am coming for your joy; you have me, your servant and lover. We will rejoice together, we will love faithfully, and that at every moment until the very grave."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Sing, nightingale! call for my girl, she is sleeping in the attic: Get up, Ančička! prepare breakfast, the day is already bright. "Why are you calling me, won't you let me sleep with my beloved? sweet sleep after last night's lovemaking!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Get up, my dear, the day has come: we will harness the little horses and go. Just carry me nicely, little horse, you will get your feed today.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Sing, Klášterský roosters, so my little heart won’t grieve; sing – until the day comes: we’ll harness the horses and go. When I ride here, I will play, stop the horses by the gate: I will play – and a song: that they have taken Ančička away from me.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Under our windows the little honey bees: Marjánka is getting ready by her mother, getting ready, getting ready, she will cry: harness the little carriage, we will go.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: All dogs bark, the daggers howl: Adamka ran out, what are they buying? We’re buying, we’re buying your daughter. Šimanka ran out: "Buy ours!" We don’t want yours, she is lazy: she only washes pots once a week. When she washes the pots, she doesn’t want to talk: she says her hands already hurt from it.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: God bless you, my father, back then: I thank you, I thank you, father, this time a hundredfold! God bless you, my mother, back then: I thank you, I thank you, mother, this time a hundredfold! Thank you for your upbringing, for your worries from my youth until my marriage. God bless you, my dear girls who used to be, who used to be my friends. God bless you, my dear young men who used to be, who used to be my lovers. You used to be, and you will no longer be: will you sometimes remember me, sorrowful, distressed? God bless you, all my acquaintances: thank you for everything, I wish you happiness, may the Lord God be with you! They are taking me beyond the mountains, beyond the forests: who will then comfort me there, sorrowful, distressed? They are taking me, and I do not know where; and when they bring me there, everyone will leave — only I will stay there! Beyond the mountains, beyond that dark forest: there I must live, I will not know, my mother, where I am! I will go out, I will hurry up the mountain: if by any chance I hear my dear mother there somewhere in the breeze. I will go out, I will look down from the mountain: if by any chance I see my dear mother somewhere in the valley. I will not see, nor will I hear: with whom then, my dear golden mother, will I, sorrowful, find comfort! "My dear daughter! God is everywhere: just pray and work and sing, He will comfort you."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I am sorry, my daddy! I am sorry, I am sorry for you, that you raised me with difficulty, and now I have to leave you! Thank you, my daddy! Thank you a hundred times, for your hard upbringing I thank you a hundred times. I am sorry, my mommy, I am sorry, I am sorry for you, that you raised me with difficulty, and I have to leave you! Thank you, my mommy, thank you a hundred times, for your hard upbringing I thank you a hundred times!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I am already leaving you, daddy, and my dear mother: thank you for your care until this marriage. My heart, my innermost being withers from the great sorrow that I must say goodbye to you and go away to a foreign land.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Thank you, my dear mother! For raising me like an apple. Like an apple, that beautiful red one: I have always been your joy!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The black hairs are already, they are already covering, they are already coated with white cobweb, oh dear! they are coated. My dear daddy walks around the yard, and he does not speak a word to me, oh dear! he does not speak to me. My dear mother stands in the kitchen, and she does not speak a word to me, oh dear! she does not speak to me. My dear little brother stands in the stable, putting four black horses into the little cart, oh dear! putting them into the cart. My dear little sister stands in the pantry, dividing her little blankets in half, oh dear! dividing them in half. Twelve musicians sat at the table: Andulka’s heart was breaking, oh dear! her heart was breaking. Gentlemen musicians! play merrily for me, as long as I am still sitting at the mother’s table, oh dear! at the mother’s table. My dear mother! say a word: you will leave me like a tree leaves an apple, oh dear! like a tree leaves an apple. The apple falls and the tree remains standing: do not forget me, my parents, oh dear! my parents, me.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation:
Stop, musicians, in front of our house: when I say goodbye to my dear Lord God.  
Stop, musicians, in front of our garden: when I say goodbye to my father and mother.  
Stop, musicians, under our roof: when I say goodbye to my dearest brother.  
Stop, musicians, in front of our path: when I say goodbye to my dearest sister.  
Stop, musicians, under our windows: when I say goodbye to the neighboring godparents.  
Stop, musicians, in front of our gate: when I say goodbye to the boys and the girls.  
Stop, musicians, in the village in front of that oak tree: when I say goodbye to all the people of Stráž!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: May the Lord God protect you, all the boys from Stražský! May the Lord God protect you, I am already leaving: I am going into the world where the rose blooms, I will never come back to you again until death!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I'm giving it to you now, you Stražský boys! You who took me to dance in the pub! You took me, you took me – but you won't anymore: once I leave, you'll be the ones crying! I'm giving it to you now, you Stražský girls! You who used to be fond of me. Used to be, used to be – but you won't anymore: will you forget about me then?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: She has already said goodbye to all the girls, and also at the back to the boys. Thank you very much, you our boys, who used to take Hančička to dance. You used to take her, but you won’t anymore: will you then forget her? If you forget, she will cry: who then will walk the path for her!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: When you leave the door, take care: May the Lord God watch over you, my dear father. When you leave the door, send greetings: May the Lord God watch over you, all my friends!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Bětuška does not cry, she is not sorry that they will take her from here across the green rye. Daddy will stay here and mommy too: ah, who will take care of them when they fall ill?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I said goodbye to everyone: I didn’t see my mother anywhere! I will still look around the yard, to see if I can spot my mother there. Where then is my dear, my mother, to see me off from the yard? No matter how much I call and look around, I just don’t see you, my mother! – “How can you look for her if you don’t have her? If she sleeps in the cemetery, you can’t call her!”

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Where are my parents, who raised me, to give me their blessing one last time? They gave their blessing, they saw me off, did they say goodbye to me one last time?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Thank you, Bětuška, your dear mother, for raising you in a patchwork quilt. When she raised you, she gave you to Jozífk: which is what the mother did well. Thank you, Bětuška, to your father, for he raised you for your Jozífk. "How can I thank him when I no longer have him? I will never be able to protect my father!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The bride was crying, that she was sorrowful, that the journey from home was sorrowful: "I, a sad bride, I would complain, I would complain – I have no one to complain to!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What kind of spice is fern? They will take you, my maiden, to Prague; they will take you, my dear, to where you have never been alive, they will take you, my dear, to Prague! What kind of spice is calamus? They will take you, my maiden, to Němec; they will take you, my dear, to where you have never been alive, they will take you, my dear, to Němec!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Parting, oh parting! What a difficult thing it is, when one must say goodbye, when one must part! From you, mother, your little daughter! Do not be upset, mother, that I am leaving you: I do not want to forget, but still, until death, I will be your child. "I am not upset, my daughter, that you are leaving me: but most of all, my dear daughter, I am upset about how you will fare." How then would I fare? Well, my mother: for I have only received what I myself wanted to have, what my heart desired!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The patchwork quilt hangs on the perch: take care, Vavroušek, of the horses, please. "I have taken care of the whole wagon too: come sit, Hančičko, we are about to leave." I don't want to, I won't sit, the horses are scared: our people might cry for me. I don't want to go, I won't go, only tomorrow morning: God bless you, my dear mother!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Four black horses, the fifth one is white: tell me, Ančička, who lives with you? "There lives with us a young lad: to whom I gave a wreath from my head.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Four black horses, a harnessed carriage: get ready, Hančičko, we are leaving now. "And I have been ready for a long time: if I don’t sit down, someone else will!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Be careful, Hančičko, where they take you so that you know when they will drive you out. When they will chase you, when they will beat you: so that you know which way to go!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: How much I bundled sheaves in our yard: I have to go to the threshing floor, leave everything behind – that is what I dread the most!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Wait, stop, little lad! until I offer my hand to my dear. "If you wanted to offer your hands, you shouldn't have promised before!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We won't just go over the top anymore: we're carrying a full load of new cushions.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Just don’t topple us, foreman: we’ll give you a bladder filled with plum jam. Just take us nicely, foreman: you’ll get the bladder even today.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: When we ride through the forest, don’t step, little horse, over the stump. When we ride along the dam, little horse’s horseshoes clatter. Wait, little horse, lift your little foot, I will nail your little horseshoe!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Our coachman has horses held high: they carry their heads like stags; if he cracks the whip at them twice, they will race out of Němčice like a bird. Our coachman has fine little horses: the maids were talking about it; I listened to them quite well: they were talking about it in the chamber.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation:
Neigh for me, neigh, my black horse, running across the field: let my beloved on the wagon hear and be comforted. She was immediately joyful when she heard the voice of the black horse: "Hurry, hurry, my dearest! Let me see you again. When I left everything for you, my dearest, what do you have for me at your mother's to make me happy?" I have a little white room for you at my mother's: there we will live like turtle doves, always dear to each other. I will reap, I will thresh, and I will plow; when I come home, we will sit together and love each other.  
"If I had known, my dearest, that you would be like this: I would always have made you a poppy seed cake for dinner. If I had known, my dearest, that you were beyond the water: I would have bought a golden boat and sailed to you. If I had known, my dearest, that you were beyond the woods: I would have bought a little chestnut horse and ridden to where you are. If I had known, my dearest, that you would deceive me: I would not have gone to your mother’s place, not even for a long time!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I have four black horses in the stable, I have four horses in the stable: they are not my horses, they belong to the landlord: wait, Jeníček, they will give them to you!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What kind of little horses are those on our eel? Who will graze them when I am sleeping with my Verunka in the chamber?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The young men wandered around that Old Quarry. They wandered and wandered until they reached the Čížeks. The old woman calls from the room: "Sit down, young men!" "We won't sit with you: we're coming for Marjánka." "And we won't give her to you until we have arranged her clothes." – "Her clothes are already arranged, the blue chests are lined up." The young men cheered that they had gotten Marjánka. At the Čížeks, they lament that they no longer have a daughter.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Flying, flying a bird 1) over the green orchard; above the courtyard it circles, raises its eyes to the sky, calls Andulka. "My dear Andulka! If you are sincere, if you are not so proud, come out just a little, all alone." She could not come out, she sent a messenger: "Go, my dear messenger, tell him well, as if I myself were saying it." The messenger lied boldly, and persuaded her himself. "Oh, poor me, that I did not come out alone!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: From that Spáňov instinct, which I will watch over: I will watch once, twice, the third time I will already cry. My golden Spáňov path, which makes me long for you: not so much for you, my little path, as for you, my little kiss! My golden Spáňov mountains, which make my little head ache: it aches, it aches, it hurts a little, because the boy is responding to me.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: So what do you think, my boy, when you see me or hear me? "When I see her, I say nothing; when I hear you, I sigh sadly!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What did your mother give you to marry you off so young? "She gave me a tormenting husband: he will smash my head!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The path to Pušperk is golden, entirely covered with briars; the briars bloom white: my maiden, open up! "Why would I open for you, since I am not your beloved?" If you are not, you will be; just open up for me! "But I heard well what your mother said: that she does not want me as a bride, that I do not have two hundred in the chest. If I were rich, I would not beg you: I would take a boy with a farmstead, and you may go with God!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: How nice it is in Stráž, so nice! Plum trees grow all over the village, plum trees: some people don’t have any, any – when they defend my son, defend!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Tell me, Vavroušek, tell me, tell me, what’s up in Klatovy, what’s up? About twenty-five Czech [crowns], Czech [crowns]: that our Vavroušek is 1) handsome, handsome!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We arrived in a land unknown, we brought painted cushions, painted blue, white: how will you sleep, my little Jeníček!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Hey, hey, hey! We have for sale: we have nice goods here, on which it lies nicely; hey, hey, hey, we have for sale!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We have nice goods, but to whom shall we sell them? The one who buys them needs them, so we will sell them to that person.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Spotted, white. Move, spotted, white! Move, spotted, white: what is that underneath you for us, us, us, what is that underneath you miles!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Open your gates: 1) we are bringing you a bride made of silver and gold. Open up 2) your little door: we are bringing you a bride, she has new clothes. Remove the bolts and bars: we are bringing you a bride with all her trimmings. Bring a chair for your bride: we are bringing you furniture worth two hundred. Bring a chair, 3) bring it to the carriage, so the bride does not break her leg. Come down, bride, come down from the carriage, go look at your house. At your house, at your dowry, go see your mother. When you enter the room, give greetings: "Are you all here, 4) my friends?" Set the table so it doesn't tip over, so the bride does not return to us. Then, mother, come welcome her: Hančička will listen to you well. Then, father, come welcome her: Hančička will obey you well. She will obey you on Friday, on Saturday, she will do all the chores for you. Come welcome her, all friends: Hančička is yours, she will be no other way!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Dear lady! Open the gates: so I don’t break the gold on my little hat. Dear lady! Open the little door: so I don’t break the feathers on my little hat.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Madam Jersouc! Come out in front of the house: do not be afraid of us, there are seven of us riding; there are seven of us riding, and a few more: if you do not come out, we will ride away again. Madam Jersouc! Sweep the path: we are bringing you a fine bride; she has a new chest, all ducats, colorful quilts, and a halmara too. Madam Jersouc! Welcome us: we are bringing you a bride, she will be for you; she is young, good, and knows how to work: she will not harm you in any way.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Come down, Hana, come down, we are leaving for home now: you will get Honzíček, you will stay with him alone, come down, come down, come down!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Welcome, bride! To our cottage: if you are sincere, we will be too. We have sincerity, we will give it to you: we accept it for our little daughter.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Who else to complain about than our mother, for stuffing straw into my pillows. Straw into pillows, stones into chests, as if I were not from that generation!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I got married, I got married! What is there after my marriage, when I have gone far from my mother. Far from my mother, from my dear little mother: my black little eyes will cry for her!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I feel sorry, sorry for our puddle, that I have gone from pleasure to distress. Where they have no bread, they never bake it; that I have become a childless bride!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: How boldly I stamp on the table! Where are you, my partner, come here for me. Where are you? my partner! Come and sit me down: I am already tired of sitting here at the table! Where are you? my partner! Just come and sit me down: two dances or three we will dance.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Play, little fiddle, how you drone, how you understand my little legs! The little fiddle droned as it hummed, how it understood my little legs!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: And what, am I not a bridesmaid by fashion? When I bring everything to the Guard: thirty on the buckles, only they shine! So you don’t want me, you boys from the Guard? I am a bridesmaid like a raspberry; behind me stands a best man like rosemary: handsome boy! Just don’t be haughty; when you leave us, just sing out loud!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Hanička, little kiss, my heart! Don’t let your headdress be ruined. If you let your headdress be ruined, you will surely regret it, remember!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Oh girl, girl, my maiden! They will tear down your crown: they will destroy your little crown, and give you a little cap instead; for your green wreath, you will receive a white little cap!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Liduška, how are you? You have a red ribbon; they will take your ribbons, give you a cap, just wait and see! "I am not afraid of that white cap, nor do I regret the green wreath for my beloved."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: When you wanted to get married, you have to leave the maids, you have to send the woman to the chamber: they promise you that they will take the wreath from you, that they will put a white cap on you instead.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My beloved hair, a pity for your beauty: that I must wrap you up for all these times!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Why then, my dear, do you cry, cry, cry so bitterly? – "Because, because, because I cry, cry, cry so bitterly: the wreath is gone, the cap is here, I was a maiden and I am no longer; therefore, therefore, therefore I cry, cry, cry so bitterly."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My golden red headdress used to be braided: yesterday I was adorned by bridesmaids, today I am crowned by women.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My curly hair, you have always been beautiful. Always nicely combed. Decorated with little ribbons. Now you are untied, wrapped with a piece of cloth.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: How everything is dancing, everything is upside down! The groomsman stands at the door, knocks on the room: I sit here in the middle, all sorrowful; my little heart: vet, vet, vet? poor bride!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Oh, my green little wine, I am already laying you down from my head! To you, my dearest! I now entrust him: that you may cherish him, always love me, and remain in that love until my death. Ah, now I bid you farewell, my dear bridesmaids! And to you all I say goodbye – this moment is sorrowful: times have changed, hair has turned gray, I kindly thank you for your service! Other friends have now taken me into their number, they have placed a white cap on my head: I must be with them and leave you behind: may the Lord God reward them all for this!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: White cap. Oh my green wine! I am already taking you off my head, and I am putting that white cap on my little head: so that I may wear it, and rejoice in it, until my longest death I will not take it off my head! Oh you dear bridesmaids! who stand behind me, and do not defend me, the sad bride, at all? When your time comes, I will not resist either, I will take the white cap and put it on your head again.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: It’s already done, it’s already finished: a few eggs into a small pan, a bit of butter added.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Already, already, already Honzíček is a man; Andulička his wife, she is already veiled too: already, already, already Honzíček is a man

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We have a young woman, a woman, we have a young woman: whoever wants to see her must pay us, or we will take his hat!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Hopsá, hejsa! She lost the wreath, hopsá, hejsa! She lost the wreath: she lost the little wreath, she got a cap; hopsá, hejsa! she got a cap. Here you go, now you have what you wanted to have, here you go, now you have what you wanted: you got a husband, who will help you now? here you go, now you have what you wanted to have.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Yesterday she was still a virgin: but you are no longer, you are no longer, you are now a woman. Yesterday she still had a wreath: but you no longer do, you no longer do, you now have a cap.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: You used to be at weddings, you used to be a bridesmaid: now your little head is already covered with a shroud!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Roosters are singing beyond the forest: I was a maiden, but I am not anymore. Roosters are singing beyond the meadow: I was an honest maiden. Sing, our roosters, so that my little heart does not grieve!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Take it off, Vavroušek! Take off your wreath: after all, you are no longer a young man. Hanka was a maiden, she wore ribbons: she had to take them off for you.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Don’t stop me, Mom, from music, for I am a pretty girl: I will go to the music, I will dance, which will please your heart!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I, a young girl, like to dance in Štěpán's pub; behind me, someone spins around, he has black eyes, that is my pleasure.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: If my feet did not walk in manure, they would know how to dance; if my hands did not work hard, they would know how to embroider.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Friend, friend, little friend! be careful with the bridesmaid; if you are not careful, you will lose her before you realize it. Friend, what have you done to the bride? she says she doesn't want you. "I didn't do anything to her, I just gave her a little kiss."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Return to me, my dear, sweet Honzíček, all those little kisses you gave me. "I'll have them counted: seventy and sixty! I may not be able to give them back to you."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We women are all pretty, because we are from Varvažov. Kalousková is pretty too, once she becomes from Varvažov.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We are women from Sušice, we would like some liquor. We are women from Protivín, we would like a mug of wine. A mug of wine, a mug of water: soon we will be preparing the feast.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: A pity about the handkerchief that dust falls on it; a pity about the handkerchief that it gets dirty: it would not be a pity if it were dirtied by pleasure!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: And I caught a flea in flight, I didn't have any pallet for it. And that little flea was tiny: each little leg weighed six pounds!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I am, I am truly glad that I got a beautiful wife; I am, I am truly glad that I got Bělohrad: Bělohrad is a nice town, my dear wife, don't tell anyone; I am, I am truly glad that I got Bělohrad.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: We have green wreaths, we have them, boys, we won’t give them to you: if you want to have them, have them made, we will make them for you. Look, groomsman, groomsman! I have an empty glass: pour some spirits, red jelly, so I have enough courage!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Alright then, for Kačena, alright then, for Káča: yesterday they put the wedding wreath on her, today we'll have fun with her.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Praise the Lord God! I am married now, praise the Lord God! I have a wife now: I am married, I have exchanged freedom; praise the Lord God, I have a wife now. Praise the Lord God! I am married now, I praised the Lord God, I have a husband now; I am married, I received a bridal cap; praise the Lord God, I have a husband now!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: At our wedding, the dogs fought over the bone by the little finger; the musicians received a nice amount of money: a total of four haléř.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Listen little to the people about what happened years ago, how a wedding was held that attracted many spectators. The groom was from Chudá Třída, and the bride from Ženská Bída; they were declared in Jekov and married in Nebylov. The best man was from Drbalov and the young man from Vyhnanov, the bridesmaid was from Strojetice and the cook from Odřetice. There was plenty of beer and wine, everyone drank as they pleased; and when the meal was over, everything was ready for dancing. The violin was played by someone from Šumava, the shawm from Vratislav; and the trumpeters who blew the trumpets were from somewhere near Mrázov. From Strakonice came the bagpipers, from Trhanov the players of kobzas (a type of lute), with the drum from Dobříň and with kettledrums from Kokořín. One frkoč (a type of folk instrument) was blown by someone from Lom: people marveled at where such a band was found for this wedding. It was a feast, dancing across the ditches! After that time they parted – I do not know where they will meet again.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I know a certain bird, a hoopoe by name, who wants to get married: he will marry a jay. Many guests were invited to that merry wedding, from various birds, both large and small. The golden-headed bunting was the best man, and the joyful lark invited the guests to the wedding. The quail was the bridesmaid, the wreath fairy, leading the maiden jay, the bride, to the ceremony. The raven officiated, the hawk stood as a witness, forbidding any friends to be present. The crow was the cook, preparing their lunch: everyone could see how he blackened himself. The nightingale played music after the meal, and all the guests danced. The jay became tired and went to sleep, while the lively magpie made her bed. The wedding was completed, I end this song, at which I was also present and watched it. It happened that year and still happens today: when the hoopoe comes to the jay, the wedding is done.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The cat was getting married, the dog was groom; our lame gelding was the best man, and the blind mare was the bridesmaid: she gave him a handkerchief and a bouquet.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The golden beetle got married, he took a fly for himself: he threw the fly under the pillow, considering her his wife.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The wedding of the vegetables. The turnip was getting married, the celery was cheering, the carrot was dancing, and the horseradish was whistling.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: When the shoemakers got married, we were not at the wedding: neither the master, nor I, nor our female master.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: He cared for the poor man and the poor woman, how they would live together; the Lord God told them that He has more than He gave: that He would give them a sack of flour so they could bake bread.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: People always say that we have nothing: but we have half a bale of straw in the attic!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: She is no better than a young woman, for she cheers up on her own: in the morning she gives a kiss, at noon two, and in the evening she sits down next to me by herself.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My wife, my wife, my little wife! I love you more than a hussar loves his horse. A hussar has a horse worth two hundred thalers: I get you, my wife, for free. The hussar has a horse for his riding: and I have you, my wife, for my pleasure.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: You can thank me, woman, for teaching you how to care for children, care for children, cook porridge: I will still teach you how to manage the household.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I won’t drive those oxen; I have a young wife at home: if I were to drive those oxen, someone would be coming after her.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What kind of livelihood is it when the old man grabs the young? What kind of livelihood is it when the young man grabs the old?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: This is how the old man treats the young woman: she does not laugh at him at all. He presses himself against her, she frowns: "Get away from me, old bearded man!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What’s up, Andulka, what were you doing that you got so wet? "They didn’t close the gate, our goslings flew out. They ran into the nursery, from the nursery into the wheat. From the wheat then into the millet, which was heavy dew!" What’s up, Andulka, what were you doing that you married such an old man? Better with a young one in dirty clothes than with an old one all in gold. The old man sits drooping beside the stove, counting how many years he has. The young man sits beside the cheek, chirping like a swallow.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: She didn't want the young one, she liked the old one: she was fond of the gray beard. "My dear grandpa with the gray beard, do not leave me, young girl!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: An old woman was getting married, an old woman was getting married: she had a torn skirt and slippers without insteps.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Well! I had a nice girl named Majolenka, to have a nice girl: the devil pushed this old woman on me, pushed her away from Majolenka! Well! I had a nice girl named Majolenka, to have a nice girl. Well! I had a nice girl named Majolenka, to have a nice girl: if only the devil would take that old woman and I would get my girl back! Well! I had a nice girl named Majolenka, to have a nice girl.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I married an old grandmother, I thought she had something: she has a rooster in the pantry, it crows there for her.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Chval got married, God granted it to him: he took an old hag, put her in a boat, the wind took her away – he did well!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Our dad praises mom for burning little wood: with a small log, she cooks him soup. Our dad has a kind mom, I won’t get one like that: and even if I went far away, I probably wouldn’t find her.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I have leather pants, only they are borrowed: my dad lent them to me so that I can practice sitting at home with a woman.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: To the groom. Wait a year, two years, you will forget about the pub: you will be at home with your wife, pumping water! Wait a year, two years, you will forget about music: your wife will bring it to you on her tongue!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I always cry for money when I remember Káča – what I had to pay for her when I wanted to marry her. For she was on someone else's estate; the lord allowed me to have her: whoever wants to have Káča as a wife must pay well.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I got cut while mowing the grass: a hundredfold pity that I asked you! "I regretted it when I was reaping the grass: a hundredfold pity that I took you!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What a pity this time that I got married! That golden freedom I have lost! I loved a maiden, I got a wife: I will never forget that until my death!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Do not rush, young people! You must raise children: I got married young, I can't finish my work. What a pity of my young years, that I spoiled my world: I could have served the emperor for another ten years.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I have been married for two weeks: already a little boy is being brought to me. Don’t bring him, don’t bring him, he is not mine: I have married an honest man.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Everyone asks me where my wife is. Everyone asks me where my wife is: she went to Prague, carrying capons; she will sell the capons and give me the money. She sold the capons but drank the money away: how can she give it to me when she no longer has it?

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I have a good wife, I’m not bragging: she is a good housekeeper, wherever she fancies, she sits down, I’m telling you. She kneads the bread with cold water: if any crumb is left over like a brus, she must not give it to anyone, she blames me. That I chopped the firewood crookedly; that the bread must be straight, with no knots—that’s what she blames me for. Your place is not like ours: one shoe under the bench, the other on the chopping table—that’s not your custom! What else can I tell you: she wears a skirt as white as a chimney, tilted to one side, the corner of her scarf on her shoulder—I won’t tell you more!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: For fifty gulden, little wolves – those – those, little wolves: how I tricked myself into getting married, dear girls – those – those, dear girls! Not so much for oxen, as for a wife – a – a, as for a wife; I can sell oxen and lose the money, but not a wife, no, no, not a wife. I can sell oxen for a hundred thalers – ers – ers, for a hundred thalers: but woe to the wife, no one would give even ten kreuzers – ers – ers, ten kreuzers for her. She kneads the dough with cold water – ter – ter, cold water; rolls it over her knee, and still praises it to me that it will be good – ter – ter, that it will be good!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: A good cook. The tailor quarreled with his dear wife because she gave him undercooked porridge.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: My wife is angry with me, she isn’t speaking: if it lasts long, she will become mute!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: What a disgrace it would be for a woman to hit a man; what a disgrace it would be for him to allow it: she would hit him with a glove, throw him under the bench; what a disgrace it would be for a man to let it happen!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: On that bridge, on the stone, a woman tied a man with a strap. "Golden woman! Let me go from here: I will not call you a pack of dogs."

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: When I come home from work tired, there stands the stove, staring into the stove. If I ask: "Wife! What did you cook?" "What you put in the kitchen, that's what I made!" If I say: "Wife! Do me a count;" then she starts to get angry, she has a mouth like the devil.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: The cuckoo sang at the crossroads in the grove: every woman who beats her husband will be in heaven. The cuckoo sang nicely in the open field: every man who beats his wife will burn in hell. The cuckoo sang at the crossroads in the grove: every man who beats his wife, God will give him luck. The cuckoo sang nicely in the open field: every woman who beats her husband is fit for the knacker's yard.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I have a nice pit at home, I'll hide my mother in it; then I'll climb onto the juniper, people will say I am a widower.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I got married on Saint John's Day: my wife died, she is already lying in the grave.

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: That is why I got married, married, married, ‘) so I wouldn’t have to work hard: now I have to beat, bang with my little hands, I never expected this!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: Ah women, women! Advise me, I have a husband who torments me: he drinks all day, beats me at night, I don't even know the world! "Go out to the field, pick yourself some devil's cabbage: once he tastes it, he must die within nine days. Go out to the field, cut some onions, rub them under your eyes: so that people won’t say in gossip that you don’t love him." She had the onions, rubbed her eyes, sadly lamented: "Oh woe woe! my dear husband, what have I come to!"

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I had a husband – oh dear God! Now I remember him every single moment. What a pity for my husband that I sadly lost him! He cut noodles, chopped grass, looked after the chickens, milked the cows. What a pity, etc. He swept the yard for me, chopped firewood, lit the stove, cleaned the pens. What a pity, etc. He carried water for me, cooked meals: yet he did not enjoy a good life with me. What a pity, etc. He spun thread for me, sorted the groats: and I told him, "You foolish fool!" What a pity, etc. I had pants and he had a skirt; that is why sad times came to me! What a pity, etc. I always had a husband according to my will: now I have a husband who beats me with a stick! What a pity for my husband that I sadly lost him!

label: SVATEBNÍ
translation: I had a husband, death took him from me: who will help me – so that I can marry! Oh men, men! I cry for you, that you had a little head – like a kitten. Those white eyes in that black body looked at me – like a calf! Oh nose, nose! I pity you, that you were as crooked as a hoof. And those lovely little lips entirely: when they smiled, they had triple dimples. Oh men, men! I cry bitterly: when I hear bagpipes, I jump to the ceiling.

label: Písně společenské
translation: May the Lord God love us, love us, forgive our sins, grant us heaven, grant us heaven! We ask for nothing else, only the same: that the Lord God love us, love us!

label: Písně společenské
translation: God grant us health in the Chrudim region, God grant us health, grant it!

label: Písně společenské
translation: When our people used to drink, so will we; they kept little, we will have nothing. We will stay, we will not, we will not outlast the world; and what will remain after us, who knows, who will get it! But I don’t know how long? I am alive, I don’t know for how long? I will die, I don’t know soon or late? At home or in the field: and what will remain after me, the cheerful will get – but I don’t know how long?

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our people drank, we drink too, debts remained unpaid, let’s remain so too: spend everything you can, there will be no judgment after you die.

label: Písně společenské
translation: The lark neither plows nor sows anything, yet it happily sings in the air. We will die, we will not be, we will not conquer the world; and whatever we gain here, we will take nothing with us!

label: Písně společenské
translation: The skylark, a small bird, never idles: by day and by night it works, praising the Lord God. Let us do the same as the skylark: 'a doll in one hand, a jug in the other.'

label: Písně společenské
translation: Hurrah, long live! We will sing: as long as there is beer in the barrel, the tavern keeper will keep pouring. Hurrah, long live! We will sing: when the beer foams like this, the song is not far away.

label: Písně společenské
translation: The beer is ours, the innkeeper’s is the jug; who brewed it for us? That brewer from Chuděnice.

label: Písně společenské
translation: We drink beer slowly, as long as it flows from the keg: we drink beer, we drink it, the barmaid pours for us. Even if I was a rascal, as long as I have a jug of beer: beer, beer, red, that is my pleasure!

label: Písně společenské
translation: When we have gathered like this, friends from the wet district! When we have gathered like this together, let's pour ourselves a drink, friends from the wet district! Let's pour ourselves a drink again; the wife manages the household at home, she doesn't know how the man is doing: we are doing well, the money is in hell; we are doing well, the money is gone!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Don't worry, my wife! Where there is beer, there I am; don't worry, my wife, where you find me: where there is a glass of beer, there is also my heart; don't worry, my wife, there you will find me!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Tavern keeper, pour us a drink, I still have this krejcar: if I lose it, how will I pay you? May God Himself bless you!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Drink, little brother, drink, just don’t get drunk! If you drink away two crowns, you have a nice girl: drink, little brother, drink! Drink, little brother, drink, just don’t get drunk! If you drink away four crowns, no one will pay for you: drink, little brother, drink!

label: Písně společenské
translation: I have a little bit, I'll probably drink it easily: I'll throw the jug against the beam, break it into pieces! Beer here, beer there, musicians play! I'll pay you, don't worry about anything!

label: Písně společenské
translation: That I won't drink that beer? I will drink that beer! That jug off that beam, I'll break it too. That I won't drink that beer? I will drink that beer! Over there stands my friend, I'll toast with him too!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Drink, sonny, slowly, drink, sonny, slowly: you have a tiny little barn, you have a tiny little barn! Beer here, beer there! Musicians play; I will pay you, don’t worry about anything!

label: Písně společenské
translation: There are two of us here, my friend and I; we’ll pay for our beers, we’re not afraid of anyone, my friend and I! There are two of us here, my friend and I: once we drink our beers, we’ll get kicked out of the pub, my friend and I!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Hey, gentlemen musicians! Take your instruments, play me an uplifting tune so I can have a little drink.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Glass glass! Be greeted: how it drinks from you when the innkeeper pours into you!

label: Písně společenské
translation: You tapster's rogue! I love you: it just flows from you as if on a string!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Pour, pour, beautiful little tapster! My glass is already empty right away. Pour, don’t wait, beautiful little tapster! The beer is already quickly drunk up.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Tavern keeper silent – can dry! Pour me, pour, it's small enough anyway. Tavern keeper silent – glass dry! Pour into it again, so it doesn't rattle for me.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Just you, barmaid, pour me beer, don’t look at me as if I’m little: if you have beer, pour it for me, and I have money, I will pay for it. Just you, barmaid, pour me beer, don’t look at that I’m little: pour, pour, as long as it flows, as long as your profit doesn’t run away.

label: Písně společenské
translation: If I drink, I drink, my pocket pays, my pocket, pocket, little pocket, money is given by mother.

label: Písně společenské
translation: This little glass, it’s tiny: my Anička got drunk from it. I drank five of them, five more after that: she only one, and got drunk immediately!

label: Písně společenské
translation: If I knew what Matěj was doing, I would send him a golden beer, a golden beer, a nice round one: so that my golden Matěj would get drunk.

label: Písně společenské
translation: I servant, you servant, we servants, let's have a drink, to the health of the local pretty girls: we loved them – but no longer will we!

label: Písně společenské
translation: A grudge against someone in our house, a grudge against someone at our gates: no one can take our money, no one can give us orders: we can drink beer and have nice girls, no one can forbid us.

label: Písně společenské
translation: I will not go through that grove where the shepherd drove the sheep: I will rather go by the road, to that pretty girl, if God would grant her to me! I will not go through that forest where the butcher carried the sheep: I will rather go by the road where the lads walk, where the innkeeper brings the beer.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Mr. Little Brother, good day, day, day, good day! Lend me a basket for a week – a day – a day, for a week. “I won’t lend it, I won’t give it, I need it myself every day, day, day, every day.”

label: Písně společenské
translation: There is a brewery in that monastery, in that monastery there is a brewmaster; there is a kind brewer, he makes good beer: I would buy it all myself!

label: Písně společenské
translation: I have a little cottage on the hilltop without thatch, torn apart: when I get drunk, I will patch it up with peas– peas– peas– peas– peas– peas– pea pods.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Dad drinks, mom drinks: but the cow is already running around them; dad drinks, the children drink, there is already skin on the calf.

label: Písně společenské
translation: A glass, a glass of eightkreuzer! Whoever holds on to you retains nothing. I held on to you, I retained nothing! What I earned by day, I buried by night.

label: Písně společenské
translation: When beer was expensive back then, times were bad: I had a coat completely torn on me; some cheap beer, I have a different coat – only that it hangs on the hook by the Jewish place!

label: Písně společenské
translation: We used to have coats, damn them! Once we drink through the jacket, we'll move on,

label: Písně společenské
translation: I served at Švamberk, I served in Lhoty: I drank everything there thoroughly, I only stayed in my trousers.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Why wouldn't we be merry when the Lord God favors us? He gives us beer in this world and heaven after death. Why wouldn't we be merry when death has already fled? Hell is sown with water spirits: we will not go to hell! Or: Why wouldn't we be merry when the devil has long since died? Hell is sown with wheat and the devil has fled.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Brothers, brothers! Let us just be cheerful, brothers, brothers! Let us rejoice: want has ended for us, hardship has begun for us; brothers, brothers! Let us rejoice.

label: Písně společenské
translation: We are prodigal young men, money is useless to us: half we give to the innkeeper and half to the girl, and nothing remains for us young men.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Drink, prodigal son! Money is worthless to you. What good is a young wife to you when you’re not home all week? What good are fine horses to you when you don’t take care of them all week? What good is a nice farm to you when you’re always carousing, it falls into ruin. You don’t come home all week, Sunday comes, and you drink again.

label: Písně společenské
translation: What a trouble I have: linen trousers, pocket torn – no money! If I had money, it would make me happy, it would be worth showing off. When I had enough money, I went wherever I wanted, I went to the pub for wine: now that I have nothing, I sadly remember, well, I drink water at home.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Oh Velvary, oh Velvary! Where are my thalers? I ate, I drank, I feasted, I loved pretty girls: oh Velvary, oh Velvary! Where are my thalers?

label: Písně společenské
translation: Oh, what do our women say then, that we have drunk away their twenties, twenties, new pennies? How they lament, our women!

label: Písně společenské
translation: We won’t go, we won’t go, until we drink a lot; we won’t go, we won’t go, until we drink a lot: until we drink a lot, we still won’t go home; we won’t go, we won’t go, until we drink it all.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Once again, let us be healthy, all good friends! Whoever wants to be with us must know how to drink, honor the gentlemen brothers, and be sincere to them: this is, brothers, our virtue, tomorrow our duty to come.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Vávra is already dead, Vávra is no more: may the Lord God grant him eternal glory, he will no longer drink with us!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Tavern keepers! tavern keepers! they will carry you to the cross, they will bury you in a hole because you have such small measure. Tavern keepers! what are you doing that you pour so badly? pour, pour properly, or the devil will catch your soul!

label: Písně společenské
translation: As I blew the innkeeper away, away, that I blew him out of the line, blew him out. That’s why I blew him out of the line, blew him out, because he blew back into the line at me, blew back.

label: Písně společenské
translation: If it weren't for Mr. Pavlík, there would be no music: but our Mr. Pavlík makes sure that there is music.

label: Písně společenské
translation: If it weren't for the old master, there would be no good beer: but our master brewer keeps order, for which we should praise him. If only the women didn't sulk when we go for beer, if they didn’t get upset, then there would never be any quarrels, shouting, or mess. After all, we stay silent when they drink coffee, pouring it from the enamel pots: and in the morning they bring us some crumbs for breakfast. Let us hold to what has always been and what is proper, dear brothers: a cup of coffee for the women, a jug of beer for the men, that's how it should be!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Where there is a brewer, there is a young lad, there is also a brewery; where beer is brewed, life goes well: let’s go there and drink it until the bright morning! We drink beer to know if we understand each other; whether we love each other, whether we are friends: let’s go there and drink it until the bright morning! To drink beer, to be cheerful, to have a lovely girl in love, that, dear brothers, cheers us every moment: let’s go there and drink it until the bright morning! To drink beer, to live for the homeland, to respectfully honor language and king: that is our right, may Czechia be healthy; let’s go there and drink it until the bright morning!

label: Písně společenské
translation: We drink beer with a cluster of berries, we eat bederník; we will not fall ill, we will not die: that's what the old woman used to say when she went into the forest for that bederník, for that bederník.

label: Písně společenské
translation: A woman had a husband; he was afraid of death, he wouldn’t lie down at home, even all night; at home, at home, at home, at home, at home he wouldn’t lie down because he was afraid of death. Oh my dear wife! if that death comes, for God’s sake I beg you, shove me into a sack; drink, wife, drink, drink! shove me into a sack if that death comes. “Oh my golden husband! I don’t have a sack: I have an old basket, into that I will put you: basket, basket, on my crossbow! I will put you into that if I don’t have a sack.” Oh my dear wife! the basket is full of holes: if the Dry One comes, she will catch me; catch me, catch me, catch me, catch me, she will catch me, and it will be misery! “Oh my golden husband! I have another plan; if you wander about, I will do this to you: I will shove you into the pantry, lock all the locks, that’s what I will do to you, as I tell you.”

label: Písně společenské
translation: It is hard to suffer thirst, dear brothers! Beer is good, it refreshes the veins, as we have them, here we sing: We won’t go home until morning, until the bed is made; we won’t go home until morning, until it is day. The draft beer tastily tastes after the draft, everyone drinks, it is like a fountain, it raises glasses, nicely singing: We won’t go home, etc. The wife runs in: "Husband, come home!" The bartender welcomes her, leads her to the table: "Only the master drinks the draft, your husband!" We won’t go home, etc. The woman begins to sing a song without notes: "You will drink away your coat, pants for the last time! You don’t care about dust, you only sing: We won’t go home, etc. Beer foams nicely in the glass; everyone has their head full of sense: glory to the brewer, and also to the lad! We won’t go home, etc.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Blessed is the brewer who brewed the first beer: but cursed is the beast who paid for it first. Choir: As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end, amen. Blessed is the innkeeper who trusts the drinkers: but cursed is the beast who measures their foam! As it was in the beginning, etc. Blessed is the innkeeper who waits on the drinkers: but cursed is the beast who strips off their coats. As it was in the beginning, etc.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Grass, grass, green grass! Who will live on you, grass, when I am rotting in the grave? Grass, grass, green grass! Beer, beer, red beer! Who will drink you, beer, when I am rotting in the grave? Beer, beer, red beer! Girl, girl, lovely girl! Who will love you when I am sleeping in the grave? Girl, girl, lovely girl!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Beer, beer! Who will drink you? Who will drink you, beer, when I am rotting in the grave? Beer, beer! Who will drink you? Brothers, brothers! You will drink! But who will drink them when you too are rotting? Brothers, brothers! Who will drink them? Then our boys will drink them: they will have boys too, they will also drink beer: beer, you will be drunk forever!

label: Písně společenské
translation: We drink, brothers, we drink beer, let the water flow away: we wash ourselves with water and drink beer. Those who lived before us also gladly drank beer; and those who will live after us will also drink beer.

label: Písně společenské
translation: He will drink, little brother, he will drink, but the landlord will write that off; he will drink, little brother, drink, as long as dad dresses you: once he stops dressing you, you will hardly be able to drink; he will drink, little brother, he will drink, but the landlord will write that off!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Oh son, son! What are you doing? You just keep drinking and don’t care about anything; what kind of farmer will you be!

label: Písně společenské
translation:
Friend! Don’t drink, your cottage is burning, it will kill your wife, your children will remain: don’t go there, don’t drink it, that bitter beer!  
“Friend! Just drink, let the cottage burn; if the devil takes the wife, he will take the children too: just come there, have some of that bitter beer!”

label: Písně společenské
translation: Well! Let God's will be done, I care about nothing anymore! I will sell my wife, I will sell my children, I will spend the money on drinking: well! Let God's will be done, I care about nothing anymore!

label: Písně společenské
translation: We met in good will, we will part in good will: before we part, we will drink once more, with the Lord God we will go. With the Lord God we will go, we will remember: we had a good time, we were glad to see each other, we will come again another time. We will come again another time, we will stay longer here: you brotherly love, and not Pharisaic love, stay with us, amen. Stay with us, amen! 1) I will not cease to proclaim that our God is love, to Him be praise, honor forever and ever, amen!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Oh, we poor orphans! We drink, we drink, we drink, we drink, we drink day and night. If the beer were to end, what would become of us, what would become of us, what would become of us, what would become of us, what would become of us orphans! Beer has its custom that it turns the wise into fools, the fools into wise, that it makes fools out of the wise. Two lead him by the shoulders so he doesn’t, so he doesn’t, so he doesn’t, so he doesn’t, so he doesn’t kick the stone. A third one straightens his legs, the right over the left, the left over the right, crossing one leg over the other. Puddles like pavement, to the poles – pum – pum, pum – pum – to the poles, he sends greetings to the poles. People look out the windows, thinking, thinking, thinking, thinking, thinking that they are walking with Nedvěd: but they are with godfather Matěj!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Have a good time here, friends from the wet district, have a good time together here: we will have a game, friends from the wet district, we will have a game upstairs!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Little Honzík, little boy! Who are they playing for, for whom, like us, little legs, when we walk home together.

label: Písně společenské
translation: The sledges are going with a winch, saying that we are not going home: from Saturday to Saturday, it will soon be a week.

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I leave the pub, my wife, be silent for me! When I leave drunk, you, my wife, support me.

label: Písně společenské
translation: They are already leading him, Martina, his pocket has thinned out: play for him, blow for him, musicians, march, so Martinek has courage in front of the woman! They are already leading him through the garden, one in front, the other behind: play for him, blow for him, musicians, march, so Martinek has courage in front of the woman!

label: Písně společenské
translation: If my wife says something to me, I’ll slap her so hard it will leave a mark.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Which then you, my wife, do not know, do not know? After all, I am a cooper from Podmokly, a cooper. I make barrels for beer, barrels, the tavern-keepers know me everywhere, everywhere.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Don’t bluff, woman, I’ll buy you a mill: if you bluff, I’ll give it to someone else!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Don’t whine, my woman, I’ll buy you a dress; if you keep whining, I’ll twist your neck!

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I was walking home from the pub at midnight, I had no fear of the dark night; when I arrived home, I knocked on my wife’s door: “Open up, my dear wife, I’m coming home.” Then my wife began to greet me sadly, tearing my wig off my head, and that was a welcome until dawn; then I got a bitter breakfast from my wife. I sadly remembered the brewer, that an unhappy mother once gave birth to him; he brews good beer, but he does not manage his pocket well: now I was punished – I have to pluck feathers.

label: Písně společenské
translation: A drunk man. What is happening to me, no one wishes me well; they point their fingers at me, they consider me a drunkard: as if I were not worthy to be a little merry and drank away my money! If I drank away, say, a ducat, the landlady would not be angry; the landlord does not quarrel with me, he pours me beer: as if I were not worthy, etc. Come on, gentlemen musicians! play cheerfully for me; play, trumpet in all directions, and cheer me up: as if I were, etc. A head full of merriment, not a penny in my pocket; if my wife has any money, I take it all from her: as if I were, etc. Yesterday I went to the pub, she was running after me; as soon as I had a half-liter of beer, she attacked me: as if I were, etc. She always blames me – believe me, on my word – that my coat is full of patches, my trousers full of holes: as if I were, etc. Oh, my dear little wife! just don't be angry with me, rather earn me some money for a beer: as if I were not worthy to be a little merry and drink some beer!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Heydumda! Heydumdal, but the Lord God will not abandon us: if He puts us in the mud, He will claw us out again; heydumdal heydumda! but the Lord God will not abandon us. Heydumda! Heydumda! but the Lord God will not abandon us: if He puts us in a puddle, He will help us again; heydumda! heydamda! but the Lord God will not abandon us.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Blessed is the one who has nothing: they do not worry where to hide anything. They lie down boldly, get up boldly, no one steals anything from them.

label: Písně společenské
translation: God forbid! If I were to be ill, who would then serve me? Who else but my dear, my charming Andulka. God forbid! If I were to die before I pay what I owe. I owe nothing more than two Czech coins, a pretty girl will pay that. What she pays with groš coins, I will repay with kisses.

label: Písně společenské
translation: I have a coat, I do, but without sleeves: if God grants health, there will be sleeves too.

label: Písně společenské
translation: I have new pants, my dad bought them for me so I would go after girls, and I looked good twisting in them.

label: Písně společenské
translation: If I were the son of a farmer, how well I would carry myself: but since I am a cottager’s son, I wear a coat without buttons.

label: Písně společenské
translation: They offered me four pounds of diamonds for my plant, but I did not want to give it away: a plant is better than a coat. I will sleep on the stove in the plant, and it is nice after all; I have to polish a coat: that is why I do not want to give the plant away.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Torn pants, the wind blows into them; I will sew them up: a spider is spinning thread!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Everyone worries about my poverty: and I do not care, praise the Lord God!

label: Písně společenské
translation: No one is as well off as those in hell: they never buy firewood and sit in warmth.

label: Písně společenské
translation: I have a house bought in Prague, only I don't know which one is mine, the emperor has the keys to it; there is money in it worth thousands! Later addition: If he lent me those keys, I would diligently learn: to read, to write, to count, to love pretty girls.

label: Písně společenské
translation: If I had what I would want, what I would like: a nice girl – a house in the city – how it would please me!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Praised be Jesus Christ! – "Forever and ever, amen." – If I had a lot of money, I would be a great lord!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Mikuláš lost a honeycomb, Mikulášek lost a skirt: they searched, did not find, and both were sad.

label: Písně společenské
translation: I am from Vorlík, I have a father there, a mother too, a sister too, and a brother who is a bricklayer.

label: Písně společenské
translation: I am from Roudnice, I have parents there, I am the son of honest parents: my father is a butcher, a great brute; I am the son of honest parents. I am from Roudnice, I have parents there, I am the son of honest parents: my mother served as a Voršila at the rectory; I am the son of honest parents. I am from Roudnice, I have parents there, I am the son of honest parents: I have a godfather who is a mayor, he can prove it; I am the son of honest parents.

label: Písně společenské
translation: There is nothing good in that world2:  ‘) the poor robbed the needy. He caused him significant damage: he cut off his empty pocket. And in that pocket was a plum, and in that plum was a pit. And inside the pit was a kernel: here we have it, godfather Vávra!

label: Písně společenské
translation: The legless one climbs the pear tree, choosing his mates. The armless one throws at him, hoping to hit and knock him down. The mute hums along the way, the deaf one listens to him in the thorns. The dead one runs across the road, the blind one measures him with his eyes.

label: Písně společenské
translation: The old woman was practicing magic, turning barley into groats. And from millet, she made pearl millet: those were her spells!

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I walked past the green thornbush, a goat jumped out of the thornbush onto the m2; the goat jumped out and scared me so much that I was pale from it.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our dog, our dog broke his tail: out of his kindness, he stuck it into the fence; our dog, our dog broke his tail.

label: Písně společenské
translation: The cat crawls through the hole, the dog through the window: if it doesn't rain, we won't get wet.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our tomcat broke his leg, he didn't come home for a week: he was a pretty tabby, he had a broken tail.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our tiny kitten caught a linnet bird. And our even tinier one caught a bird – half a mouse. She bit off its little wings, how will the poor thing live then? She bit off its tail, how will the poor thing live then?

label: Písně společenské
translation: A cat asked another cat if a tomcat has claws? "As old as you are, that’s how stupid you are: how would he lick into the hole?" A cat asked another cat if a tomcat has kittens? "As old as you are, that’s how stupid you are: he is our boss after all!"

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our tabby tomcat has a cut-off tail, cut – cut – cut – cut – cut – cut – cut-off.

label: Písně společenské
translation: The fly landed on the spider: the spider got angry and crushed the fly – foolish fly!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Which way would you go, Janko, if your master caught you? "I would make a door out of the window, I wouldn’t wait for him to open it."

label: Písně společenské
translation: Where are you going, Jiřík? "To the pub, daddy!" Do you have money, Jiřík? "Not a penny, daddy!" Stay home, Jiřík! "You come too, daddy!"

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our dad went mushroom picking, what if he gets lost there! "He won't get lost, don't worry, he will bring you mushrooms."

label: Písně společenské
translation: Don't go there, it's raining, there's a lot of mud; you'll fall, you won't get up, you'll be calling "Dad!"

label: Písně společenské
translation: Mud. Hop, dad! through the mud. "Be quiet, boy, it’s splashing!"

label: Písně společenské
translation: It was winter, the wind was blowing, our daddy was looking out the window: don't look out the window, daddy, the wind will blow your head away.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Now you are ours, daddy: how then you used to walk, how you used to turn around, until you became ours?

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our old daddy! You have a gray head: as long as you are among us, things are still good with us, our old daddy! Our old daddy! may you be preserved for us: as long as your head is alive, our management will be good, our old daddy!

label: Písně společenské
translation: I have gray hair, I have gray hair, I have a gray head; I have gray hair, I have gray hair, I have a gray beard: a head from worries, a beard from mischief; I have gray hair, I have gray hair, I have a gray head.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Our dear late father, may the Lord God grant him the kingdom: he often helped old maids to some joy. Our dear late father, may the Lord God grant him heaven: he used to carry old maids in a wheelbarrow to the forest.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Oh my God! That’s Švanda, in our house there’s nothing but Mandas: old woman Manda, mother Manda, daughter Manda, whore Manda, and even that scoundrel is also Manda!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Pepík, Pepík! What is grandma doing? "She is sitting on the little stove, grating apples."

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I walked to Drahoslavice today a week ago, I met seventeen old women on the road: hey you old women, old crones! Tell me, where is Drahoslavice located?

label: Písně společenské
translation: The old woman rocked the devil on a willow stump: "Hush, my little Devil! I won't tell on you."

label: Písně společenské
translation: The Hajdaláci always wondered why the Hajdalačky would lose their teeth? An old woman explained with a brush: that they supposedly talk about them a lot.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Hajdaláci. The hajdaláci on the hill were dragging a carcass; they couldn’t pull it out, so they had to hitch four cats and four dogs; they dragged it all the way to the village, ha, ha, ha, ha, ha!

label: Písně společenské
translation: Violets for maidens, roses for young men, nettles for widows, and nettles for old women. Cakes for maidens, buns for young men, dumplings for widows, potatoes for old women. Shoes for maidens, boots for young men, slippers for widows, slippers for old women. Paradises for maidens, heavens for young men, purgatory for widows, and hell for old women.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Dad caught the jay, mom caught it too; dad caught it, mom caught it, all the children caught it; the jay won’t fly away. Instead of "caught" – "caught" (feminine) etc., in the other verses it is sung: killed – plucked – baked – cut – dad ate the jay etc.

label: Písně společenské
translation: We caught a salmon, we caught it, we caught it, we caught it at night. Instead of "we caught" in the other verses, it is sung: we killed, we partied, we cooked, we fried, we cut, we ate the salmon, etc.

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I served that first summer, I earned a chick for it: and that chick crows around the yard; my girl is crying at home in the chamber. When I served that second summer, I earned a duck for it: and that duck waddles like a duck, and that chick crows around the yard; my girl is crying at home in the chamber. When I served that third summer, I earned a goose for it: and that goose walks barefoot, and that duck waddles like a duck, etc. When I served that fourth summer, I earned a piglet for it: and that pig is like pepper, and that goose walks barefoot, etc. When I served that fifth summer, I earned a calf for it: and that calf chatters with its mouth, and that pig is like pepper, etc. When I served that sixth summer, I earned a cow for it: and that cow gives milk and that calf chatters with its mouth, etc. When I served that seventh summer, I earned an ox for it: and that ox is like a post, and that cow gives milk, etc. When I served that eighth summer, I earned shoes for it: and those shoes go to work, and that ox is like a post, etc. When I served the last summer, I earned a girl for it: that girl like a bud, and those shoes go to work, and that ox is like a post, and that cow gives milk, and that calf chatters with its mouth, and that pig is like pepper, and that goose walks barefoot, and that duck waddles like a duck, and that chick crows around the yard; my girl is making the bed in the chamber.

label: Písně společenské
translation: Hejdum dá, mother dear, how many sons do you have? "One Mr. Mates, the curly one the second, and the little poor third one." Hejdum dá, mother dear! What do you give them to do? "For Mr. Mates featherwork, for the curly one manure to shovel, and for the little poor one to play with dolls." Hejdum dá, mother dear! What do you give them to eat? "For Mr. Mates a piece of meat, for the curly one offal, and for the little poor one a chick." Hejdum dá, mother dear! What do you give them to drink? "For Mr. Mates beer from the tap, for the curly one from a shard, and for the little poor one a little wine." Hejdum dá, mother dear! Where do you put them? "Mr. Mates on a board, the curly one under the board, and the little poor one into the blankets."

label: Písně společenské
translation:
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! How shall I welcome them? “The hairy one: Go away again! Mr. Vít: I welcome you! And the young, knowledgeable gentleman: I kiss you!”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Where shall I seat them? “The hairy one by the stove, Mr. Vít at the table, and the young, knowledgeable gentleman on the bench.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Which tablecloth shall I put? “Hemp for the hairy one, linen for Mr. Vít, and damask for the young, knowledgeable gentleman.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Which plate shall I give? “Wooden for the hairy one, earthenware for Mr. Vít, and silver for the young, knowledgeable gentleman.”
