Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Which spoon shall I give? “Wooden for the hairy one, pewter for Mr. Vít, and silver for the young, knowledgeable gentleman.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Which meat shall I serve? “Mutton for the hairy one, beef for Mr. Vít, and game for the young, knowledgeable gentleman.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! What shall I give them to drink? “Beer for the hairy one, wine for Mr. Vít, and Malvasia for the young, knowledgeable gentleman.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Where shall I make their beds? “The hairy one by the stove, Mr. Vít at the table, and the young, knowledgeable gentleman on the bed.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear little one! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! What shall I cover them with? “The hairy one with straw, Mr. Vít with a bundle of sticks, and the young, knowledgeable gentleman with a featherbed.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! When shall I wake them? “The hairy one at dawn, Mr. Vít for breakfast, and the young, knowledgeable gentleman for lunch.”  
Hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! Guests are coming to us: hejdum, hejdum, my dear mother! What shall I have them do? “The hairy one to shovel manure, Mr. Vít to mow the meadow, and the young, knowledgeable gentleman to court.”

label: Písně společenské
translation: Where have you been, little goat, little goat, little goat! Where have you been, my little goat? "At the mill behind the village headman, sir, sir, at the mill behind the village headman, my lord!" What were you doing there, etc.? "I was sweeping up the dust, etc." Where did you lie down there, etc.? "In the shed under the stove," etc. What did you drink then, etc.? "Diluted beer, diluted beer," etc. What were you beaten with then, etc.? "With a small log, a little shovel," etc. How did you cry then, etc.? "Me-e-e, me-e-e!" etc. How did you trot then, etc.? "Tip-tap, tip-tap!" etc.

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I went from Kutná Hora, I saw Nové Dvory there; when I went from Nové Dvory, I saw Kutná Hora there.

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I am away, I am not home; when I am home, I am not away. Better to be in the army than a soldier, better a soldier than in the army.

label: Písně společenské
translation: When I went to ring the bell, he was lurking there, waiting for me. I saw him crouching against the wall. Do you think I don’t know you? You’re Martin from the parish! He was raising a stone at me and said, "I’ll hit you!" I’m not afraid of you, even though you’re a bit bigger! I slammed them down on the ground and ran home.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The Guardian. A gentleman came to us, they call him the guardian, he has a wig made of dog hair, he tosses it back and forth: this is a gentleman, this is a gentleman, this is a true guardian. What more can I tell you: he spit on his shoe, from the shoe onto his hand, smeared his wig: this is a gentleman, this is a gentleman, this is a true guardian. He comes from Kosmonosy, his nose is a little crooked; he snuffs tobacco, just crunches it, one lot a day is not enough for him: this is a gentleman, this is a gentleman, this is a true guardian.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Our old Capuchin has a long beard: Vávra polishes it, sharpens a knife on it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am a Franciscan friar, I have nothing else but these few little wooden slippers made of linden wood. I carry a rosary at my waist and a breviary: I pray to the Lord God for unfaithful girls. "If you are a Franciscan friar, say: Pater noster; leave the girls alone, mind your own church."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: If you might come to the little window, to look at my Babrlička: Babrličko, Babrle! come and cheer my heart. "Why would I then cheer your heart, since I am not your beloved: you have nothing to do with girls, you pray the breviary!"

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: In my life, I have never seen a chaplain wandering after girls until morning. The priest came to him and said: 1) Hey, chaplain! What are you doing? "Kyrie eleison, Christe eleison – I am praying the breviary."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am a zeměmělec, I have no bread: I only lead a little mare to the alley, there I will sell her. I will buy a beautiful lady with it: she will sit, nicely look at me, and I at her.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: There was a greedy goat that went to the lord's to graze; misfortune struck unexpectedly, the choirmaster broke her head. "Goodbye, Mr. Choirmaster! I am bidding you farewell now, and I entrust my hairy skin to you for a wig. If you don't wear wigs, you can keep it: you can wrap your gout in it!"

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When the lords of Mrákot went to gather beech nuts, they lost a wig, and never found it again. When they then came home, they gathered in counsel and discussed where they might find it again. The wig is in the forest, all torn apart, lying there under the beech tree like a dead crow. The hunter aimed, thinking it was an owl; but it was the wig of the council primate! The hunter from Řásenice is their messenger, and he knows best what troubles the Mrákot lords: oh, it troubles them, troubles them, that they have no cloaks, because they tore them on the beech nuts in the brushwood.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: And those boys Hajdaláci bought a bass: they didn't know where to put it, so they went with it to the primas player. And the bass stood by the stove at the primas player’s place: when the primas player lit the fire, the bass played by itself.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: My dear father, old and gray, made me study; he had me learn, study, so that I would know something: and instead of studying, I set off to love; instead of a chronicle, I took a charming girl into my hands.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The scribe can write nicely and count well: he writes zeros only for his own sake, when the digit matters to him.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I went to Prague to get blood sausages, I met a fop on the way. He was not a fop, he was a daredevil: if I hadn’t run away, he would have bitten me.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The craftsmen are worthy, they carry themselves like gentlemen; girls follow them, bring them buns and pies, and when they bring them – they are sometimes even beaten.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Our mother always says: Don't take a craftsman, he has no means to transport firewood, you have to carry it in a basket. A farmer has three or four horses, harnesses them and goes for it: you will be the housewife going from the pantry to the kitchen. And yet I don't want a farmer, let me take firewood wherever I want: a craftsman has money, he can buy it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: What will you give me? I am a cooper, I will make you a tub, a bucket, a small tub – if you give me a dog as payment.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: And I keep asking, who is it that won't leave us alone? And I keep asking, who's knocking, and he the cooper on the hoop: and I keep asking, who is it that won't leave us alone.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Onion sellers are coming, bringing onions, on– on– on– onions, they will sell them in threes. Sweet onions, the doll is smooth, on– on– on– onions, we will chop them into a cup.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Hop hey! onion sellers, onion sellers are coming, hop hey! they are carrying onions: sweet onions, my doll is smooth; hop hey! onion sellers, onion sellers are coming.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I was carrying rags, I am carrying rags: I broke the handlebar, my dear Ančička! I had to carry, carry, carry, carry, I had to carry.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Behind the gates, behind the gates, behind the gatehouse, the ragman defeated the ragwoman. "Since you defeated me, help me get up, help me put these rags into the basket."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Beast gamekeeper, gamekeeper, gamekeeper, beast gamekeeper, gamekeeper: if you hadn't begged, you wouldn't have been carrying chicks, but you wouldn't be a gamekeeper!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation:
Son: I’m not going to the forest, I won’t go to the forest: if the forester catches me, he would take my axe. The little axe costs two gold coins and the handle one toral: if the forester catches me, he would take it all from me.

Father: You will go to the forest, you will surely go to the forest: if the forester comes for you, you will have nothing to fear. That forest belongs to our community: am I some kind of dog in it? If you carry away brushwood, the forester will carry it away a hundredfold.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The farmer is cutting in the forest, the wolf is carrying the gamekeeper: "Wait, farmer, you must pay seven coins! Give me some cabbage, I'll give you whole wood; give me some mushrooms, I'll give you a whole oak." Gamekeeper, gamekeeper, gamekeeper, 1) that is a secret beast: he sold the little oak for a salt barrel.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The gamekeeper is already a dead man, the gamekeeper is already a dead man: may the Lord give him barley straw, and oats for the ladder. 1)

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I was walking and I had a brand-new purse, I was walking and I had a bag full of eggs: the hussars met me, they took my eggs; I was walking and I had an empty purse.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Franta went among the men, sold his coat, bought cards. "Better cards than a coat: we can play cards."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Don't be angry, cutie! Just give us all some tobacco; for this little song, pull out the little box, give to all of us—give—give, give to all of us around!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Kudrna is riding, 1) he is going to Brno, carrying grease, his bike is creaking. Mr. Kudrna! Grease your bike so it doesn’t creak all over Brno.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The ugly chimney sweep wanted to get married: no maiden wanted him because he was as black as a crow, so he had to marry a widow.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Chimney, chimney, my chimney! For that, I ask you, just stand still a little. If I hadn’t held you, chimney, you would have long ago been lying in the sand. – What are you bricklayers, you craftsmen! You worked by hand, but took the money first: that’s why, for your gain, the chimney lies in the sand! – Chimney here, chimney there, but I will make another: I’ll add a handful of ashes, the chimney will be whole again: chimney here, chimney there, but I will make another!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am from Kutná Hora, from Kutná Hora, the son of a tow merchant: I have three houses in Prague – when I buy them, they will be mine; I am from Kutná Hora, from Kutná Hora, the son of a tow merchant.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Too much tow, too much tow, dear lady: will you also put some on our shirt?

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: And you blacksmiths, you filthy ones, you just wait for Sunday: when Sunday comes, you drink happily; then suddenly you make a mess, until there is nothing left in the mug.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: No one knows like me what blacksmiths drink for: they hammer new nails from old iron.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I do nothing, yet I am well, I do nothing, I am well: blacksmiths work, yet they have nothing; I do nothing, I am well.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Blacksmith, blacksmith, blacksmith, one more time blacksmith, you broke your anvil, what will you smith on?

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The tailor was born, let us rejoice; he went for water, breaks the jug, let us be glad.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Left, right! – the tailor and the shoemaker are two knights: one repairs clothes, the other glues shoes.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The bagpipes played at Pobuda’s, I heard them – they were offering me a gardener, but I didn’t want him; and I preferred a tailor, he’s somewhat better: he will sew me a corset from some portela.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am, I am from Rakovník, I have a lover there, what kind, a handsome journeyman tailor!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Hello, hopsa, my dear! Be honest with me: I will sew you a new dress, dad will make a coat.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A tailor got married, he had no bread for tomorrow: he trimmed the crust for the soup so that it would taste better.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Tailor, tailor, tailor, once again tailor, he broke the needle, and it was the thinnest!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The hunchback argues with the tailor that he sewed her back crooked. "Be quiet, hunchback! Don't argue, take the mirror, look." The hunchback trembles with anger, her bones rattling in her body. "Don't mock me, you're crooked all over yourself!"

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I bought myself corduroy for a cap, corduroy, corduroy: the tailor cut it nicely for me, I had some left for a vest, for a ve— for a vest.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Klimánek and Klimánka wandered to the fair. He bought clothes for twenty-five. He had a coat tailored from head to toe. Then he still had enough left for two gloves. The gloves remained – they didn’t have a thumb!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A flea was wandering, met by a louse: Where are you going, dear auntie, where are you wandering? "I’m wandering to a tailor’s sleeve for a fitting; I’m supposed to be there today."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Tailor's strength. Four tailors were lifting a hop pole onto a calf, and they were still asking it to kneel. Nine tailors were swimming on a leaf, and the shoemaker threw them off.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: My goodness, what are you doing? Your head is bandaged! Does it hurt? Does it hurt? I am a doctor, I will heal you. "I don't want a doctor, I prefer to heal myself: my dear, sweet one is my only doctor."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: They are millers, they are tough men when the stonemasons are quarrelling with them; they are millers when their mills are running. When their mills do not want to grind, they start crying like beasts; they are millers, indeed, when their mills are running.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The miller in love. Three, four Sundays now, our miller hasn't been milling. It's not true, it's a lie: the miller is still milling to this day; the mills are running, rattling – he is off to see his beloved.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Dear lady! Is Ančička at home? "She’s not at home, she went to the mill, is the wheat being ground?" The wheat is not being ground: the miller looks grumpy; the mill helper says he will fix it, that it will be ground by morning.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Ančička from Tejn, from Tejn, do not go to the mill: the elder is coughing, the young man is clumsy; Ančička from Tejn, from Tejn, do not go to the mill.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: That miller’s gang carries themselves proudly: no ordinary girl is right for them.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: She walked by the stream, catching small fish. Small fish for the fishermen, pretty girls for the millers. Even prettier for the brewers, those are the more proud lads. For the huntsman, all sorts of girls, wet up to the waist.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Wake up, little huntsman! It’s a white day, the farmer is bringing a load of wood from the forest, a load of wood, pinewood: go out to meet him on the path. Wait, little huntsman! I will tell you that you are selling the wood while standing, standing, by the stand, and you are taking the money to your Marjánka.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: My dear went to the grass, took a sickle and a small sheet. A little hunter came to her, took her sheet and sickle.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Who is walking along the lord's dam? Who is walking by the pond? It is the lord's huntsman and his young man: they are waiting there for a hare.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The hunters are already coming out of the forest, coming out of the forest, the gamekeeper is still in the forest: everyone has a flower behind their hat, only she trembles all over.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The dog jumped over the oats, over the green meadow; the hunter followed him, a feather on his hat. The dog jumped over the oats, on a hare's trail: more than one young lady watched the hunter.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Green little hunter, what is running out of the cabbage? Running, running is a little roe deer, a black-eyed girl, little hunter, shoot her for me!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: On that green meadow, deer are grazing: a little hunter in a green vest is watching them. Wait, wait, my dear! I will shoot a deer for you: so that you will like the green vest. What do you say, girls, what do you think yourselves: whether you prefer the vest, or do you see the hunter? “No, not like that, the hunter! But it doesn't come from the heart: I don't like the vest as much as I like the hunter!”

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: In the green grove, the mountains crack; green hunters are doing well: they watch a roe deer in the morning, a gunshot strikes the heart, legs give out.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Near Hosín there is a green meadow, a little hunter killed a deer on it, hupa, hupa, hupa! he killed a deer on it, hup!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Across the forest, across the grove, across the little lake, a young buck chased a young doe; she seemed pretty to him, as she smiled at him: Come here, come closer, I'll give you a little kiss!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The hare runs along the road, the hunter lurks behind the wheat: "Wait, little hare! I'll give you a hit when your little head turns to the side."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Oh, how unfortunate I am, the poor little hare! When the beautiful sun rises, I go out to the field, stretch my runs, turn here and there, feel disheartened: oh, I am afraid of the huntsman! I sit down in the clover at the edge, listen for where the dogs are barking: if they are running towards me, I shudder inside, hurry back to the forest – where, poor me, can I hide! The huntsman approaches through the valley, and behind me the trackers through the bushes; then my time has come: the huntsman aims, shoots, comes to get me, he is cheerful!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A wild goose was flying, it landed on our dyke: wait, you wild goose! The hunter is lurking there for you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The duck is swimming on the pond, the drake behind her even more: wait, duck, there will be a fight once we meet on the hill.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Whistler. The young stag snaps at the whistler, the whistler: the young stag nips at the young whistler, trying to catch it, little hunter, but it cannot be caught.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Jíra went to catch quails: they all flew away from him. He came home, cried for them: he took a slice of bread – and went after them again.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A crow flew in, sat on the thorn; a second flew in, sat beneath it: a young hunter is hurrying to the forest, intending to catch her for her beauty.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A hunter shot a blackbird, gave it a small wound: the blackbird trembled until it reached a green grove. It sat on a little fir tree, singing a little song: "Hunter, tralala! I have won against you, little hunter!"

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A hunter shot at a jay, hitting it on the collar: it got scared and escaped to a green grove. He wanted to kill the hunter, but he didn’t know how to load for it: it perched on a twig, bent its little head, and he had to catch it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Where have you been, little cuckoo, that you haven’t cuckooed for so long? “I was sitting on a beech tree, calling cuckoo! I cuckooed to the doll that she’s going to marry a young man: I was sitting on a beech tree, calling cuckoo!”

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I met hunters, they were leading dogs, dogs, dogs; one went woof, woof, woof! and the other one arf, arf, arf! I will go with you, hunters! leave those dogs at home, dogs; I am afraid of dogs, ba, ba, ba, but I like you, la, la, la! If you want to hunt with us, you must shoot like we do; sing tralala with us! laugh hahaha with us!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Mr. Zeman sent me to hunt a doe: and I ran into the chamber, grabbed the lady. Hey, Mr. Zeman! here is the doe for you. "Oh, foolish fool! that is my lady."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Little huntsman, young lad! Move your little gun aside. "I won't, I won't move until I kill her, I won't, I won't move until I kill her." I have five coins, to whom will I give them if I do not have my huntsman here! I have lost my huntsman, my charming huntsman!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The doll walks sadly because her heart aches; yesterday she was on the grass by the green grove. My dear was walking home from the grass, the lady came to her: Oh my little daughter, my daughter! Where have you been so long? "My dear lady! I love the huntsman: the huntsman loads his gun and kills the little hare." Záb. mysl. I, 50.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Oh, what a lovely thing it is that I am a hunter! I shot a little dove, it fell into the juniper. My dear saw it and ran there; the little dove flew from the juniper to the maple. And then a leaf from the maple fell on both of us – escort me, my maiden, to our courtyard. "I don’t want to, I won’t go, I know I won’t be yours: you are the son of wealthy parents, and I am a poor girl." Don’t worry about that I am rich: I will still marry you, just don’t be angry with me. "I’m not angry with you, but I love you: come to the window in the evening, I will give you a nice kiss."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: We will go hunting, hunting, my comrade, my, my, my, my comrade: hunting, hunting, through the pine grove, my comrade, my, my, my, my comrade! The hare is running, running, my comrade: let’s release the hounds, so they catch the hare, my comrade! The roe deer is running, running, my comrade: let’s release the hounds, so they catch the doe, my comrade! The deer is running, running, my comrade: let’s release the hounds, so they catch the young buck, my comrade! The maiden is running, running, my comrade: let’s release the hounds, so they catch the maiden, my comrade! We will share, share, my comrade: you get the hare and the doe, I get the young buck and the maiden, my comrade! If you think that’s too little, too little, my comrade: you get all the game and I get the charming girl, my comrade!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Wake up, wake up, little huntsman! The dawn is rising, go to the grove; call the puppies, let them loose into the traps there on that doe. "She won’t go into the traps, all in vain, for she has a cunning heart: I will set the greyhounds on her and won’t let her go until I shoot her." He shot her fatally in the heart, then the little huntsman realized: how he shoots sharply right into her heart, the graceful doe. She still ran into the grove, there she lay down under a little fir tree; she left only tracks, she was shot, she collapsed.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Waiting. There is nothing better in the world than a green grove: when that beautiful little moon clears up above it! And here I am with my beloved, waiting again for wild game, for wild game, for roe deer, stags, and hares. A hare runs through the valley, a roe deer runs through the grove; the little hare approaches my maiden, he does not know her. "I shot at the hare, only that I missed him: oh, my dear God, how I aimed! Teach me, little hunter, teach me to shoot: I want to gladly reward you for it sometime. I will shoot a roe deer for you, give you a beautiful little kiss; you will be happy about it, we will both have enough." Záb. mysl I, 50, 51.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation:
Get up, my dear! Prepare breakfast: I’m going to the grove to hunt hares. To hunt hares, to catch squirrels: get up, my dear! before it dawns.  
"I won’t get up, my head hurts: if you’re sincere, come and bind it for me." He bound her head, his hands dropped: "What is it, my dear, what has frightened you so? You are, my dear! you are to blame for getting me drunk with red wine. Red wine, white brandy: I could have been a maiden till now. I a maiden, you a lad, we could both have walked under the wedding wreath."  
You are, my dear, more guilty, for luring me into your hall. Into your hall, then into the little room, you gave me sweet kisses there. About our pilgrimage, about the feast, we will be sanctified together already. Then I will take you around the neck, give you a kiss, squeeze your hand.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation:
The Little Dove as a Messenger.  
The little dove flew high to the oak on the green hillside – oh, who will save him!  
With a green hat, the hunter stands under the oak: my dear little dove, fly away from the oak!  
The little dove flies away far beyond the oak; the hunter with the hat always stands under the oak.  
The hunter under the oak with the green hat: "Dear little dove! Fly to my beloved.  
Fly to my beloved beyond the forest at a mile; tell her, little dove, that I am standing by the oak.  
With the green hat, that I am standing under the oak, so that she may weave for me what she promised me.  
What she promised me when she was here with me: a little bouquet for the hat on a blue ribbon."  
Pleasant amusements. I, 58.  
This delicate song is merely a fortunate imitation of folk songs, presumably the work of a girl.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Nunváři, nunváři! bad stewards: from early morning you are staring into the jug.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I had a kind wigmaker, he drowned in the brewery: look for him, find him; the wig lies there under him.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The baker bakes rolls, pinching off pieces; the baker’s wife helps him, the two of them pinch off pieces.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Gingerbread maker. The gingerbread maker came from the town, came, girls, to us: come, come buy marzipan for the boys.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Get up, Andulka! Serve breakfast, and I have to sail: the ship is loaded. Andulka got up, served breakfast, and even saw me off to the ship.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Who is that man by the roadside crosses? I saw him, he was embarrassed – he was tending the ram; his little hands are dirty, yet he wears gloves on them: pity you, my lady, that he kisses you!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have it worse than a dog, I have to guard the village: a dog crawls into the straw somewhere, falls asleep while thieves are coming; and I defend the barns with my stick. Others sleep soundly with their wives; others have good homes, and I have to walk around the village like a ghost and wake the dogs.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Old Czech song of the night watchman. At every hour: Praise every spirit of the Lord and Jesus, his son: it struck ten (eleven, etc.) o’clock. Watch the light, the fire, so that no harm comes to anyone: rest with the Lord God! At every quarter hour: The tenth hour and the first (second, third) quarter: life and death to many people; it comes suddenly, taking both beggar and king. At dawn: Praise every spirit of the Lord, and Jesus, his son: it struck the third (fourth) hour. Rise, rise, faithful soul, and kneel down, invoke God the Lord!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Rides, rides the Count of Hořovice, rides, rides, the Lord of Hořovice: he has black horses like two little fish, rides, rides to Rokycany.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: There are, there are crayfish in the creek, there are, there are crayfish in the creek: we will go for them, we will catch them by the moonlight at night. Fish, fish, fish, crayfish, crayfish, crayfish! We will do it like this, like this, like this: we will make a bag for them, we will watch to see if the crayfish get caught. Take, take, my maiden, a little basket, take, take, my maiden, a basket: and I will also send you some crayfish to the kitchen. Fish, fish, fish, crayfish, crayfish, crayfish! We will do it like this, like this, like this: we will make a bag for them, we will watch to see if the crayfish get caught.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: They are not better craftsmen, like butchers: they get up in the morning, go to the shop, hold a glass.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The girl went to the shop for a pound of meat, she didn’t want it from the breast, only from the tail: “I don’t want it from the breast, it’s not tasty; just you, butcher, cut me what I want.”

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Pond keeper. Open the door! The pond keeper is coming: my dear lady! Cut a piece of butter, cut a big one from deep down, so your hand doesn't shake for a year and a day.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Maltster’s glory! Where have you gone, who shone three hundred years ago: glory, like grass, slowly withers; God knows how it will be from now on! This is how it is seen in the world today: when a craft migrates somewhere, the brewer gladly welcomes it – this must be said: if not when it arrives, at least when it leaves.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Shoemaker. Let us rejoice, let us be glad: He brings shoes from Olomouc.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: My dear little rascal! Where there is a shoemaker, there is an awl; where there is an awl, there is a wire; the shoemaker stands in the gate in red breeches.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I don't want you anymore, Andulka, I don't want you: cobblers come after you, cobblers. They come after you worse and worse: you smell of cobbler's tar, tar.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Why does the Lord God punish us? Because of those damn cobblers: they sew on Sunday, and drink on Monday, so He does not want it to rain.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Behind our meadow, the cobblers knock: some with a mallet, some with a hand.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The shoemaker got angry over a miserable thing: that fleas were biting him; he had no help against them – so he made himself a cage!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The swallow chirps, it has white and black feathers, it gets up in the morning, calls out, the shoemakers grumble. "Master, Satan! we have no bristles: get up in the morning and pray, and go to the stable." I will not go to the stable, I would rather go to the field: our master’s servant is herding the pig, I will ask him. So that he will allow me to shave the pig: that we will also toast him, I will beckon to him.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: On Mount Tábor, an ox died there; the cobblers were coming from the fair, they took half of it. The cobblers ran over, took the tail, and kept it for beet for a long time.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A young shoemaker was born, let us rejoice! The old one made a cage for him, let us be merry! From a life of hardship, from the shoemaker's family, he was born to us.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A boot climbs through the window, the shoemaker calls to it: "Come here! If you have something torn, I'll sew it with thread." Kramflek's awl was at work: it jumped onto the spindle, broke the stick. That round stone jumped into the stones, from the stones onto the shelf – now it's all over.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: We carpenters must try a lot: whether it's cold or hot, we must get to work. We get up in the morning, go to work before the sun rises: my dearest walks through the market square. She brings me breakfast, coffee with good cream: "Here you go, my dear Jeníček! From my lady mother."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am a weaver from Nymburk, from Nymburk, I spin linen like a string, like a string; I spin linen thick, thin, for pretty girls' skirts.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have a nice little garden– coo-, coo-, I have a nice little garden: roses are growing in it, no one can get to them; I have a nice little garden– coo-, I have a nice little garden.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Our oven has fallen, our oven has fallen, who will build it for us? The bricklayer is not home, the bricklayer is not home, the carpenter won’t fix it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: We are lads, we are lads, we are free lads: we can wander even into foreign lands. And you, brother comrades! Pack my bundle for me, then escort me a bit with cheerful music. The bundle is already packed, it lies on the table: carry it out for me, my maiden, carry it out of the door. She took the bundle, carried it outside, and cried bitterly: "I wish I had never known you, my dearest!" There was love, there was love, four loads of coachman's love: now it is gone and will not return even for a tailor's thimble. That thimble is bottomless, love has run out of it: do not think, my maiden, that you will be my wife.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I wander away from here, I will tie knot to knot: that I have, my lady, so quickly grown tired of you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: She didn't want salad, nor parsnip with oil, with oil; she tied her dress, she wanted to wander with Matěj, with Matěj.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I was just away from home for a week, and they were already asking me if they had prepared something for me, if they were going to give it to me: what do you want? Here I am, what are you going to give me, give it here!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Thieves. Thieves, thieves walk day and night, and whatever they see, they take everything.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I was going to that Šárová Lhota, I saw nine shirts on the fence. I took five of them and rode away; I left four still on the fence. When I was approaching the black forest, I looked to see how many I was carrying. If only a bad wind hadn't blown into it, so that I would have rather taken all nine!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: We are two from the same village, stealing geese and chickens. If the overseer were also a dog, the whole village would steal with us.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Saints, saints, saints! Lend me three gold coins: when I become a saint, I will repay you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A Jewish woman went to Slaný, carrying a soaked Jew: the sly Jew did not want to go! The Jewish woman went behind the shop, bought herself marzipan; the Jew also bought wafers.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Our Jew Lieberman wandered for seventy years: he came to Krakow, bought potatoes.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: People listen, pay attention, I’ll tell you what I want to say, pay attention, oh dear! oh dear! pay attention. I’ll tell you, you will be amazed: I have a crush on Kháčo, you don’t know, but I do. I ask Kháčenka if he’s home alone, if I could come to a party there, if I could give her something. I shout: Kháčenka! my dear lover! I will give you a Turkish scarf or a nice cloth. She then says to me that it can’t be: if I give her a little treat, she will then want me. If I gave her a treat, where would I get the money? You’d better be quiet, Kháčenka, so I don’t recognize you! No! if it’s already going to be like that, damn! now that’s enough: if I give her a little treat, I’ll take Kháčo then.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The Gypsies are coming from Talina, from Talina, I will let them talk to me: talk to me, dear Gypsies! Who am I supposed to catch?

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: It was winter, it was muddy, a gypsy girl was born; she was born on a Sunday, so that everyone would know about it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The Sedláčeks have plenty: whatever they earn, they blow it all; a loaf of bread costs four guilders, wheat is a ducat, and lentils the same.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: They always ask, who are they playing for? For the farmer! Don’t ask anything, just play for him – for the farmer! The farmer must get up to cut the slice for the little horse.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Be cheerful, farmer, even if you haven't sown a single grain: the skylark doesn't sow, yet it sings happily. Be cheerful, farmer, be cheerful: in your pantry you have a tub of cabbage, in the cellar some potatoes, have a drink upstairs!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: You can stomp your feet, little farmer, you can stomp your feet when you sell a bushel of wheat for a ducat!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Rychtář of Klukov, don't carry anything for yourself: you have a lot of grain and fat mares, you boast a lot!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A peasant, God's creation, a beast from birth: he kneels before the priest, groans before the master, gets drunk in the pub, kills the villagers.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The farmer is a big predator, he smeared the cottager; then he thought again about how to clean him up: he threw him into the pond.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A farmer from Prague is traveling, taking a farmer's wife with him: she promises him that she will give him whatever he is carrying on the wagon.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The farmer took a blind maid, a deaf mare, a dull scythe; he is cutting grass behind the cottage. Dry grass, blind maid, deaf mare, dull scythe: the farmer only cuts what crunches.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Thistle feathers. A bird flew onto a thistle, from the thistle onto a pear tree, tore a cushion there: a farmer and a farmer's wife are running, they want to collect the feathers.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Careless farmer. What are you doing? You’re not plowing or harvesting: farmer, what are you doing? Winter will come, frost, how will you survive? “I don’t care, as long as I have a jug in my hand: when I drink a lot, I don’t ask how I’ll sow this year!”

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The farmer is carrying manure and manure and manure, the farmer is carrying manure; he loaded it with two shovels, when he came through the gate: "Huja, ploši, huja huja huj, huja, ploši, huj!" The farmer is carrying manure and manure and manure, the farmer is carrying manure; he does not want to do well because he does not know how to farm: "Stop, little horse, stop and stop and stop, until I clear the manure."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: We are peasants in our village, plucked like dogs: if the mayor were a dog, the schoolteacher a dog, the steward a dog, the village would be full, full, full of dogs!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The farmer sold the horse, and cried for it for a week. And the lad [complained] to the oxen that they had no water. And the peasant woman to the cows that they had no grass. And the maid to the goats because of great hardship.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Unploughed field! Who will plough you? The wheels are broken, the ropes are tied, the oxen are poor.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Whose field is that unplowed? Mark's. We'll go to it, we'll plow it finely.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Whose plowed but unplanted field is that? It belongs to my son Fabian. Why didn’t you plow it, my son? "Because my plow broke in the field." If it broke, have it repaired: how could I do without you, my son? "Then why don’t you love me, father, if you send me away? Don’t send me away, I will go myself: I will mount a horse and become a hussar."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: What kind of farmer will you be, what kind of farmer will you be, if you don't know how to work with a plow, if you don't know how to work with a plow?

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Farmer, farmer, farmer, once again a farmer, he broke his plowshare, what will he use to plow then?

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The wild duck flew in from above, called to the raftsman to steer from deep waters. He steers as best as he can in that green foliage; his little horses stand up, and he can't go anywhere.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I am above the yard, rafting under the yard, pretty black-eyed girls will call out to me. When I am above the yard, rye grows under the yard, pretty black-eyed girls will chirp to me.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I was driving the softwood from fence to fence: a pretty girl in a spotted kerchief came to me. I would have been driving even more if I had been driving a pretty blue-eyed girl whom I would have loved.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Look at the peasant, how he shouts to himself, look at the peasant, how he yells: he yells from deep within: blue-eyed girl, take care, take care!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I hired someone to work the lord’s field: I had it plowed by Jewish oxen. I sowed it, sowed pennies, sevens: nettles and thistles grew on it!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The doll has a mare on the hillside: see how those little horses are neighing! hop, hop, little steed! lift your foot, I will nail a horseshoe on you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Oh my God! What shall I endure: I have to trudge after that plow the whole week. I don’t get enough sleep, I don’t eat properly: I wear myself out the whole week following that plow. When I enter a pub, the farmer stands in the doorway, threatening with a stick. Come on, boy, come home with me: if you don’t, I’ll send for the patrol.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Whose little horses, whose are they? They trampled our rye, they trampled our barley, it was not yet ripe. Whose little horses, whose are they? They trampled our rye; they trampled our barley, from end to end, all of it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Whose ponies are these, these ponies with little ribbons hanging on them, hanging? The ponies belong to the farmer, the ribbons belong to the farmhand.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: My raven-colored horse jumps nicely when I bring him oats on the field; he jumps nicely, he neighs nicely when I bring him oats on the meadow.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have horses, nice horses, they graze for me in the pasture: I have little horses, I do, I graze them myself.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have hobbies, lyres, lyres: they will bring me to my dear one.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have a dun horse, it has golden stripes, steel horseshoes, and silk manes.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have horses, black horses, these are my horses: when I give them oats, they jump hop! I have horses, black horses, these are my horses. I have horses, black horses, these are my horses: when I give them feed, they run into a leap; I have horses, black horses, these are my horses. I have horses, black horses, these are my horses: when I give them clover, they neigh happily; I have horses, black horses, these are my horses.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have a Valach, a lazy Valach, he grazes: when he grazes enough, he then carries me to my dear, lovely one in a short time. I have a mare, she has a beautiful white blaze: when I give her oats, I will saddle her, she will carry me to my beloved.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I was going to my beloved, the larks were already singing; when I arrived at the stable, the black horses were neighing. Don't neigh, black horses, I will give you a bit of hay; before you eat the hay, I will take a little nap.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I was pasturing the horses, sleepiness came over me, directly – directly – a little nap, the horses entered the rye. A sneaky farmer came to me, the steward of that rye: "What are you doing here, you scoundrel! Why do you have horses in the rye?" And I am not a scoundrel, I am a good mother's son: if someone else said that to me, I would stand up to them! I have served you for seven years, and I have lost nothing from you: only one hoof injury, and I paid for that. I have served you for seven years, and I have stolen nothing from you: only one small loaf, for which I fell from the attic. I have served you for seven years, and you gave me nothing: only one little shirt, and you took it back from me. I am an honest lad, no one has anything against me: only your Bětulinka, and she will not say anything against me. If she wanted to tell, she could not: because she herself used to take me to the rose garden.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: And I myself, always alone, clean my hobbies, and I myself, always alone, groom my hobbies; he cleans his hobbies, makes love to a pretty girl: and I myself, always alone, groom my hobbies.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Back then I felt good when I was grazing horses, back then I felt good when I was grazing black horses, nicely loving the girls; back then I felt good when I was grazing horses.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I was grazing the horses, when I was grazing the horses, girls used to come after me and I used to tease them.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: As long as I live, I have not harmed anyone, as long as I live, I have not saved anyone’s livestock: only once some wheat for our gamekeeper, and he reported me to the office.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: And those gentleman's horses have nice equipment: they have proper grooms and take good care of them. And those gentleman's cows have little grass, they have lazy maids who don’t want to go to them.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Janeček’s mares don’t want to eat grain, only grass from the wasteland – where will I sadly get any? I’ll go to the meadows for it, grass with clover grows there, I’ll go there, pick it, and give it to the horses in the trough.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Behind our brown barn grazes: once it has grazed there, it will move on again.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I was going to Prague for peas, for peas, I dropped a wagon on the way, a wagon. If I had given it oats, oats, it would have brought me to Prague.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Filípek’s shepherds, they were herding at night: when they were herding, they were shivering from the cold, those Filípek’s shepherds. That poor old shepherd has already laid down his bones out there on the trails: “But I drove him out when it was broad daylight – it was exactly Christmas Eve!” Master Filípek runs out of the house: “Damn it – the old man is lying there! I did drive him out just on Christmas Eve, but the beast is still lying there!” Godfather! Our Tomáš Filípek sends his regards: so you don’t delay, take the cart and the winch, and bury the shepherd.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The peasant went to yoke the ox, but forgot to yoke it. Go, my dear, for it, but you know well about it. It is in the stable, in the corner, hanging on the yoke. "I will not go for it, I know I will not be yours." Go, my dear, anyway, I have little horses in the river. When I water them, I will persuade you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A peasant was going to the mill, leaving the whip at home: "Peasant, go back, take the whip with you, then go to the mill." The peasant keeps driving, singing happily: just as he was unaware, his horses got spooked, and then trouble happened. The peasant shouts: "Oh no! There's a big ditch here." He jumps down from the cart, tries to catch the mare, but she threw him off. The peasant lies in the ditch, the horses keep running: "What a fool I was, not to take the whip!" he sadly reflects. Well, boy, too bad for you, why didn’t you take the whip! Go back again, take the whip with you, we won’t laugh at you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Foreman, foreman! The shepherd dog won't pull for you: cut the hat, make a yoke, then it will pull for you. Foreman, little foreman! Don’t take from the manor: rather take a girl nicely from the village than some fancy lady.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am a teamster, I know that well, I understand my wagon well: you are onion traders, carrying onions: will you also take me with you?

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am a coachman from Prague, I have black horses, horses that are black, golden horseshoes. I am a coachman from Vienna, I have dapple gray horses, chestnut horses, they look nice.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I am a guy from Vienna, I have a dapple gray horse, I have dapple gray ponies, I will put golden bridles on them. When I go plowing, I will call to you: come, my dear, come drive, my horses don’t want to walk.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Our Jirka was fording, the valach struck, uho – uho – uho – uho – uho – uho – struck.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When you, peasant, are the master, cut your own strip: cut a tiny one, I will go after the girl; cut a tiny little one, I will go after a little mouthful. My dear Andulinko! let's go outside, it’s hot here: once we cool down, we will talk about something; we will have a little kiss, then we will go back to the room.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I served at the rectory for a year, I had a poor allowance there: I served at the rectory for the priest; I couldn't lead the mare, I had to ride it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Pay me, farmer, for my service, I will not serve: I have promised a girl to myself, I will get married.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I was little, I was raised like a shiny red apple. Now I have grown up, I am quite big: I am made entirely of sorrow.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I served under the major, I used to drive the gentlemen: now they put me in the yard, I have to herd the cows.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: There is no bread in the Lhota estate; whoever goes there to serve will not get enough to eat. Little at noon, and nothing for supper: if it continues like this, I will leave here.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When the lark starts to chirp, I don't want to get out of bed in the morning. It chirps the first time, I wake up; it chirps a second time, I turn over. It chirps the third time, the day is white: then I have to get up, dear people!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: They were digging, plowing, I was still lying down: they sang, yelled, I was running too. Dig, plow, I can't anymore: sing, yell, I will help you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Whose meadow is that by the pond? Típal's. Hey, hey, hey! Típal is not home: we'll go on it, mow it down to the ground.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A farmer is mowing in the meadow; his scythe does not want to cut; he always looks around, raising his eyes to the sky to see if the sun is high. When it was past noon, he took the scythe on his shoulder: he ran home and scolded his wife because she did not prepare lunch.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Our little clover! You have a nice scent: on our field, everything is wilting, but you are green.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Green grass, that is my pleasure; green grass, that is my blanket: when it begins to sprout in spring, I don’t want to suffer at home: green grass, that is my blanket. Green grass, that is my resting place; green grass, that is my blanket: when I feel like it, I sit on it, when I feel like it, I lie down on it: green grass, that is my blanket.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: In our little orchard by the valley, the grass never dies, never dies and never burns: because young men come to it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I have a meadow by the valley, on it the grass does not wither, does not wither, hup, hup, hup! does not wither, hup hup, hup hup!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: If you girls tell me where you go for that grass? "We go for it to Březí: there is plenty of grass there, enough to lie down."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation:
Will you, maidens, tell us where you go for the grass?  
“We know well, but we won’t tell where we go for the grass. We go to the perennials, where grass with clover grows: we know well, but we won’t tell where we go for the grass.”  

“Will you, lads, tell us where you ford your horses?”  
“We know well, but we won’t tell where we ford our horses. We ford them in the Danube, only their ears are visible: we know well, but we won’t tell where we ford our horses.”

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: All, all the Klášter girls, all, all the Klášter maidens! You walk on the grass of the venerable lord: beware, beware, do not do that!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: What does my darling do at Christmas, what does my darling do in the summer? She picks darnel in the wheat, she picks darnel in the rye.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Girls, go to bed, you have to get up in the morning: when the shepherd blows the horn, you have to go mushroom picking.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The fish daughters are cutting cabbage under the grove: prepare the horses, we will go for them with the carriage!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The girl swept the room, the girl swept the hall; she found a button under the bench and was pleased with it. When the farmwoman saw it, she said: "Hey, girl, hey! Whatever you find in the room, just bring it here!"

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The Leskouce are in the valley, there are good housewives there: they sell small wheat and drink good jelly.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I grow up, I will be a farmer's wife: come to us, I will give you a good jug of milk.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: There is no greater pleasure than a cow in the barn: in the morning we milk her, in the evening we eat the milk.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Goat. Have you seen my goat, my goat white? The goat is white, full of tallow, full of tallow, full of marrow, full of lard.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Where do you look for happiness when you don't have it yourself? You have one goat, you tie it to the cart, and four are waiting for it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Káča, Káča Kulichova! Do you, do you have geese at home? The geese are in the yard, Káča is in the pantry, cutting, cutting bread for the geese.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Duckling, little duck! Where are our geese? "Our geese are not at home, a great flood came for them, they are spinning there."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Behind our little barn, behind our barn, there stood a farmer with spring rye, the geese will take it from him. Go, my dear, go after them, go, my dear, chase them away: wait, crested goose, you will be beaten by me!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I was peas on the mountains, in the valley a mixture: two deer came to it, they ate it all from me..

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: In the mountains, they sow peas; in the valley, lentils: come to us, I'll give you a little in a cup; in the cu– in the cu– in the cup, since you used to be my little girl, in the cu– in the cu– in the cup, since you were my doll.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The shepherd grazes in the green grove, the shepherdess grazes in the black forest: come to me, my dear, lovely maiden! let us delight our hearts.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Shepherded the little sheep in the green grove; shepherded the little sheep in the black forest: I shouted: Hale halihou! and my dear again: Hela helilou! hey, there under the grove we met.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: She was herding sheep, she fell down the hill, she fell into a wide ravine; she was herding rams, she fell into a pit, she fell into a deep pit.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: There is a small grove over there, there is a meadow over there; shepherds are grazing there, they have a fire there, on the fire a kettle, on the kettle a sheep: they have already sold the skin to the Jew in the village.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The sun sets beyond the mountains: herders are baking potatoes. The sun sets behind the church: herders are climbing onto the bed.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: On the meadow walked a fairy with three wreaths: one for herself, the second for you, the third she hung up. She hung it on green spruce branches: do not give me away, mother, to the poor lad! "I won't give you to a poor one, the rich one doesn't want you." I would like someone who herds sheep. The one who herds sheep has a lot of butter, a lot of butter, a lot of cheese: with him I would like to graze.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: At Christmas, the long night, a man sleeps long; one does not hear the swallows, only the wind blowing: blow, little wind, from the Danube, so that this winter melts quickly, so that the sheep can graze in the common again.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Váša’s servant has an oven, he blows the horn on it; the reeves have Andulka, he goes there for her. “Come, Andulka, come with me, take the green skirt: we’ll go under the little grove to the mowed meadow.”

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Shame, girl, shame on you, that the servant is dealing with you; shame, girl, shame on you, that the servant is going here with you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The redhead drives oxen; they drive her with a stick: Redhead! drive them home, "Redhead! I won’t drive them."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The parakeet peed on the hemp, the frog jumped into her pocket; the next day she only peed, the frog jumped out of the little pocket.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: What work will be done in the Sedlec Castle? The girls will be planting 1) fragrant marigolds. One will be hoeing, the other will be planting: come and see how they will grow.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The sun is setting behind the mountains: let us off work, overseers! Let us go, oh please: we will come back to you again tomorrow.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The sun sets behind the mountains: let us off work, foremen! Lightning flashes around, I feel lonely here – the way to my beloved is not near.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Please, Mr. Administrator, kindly forgive me for the work: I will make it up to you, God willing, on Saturday.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The kind steward of Lužany: he punishes his people as he wishes; he punishes them, leads them to good: so that they please everyone.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Guys, you’re damn stubborn! If you don’t want to listen to the officers – you’re the best for the army!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Over the dam, over the dam, over the little pond, the bailiff chased the lady's maid; she laughed at him, she seemed beautiful to him: "Come closer, come closer! I'll give you a little kiss!"

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Foreman! Half a gold coin that the foreman's wife has something new. Half a gold coin, a whole gold coin: that the foreman's wife has a spotted scarf.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Caretaker, where is the caretaker to graze the horses? He is sitting by the stove, he has a pot of cream; caretaker, take it from him, put it in the pantry.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Why do you ask where the bailiff is? If the bailiff's wife comes, she will beat you like a bailiff, like a bailiff!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: If you, steward, do not give me your daughter, I will tell on you that you are cheating: you climb onto the wagon, take some; you bring it to the mill, and grind it for yourself.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: On the lord's threshing floor, there are long slats: don't go there, girl, they will fall on you.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Why do you cry, my dear, beloved Andulka? – How can I not cry, lament for myself? I have to go into service, serving at the courtyard. The service at the courtyard is bad, the housekeeper is not good. Peas undercooked, tasteless and bland. Porridge from rancid flour – how my heart grieves! Meat is never served, except on the feast day! Garlic soup, work without rest. The housekeeper walks with a stick, chasing us over the field.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Nobody knows what I will try, I will try, nobody knows what I will try: I have to eat roasted soup in a dry pan the whole week.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Are you, girl, the butcher’s? “I’m not, I’m not.” Do you want some sausage? “Give it here, give it here.”

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: A little sour, a little sweet: it was cooked by mother.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Something round, something smooth, something sour, something sweet. The pea is round, the lentil is smooth, the cabbage is sour, the carrot is sweet.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Ah, ah, ah! I burned the peas; the soup boiled over, how sadly I endured it – ah, ah, ah! I burned the peas.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Whose cabbage is this left unhoed by the forest? We will go after them, hoe them – hop, here we come!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Not all cabbage is the same: some are sauerkraut; some are ungreased, some only slightly.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: For a penny's worth of scraps and for two pieces of meat, so that our gang can eat.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Pučala has a dear little pea, she carried it to her beloved on a little plate: "From forest to forest, little pea I carry, pu – pu – pu – pu – pu – pu – little pea, I carry it to my beloved on a little plate."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Káča got angry at Kuba: she cooked him a porridge from offal; when he eats it all, he will be very full.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Peas and groats, that’s silly! We have those every day: but dumplings made from white flour are only once a week.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I went to my little lady, she was baking dolky; I asked her for a piece – she said they were hot. She always slowly said: "Go away, Honzíček!" I didn’t want to go away, so I grabbed her twice.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: She baked small cakes from white flour, sprinkled them with gingerbread spice, and threw them to Honzíček through the open window: "Eat, Honzíček! They are good, they are sprinkled, dotted; if you like them, there are more like that."

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Good hills from Barunka used to be good hills, good hills from Barunka are good hills: she sprinkled them with gingerbread, she pushed them through the window; good hills from Barunka, good hills they are.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The girl served the bailiff: she didn't want to eat black bread. She climbed up to the attic, pouted: Mr. Bailiff! I won't stay here. I'd rather serve in the mill, I won't long for the valley there. On weekdays, pastries; on Sunday, meat: eat up, eat up, hungry crowd!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: She baked buns, she baked poppy seed pastries, she sent them with a soldier to Maří in Prague: they were taken from her at the gate.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The whole week cheese buns, and on Sunday doughnuts: may the old witch be hit by the hangman for making them so small! The whole week cheese buns, and on Thursday dumplings: may the old witch be taken by the devils for making them so big!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: None is like our brewer's wife: she bakes buns with poppy seeds and sends them to the soldier. Before she makes the buns, she kneads money into them: when the buns are cut, the money rolls out.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: If I had what I wanted, I wouldn’t complain: a slice of bread and a piece of butter, I would rejoice.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: I lost my red clay pipe, I don't know which hour, whether yesterday or today – or if a bird took it from me.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Pušpersky girls were baking rozpek: it melted all over the oven; they sent for Urban to sprinkle bran under it.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Under the lordly stables flow little streams: that lordly maids bake flatbreads; they kneaded them with cold water: nevertheless, they praised them saying they would be good. Marjána with long fingers came there, she took away all the flatbreads: give her a bell, a little pot around her neck, so she can ring it for that flatbread.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Kačenka has a sloe, she cooked it in a mug, cooked it, steamed it on a green turf.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Our mom is dancing today, she invited the whole village: from the whole village all the dogs to our buttermilk!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: In that Láza, there is a kind neighbor who managed a nice feat: he took a gulp of strong buttermilk until his lungs trembled.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: The peasants are scaring us, scaring that potatoes will be more expensive: but no, my dear son! we have enough for the whole winter.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Horo, horo, potato! My darling is not at home, not at home, she is in the field: digging up potatoes there.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: When I went to heaven, I stopped by hell; the oldest Lucifer was hanging around there. And I nicely asked him for something: but he said no, that I hadn’t contributed.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Driving out the bat. When a bat stays in a house, the farmer, wanting to expel it, takes a drum and beats it all around the house and its surroundings, saying: I beat, I beat the drum: come out, bat, out of the house!

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: We are the boys, we are, we would love: we must not love, we must go to the army.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: My saber fell from my black horse: give it to me, my dear, if you are so sincere to me. She handed over the saber and began to cry: don’t cry, girl, don’t cry for me, I went voluntarily.

label: Písně o stavích, živnostech a jiných stránkách života občanského
translation: Four horses in the yard, none of them restless. The little son drives them, Nanýnka's beloved. He rides with them to the field to plow the corn cockle. When he has plowed everything, he calls to his beloved. "Come, my dear, come to me, I will tell you how I feel. My little head hurts, my mother beat me. She beat me because of you, so that I would leave you." "Let them not beat you for me, you can go to another." "I won't go to another, I'd rather join the army."
